description: Vaja bori is a Bengali vegetable dish made with sun-dried lentil dumplings (bori) that are deep-fried until crisp, often combined with seasonal vegetables such as pumpkin, potato, or leafy greens and tempered with spices like panch phoron, turmeric, and mustard oil. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice at the start of a traditional Bengali meal.

Chingri kochuri is a Bengali pastry consisting of a deep-fried puffed bread filled with spiced shrimp (chingri) and typically lentils or split peas. It is traditionally served as a breakfast dish or appetizer, often accompanied by potato curry, pickles, and chutney.

Kumro chingri machher botti is a Bengali dish consisting of shrimp and fish combined with pumpkin, typically bound together and fried or baked into patties or croquettes. The mixture is seasoned with spices and herbs, then served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by chutney or rice.

Chingri machher muri ghonto is a Bengali dish combining shrimp and rice flakes (muri) with onions, spices, and often mustard oil, typically prepared by stir-frying the ingredients together. The dish is traditionally served as a light meal or snack, particularly popular in West Bengal and Bangladesh cuisine.

Aam kasundi chingri is a Bengali dish of prawns cooked in a tangy mango pickle-based sauce called kasundi, which typically contains mustard seeds, fenugreek, and spices. The dish is traditionally served with rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Fried eggplant is prepared by slicing eggplant, coating the pieces with flour or breadcrumbs, and deep-frying them until golden and crispy. The dish is commonly served as an appetizer, side dish, or vegetable course in Mediterranean and Asian cuisines, often accompanied by dipping sauces such as tahini or soy sauce.

# Pasticcio Caggianese

Pasticcio caggianese is a baked pasta dish from Caiazzo in Campania that combines short pasta with a ragù made from meat, tomatoes, and local cheeses, typically layered in a baking dish and finished in the oven. This rich, traditional dish is customarily served as a main course during festive occasions and family celebrations in the Campania region.

Crudités is a French appetizer consisting of an assortment of raw vegetables such as carrots, celery, radishes, bell peppers, and tomatoes, typically cut into strips or bite-sized pieces and arranged on a platter. It is traditionally served as a starter or part of a charcuterie board, often accompanied by dipping sauces such as vinaigrette or aioli.

# Mochar borar dalna

Mochar borar dalna is a Bengali curry made by sautéing banana flower buds with onions, tomatoes, and spices, then simmering them in a lentil-based gravy typically seasoned with turmeric, cumin, and ginger-garlic paste. This vegetarian dish is commonly served as a side curry with rice or bread during meals in Bengali households and restaurants.

Alur dom is a Bengali potato curry made by simmering potatoes with onions, tomatoes, and spices such as turmeric, cumin, and bay leaves until tender and infused with flavor. The dish is typically served as a vegetarian accompaniment to rice or bread in everyday Bengali meals and at family gatherings.

Enchor diye chholar dal is a Bengali lentil dish made by cooking dried chickpeas with a tempering of mustard seeds, cumin, and dried red chilies in ghee or oil, then finished with a paste made from grated coconut and poppy seeds. It is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or flatbread during everyday meals and festive occasions in Bengal.

Begun bahari is a Bengali fish curry dish prepared by simmering eggplant with fish, onions, tomatoes, and spices such as turmeric, cumin, and chili powder in a seasoned gravy. It is typically served as a main course alongside steamed rice in Bengali cuisine.

# Dana Methir Torokari

Dana methir torokari is a Bangladeshi vegetable curry made by stir-frying or sautéing cauliflower florets with onions, garlic, ginger, and spices such as turmeric, cumin, and coriander until tender. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or bread at family meals and celebrations.

# Khasta Patabahar

Khasta patabahar is a Pakistani fried pastry dessert made by deep-frying layers of dough with ghee, then binding them with a mixture of condensed milk, cream, and nuts such as almonds and pistachios. It is typically served as a festive sweet dish at celebrations and special occasions, often accompanied by tea or traditional beverages.

# Mocha diye kolaier dal

Mocha diye kolaier dal is a Bengali lentil curry prepared by simmering red lentils with okra (mocha), onions, garlic, ginger, and spices such as turmeric and cumin, typically finished with tempering in ghee or oil. The dish is commonly served as an everyday accompaniment to rice or bread in Bengali households and regional restaurants.

Motor dal er torkari is a Bengali dish made by cooking dried yellow or green split peas (motor dal) with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and dried red chilies, often combined with vegetables like potatoes and finished with a tempering of oil and aromatics. It is typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice or flatbreads as part of a larger Bengali meal.

Monohar bori is a type of dried lentil dumpling originating from Bengal, made by grinding urad dal (black gram) into a paste, which is then shaped into small rounds or drops and sun-dried until hard. These bori are typically used as a crunchy ingredient in Bengali vegetable dishes, fish curries, and stir-fries, where they are fried in oil before being added to the preparation.

Semai er payesh is a Bengali milk pudding made by simmering vermicelli (semai) in full-fat milk along with sugar, cardamom, and often garnished with raisins, cashews, or pistachios until the mixture thickens to a creamy consistency. It is traditionally served during festive occasions, Eid celebrations, and religious gatherings in Bangladesh and West Bengal, and can be presented warm or chilled.

Dimer korma is a Bengali egg curry in which hard-boiled eggs are simmered in a spiced sauce made with onions, yogurt, tomatoes, and a blend of aromatics including ginger, garlic, and whole spices such as cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon. It is commonly served with rice or flatbreads such as roti or paratha as part of a main meal.

Saboor papad is a Bengali-style thin, crisp flatbread typically prepared from lentil or chickpea flour seasoned with spices such as cumin, black pepper, or chili, then dried and either roasted or deep-fried before serving. It is commonly enjoyed as an accompaniment to rice-based meals or served as a light snack alongside chutneys and pickles.

Rat-on-a-stick is a street food preparation in which a whole rat or rat meat is skewered on a stick and cooked over an open flame or grill, often seasoned with local spices or marinades. It is consumed in various parts of Asia and Africa, typically sold by street vendors as a ready-to-eat snack or light meal.

Motor dal vate is a Maharashtrian Indian dish made from dried whole peas (motor/matar) cooked with spices such as mustard seeds, turmeric, and chili, typically prepared as a semi-dry or lightly sauced preparation. It is commonly served as a side dish or snack, often accompanied by flatbreads such as bhakri or chapati.

Shoshar shukto is a Bengali vegetable dish prepared by cooking cucumber (shosha) with a blend of bitter and mild vegetables, tempered with panch phoron, ginger, and mustard or poppy seed paste, and finished with a touch of ghee. It is traditionally served at the start of a Bengali meal alongside steamed rice.

Alur papad is a crispy Indian snack made by thinly slicing potatoes, mixing them with spices such as cumin, chili powder, and salt, and then deep-frying the mixture until golden and brittle. It is typically served as an appetizer or side dish with meals, often accompanied by chutneys or enjoyed on its own as a tea-time snack.

# Vapa doi

Vapa doi is a Vietnamese steamed custard dessert made by whisking egg yolks with sweetened condensed milk and evaporated milk, then steaming the mixture in individual bowls or a large pan until it sets to a smooth, creamy texture. It is typically served chilled or at room temperature as a sweet ending to meals or as a casual dessert snack.

# Chaler Papad

Chaler papad is a Bengali crispy wafer made by mixing rice flour, lentil flour, salt, and spices, then rolling the dough thin and sun-drying it until it becomes brittle. It is typically fried in oil until golden and served as a crunchy accompaniment or snack during meals in Bengali cuisine.

# Sorse Kochu

Sorse kochu is a Bengali condiment made by grinding mustard seeds with green chilies, salt, and spices into a paste, sometimes combined with fish or other proteins. It is typically served as an accompaniment to rice and fish curries in Bengali cuisine, particularly in West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Macher dim diye ombol is a Bengali fish curry where fish is cooked with eggs in a spiced tomato-based gravy, combining the protein of both fish and eggs with aromatic spices. The dish is typically served with rice or bread as part of traditional Bengali cuisine.

Fattoush is a Levantine salad made with mixed greens, tomatoes, cucumbers, radishes, and other fresh vegetables, topped with crispy fried pita bread pieces and dressed with lemon juice and olive oil. The dish is typically served as a light meal or appetizer and is common throughout the Eastern Mediterranean region.

Huevos estrellados are fried eggs served with crispy fried potatoes, traditionally prepared by frying potatoes until golden and then cracking eggs directly over them. The dish is typically served as a main course or light meal, often accompanied by jamón ibérico or other cured meats, and is common in Spanish cuisine.

Korolar shukto is a Bengali vegetable dish prepared by cooking bitter gourd (korola) with other vegetables, tempered with spices such as panch phoron, mustard, and ginger, and often finished with a touch of milk or ghee. It is traditionally served at the beginning of a meal alongside steamed rice, valued for its characteristic bitter flavor profile.

Torta de cañamones is a traditional Spanish flatbread or cake made primarily with hemp seeds (cañamones), which are incorporated into a dough or batter and baked until firm. It is typically served as a snack or dessert, and the hemp seeds give the finished product a slightly nutty flavor and dense texture.

Sanganhèta is an Occitan dish made from poultry blood, typically from chicken or duck, which is coagulated and then cooked with onions, garlic, and sometimes vinegar or wine. It is traditionally served as a rustic main course, often accompanied by bread or potatoes in the farming communities of southern France.

Lauer ghonto is a Bengali vegetarian dish made by finely chopping or grating bottle gourd (lau) and cooking it with mustard oil, spices such as cumin, turmeric, and green chilies, often combined with lentils or grated coconut. It is typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Mulor ghonto is a Bengali dry curry prepared by cooking finely chopped or grated white radish (mulo) with mustard oil, whole spices such as bay leaf and cumin, turmeric, and often incorporating radish greens or fried lentil dumplings (bori). It is a traditional vegetarian dish typically served as a side alongside steamed rice as part of a multi-course Bengali meal.

Kharkhari is typically prepared as a savory dish featuring a blend of spices and regional ingredients, often simmered or slow-cooked to develop a rich, layered flavor. It is commonly served warm as a main course, frequently accompanied by flatbread, rice, or a simple side salad depending on local dining customs.

Mulo shaker chhenchki is a Bengali dry stir-fry made from the stalks and leaves of the radish plant (mulo shak), typically cut into small pieces and cooked with mustard oil, panch phoron (a five-spice blend), dried red chilies, and turmeric. It is a common everyday side dish in Bengali households, served alongside steamed rice as part of a traditional meal.

Fulkopi patar Chhenchki is a Bengali stir-fry preparation made from cauliflower leaves (fulkopi pata), which are chopped and cooked with mustard oil, panch phoron (a five-spice blend), dried red chilies, and turmeric. It is a simple, dry-style dish typically served as a side with steamed rice as part of an everyday Bengali meal.

Shim Chhenchki is a Bengali dry stir-fry made with flat beans (shim/hyacinth beans) cut into pieces and cooked with mustard oil, panch phoron (a five-spice blend), dried red chilies, turmeric, and salt. It is typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Bata mulo shaker ghonto is a Bengali dry stir-fry dish made with radish (mulo) greens or stems, typically cooked with mustard paste (bata), mustard oil, and spices such as turmeric, dried red chilies, and panch phoron (a five-spice blend). It is prepared by sautéing the chopped radish plant parts with the ground mustard mixture until the moisture evaporates, and is traditionally served as a side dish alongside rice in Bengali households.

Hansher Dimer Dalna is a Bengali curry dish made by cooking duck eggs in a spiced gravy prepared with onion, ginger, garlic, tomato, and a blend of whole and ground spices including cumin, coriander, turmeric, and garam masala. The eggs are typically hard-boiled, lightly fried, and then simmered in the sauce until the flavors meld, and the dish is commonly served with steamed rice or flatbreads as a main course.

Dim shorshe is a Bengali preparation of eggs cooked in a mustard-based sauce, typically made with ground mustard seeds, green chilies, turmeric, and mustard oil. It is commonly served as a main dish alongside steamed rice.

Badhakopir ghonto is a Bengali vegetable curry made by stir-frying grated radish (badhakopi) with spices, onions, and sometimes potatoes until softened and lightly caramelized. This dish is typically served as a side accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Potoler ghonto is a Bengali vegetable curry made by stir-frying pointed gourd (parwal) with onions, tomatoes, and spices until the vegetable is tender and slightly caramelized. The dish is typically served as a side accompaniment to rice or Indian bread in Bengali cuisine.

Bhetki machher ghonto is a Bengali fish curry made by cooking bhetki (barramundi) with onions, tomatoes, and spices ground into a paste, creating a thick, flavorful gravy. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Chal bandhakopir ghonto is a Bengali vegetable stir-fry dish made by cooking cabbage and rice together with onions, ginger, garlic, and spices until the ingredients are well combined and softened. The dish is typically served as a side dish or light main course in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by dal or curries.

Omelette er torkari is a Bengali dish in which beaten eggs are first cooked into an omelette, then cut into pieces and simmered in a spiced gravy made with onions, tomatoes, green chilies, turmeric, and other aromatics. It is typically served as a side dish with rice or flatbreads in everyday home cooking across Bangladesh and West Bengal.

Chichingar ghanto is a Bengali vegetable dish prepared by slow-cooking chopped snake gourd (chichinga) with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and green chilies, often tempered with panch phoron and finished with grated coconut or potatoes. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Isi ewu is a traditional Nigerian dish made with goat head meat that is cleaned, cooked, and shredded, then mixed with palm oil, spices, and vegetables such as onions and peppers. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice, cassava fufu, or other starchy accompaniments.

Dimer bora is a Bengali fritter made by coating hard-boiled or beaten eggs in a spiced chickpea flour (besan) batter and deep-frying until golden and crisp. It is commonly served as a snack or side dish, seasoned with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and chili.

Hanser dimer jhol is a Bengali dish consisting of duck eggs cooked in a thin, spiced gravy typically made with mustard oil, turmeric, cumin, coriander, and green chilies. It is commonly served with steamed rice as a everyday home-style meal in Bengal and neighboring regions.

Kanika is a sweet rice dish from Odisha, India, prepared by cooking rice with ghee, sugar or jaggery, and aromatic spices such as cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon, often garnished with raisins and cashews. It is traditionally served as a prasad (religious offering) or festive dish, particularly during temple rituals and ceremonial occasions.

Tuna pasta is prepared by combining cooked pasta, typically spaghetti or penne, with canned or fresh tuna, often sautéed with olive oil, garlic, and tomatoes or a cream-based sauce. It is commonly served as a main course and is a widely prepared everyday dish across European and international home cooking.

Kashmiri pulao is a fragrant rice dish prepared by cooking basmati rice with whole spices such as cinnamon, cardamom, and bay leaves, along with meat (typically mutton or chicken) and dried fruits like apricots and cherries. The dish is traditionally served as a main course in Kashmiri cuisine, often accompanied by raita or yogurt-based condiments.

Masala bhat is a Maharashtrian rice dish prepared by tempering rice with spices, typically including cumin, mustard seeds, curry leaves, and dried chilies in ghee or oil, then mixing in vegetables and sometimes lentils. It is commonly served as a main course alongside yogurt or pickle as part of everyday meals in Maharashtra.

Rice himpit is a Malaysian dish made by cooking rice and then compressing it into a dense, compact block, typically wrapped in banana leaves during the cooking process. The compressed rice is sliced and served as an accompaniment to curries, sambals, and other dishes in traditional Malay meals.

Panta bhat is a Bengali dish prepared by soaking leftover cooked rice in water overnight or for several hours, creating a fermented, slightly sour rice porridge. It is typically served for breakfast or light meals, often accompanied by vegetables, pickles, and fried ingredients such as dried fish or potatoes.

Chitranna is a Karnataka rice dish prepared by mixing cooked rice with spices, lemon juice, and typically vegetables or peanuts. It is commonly served as a light lunch or snack, often accompanied by yogurt or pickle.

Garlic rice is prepared by stir-frying cooked rice with minced or finely chopped garlic, typically in oil, until the garlic is fragrant and the rice grains are heated through and separated. It is commonly served as a side dish or base for rice bowls across Japanese and international cuisines.

Black pasta is made from wheat flour dough colored with squid ink (nero di seppia), which gives it a distinctive dark appearance and subtle briny flavor. It is typically served with seafood-based sauces, often featuring clams, shrimp, or other shellfish, and is a traditional dish from Italian coastal regions.

Phirni is a rice-based dessert made by grinding rice or rice flour with milk and cooking it until thickened, typically flavored with cardamom, rose water, and garnished with nuts like pistachios and almonds. It is traditionally served chilled in shallow earthenware bowls as a sweet course in Indian cuisine, particularly popular during festivals and special occasions.

Char siu egg rice consists of white rice stir-fried with diced char siu (Chinese barbecued pork), scrambled or fried eggs, and vegetables such as peas and onions. This dish is commonly served as a quick meal or casual lunch in Cantonese cuisine and Chinese restaurants.

Dal bhat is a rice and lentil dish where cooked lentils (dal) are served alongside steamed or boiled rice as the base components. The dish is traditionally prepared by simmering lentils with spices and water to create a thick curry, then served with plain rice and typically accompanied by vegetables, pickles, and other condiments.

Saealsim is a Korean rice ball prepared by shaping cooked rice into small bite-sized balls, often mixed or coated with ingredients such as sesame seeds, seaweed, or vegetables. It is typically served as a portable snack, side dish, or light meal, commonly included in lunchboxes or picnic spreads.

Apple er payesh is a Bengali-style rice pudding in which small pieces of apple are cooked together with rice, milk, and sugar until the mixture thickens to a creamy consistency, often flavored with cardamom or bay leaves. It is a variation of the traditional South Asian dessert payesh, typically served chilled or at room temperature during festive occasions or as a dessert course.

Komolalebur payesh is a Bengali rice pudding made by simmering rice in milk with sugar and flavored with orange (komola lebu) juice or zest, resulting in a citrus-infused variation of the traditional payesh. It is typically served chilled or at room temperature as a dessert during festive occasions, religious ceremonies, or family gatherings in Bengali-speaking regions.

Chili shrimp is a dish of prawns cooked in a thick, spiced tomato-based sauce made with chili, garlic, ginger, and eggs, resulting in a savory, mildly spicy gravy. It is commonly served with steamed rice or bread for dipping and is a widely recognized preparation in Chinese and Southeast Asian cuisine.

Mulor payesh is a Bengali rice pudding prepared by simmering rice in milk with sugar until the mixture thickens, traditionally flavored with cardamom and sometimes garnished with dried fruits or nuts. It is served as a dessert or sweet dish, commonly prepared during festivals, religious ceremonies, and celebratory occasions in Bengali households.

Sim posto is a traditional Bengali dish typically prepared by cooking flat green beans (sim) with a ground poppy seed paste (posto), often tempered with nigella seeds, green chilies, and mustard oil. It is commonly served as a light vegetarian side dish alongside steamed rice and dal as part of a home-style Bengali meal.

Komola pulao is a Bengali rice dish in which long-grain rice is cooked with aromatic spices such as cardamom, cinnamon, and cloves, then garnished with segments or juice of komola (mandarin orange or sweet orange), giving it a distinctive citrus flavor. It is typically served on festive occasions and special gatherings, often accompanied by rich meat curries or korma.

Man kochu bata narkel diye is a Bengali preparation made by grinding taro corms (man kochu) into a paste and cooking them with grated coconut, mustard paste, green chilies, and spices. It is typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice in traditional Bengali meals.

Vegetable chop is a deep-fried croquette originating in the Bengal region, made from a spiced mixture of boiled vegetables such as beetroot, carrot, and potato, shaped into an oval or cylindrical form, coated in breadcrumbs, and fried until golden. It is commonly served as a street food snack or appetizer, typically accompanied by mustard sauce or kasundi.

Aamer achar is a South Asian pickle made from raw green mangoes that are cut into pieces and preserved with mustard oil, turmeric, chili, fenugreek, and other spices through a curing process that may take several days. It is typically served as a condiment or side accompaniment alongside rice, flatbreads, or main dishes in Bengali and broader South Asian cuisine.

Aam pulao is a rice dish prepared by cooking basmati rice with raw mango pulp, ghee, and spices such as cumin and bay leaves, creating a tangy and aromatic one-pot meal. It is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by yogurt or pickle, and is popular in Indian cuisine during mango season.

Baked pork chop rice is a Hong Kong dish consisting of rice topped with a breaded and pan-fried pork chop, then finished in the oven until the rice is crispy and the meat is cooked through. The dish is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by a light sauce or gravy.

Alu posto is a Bengali vegetable curry made with potatoes cooked in a paste of ground poppy seeds, typically tempered with mustard oil and spices. The dish is commonly served as an accompaniment to rice or flatbread in Bengali cuisine.

Jhinge alu posto is a Bengali vegetable dish made with ridge gourd (jhinge) and potatoes cooked with a paste of poppy seeds (posto), along with spices and mustard oil. The dish is typically served as a side curry with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Potol posto is a Bengali vegetable curry featuring pointed gourd (potol) cooked with a spice paste made from poppy seeds (posto), mustard oil, and aromatics. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or flatbread in Bengali cuisine.

Dhokar dalna is a Bengali curry made with dhokar—steamed cakes of ground lentils and spices—that are cut into pieces and cooked in a spiced gravy of onions, tomatoes, and mustard oil. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Begun sorse is a Bengali dish in which eggplant (begun) is cooked in a mustard seed-based sauce (sorse), typically prepared by frying sliced eggplant and simmering it in a paste made from ground mustard seeds, green chilies, and mustard oil. It is commonly served as a vegetarian side dish accompanied by steamed rice.

Doi paneer korma is an Indian dish in which cubes of paneer (fresh cheese) are cooked in a sauce made with dahi (yogurt), aromatic spices such as cardamom, cinnamon, and cloves, and often onion and cashew paste to create a creamy, mildly spiced gravy. It is typically served as a main course alongside rice or Indian flatbreads such as naan or roti.

Kancha lonkar achar is a Nepali pickle made from fresh green chilies preserved with mustard oil, fenugreek seeds, turmeric, and salt, sometimes combined with other spices such as jimbu or asafoetida. It is typically served as a condiment alongside rice, lentils, and curries in everyday Nepali meals.

Dhirbalar potol is a Bengali dish typically prepared with pointed gourd (potol), which is commonly stuffed or cooked in a spiced gravy using mustard oil, ginger, cumin, and turmeric. It is often served as a side dish alongside steamed rice or Bengali flatbreads as part of a traditional meal.

Begun methi kopta is a Bengali preparation in which minced eggplant (begun) is combined with fenugreek leaves (methi) and spices, shaped into balls (kopta/kofta), and either fried or cooked in a spiced gravy. It is typically served as a vegetarian main course alongside rice or flatbreads.

Dal moshla kopta is a Bengali dish consisting of spiced lentil dumplings (kopta) prepared by mixing cooked dal with aromatic spices such as cumin, coriander, and chili, then shaping and frying or simmering them in a seasoned gravy. It is typically served as a main course alongside rice or flatbreads.

Enchor doi kopta is a Bengali dish made from raw jackfruit (enchor) shaped into kofta balls, which are fried and then simmered in a spiced yogurt (doi) based gravy containing ingredients such as ginger, cumin, and garam masala. It is typically served as a main course alongside rice or flatbreads and is a common vegetarian preparation in Bengali cuisine.

Doi chhatu kofta is a Bengali vegetarian dish in which koftas made from chhatu (roasted gram flour) bound with spices are fried and then simmered in a yogurt-based (doi) gravy. It is typically served hot with steamed rice, pulao, or Indian flatbreads as part of a main course.

Kharkol pata bata is a Bengali vegetable preparation made by grinding kharkol (taro) leaves into a paste, typically with green chilies, garlic, and mustard oil, then lightly cooking or tempering the mixture. It is generally served as a side dish with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Kankroler narkel korma is a Bengali dish made by cooking kakrol (spiny gourd) in a korma-style sauce prepared with coconut (narkel), typically combined with spices such as cumin, coriander, and turmeric along with yogurt or cream to create a rich, mildly spiced gravy. It is generally served as a vegetarian side dish alongside steamed rice or flatbreads as part of a larger Bengali meal.

Panong kopta is a Bengali-style dish that typically features koftas—soft, spiced dumplings or balls commonly made from minced meat, paneer, or vegetables such as green banana or bottle gourd—simmered in a fragrant, mildly spiced gravy. It is usually served hot with steamed rice, pulao, or Indian breads like luchi or paratha as part of a main course.

Lichu lonkar sorbot is a Bengali beverage prepared by blending lychee pulp with salt and chili, yielding a sweet, salty, and spicy flavor profile distinct from conventional fruit drinks. It is typically served chilled as a refreshing drink, with ripe lychees forming the base and the addition of black salt and green or dried chili providing its characteristic savory-spicy contrast.

Kanch kolar dalna is a Bengali curry made with raw banana flowers (kanch kola) cooked with lentils and aromatic spices including turmeric, cumin, and mustard seeds. The dish is typically served as a vegetarian accompaniment to rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Jhinge narkel is a Bengali vegetable dish made with ridged gourds (jhinge) cooked with grated coconut, mustard oil, and spices including cumin and green chilies. The dish is typically served as a side curry accompaniment to rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Masur dal is a lentil dish made from red lentils that are cooked until soft and often tempered with spices, onions, and ghee or oil. It is typically served as a side dish or main course with rice or flatbread in South Asian cuisine.

Alur chokha kathi roll is prepared by wrapping spiced mashed potato (alur chokha) in a thin flatbread along with onions, green chilies, and chutneys, then rolling it tightly for serving. This Indian street food roll is typically served warm as a handheld snack or light meal, often accompanied by tamarind chutney or yogurt-based dips.

Tucumana is a deep-fried or boiled empanada originating from the Tucumán region of Argentina, typically filled with a mixture of potato, hard-boiled egg, and minced or shredded meat seasoned with spices such as cumin and chili. It is commonly served as a street food or appetizer, often accompanied by salsa criolla or other condiments.

Panthar mangser patla jhol is a Bengali-style thin curry (jhol) prepared with goat or mutton (mangsho) cooked in a light, watery broth seasoned with turmeric, cumin, coriander, ginger, garlic, and green chilies. It is typically served with steamed rice and distinguished from thicker meat curries by its soupy consistency and restrained use of oil and spices.

Bread pakora is an Indian appetizer made by coating sliced bread in a seasoned chickpea flour batter and deep-frying until golden and crispy. It is typically served hot as a snack or starter, often accompanied by chutneys such as tamarind or mint chutney.

Korean black goat stew is a traditional soup dish made by simmering goat meat with vegetables and perilla leaves in a seasoned broth, often including ingredients like perilla seeds, sesame oil, and doenjang (soybean paste). The dish is typically served hot in a stone bowl or regular bowl as a main course, particularly during autumn and winter months in Korean cuisine.

A traditional Argentine sandwich made with bondiola, a seasoned pork shoulder cut, typically served on brioche bread. The meat is often prepared by roasting or slow-cooking and may be finished with lemon juice, then assembled into the bread for serving as a casual meal or street food.

Dheros er kopta is a Bengali vegetable dish in which okra (dheros) is mashed or minced, combined with spices and a binder, and shaped into small balls that are fried before being simmered in a spiced gravy. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice or Indian breads as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Onion rings are prepared by slicing onions into rings, coating them in a seasoned batter, and deep-frying until golden and crispy. They are typically served as a side dish alongside burgers and other entrées.

Chingrir jhol is a Bengali curry prepared by simmering prawns in a light, spiced broth made with onions, tomatoes, and aromatics such as ginger, garlic, and green chilies. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is a staple of Bengali cuisine, particularly in coastal regions of India and Bangladesh.

Bhetki machel jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with bhetki (Asian sea bass), typically cooked in a light, spiced gravy with onions, tomatoes, and mustard oil. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

aliases: kalo jeera begun diye ilish machher jhol
description: Begun diye ilish jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with hilsa fish (ilish) cooked in a broth flavored with eggplant (begun), turmeric, and spices. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is a staple preparation in Bengali cuisine, particularly during the monsoon season when hilsa is in season.

Singi machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with singi fish (a type of catfish), prepared in a spiced gravy with onions, ginger, garlic, and traditional Bengali spices including turmeric and chili. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Bata machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with freshwater bata fish cooked in a light, spiced broth with turmeric, ginger, garlic, and green chilies. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Tangra machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made by simmering fish in a spiced broth with onions, tomatoes, and aromatic spices including turmeric, cumin, and bay leaves. The dish is typically served with steamed rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Khaira machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made by simmering fish in a spiced broth with turmeric, mustard oil, and aromatic spices, creating a light gravy. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Chitol Machher Jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering chitol fish (a freshwater fish native to South Asia) in a gravy made with onions, tomatoes, and spices including turmeric and ginger-garlic paste. The dish is traditionally served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Begun chorchori is a Bengali vegetable curry made by cooking eggplant and other vegetables like potatoes, beans, and peas with mustard oil, nigella seeds, and spices until softened and lightly caramelized. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Pepe diye shol machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering snakehead fish (shol maach) with raw papaya (pepe) in a light, spiced broth typically seasoned with turmeric, cumin, and green chilies. It is commonly served as a everyday home-style dish accompanied by plain steamed rice.

The Among Us chicken nugget is a breaded and fried chicken nugget molded into the shape of the crewmate character from the video game Among Us, typically made from ground or formed chicken meat coated in a seasoned breadcrumb or batter crust. It is served as a snack or children's meal item, often accompanied by dipping sauces such as ketchup or barbecue sauce.

Skefkef is a Moroccan fried dough fritter made from a leavened batter or dough, cooked in oil until golden and puffed. It is typically served as a snack or breakfast item, often accompanied by honey, jam, or butter.

canonicalName: alu tomato diye foli machher jhol

aliases: alu tomato diye foli machher jhol
description: Alu tomato diye foli machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering foli fish (Notopterus notopterus, also known as the bronze featherback) with potatoes and tomatoes in a lightly spiced broth seasoned with turmeric, cumin, and green chilies. It is typically served as a main course alongside steamed rice in everyday Bengali household meals.

Ilish machh kancha kolar jhol is a Bengali curry featuring hilsa fish cooked with raw banana in a savory broth, typically prepared with onions, spices, and often turmeric and mustard oil. The dish is traditionally served with rice and is common in Bengali cuisine during the hilsa fishing season.

canonicalName: pangas machher alu tomato diye jhol
displayName: Pangas machher alu tomato diye jhol
aliases: pangas machher alu tomato diye jhol
description: Pangas machher alu tomato diye jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with pangas (swai) fish, potatoes, and tomatoes cooked together in a light, spiced broth. The dish is typically seasoned with turmeric, cumin, and other spices, and served as a main course with rice or bread.

Roshun sorshe rui machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with rui (a freshwater carp), mustard seeds, and garlic, simmered in a light broth with spices. The dish is traditionally served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Pabda fish is simmered in a light curry sauce flavored with kalojeera (black cumin seeds), along with onions, garlic, ginger, and spices to create a delicate broth. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Narker korma is a Bengali vegetable dish in which coconut is simmered in a mildly spiced korma gravy typically built from yogurt or cream, onions, ginger, garlic, and aromatic spices such as cardamom, cinnamon, and cloves. It is usually served as a main course alongside rice, pulao, luchi, or paratha.

canonicalName: parse machher jhol alu potol diye
displayName: Parse machher jhol alu potol diye
aliases: parse machher jhol alu potol diye
description: Parse machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering fish pieces in a light, spiced broth base with potatoes and pointed gourds (potol), creating a soupy consistency. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is a staple of Bengali cuisine, featuring aromatic spices and often finished with mustard oil or ghee.

canonicalName: alu begun diye bhola machher jhol
displayName: Alu begun diye bhola machher jhol
aliases: alu begun diye bhola machher jhol
description: This Bengali fish curry features freshwater fish (typically rohu or similar varieties) cooked in a light, aromatic broth with potatoes and eggplant. The dish is prepared by simmering the ingredients with spices and served as a main course with rice or bread.

displayName: Begun alu diye punti machher jhol
aliases: begun alu diye punti machher jhol

Mola machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with mola fish (a small freshwater fish) cooked in a spiced broth with onions, tomatoes, and traditional Bengali spices including turmeric and chili. The dish is typically served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Grilled cold noodles are prepared by grilling noodles until they develop a charred exterior, then chilling them before serving. The dish typically combines the grilled noodles with vegetables, sauce, and protein, served cold as a refreshing main course or side dish.

canonicalName: alu tomato diye shingi machher jhol
displayName: Alu tomato diye shingi machher jhol
aliases: alu tomato diye shingi machher jhol
description: Shingi machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with freshwater shingi fish (a type of catfish) cooked in a tomato-based broth with potatoes. The dish is typically prepared by simmering the fish and potato pieces in a spiced tomato gravy and served with steamed rice as a main course.

Fricassée is a stewed dish in which meat, typically chicken or veal, is first sautéed in butter then braised in a creamy white sauce made from stock and egg yolk liaison. The dish is traditionally served hot with rice, noodles, or potatoes as an accompaniment.

Kajoli machher jhol is a light Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering kajoli fish (Corica soborna, a small freshwater fish from the rivers of Bengal and Bangladesh) in a thin, spiced broth typically made with mustard oil, turmeric, cumin, green chilies, and sometimes tomatoes or potatoes. It is commonly served as a main course alongside steamed white rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

canonicalName: pepe totato diye telapia machher jhol
displayName: Pepe totato diye telapia machher jhol
aliases: pepe totato diye telapia machher jhol
description: Pepe totato diye tilapia machher jhol is a Bengali-style light fish curry prepared by simmering tilapia pieces with raw papaya (pepe) and tomato (totato) in a spiced broth typically seasoned with turmeric, cumin, and green chilies. It is commonly served as a everyday home-cooked dish accompanied by steamed rice.

Jhinge pabdar jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering pabda (Ompok pabda, a freshwater catfish) with ridge gourd (jhinge) in a light, spiced broth typically seasoned with turmeric, nigella seeds, green chilies, and mustard oil. It is traditionally served with steamed white rice as a everyday meal in Bengali households of West Bengal and Bangladesh.

canonicalName: sorse shim diye parse machher jhol
displayName: Sorse shim diye parse machher jhol
aliases: sorse shim diye parse machher jhol
description: Sorse shim diye parse machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by cooking parse fish (a small freshwater fish) with flat beans (shim) in a mustard-based sauce, typically made from ground mustard seeds, green chilies, turmeric, and mustard oil. It is a light, pungent jhol (thin curry) commonly served with steamed white rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Alu diye ban machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry where freshwater fish is simmered in a light gravy with potatoes as the primary vegetable component, typically flavored with mustard oil, onions, ginger, garlic, and spices. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as part of Bengali home cooking and regional cuisine.

canonicalName: bori fulkopi diye koi machher jhol
displayName: Bori fulkopi diye koi machher jhol
aliases: bori fulkopi diye koi machher jhol
description: Bori fulkopi diye koi machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering fish with cabbage and bori (lentil dumplings) in a light spiced broth. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Shingi machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with shingi fish (a freshwater catfish) cooked in a spiced gravy that includes fulkopi (cauliflower) as a key vegetable ingredient. The dish is typically prepared by simmering the fish with onions, tomatoes, ginger-garlic paste, and spices, then served with rice or bread as a main course.

canonicalName: alu fulkopi diye bata machher jhol
displayName: Alu fulkopi diye bata machher jhol
aliases: alu fulkopi diye bata machher jhol
description: Alu fulkopi diye bata machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering fish with potatoes and cauliflower in a spiced broth. The dish is traditionally served over steamed rice as a main course component of Bengali meals.

canonicalName: lau shaag diye shingi machher jhol
displayName: Lau shaag diye shingi machher jhol
aliases: lau shaag diye shingi machher jhol
description: This Bengali fish curry features shingi fish (a freshwater catfish) cooked in a spiced broth with leafy greens, typically spinach or similar vegetables, creating a lighter preparation compared to richer curries. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Alu diye topse machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry where fish pieces are cooked with potatoes in a spiced broth, typically flavored with turmeric, cumin, and other aromatic spices. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Thor sorse is a Bengali vegetable dish prepared from banana stem (thor), which is finely chopped and cooked in a mustard seed paste (sorse) along with green chilies, turmeric, and mustard oil. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

canonicalName: jhinge alu diye rui machher jhol
displayName: Jhinge alu diye rui machher jhol
aliases: jhinge alu diye rui machher jhol
description: Rui fish is poached in a light, spiced broth with ridge gourd (jhinge) and potato (alu), creating a mild curry typical of Bengali cuisine. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as an everyday home-cooked meal.

Magur machher tel jhal is a Bengali fish curry made with catfish (magur) cooked in a spiced oil-based gravy with turmeric, chili, and mustard oil, creating a pungent and flavorful dish. It is typically served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Sankar machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering fish pieces in a spiced broth made with onions, tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and traditional Bengali spices. The dish is typically served with steamed rice and is a staple preparation in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: kalojeera diye pabda machher patla jhol
displayName: Kalojeera diye pabda machher patla jhol
aliases: kalojeera diye pabda machher patla jhol
description: Pabda machher patla jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with pabda fish (a freshwater catfish) cooked in a thin, broth-like gravy seasoned with kalojeera (nigella seeds), turmeric, and chili. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a light, everyday meal in Bengali cuisine.

Pabda machher tel bori jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with pabda fish (a freshwater catfish), lentil fritters (tel bori), and aromatic spices cooked in a savory gravy base. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Begun basanti is a Bengali dish in which eggplant (begun) is cooked in a mustard-based sauce with turmeric, giving it a characteristic golden-yellow color. It is typically prepared by frying slices of eggplant and then simmering them in a sauce made from mustard paste, yogurt, and spices, and is served as a vegetarian side dish alongside rice.

Potol rosha is a Bengali dish made by cooking pointed gourd (Trichosanthes dioica, known as potol or parwal) in a spiced gravy typically prepared with mustard paste, turmeric, green chilies, and mustard oil. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Mouri paneer is a Bengali-style preparation that typically features cubes of paneer (fresh Indian cheese) simmered in a fragrant gravy flavored with mouri (fennel seeds), along with common aromatics such as onion, ginger, and green chilies. It is usually served warm alongside steamed rice, pulao, or Indian flatbreads like luchi or roti as part of a main course.

Alur khosa bata is a Bengali preparation made by charring or roasting potato skins, which are then mashed and mixed with mustard oil, green chilies, onion, and salt to form a coarse paste. It is typically served as a side dish or accompaniment with rice and dal in traditional Bengali meals.

Ullikkary is a South Indian dish made by cooking onions (ulli) in a spiced preparation using ingredients such as mustard seeds, dried red chilies, curry leaves, and coconut oil. It is typically served as a side dish alongside rice and other accompaniments in Kerala cuisine.

Chirer pulao is a Bengali dish made by soaking flattened rice (chira or poha) and combining it with ingredients such as ghee, nuts, raisins, green chilies, and sometimes vegetables or coconut. It is typically served as a breakfast or light snack and requires no cooking over heat, as the soaked flattened rice softens sufficiently to be mixed and eaten directly.

Mulo diye motor dal is a Bengali lentil dish made by cooking yellow split peas (motor dal) with white radish (mulo), tempered with spices such as cumin, turmeric, and dried red chili in oil or ghee. It is typically served as part of a multi-course Bengali meal alongside steamed rice and other vegetable or fish preparations.

Aam potol is a Bengali dish made by stuffing or cooking pointed gourd (potol/parwal) with raw or ripe mango, typically combined with spices such as mustard, turmeric, and green chili. It is prepared by sautéing or simmering the gourd with a mango-based sauce and is commonly served as a vegetable side dish alongside rice in a traditional meal.

Thor er dhoka is a Bengali vegetable dish made from banana stem (thor), which is finely chopped and cooked with spices such as cumin, turmeric, and ginger, often bound with chickpea or lentil paste and shaped into small cakes before being pan-fried or simmered in a light gravy. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Shalgam Diye Rui Macher Jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering rui (rohu) fish in a savory broth with turnips (shalgam), onions, and spices including turmeric and cumin. The dish is typically served hot with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Kumro shaak bhaji is a Bengali vegetable fritter made by coating diced pumpkin or squash pieces in a spiced chickpea flour batter and deep-frying until golden and crispy. The dish is typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by chutney or as part of a meal.

Kancha peyaj bhorta is a Bengali preparation made by mashing raw onions with green chilies, salt, and spices to create a pungent side dish. It is typically served alongside rice and curries as a condiment to add sharp flavor and texture to meals.

Potoler dalna is a Bengali lentil curry made with pointed gourd (potol), split lentils, and aromatic spices including turmeric and cumin, simmered until the gourd becomes tender. The dish is typically served as a vegetarian accompaniment to rice or flatbread during meals in Bengali cuisine.

Narkel potol is a Bengali vegetable dish made with pointed gourd (potol) stuffed with a spiced coconut and lentil mixture, then simmered in a light gravy. The dish is typically served as a side course with rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Alu panch phoron is a Bengali potato dish where potatoes are cooked with panch phoron (a five-spice blend of fenugreek, nigella, cumin, fennel, and mustard seeds) along with turmeric and chili. The dish is typically served as a vegetable side accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Sorshe potol is a Bengali vegetable dish featuring pointed gourds cooked in a mustard-based sauce made from ground mustard seeds, turmeric, and green chilies. The dish is typically prepared by stuffing or cooking the gourds whole in the spiced mustard gravy and is served as a side dish with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Giurgiulena is a traditional Southern Italian confection, particularly associated with Calabria and Sicily, made by toasting sesame seeds and combining them with honey or sugar syrup, sometimes with the addition of orange zest or spices, then pressing the mixture into a flat layer that is cut into bars or rectangles as it cools. It is typically served as a festive sweet, especially during Christmas and other seasonal celebrations, and belongs to a family of sesame-based candies found across Mediterranean and Middle Eastern culinary traditions.

Gizdodo is a West African dish made by frying together diced meat, plantains, and vegetables including carrots, bell peppers, and onions until golden and caramelized. It is typically served as a main course or side dish, popular in Ghanaian and other regional cuisines.

Ol er tok is a traditional Bengali dish typically prepared with elephant foot yam (ol) cooked in a tangy, tamarind or raw mango-based sauce, often seasoned with panch phoron, mustard seeds, and a touch of sugar to balance the sourness. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice, usually toward the end of a meal in the sequence of Bengali cuisine where sour preparations are traditionally eaten.

Shaplar paturi is a Bengali-style preparation in which shapla (water lily stems) are typically seasoned with mustard paste, grated coconut, green chilies, and spices before being wrapped in banana leaves and steamed or lightly pan-cooked. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice, forming part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Ol pora bata is a Bengali preparation made by roasting or charring taro (ol/elephant foot yam) directly over an open flame or in embers, then mashing the flesh with mustard oil, green chilies, onion, and salt. It is typically served as a side dish or accompaniment with rice in traditional Bengali meals.

Narkel diye Shapla is a Bengali dish made from water lily stems (shapla) cooked with grated coconut (narkel), typically prepared by sautéing or simmering the stems with mustard, dried chilies, and spices until tender. It is commonly served as a vegetable side dish alongside steamed rice in Bengali households, particularly in Bangladesh and West Bengal.

Brahmi shaag ghee diye vaja is a Bengali preparation in which brahmi leaves (Bacopa monnieri) are stir-fried in clarified butter (ghee), typically with garlic and dried red chilies. It is served as a simple side dish alongside steamed rice, common in Bengali household meals.

Alu diye bramhi shaag vaja is a Bengali stir-fried dish made by sautéing brahmi leaves (Centella asiatica) with potato pieces in oil, typically seasoned with mustard seeds, dried red chili, and salt. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Alu morich is a Bengali dish made by cooking potatoes (alu) with black pepper (morich), mustard oil, and simple spices until the potatoes are tender and coated in a peppery seasoning. It is typically served as a side dish alongside rice and dal in everyday Bengali meals.

Dumur bhaja is a Bengali fried dish made from sliced fig (dumur), typically coated in a spiced chickpea flour batter and shallow- or deep-fried until golden and crisp. It is commonly served as a snack or side dish, often accompanied by rice or as part of a larger Bengali meal.

Chhanar bora is a Bengali sweet preparation made by combining paneer (fresh cheese) with semolina and jaggery or sugar, formed into balls, and deep-fried until golden brown. The dish is typically served as a dessert or sweet accompaniment, often accompanied by a light sugar syrup or as part of festive meals.

Potatoes are peeled and boiled in salted water until tender, typically served whole or cut into pieces. This simple preparation is commonly served as a side dish across many cuisines, often accompanied by butter, sour cream, or other condiments.

Shapla diye mug dal is a Bengali lentil dish prepared by cooking split mung dal (mug dal) with shapla, the edible stems and flowers of the water lily (Nymphaea nouchali), along with aromatics such as onion, garlic, ginger, turmeric, and mustard oil. It is typically served as part of a multi-dish meal alongside steamed rice, and is a traditional preparation common in Bangladesh and West Bengal.

Gondhoraj Paneer is an Indian dish in which fresh paneer (cottage cheese) is marinated and prepared with Gondhoraj lime, a fragrant variety of lime native to Bengal known for its distinctive floral aroma, along with spices such as cumin, green chili, and mustard oil. It is typically served as a starter or main course accompaniment, often grilled or pan-fried, and is common in Bengali cuisine and modern Indian restaurant menus.

Olkopi aloor jhool is a Bengali vegetable curry prepared by simmering kohlrabi (olkopi) and potatoes (aloo) in a lightly spiced gravy made with mustard oil, turmeric, cumin, and green chilies. It is typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice as part of a everyday Bengali meal.

Burger King Japan's pineapple pie consists of a sweet pineapple filling encased in a deep-fried pastry shell, resulting in a crispy exterior with a warm, fruit-based interior. It is served as a dessert or snack item at Burger King locations in Japan, similar in format to the chain's other fried pie offerings.

Til er bora is a Bengali fritter made from a batter of sesame seeds combined with rice flour or lentil paste, seasoned with salt and chili, then deep-fried until crisp. It is typically served as a snack or side dish alongside rice and dal in traditional Bengali meals.

Pempek lenjer is an Indonesian fish cake made from a mixture of fish and tapioca flour formed into long cylindrical shapes, then deep-fried until golden brown. It is typically served with a sweet and spicy vinegar-based sauce called cuko and is a popular street food from the Palembang region of South Sumatra.

Dhone patar bora is a Bengali fritter made by coating fresh coriander leaves (dhone pata) in a seasoned gram flour batter and deep-frying until crisp. It is typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by tea or a dipping chutney.

Pui patar bora are fritters made from Malabar spinach (pui pata) leaves coated in a spiced chickpea flour (besan) batter and deep-fried until crisp. They are a traditional Bengali snack, commonly served as a teatime accompaniment or light appetizer.

Kucho chingrir bora are fritters made from small shrimp (kucho chingri) mixed with a spiced batter typically containing onion, green chili, and chickpea flour, then deep-fried until crisp. They are a traditional Bengali preparation commonly served as a snack or appetizer.

Polta chorchori is a Bengali dry-style stir-fry dish made primarily from the leaves and stems of the polta plant (Enhydra fluctuans, also known as hingcha or marsh herb), cooked with mustard oil, green chilies, and spices. It is typically prepared as a simple vegetable side dish and served alongside steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Gugli chorchori is a Bengali charchari-style dish made by slow-cooking freshwater snails with mustard oil, onions, garlic, ginger, green chilies, and a blend of ground spices until the masala reduces and clings to the snail meat. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Hing chorchori is a Bengali dry-style stir-fry in which vegetables—commonly potatoes, eggplant, and leafy greens—are cooked with mustard oil and prominently seasoned with asafoetida (hing), along with dried red chilies and panch phoron (a five-spice blend). The dish is typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice as part of an everyday Bengali meal.

Oler dantar chorchori is a Bengali dry stir-fry dish made from the stalks (danta) of taro or arum (ol) cooked with mustard oil, green chilies, turmeric, and typically panch phoron (a five-spice blend). It is prepared by sautéing the chopped stems until tender and is commonly served as a side dish alongside rice in Bengali meals.

Chapila machher chorchori is a Bengali dry-style stir-fry made with chapila fish (Gudusia chapra), a small, flat freshwater fish native to South Asia, cooked together with mustard oil, green chilies, turmeric, and often seasonal vegetables or herbs. The dish is prepared by frying the fish with spices over high heat until the moisture evaporates, resulting in a semi-dry preparation typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice in Bengali households.

Kachki machher jhal chorchori is a Bengali preparation made with kachki (Corica soborna), a small freshwater fish, cooked in a dry or semi-dry style with mustard paste, green chilies, turmeric, and mustard oil, resulting in a spiced, pungent dish. It is typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice in everyday Bengali meals.

Chingri bora is a Bengali appetizer made from shrimp that are coated in a seasoned batter and deep-fried until crispy. The dish is typically served as a starter or snack, often accompanied by mustard oil-based dipping sauces or chutneys.

Bori diye thor chorchori is a Bengali vegetable stir-fry made with ridge gourd (thor) and dried lentil balls (bori) as primary ingredients, cooked with spices and sometimes onions and potatoes. The dish is typically served as a side accompaniment to rice and curry in Bengali cuisine.

Machher tel er chorchori is a Bengali curry made with fish cooked in mustard oil along with vegetables such as potatoes, eggplant, and leafy greens, seasoned with spices like turmeric and chili. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as part of Bengali meals.

Bata machher chorchori is a Bengali fish curry made with catfish (bata machh) cooked with vegetables such as potatoes, eggplant, and tomatoes in a spiced mustard oil-based gravy. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as part of Bengali home cuisine.

Black Bullets are a type of hard boiled sweet (candy) made from sugar and flavored with peppermint or aniseed, traditionally produced by pulling and folding the sugar mixture to create a dense, glossy confection with a characteristic dark color from added coloring or the cooking process. They are typically sold as individual pieces or in bags and consumed as a snack or confection, and are particularly associated with traditional British and Scottish sweet shops.

Röigebrageldi is a traditional Luxembourgish dish made by pan-frying sliced or diced potatoes together with onions in fat until golden and cooked through. It is typically served as a side dish or simple main course, and is considered a staple of rustic Luxembourgish home cooking.

Polpetta is an Italian meatball made from ground meat mixed with breadcrumbs, eggs, cheese, and seasonings, then shaped into balls and either fried or baked. It is typically served as a main course with pasta and tomato sauce, or as an appetizer.

A knish is a baked or fried pocket of dough filled with various savory fillings such as potato, meat, or cheese. It originated in Eastern European Jewish cuisine and is commonly served as a snack or appetizer, either warm or at room temperature.

Maluns is a Swiss potato dish made by mixing grated or diced potatoes with flour and butter, then pan-frying the mixture until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a side dish or light main course, often accompanied by applesauce or cheese.

Poutine consists of crispy fries topped with cheese curds and covered with hot gravy, which partially melts the curds. This dish originated in Quebec and is typically served as a casual meal or snack, often with variations that include additional proteins or toppings.

Pitina is an Italian salumi made by mixing ground pork with spices, salt, and garlic, then wrapping the mixture in a casing and aging it for several weeks. It is typically served sliced as an antipasto or charcuterie board component in northeastern Italian cuisine, particularly from the Veneto region.

Patatas alioli consists of boiled or fried potatoes coated in alioli, a traditional Spanish emulsion sauce made from garlic and olive oil. The dish is typically served as a tapa or side dish, often at room temperature or warm.

Doigts de Fatma are Tunisian pastries made from thin pastry sheets filled with ground meat, onions, and spices, then rolled into finger-shaped forms and deep-fried until golden. They are typically served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by harissa or a sweet chili sauce for dipping.

Bacalhau com natas is a Portuguese dish made from rehydrated dried and salted cod combined with cream, onions, and potatoes, typically baked until golden. The dish is traditionally served as a main course in Portuguese cuisine, often accompanied by fried potatoes.

aliases: bacalhau a la gomes de sa
description: Bacalhau à Gomes de Sá is a Portuguese dish made with rehydrated salt cod layered with sliced potatoes and onions, then baked and finished with hard-boiled eggs, olives, and parsley. The dish is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by the pan juices enriched with olive oil.

aliases: bacalhau a la ze do pipo
description: Bacalhau à Zé do Pipo is a Portuguese salt cod dish prepared by combining flaked dried and salted cod with sautéed onions, bell peppers, olives, and a mayonnaise-based sauce, typically layered with mashed potatoes. The dish is traditionally baked until golden and served as a substantial main course in Portuguese cuisine.

Bubble and squeak is a British dish made by frying mashed potatoes and cabbage together, typically formed into a cake or patty. It is traditionally served as a side dish or light meal, often alongside meat or with a fried egg on top.

Irish potato salad is made by boiling potatoes and combining them with mayonnaise-based dressing, along with diced onions and sometimes celery or fresh herbs. It is typically served chilled as a side dish at meals or with cold meats and sandwiches.

Jukti fuler chorchori is typically a lightly spiced stir-fried vegetable dish featuring edible flowers, commonly prepared with mustard oil, panch phoron (a five-spice blend), green chilies, and turmeric, and slow-cooked until tender. It is often served as a side dish alongside steamed rice and lentils as part of a traditional Bengali-style meal.

Macatias are soft bread rolls from Réunion, made from flour, sugar, butter, and yeast, typically shaped into small round buns and baked until lightly golden. They are commonly served as a breakfast or snack item, often split open and filled with butter, cheese, or cold cuts.

Braised Shaak Roast is prepared by slow-cooking pot roast until tender, then combining it with sautéed kale and edible mushrooms in the braising liquid. It is typically served over cavatelli pasta as a main course.

Smoked Kaadu Ribs are smoked spare ribs served with a side of coleslaw and accompanied by a muffin. The dish is offered as an entrée at Docking Bay 7 Food and Cargo, a quick-service restaurant located within the Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge area of Disney theme parks.

Lechuguino is a traditional Spanish bread made from wheat flour, water, yeast, and salt, characterized by a decorative scored pattern on its crust that resembles overlapping lettuce leaves. It is typically served as an accompaniment to meals or used for sandwiches and tapas.

Pão da Lagoinha is a traditional Portuguese bread from Lagoinha in Palmela, made from wheat flour, water, salt, and yeast, shaped by hand and baked in a wood-fired oven. It is typically served as an accompaniment to meals, alongside cheeses, cured meats, and regional dishes.

Jallulla is a traditional Spanish sweet bread, typically prepared with a simple dough of flour, sugar, eggs, and oil or butter, sometimes flavored with anise, citrus zest, or cinnamon. It is commonly baked until golden and served as a breakfast item or afternoon snack, often accompanied by coffee, hot chocolate, or a glass of milk.

The Ovelgönne bread roll is typically a crusty wheat or mixed-grain roll, likely named after the Ovelgönne district in Hamburg, and is commonly baked fresh with a firm crust and soft interior. It is often served at breakfast or as an accompaniment to soups and cold cuts, sometimes split and topped with butter, cheese, or cured fish in keeping with northern German traditions.

A breakfast sandwich consisting of a pork sausage patty and a fried egg served on a toasted English muffin, typically with cheese. This item is commonly served during morning hours at fast-food restaurants as part of their breakfast menu.

Mondeghili are Italian meatballs made from ground meat mixed with bread soaked in milk, eggs, and butter, then formed into balls and typically fried or braised. They are commonly served as a main course or appetizer in Italian cuisine, often accompanied by tomato sauce or broth.

Bangers and mash is a British dish consisting of pork sausages served on a bed of mashed potato, typically accompanied by onion gravy and vegetables such as peas or carrots. The sausages are usually pan-fried or grilled until browned, and the dish is traditionally served as a casual main course for lunch or dinner.

Shoti is a traditional Georgian bread made from wheat flour dough that is leavened and baked in a clay oven or tandoor, resulting in a soft, fluffy loaf with a slightly charred exterior. It is typically served warm as an accompaniment to meals, particularly with soups, stews, and cheese dishes in Georgian cuisine.

Baba is a Levantine dish made from roasted eggplant that is mashed and mixed with tahini, lemon juice, and garlic to create a smooth paste. It is typically served as a cold appetizer or dip with pita bread and olive oil.

Tebirkes are pastries made from puff pastry filled with poppy seed filling, typically shaped into individual portions. They are traditionally baked until golden and served as a sweet pastry or dessert item.

Cuñapé is a Brazilian cheese bread made from tapioca flour mixed with cheese, eggs, and butter, then baked until golden and crispy on the outside. It is typically served as a warm appetizer or snack, often accompanying meals or enjoyed on its own.

A poppyseed muffin is a quick bread made by mixing flour, sugar, eggs, and dairy with poppy seeds, then baking in a muffin tin until golden. It is typically served as a breakfast item or snack, often accompanied by coffee or tea.

Khobz mbesses is a Moroccan bread made by layering dough with melted butter and herbs, then folding and cooking it on a griddle or in a pan until golden and crispy. It is typically served warm as a breakfast bread or appetizer, often accompanied by honey, cheese, or eaten alongside tagines and other traditional Moroccan dishes.

Farl is a flatbread made from a simple dough of flour, salt, and fat (typically butter or lard) that is rolled out and cut into triangular or rectangular pieces before being pan-fried until golden. It is commonly served warm as a breakfast or side dish, particularly in Irish and Scottish cuisine, often accompanied by butter or eaten alongside eggs and bacon.

Sarna is a Bolivian cheese bread, typically prepared by mixing wheat or corn flour with fresh cheese, eggs, and fat to form a soft dough that is baked until golden. It is commonly served warm as a breakfast item or snack, often accompanied by coffee, mate, or hot chocolate.

A bread loaf is a shaped and baked leavened dough typically made from wheat flour, water, salt, and yeast or a sourdough starter, formed into a rectangular or oval form before being baked in an oven until the crust is set and the interior is fully cooked. It is commonly served sliced as an accompaniment to meals, used as a base for sandwiches, or eaten with butter, spreads, or soups across a wide range of culinary traditions.

Pane di Montecalvo is a traditional bread originating from Montecalvo Irpino in the Campania region of Italy, made from durum wheat flour and leavened with natural sourdough starter before being baked in a wood-fired oven. It is characterized by a thick, hard crust and a dense, compact crumb, and is typically served as an accompaniment to regional meals or local cured meats and cheeses.

Mentai-france is a Japanese-style baguette dish in which a French baguette is sliced and spread with mentaiko (spicy salted pollock roe), often combined with butter, then briefly broiled or toasted until the topping is lightly cooked and the bread is warm. It is commonly served as a snack or light meal in Japanese bakeries and cafes, representing a fusion of French bread traditions with Japanese seasoning.

Avaro is a traditional bread from the Tuamotu Islands, prepared by mixing flour with coconut milk and baking the dough, often wrapped in leaves, in an earth oven or over heated stones. It is commonly served as a staple accompaniment to fish and other main dishes in Polynesian meals.

A conical loaf of bread is a yeasted bread shaped into a cone or tall pointed form, typically achieved by molding the dough around a conical form or shaping it by hand before baking. It is made from standard bread ingredients such as flour, water, yeast, and salt, and is served as a table bread or accompaniment to meals across various culinary traditions.

Caramanduca is a traditional Peruvian biscuit made from a dough of wheat flour, lard, eggs, and sugar, shaped into small rounds or rings and baked until firm. It is typically served as an accompaniment to coffee, tea, or hot chocolate as part of a snack or breakfast.

Zopako is a traditional Basque bread, typically prepared with simple ingredients such as wheat flour, water, salt, and yeast, and baked to produce a firm crust and dense crumb characteristic of rustic regional loaves. It is commonly served alongside hearty Basque dishes like stews, soups, or cured meats and cheeses, and often used to soak up sauces or accompany shared meals.

Pan de codos is a traditional Spanish bread, typically prepared with wheat flour, water, salt, and yeast, and baked until it develops a firm crust and soft interior. It is commonly served as an accompaniment to meals, alongside soups, stews, or cured meats and cheeses in the Spanish culinary tradition.

A soft, slightly sweet yeast bread roll topped with poppy seeds, typically made from dough containing flour, milk, eggs, and butter. Commonly served at breakfast or as a sandwich base, and frequently used in Jewish and Central European cuisines.

Pone is a cornbread made from cornmeal, water or buttermilk, and salt, baked until golden in a cast-iron skillet or baking pan. It is typically served as a side dish with savory meals, particularly in Southern American cuisine, and can be eaten plain, with butter, or alongside soups and stews.

A soft, slightly sweet bread roll made from a dough containing mashed or finely grated potatoes, wheat flour, yeast, and butter, which creates a tender crumb structure and extended shelf life. Typically served as a sandwich bread or burger bun, the potato bun is common in British, Irish, and North American cuisines.

Boba is a beverage prepared by cooking tapioca pearls until soft and chewy, then adding them to a sweetened tea base (typically black or green tea) along with milk or fruit flavors. The drink is served chilled in a tall glass with the pearls at the bottom and a wide straw for drinking, originating from Taiwan and popular internationally as a casual refreshment.

Olive bread is an Italian bread made by incorporating green or black olives into the dough during preparation, resulting in a savory loaf with visible olive pieces throughout. It is typically served as a side bread with meals or used for making sandwiches, and is common in Mediterranean cuisine.

Pan de queso is a cheese bread made from a dough of cassava starch, cheese, eggs, and butter, baked until golden and crispy on the outside with a soft, cheese-filled interior. It is typically served as a breakfast item, snack, or accompaniment to meals in Latin American cuisine, particularly in Colombia and Venezuela.

Bread is prepared by mixing flour, water, salt, and yeast, then kneading the dough and allowing it to ferment before baking at high temperature until golden. It is a staple carbohydrate served alongside meals, as a base for sandwiches, or eaten plain with spreads and accompaniments.

Harbour is likely an international-style dish with Japanese influences, typically featuring a mix of seafood such as shrimp, squid, or white fish, often prepared with light sauces that may incorporate soy, miso, or citrus elements like yuzu. It is commonly served as a main course, presented with rice, seasonal vegetables, or noodles to reflect the fusion of Japanese culinary traditions with broader international techniques.

Abacha is a Nigerian dish made from grated cassava root that is fermented, dried, and then rehydrated before being mixed with palm oil, salt, and seasonings. It is typically served as a snack or light meal, often accompanied by a spicy pepper sauce and sometimes combined with ugba (fermented oil bean seeds).

Kong Haakon is typically a layered celebratory cake named in honor of King Haakon of Norway, commonly composed of sponge or meringue layers filled with whipped cream, custard, and seasonal berries such as strawberries or raspberries. It is often served in slices as a dessert for special occasions, sometimes finished with a dusting of powdered sugar or a light glaze.

Bolu kojo is a traditional Indonesian steamed or baked sponge cake originating from Palembang, South Sumatra, made primarily from eggs, sugar, coconut milk, and pandan leaves, which give it a distinctive green color and fragrant aroma. It is typically molded in a flower-shaped or fluted pan and served as a snack or dessert during festive occasions and celebrations.

Varme hveder are soft, lightly sweetened wheat buns made with cardamom, traditionally prepared by splitting and toasting or warming them before serving. They are a Danish specialty associated with Store Bededag (Great Prayer Day) and are typically eaten warm with butter.

Crespells are traditional shortbread-style cookies from Mallorca, Spain, made from flour, lard, eggs, sugar, and olive oil, typically shaped with cookie cutters into forms such as stars, flowers, or animals. They are baked until firm and lightly golden, and are commonly prepared during religious festivals and holidays such as Easter and All Saints' Day.

Mire biscuit is a Japanese Western-style confection made from a dough of wheat flour, sugar, butter, and eggs, rolled thin, docked with small holes, and baked into a crisp, lightly sweet cracker-like biscuit. It is typically sold in packaged form and served as a snack with tea or coffee, and is also used as a base for canapés or topped with cream and fruit.

Fran is a Japanese baked confection produced by Meiji, consisting of a thin, crisp biscuit shell filled with chocolate cream, available in varieties such as milk chocolate and matcha. It is a packaged snack typically eaten as a casual treat and sold in convenience stores and supermarkets throughout Japan.

Campanile is a traditional Corsican chestnut flour cake, prepared by mixing chestnut flour with water, olive oil, and sometimes flavored with anise or fennel seeds, then baked until firm. It is typically served as a rustic bread or cake accompaniment during festive meals and everyday dining in Corsica.

Mihidana is a traditional Bengali sweet made by pressing chickpea flour (besan) batter through a fine-holed sieve directly into hot ghee or oil, producing thin, thread-like golden strands that are then soaked in sugar syrup. It is typically served as a festive or celebratory confection and is a recognized specialty of Bardhaman (Burdwan) in West Bengal, India.

Chhenabara is an Odia sweet from the Indian state of Odisha, made by deep-frying small balls of chhena (fresh cottage cheese) until golden, then soaking them in sugar syrup. It is commonly served as a dessert or sweet snack and is a staple at festivals and celebrations in Odisha.

Kodma is an Indian sugar candy prepared by boiling sugar into a syrup and allowing it to crystallize or set into small, hardened pieces. It is typically served as a sweet confection, often consumed on its own as a treat or offered during festive and religious occasions.

A Klondike bar is a frozen dessert consisting of a block of vanilla ice cream coated with a thin layer of hardened chocolate shell. It is typically eaten as a single-serving frozen treat, often held by a wooden stick for consumption.

Pradhaman is a traditional South Indian sweet dessert made by slow-cooking jaggery with ghee, roasted flour (typically rice or wheat flour), and coconut, often enriched with nuts and dried fruits. It is typically served warm as a festive dessert during celebrations and special occasions in Kerala and neighboring regions.
