Kisses are small confectionery pieces typically made from whipped egg whites, sugar, and flavorings, piped into mound shapes and baked until crisp. These light, airy sweets are commonly served as petit fours or desserts, often coated or filled with chocolate, nuts, or fruit flavors.

Nambu sembei is a crispy rice cracker from the Nambu region of Japan, made by baking or deep-frying pressed rice dough often seasoned with soy sauce, salt, or seaweed. It is typically served as a snack or accompaniment to tea and meals.

Rold Gold Waffles are a waffle-shaped pretzel produced by the Rold Gold brand, made from baked wheat flour dough that is shaped into a lattice pattern, salted, and baked until crisp. They are typically served as a packaged snack, eaten on their own or paired with dips such as mustard, cheese, or chocolate.

Bodink is a traditional bread pudding from Brussels, made by soaking stale bread in milk and combining it with eggs, sugar, butter, and dried fruits such as raisins, then baking the mixture until set. It is typically served in slices as a dessert or snack, often at room temperature or slightly warmed.

Xipa is a Paraguayan flatbread made from cassava starch, cheese, eggs, milk, and fat, mixed into a dough and baked until firm with a slightly crisp exterior. It is commonly served as a snack or accompaniment to coffee, mate, or cocido, and is particularly associated with Holy Week traditions.

Torta loca is a Mexican sandwich made on a telera or bolillo roll, typically filled with a combination of multiple meats, cheeses, vegetables, and condiments such as avocado, jalapeños, beans, and various salsas. It is commonly served as a street food or casual meal, distinguished by its generous layering of diverse ingredients that vary by region and vendor.

Casa Mira is typically presented as a signature house-style dish drawing on international culinary influences, often composed of a protein such as chicken, beef, or fish paired with sautéed vegetables and a complementary sauce. It is commonly served as a main course, frequently accompanied by rice, potatoes, or pasta depending on the kitchen's interpretation.

Poschweck is a traditional sweet yeast bread originating from the Westphalia region of Germany, made with wheat flour, butter, eggs, sugar, raisins, and spices such as anise and cardamom. It is typically baked in a round loaf and served sliced, often associated with festive occasions and holidays in the region.

Pig cake is a traditional Swedish pastry made from a mixture of butter, sugar, eggs, and flour, typically baked in a round or oval shape and finished with a crisp, caramelized sugar crust. It is commonly served as a coffee accompaniment or dessert in Swedish households and cafés.

Fallue is a brioche-style enriched bread from Normandy, France, made with flour, eggs, butter, sugar, and yeast, giving it a soft, slightly sweet crumb and a golden crust. It is traditionally baked in a round or elongated shape and served as a breakfast bread or afternoon snack, often accompanied by butter, jam, or cream.

Hojaldrinas are Spanish pastries made with a laminated dough incorporating lard, layered and baked until crispy. They are typically finished with a glaze made from orange juice and sugar, and traditionally served as a sweet treat or dessert.

Kirchtagskrapfen are fried pastry pieces made from a yeast or egg dough that is deep-fried until golden and typically dusted with powdered sugar. Traditionally associated with Austrian and Bavarian church festivals (Kirchtag), these pastries are served as a sweet snack or dessert at festive occasions and markets.

Cheese straws are thin, crispy pastries made from puff pastry or choux dough mixed with grated cheese, typically cheddar, then baked until golden. They are commonly served as an appetizer, snack, or accompaniment to soups and are popular in British and French cuisine.

aliases: la elaboracion del pan de especias en el norte de croacia
description: Licitar is a traditional gingerbread cake from Croatia, made with honey, spices, and flour, then decorated with colorful royal icing in intricate patterns. It is typically served as a festive dessert or gift during holiday celebrations and special occasions.

Ikinaridango is a Japanese sweet dumpling made by wrapping sweet bean paste and diced sweet potato in mochi dough, then steaming or boiling until cooked through. This traditional confection is typically served as a casual snack or dessert, often eaten warm or at room temperature.

Kaak warka is a Tunisian pastry made by wrapping a thin sheet of warka dough around a filling, typically containing egg, meat, or vegetables, then deep-frying until crispy and golden. It is traditionally served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by harissa sauce or eaten during special occasions and celebrations.

Mattentaart is a Dutch open tart filled with a creamy custard or almond paste mixture, traditionally made with a pastry crust and topped with a lattice pattern. The tart is typically served as a dessert or sweet treat, often at room temperature or slightly warm.

Mkharek is a Moroccan pastry made by wrapping a thin crepe-like dough around a savory or sweet filling, then pan-frying until golden and crispy. It is typically filled with ingredients such as meat, cheese, or egg, and served as a street food or appetizer, often accompanied by honey when prepared with sweet fillings.

A custard bun is a baked or steamed pastry with a soft dough exterior filled with sweet custard cream. It is commonly served as a dessert or snack in dim sum, bakeries, and cafes across East and Southeast Asia.

Alléluia is a traditional French pastry from Castelnaudary in the Aude region, made from a sweet dough flavored with candied citron and orange blossom water, then baked until golden. It is typically served as a dessert or accompaniment to coffee, and is particularly associated with the Easter season.

Sou is a light pastry made from choux dough, prepared by cooking flour, butter, water, and eggs into a paste that is piped and baked until it puffs into a hollow shell. It is typically filled with sweet cream, custard, or whipped cream and served as a dessert or accompaniment to coffee and tea.

Gelamai perentak is a traditional Malay confection made by cooking glutinous rice flour with coconut milk and palm sugar, stirred continuously over heat until the mixture thickens into a dense, sticky cake-like consistency. It is typically cut into portions and served as a snack or dessert, and is associated particularly with Bengkulu in Indonesia as well as parts of the Malay Peninsula.

Africain is a French individual pastry consisting of a moist almond sponge cake soaked in rum-flavored syrup and topped with chocolate ganache or icing. It is typically sold in patisseries and served as a dessert or afternoon treat alongside coffee or tea.

Jif is a brand of peanut butter produced by the J.M. Smucker Company, made primarily from roasted peanuts ground with sugar, salt, molasses, and hydrogenated vegetable oils for stability. It is commonly spread on bread for sandwiches, paired with jelly, or used as an ingredient in baked goods, sauces, and snacks.

Rassteegai are Russian baked pastries made from yeast dough filled with savory fillings such as fish, meat, or cabbage, characterized by open tops that allow the filling to show through. These oval-shaped pastries are traditionally served as appetizers or snacks, often accompanied by broth or soup.

Ballokume is an Albanian butter cookie made from a dough of flour, butter, eggs, and sugar, typically shaped into small balls or rounds and baked until golden. The cookies are traditionally served as a sweet treat or dessert, often accompanying coffee or tea.
A moorkop is a Dutch pastry consisting of a profiterole filled with whipped cream and topped with icing. It is typically served as a dessert or sweet treat, often enjoyed with coffee or tea.

Splitterbrötchen is a German bread roll made from yeast dough that is split or torn into irregular pieces before baking, creating a rustic appearance with a crispy crust and soft interior. It is typically served as a breakfast or snack item, often accompanied by butter, jam, or cold cuts.

Dulce de batata is a sweet paste made from mashed sweet potatoes and sugar cooked together until thick and dense, typically incorporating cinnamon and other spices. It is traditionally served as a dessert or confection in Argentine cuisine, often eaten on its own, paired with cheese, or used as a filling in pastries.

This Basque cheesecake is made by baking a simple mixture of cream cheese, eggs, and sugar in a pastry shell until the top browns while the interior remains custardy and soft. It is typically served at room temperature or slightly chilled as a dessert, often accompanied by fruit compote or served plain to highlight its creamy, slightly caramelized texture.

Dame blanche is a classic Belgian dessert consisting of vanilla ice cream topped with warm chocolate syrup and whipped cream. The dish is typically served as a cold dessert, with the contrast between the warm sauce and cold ice cream being a defining characteristic of the preparation.

Reindling is an Austrian spiral cake made from a yeast dough filled with cinnamon, sugar, and raisins, then baked until golden brown. It is traditionally served as a sweet pastry for breakfast, coffee, or dessert, typically sliced and eaten warm or at room temperature.

A Catalan pastry made from choux dough that is fried until golden and crispy, then typically filled with chocolate or cream. It is commonly served as a breakfast or snack item, often dusted with sugar and eaten while still warm.

Dole Whip is a frozen dessert made by blending pineapple juice or puree with ice and typically sugar to create a soft-serve consistency. It is commonly served as a standalone frozen treat or as a base for mixed beverages, particularly popular at theme parks and tropical-themed establishments.

Cuddura is a traditional Sicilian pastry made with a sweet or savory dough that is typically filled with ingredients such as cheese, meat, or vegetables, then shaped into a ring or loaf and baked until golden. It is commonly served as a snack or light meal in Sicily and surrounding regions of southern Italy.

Biscotti del Lagaccio are traditional dry biscuits originating from Genoa, Italy, made from a simple dough of flour, sugar, yeast, anise seeds, and fat, which is shaped into long loaves, baked, then sliced and dried in the oven to achieve a hard, crisp texture. They are typically served as a breakfast or snack biscuit and are commonly dipped in wine, coffee, or other beverages to soften them before eating.

Boulfaf is a North African dish, particularly associated with Moroccan cuisine, made by wrapping small pieces of liver in caul fat and grilling them on skewers over charcoal. It is traditionally prepared and consumed during the Eid al-Adha celebration, shortly after the slaughter of the sheep, and is typically served as a first dish of the feast.

Vlaflip is a Dutch dessert consisting of a layer of vla (a thin Dutch custard) poured over a portion of yogurt or pudding, typically in a glass or cup. It is commonly served chilled as a ready-made supermarket product in the Netherlands, with popular varieties combining flavors such as vanilla vla with strawberry or chocolate yogurt.

Ischler Törtchen are small Austrian sandwich cookies consisting of two thin almond or hazelnut biscuits joined with apricot jam and typically dipped in dark chocolate. These traditional treats are baked and assembled, then often served as petit fours or with coffee and tea.

Zuccotto is a Florentine dome-shaped dessert made by lining a rounded mold with sponge cake or ladyfingers soaked in liqueur, then filling it with a mixture of whipped cream, ricotta, chocolate, and candied fruits before chilling and unmolding. The chilled cake is typically served sliced as a festive dessert, often dusted with powdered sugar or cocoa powder.

Café liégeois is a Belgian dessert consisting of vanilla ice cream topped with hot or cold strong coffee, typically served in a tall glass. The dish is traditionally enjoyed as an afternoon treat or light dessert, often with whipped cream and sometimes a small pastry on the side.

Frittelle are Italian fried pastries made from a simple batter of flour, eggs, milk, and sugar, typically deep-fried until golden brown. These light, airy treats are traditionally served as a dessert or snack, often dusted with powdered sugar and sometimes filled with jam, cream, or custard.

Pfingststrudel is a sweet pastry made from yeast dough, rolled and baked until golden. It is traditionally prepared and served around Pentecost (Pfingsten), typically enjoyed as a dessert or accompaniment to coffee.

Gajar ka halwa is made by grating carrots and cooking them slowly with ghee, milk, and sugar until caramelized and thickened into a dense pudding. It is traditionally served warm as a dessert, often garnished with nuts such as almonds and cashews, and sometimes topped with condensed milk or rabri.

Basundi is a traditional Indian dessert made by slowly simmering sweetened milk with cardamom, nutmeg, and saffron until it reduces to a thick, creamy consistency, then cooled and served chilled. It is typically garnished with chopped nuts such as almonds and pistachios, and is commonly served as a sweet dish during festivals and special occasions in Maharashtra and other parts of India.

Mpanatigghi is a Sicilian pastry made by wrapping a filling of ground meat, cheese, and spices in thin pasta dough, then baking until golden. The dish is typically served as an appetizer or main course in Italian cuisine, particularly in Sicily.

Sale pisang is an Indonesian banana fritter made by coating sliced or whole bananas in a simple batter of flour, sugar, and eggs, then deep-frying until golden brown. The crispy exterior contrasts with the soft, caramelized banana interior, and the dish is typically served as a snack or dessert, often enjoyed warm with a dusting of powdered sugar.

Cuajada is a traditional Spanish dessert made by curdling raw milk with rennet, resulting in a soft, custard-like texture similar to panna cotta. It is typically served chilled in small glasses or bowls, often accompanied by sugar, honey, or nuts as toppings.

Struffoli are small fried balls of dough that are drizzled with honey and often decorated with colorful sprinkles or candied fruits. This traditional Italian dessert from Naples is typically served during Christmas and other festive occasions, sometimes shaped into a wreath or mound.

Nocciolini di Chivasso are small hazelnut-based confections from the Piedmont region of Italy, typically made with roasted hazelnuts, chocolate, and sugar combined into bite-sized candies. These treats are traditionally served as petit fours or gifted as regional delicacies, particularly during festive occasions.

Mille-feuille Carsolina is a layered pastry from Trieste, Italy, made by stacking thin sheets of puff pastry with a filling of whipped cream or pastry cream. It is typically served as a dessert, cut into rectangular portions and often dusted with powdered sugar.

Minna di virgini are Sicilian marzipan pastries shaped to resemble female breasts, traditionally filled with sweetened ricotta or cream and topped with a glacé cherry. They are associated with the feast of Saint Agatha in Catania and are typically served as a festive confection during the February celebrations in her honor.

'Nzuddi are traditional Sicilian cookies made from a dough of flour, honey, toasted almonds, and spices such as cloves and cinnamon, shaped into small rounds or figures and baked until firm. They are a typical product of the Catania area in Sicily, commonly prepared during festive and religious occasions, and are known for their hard texture and aromatic flavor.

Susamielli are traditional Neapolitan Christmas cookies made from a dough of flour, honey, and sesame seeds, shaped into an S-curve and baked until firm. They are a seasonal confection from the Campania region of Italy, typically served during the winter holiday period as part of festive sweet spreads.

Kuler is a traditional Gujarati dish made from the fruit of the Ziziphus mauritiana (Indian jujube) tree, prepared by cooking the tart berries with spices, jaggery, and tamarind to balance sweet and sour flavors. It is typically served as a chutney or pickle accompaniment alongside main meals in Gujarati households.

Pfirsichstein is a Swiss confection shaped to resemble a peach pit, typically made from two small domed cakes or biscuit halves joined with a filling such as buttercream, jam, or liqueur-soaked cream, then coated in tinted sugar to mimic the fuzzy skin of a peach. It is served as a sweet treat with coffee or tea, often found in Swiss bakeries and confectioneries alongside other petits fours.

Gudu lat is prepared by wrapping rat meat in leaves and roasting it, a cooking method that imparts aroma from the wrapping foliage while retaining moisture in the meat. It is typically served as a main protein dish, often accompanied by rice or starches in regions where rat meat is a traditional food source.

Pistiddu is a traditional Sardinian flatbread made from durum wheat flour, water, and salt, rolled thin and cooked on a hot griddle or in a wood-fired oven until lightly charred. It is typically served warm as an accompaniment to meals or filled with cheese, cured meats, or vegetables.

Roccocò is a traditional Neapolitan Christmas pastry made from a dough filled with a mixture of dried fruits, nuts, honey, and spices, then shaped into a ring or spiral and baked until golden. The confection is typically served during the holiday season as a festive treat, often enjoyed with coffee or wine.

Cassatella di Agira is a small Sicilian pastry made with a crispy fried pastry shell filled with ricotta cheese, candied fruits, and chocolate pieces. It is a traditional sweet from the town of Agira in Sicily, typically served as a dessert or during festive occasions.

Crema bruciata is an Italian custard dessert made from egg yolks, milk or cream, sugar, and vanilla, which is cooked in a bain-marie until set and then chilled. The top is typically caramelized with a torch or under a broiler immediately before serving, creating a crisp sugar crust that contrasts with the creamy custard below.

Banana cream pie consists of a pastry or graham cracker crust filled with a custard or vanilla cream filling, topped with sliced fresh bananas and whipped cream. It is typically served chilled as a dessert, often garnished with chocolate shavings or banana slices.

Getuk is an Indonesian steamed cake made from grated cassava mixed with coconut milk, sugar, and flavorings, then steamed until firm and sliced into cylindrical pieces. It is typically served as a sweet snack or dessert, often coated with grated coconut or sprinkled with cinnamon and brown sugar.

Cocada is a confection made by cooking shredded coconut with sugar and condensed milk until thickened, then cooled and cut into portions or shaped into balls. It is a popular dessert and sweet treat in Latin American countries, particularly Brazil and other Portuguese-speaking regions, typically served as a standalone candy or snack.

Bopis is a Filipino dish made from minced pork offal, typically liver and lungs, cooked with tomatoes, onions, and chili peppers in a savory stew. It is traditionally served with rice as a main course, particularly at breakfast or lunch.

Baiser is a meringue-based confection made from whipped egg whites and sugar, often piped into nest or kiss shapes and baked at low temperature until crisp and dry. It is typically served as a dessert or petit four, sometimes filled with cream, chocolate, or fruit.

Chingri Bhapa is a Bengali steamed prawn dish where prawns are marinated in spices and coconut paste, then cooked en papillote (wrapped in banana leaves or foil) with mustard oil and green chilies. The dish is traditionally served as a main course in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by steamed rice.

Bensone is a traditional Italian sweet bread or cake originating from the Emilia-Romagna region, typically made from flour, sugar, eggs, butter, and leavening, often flavored with lemon zest or anise and shaped into a ring or elongated loaf. It is commonly served as a breakfast or snack item, frequently accompanied by wine, milk, or coffee, and is known for its firm, slightly dry texture suitable for dipping.

Must cookie (moustalevria-based biscuit) is a Greek sweet prepared using petimezi (grape must syrup), flour, and spices such as cinnamon and cloves, which are combined into a dough and baked until firm. It is traditionally made during the grape harvest season and served as a snack or dessert, often alongside coffee or tea.

Öpke-hésip is a Uyghur dish consisting of lamb lung stuffed with a mixture based on hasip filling, typically including rice, flour, or spiced minced meat, which is poured into the lung through the windpipe before boiling. It is usually served sliced alongside hasip (lamb intestine sausage) as part of a traditional offal platter.

Valomilk is an American candy cup consisting of a chocolate shell filled with a marshmallow-based liquid center, produced by Sioux City Candy since 1931. It is typically sold individually wrapped and eaten as a snack candy, notable for its unusually fluid marshmallow filling that flows when the cup is broken or bitten.

Qereş is a Kurdish confection prepared by combining sugar with apricots (Prunus armeniaca) and buttermilk, which are cooked and set to form a sweet candy. It is typically served as a traditional treat, often offered alongside tea or during celebratory occasions.

Cadbury Eclairs are a confection consisting of a hard toffee shell encasing a soft, creamy chocolate centre, produced by Cadbury. They are individually wrapped bite-sized sweets typically sold in bags and consumed as a standalone snack or shared from a communal bowl.

Certs is a brand of breath mint produced by compressing powdered sugar, flavorings, and binding agents into small round lozenges, with varieties containing either a drop of liquid flavoring (Retsyn) or a crystalline sugar center. The mints are typically carried in a rolled paper-wrapped package and consumed individually after meals or throughout the day to freshen breath.

Torta parda is a traditional Spanish cake characterized by its dark color, achieved through the inclusion of ingredients such as cinnamon, anise, and dried fruits or nuts, which are combined into a dense, moist batter and baked. It is typically served as a dessert or accompaniment to coffee or tea, particularly during festive or celebratory occasions in certain Spanish regions.

Pilor is a traditional Kurdish candy, typically prepared by boiling sugar with water and flavorings until it reaches a hard or chewy consistency, then shaping it into small pieces. It is commonly served as a sweet treat alongside tea or offered to guests during holidays and special occasions.

Balushahi is a deep-fried Indian sweet made from maida (refined flour) and ghee, shaped into round discs and cooked until golden brown. The finished pastries are soaked in sugar syrup and typically garnished with a pistachio or almond piece on top, served as a dessert or confection.

Pantua is a Bengali sweet made by forming balls of khoa (reduced milk solids) mixed with semolina, which are deep-fried in ghee until golden brown. The fried balls are then soaked in a sugar syrup and typically served warm as a dessert or during festive occasions.

Saskatoon berry pie is a baked dessert made with a pastry crust filled with Saskatoon berries (Amelanchier alnifolia), white sugar, and lemon juice, then baked until golden. The pie is typically served as a dessert, either warm or at room temperature, often with whipped cream or vanilla ice cream.

Edinburgh rock is a hard confectionery made from sugar, cream of tartar, and flavoring, traditionally prepared by boiling the mixture to the hard-crack stage and then pulling and rolling into cylindrical sticks. The candy is typically flavored with lemon, peppermint, or other fruit essences and is commonly served as a souvenir sweet or gift from Edinburgh, Scotland.

Maltesers are spherical confections consisting of a malt honeycomb center coated in milk chocolate. They are typically served as a standalone snack or sweet treat and are commonly consumed from a box or bag.

Nata de coco is a chewy, translucent jelly made by fermenting coconut water with specific bacterial cultures, then cutting the resulting gel layer into small cubes. It is commonly served as a topping in desserts, beverages, and halo-halo, or as a standalone treat in syrup.

Jersey Milk Pudding is a set milk pudding made by heating full-fat Jersey cow's milk with sugar and a setting agent such as cornstarch or gelatin, then allowing it to cool and firm in a mold or serving dish. It is typically served cold or at room temperature as a dessert, often plain or accompanied by cream, fruit, or a light syrup.

Kujirajiru is a Japanese soup made by simmering whale meat or blubber with vegetables such as daikon, burdock root, and konnyaku in a broth typically seasoned with miso or soy sauce. It is a traditional dish associated with coastal whaling communities in Japan, historically consumed as an everyday meal in regions where whale was a common protein source.

Paporer kalia is a Bengali dish in which papor (lentil-based papads) are simmered in a rich, spiced gravy typically made with onions, ginger, garlic, tomatoes, and a blend of aromatic spices such as turmeric, cumin, and garam masala. It is usually served as a main course accompaniment to steamed rice or luchi in Bengali households.

Steem is a commercially produced peanut butter infused with caffeine, made by blending ground roasted peanuts with added caffeine to deliver an energy boost comparable to coffee per serving. It is typically spread on toast, bread, or fruit, or eaten by the spoonful as a snack or pre-workout food.

Dek Boo is a traditional Northern Ugandan vegetable dish, typically prepared by simmering leafy greens such as boo (a local variety of cowpea leaves) with groundnut paste, onions, and sometimes a pinch of soda ash to tenderize the leaves and enrich the sauce. It is commonly served as a side or main accompaniment to staples like kwon (millet or sorghum bread), posho, or sweet potatoes, often forming part of a communal meal.

Dalí bread, associated with the Catalan region of Spain, is a rustic bread preparation traditionally made with simple ingredients including flour, water, salt, and yeast, often finished with olive oil and topped with tomato in the style of pan con tomate. It is typically served as a starter or accompaniment to meals, reflecting the straightforward bread traditions of the Costa Brava area where Salvador Dalí lived and worked.

Rōmen is a Japanese dish originating in Ina City, Nagano Prefecture, consisting of thick, chewy mutton-based broth served over firm, slightly alkaline noodles along with stir-fried or simmered vegetables and mutton meat. It is typically served in a deep bowl and can be ordered as either a soup-style (wet) or a drier stir-fried noodle preparation, making it a regional specialty commonly found in dedicated rōmen restaurants in the Ina Valley area.

Bhetki kalia is a Bengali curry made by cooking bhetki fish (Asian sea bass) in a spiced gravy with onions, ginger-garlic paste, and traditional spices including turmeric and chili. The dish is typically served with rice or flatbread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Aar machher kalia is a Bengali fish curry made by braising catfish (aar) in a spiced onion and tomato-based gravy, typically enriched with ginger, garlic, and traditional Bengali spices. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Sankar machher kalia is a Bengali fish curry made by simmering fish in a spiced gravy of onions, ginger, garlic, and spices including turmeric and chili, often finished with potatoes and hard-boiled eggs. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice or flatbread as part of Bengali cuisine.

Cơm is steamed rice prepared by boiling rice grains until tender and fluffy, made from long-grain white rice as a staple ingredient. It is typically served as an accompaniment to main dishes and proteins in Vietnamese meals.

Pizza with salami is prepared by topping a yeast-based dough with tomato sauce, cheese, and sliced salami before baking in a hot oven until the crust is golden and crispy. This dish is typically served hot as a main course in Italian cuisine and casual dining settings.

Roasted vegetables are prepared by cooking cut vegetables in an oven at high heat, typically with oil, salt, and seasonings until caramelized and tender. This dish is commonly served as a side course or vegetable accompaniment in international cuisine, featuring seasonal vegetables such as bell peppers, zucchini, carrots, and eggplant.

Gohan is steamed white rice prepared by cooking rice grains with water until tender and fluffy, traditionally using a rice cooker or pot method. It is a staple carbohydrate served as an accompaniment to most Japanese meals, including main dishes, soups, and pickled vegetables.

Chicken and chips consists of chicken pieces that are seasoned, breaded, and deep-fried until golden, served alongside thick-cut fried potatoes. This dish is commonly consumed as a casual meal or takeaway food, popular in British and international cuisines.

Cuttlefish balls are spherical preparations made from ground or minced cuttlefish meat mixed with binders and seasonings, then shaped and either fried, steamed, or boiled. They are commonly served as an appetizer or snack in Asian cuisines, particularly in Chinese dim sum and Southeast Asian restaurants.

Ligurian pine nut pie is a traditional savory or sweet tart from the Liguria region of Italy, made with a shortcrust or olive oil pastry base filled with a mixture of pine nuts, eggs, sugar, and sometimes honey or amaretti. It is typically served as a dessert or snack and reflects the regional abundance of pine nuts harvested from the coastal pine forests of northwestern Italy.

Baso aci is an Indonesian street food dish combining baso (meatballs) with aci, which are chewy dumplings or balls made from tapioca starch. It is typically served in a savory broth accompanied by tofu, boiled eggs, and chili sauce, and is commonly consumed as a snack or light meal.

Pampavìa is a traditional biscuit from Ceresole, in the Piedmont region of Italy, typically prepared with simple ingredients such as flour, sugar, eggs, and butter, and baked until golden and crisp. It is commonly served as an accompaniment to coffee, tea, or sweet dessert wines, and is often enjoyed during local festivities or as an everyday treat.

canonicalName: eight immortals crossing the sea gamboling around the arhat
displayName: Eight Immortals Crossing the Sea Gamboling Around the Arhat
aliases: eight immortals crossing the sea gamboling around the arhat
description: Eight Immortals Crossing the Sea Gamboling Around the Arhat is a ceremonial Chinese banquet dish composed of premium ingredients including shark cartilage, Jinhua ham, chicken, asparagus, shrimp, and the four sea delicacies (sea cucumber, shark fin, fish maw, and abalone), braised or simmered together in a rich stock. It is traditionally served as a centerpiece dish at formal banquets and celebratory occasions, with the name referencing the Eight Immortals of Chinese mythology surrounding a central arhat figure represented by one of the primary ingredients.

Hrous is a Tunisian spice paste made primarily from dried red chili peppers blended with garlic, caraway, coriander, and olive oil, sometimes with the addition of rose petals or other aromatics depending on regional variation. It is used as a condiment, marinade, or cooking ingredient and is commonly served alongside grilled meats, couscous, and other North African dishes.

Nabari Gyujiru is a beef soup originating from Nabari City in Mie Prefecture, Japan, prepared by simmering beef with vegetables and seasoning in a clear broth. It is typically served as a warming bowl dish and is associated with local culinary tradition in the Iga region of Japan.

Pona macher kopta is a Bengali preparation made from pona fish (rohu or a related carp variety) shaped into kofta-style dumplings, typically combining minced or mashed fish with spices such as cumin, coriander, green chili, and ginger before being formed into balls and fried or cooked in a spiced gravy. The dish is commonly served as part of a multi-course Bengali meal, often accompanied by rice.

Mamebu-jiru is a Japanese soup made by simmering soybeans with kombu seaweed and other vegetables in a light dashi broth. The dish is typically served hot as a side soup or light course in traditional Japanese meals, often featuring whole or partially mashed beans for texture.

A fruit sandwich consists of fresh fruits such as strawberries, kiwi, and mandarin oranges layered between slices of soft white bread, typically with whipped cream or custard spread between the bread and fruit. This Japanese-style sandwich is commonly served chilled as a dessert or light snack in cafés and bakeries.

Belyash is a Tatar fried pastry consisting of a dough pocket filled with seasoned meat, typically made from flour, water, salt, and spices. The filled dough is deep-fried until golden and is traditionally served as a savory snack or appetizer in Tatarstan cuisine.

Chingri fulkopir kalia is a Bengali curry prepared by cooking prawns with cauliflower in a spiced yogurt-based or coconut gravy, typically featuring onions, garlic, ginger, and traditional Bengali spices. The dish is traditionally served with rice or bread as part of Bengali cuisine.

Pona macher kalia is a Bengali fish curry made by frying fish pieces with potatoes and onions, then simmering them in a spiced gravy typically seasoned with turmeric, cumin, and other aromatic spices. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Cherry pie consists of a pastry crust filled with fresh or canned cherries mixed with sugar, and often thickened with cornstarch or tapioca before baking until the crust is golden. It is typically served as a dessert, either warm or at room temperature, commonly accompanied by vanilla ice cream or whipped cream.

Paille is a thin, crispy French pastry made from choux dough that is piped into long, thin strands and deep-fried until golden. It is typically served as a light appetizer or accompaniment to soups and dishes, often salted or dusted with seasonings.

Sfințișori are small Romanian fried or baked dough figures, traditionally shaped to resemble stylized human forms or birds, made from a simple yeast-leavened dough of flour, water, and salt. They are typically prepared and consumed on March 1st in connection with the Romanian Mărțișor spring celebration, often served as a festive bread alongside other seasonal foods.

Bounante is a type of waffle, typically prepared from a batter of flour, eggs, milk, and sugar cooked between patterned iron plates until golden and crisp on the outside while remaining soft inside. It is commonly served as a sweet snack or dessert, often accompanied by toppings such as powdered sugar, syrup, fresh fruit, or whipped cream.

Kore mochi is a Japanese rice cake made by steaming and pounding non-glutinous rice until it forms a firm, dense dough, which is then shaped and typically left to harden. It is commonly sliced and grilled, boiled, or added to soups and sweet preparations, often served during New Year celebrations and other traditional occasions.

Pince is typically a braised or slow-cooked meat dish, often featuring cuts such as beef shank, veal, or pork that are simmered until tender in a seasoned broth or sauce. It is commonly served as a main course alongside vegetables, potatoes, or bread, with preparation styles varying across international culinary traditions.

Gemberbolus is a Dutch-style sweet pastry typically made from a soft, spiced yeast dough rolled into a spiral or bun shape and generously filled with pieces of candied or crystallized ginger. It is commonly baked until golden and often served at room temperature as a teatime treat or breakfast item, sometimes dusted with sugar or glazed for added sweetness.

Djenkoume is a stiff porridge made from cornmeal (maize flour) cooked with tomatoes, onions, and spices, sometimes enriched with palm oil or ground dried shrimp. It is a traditional dish from Togo and neighboring West African countries, commonly served as a side dish or main accompaniment to grilled or stewed meat and fish.

Arayech are stuffed flatbread sandwiches, typically made by filling Arabic bread (khubz) with a spiced ground meat mixture, often seasoned with onions, parsley, and warm spices, then grilled or cooked on a griddle until the bread is crisp and the filling is cooked through. They are commonly served as a street food or casual meal throughout the Levant and Arabian Peninsula, often accompanied by yogurt, salads, or pickles.

Oudnin el kadhi is a Tunisian fried pastry made from thin dough that is shaped into folded or ear-like forms, deep-fried until crisp, and then soaked in sugar syrup or drizzled with honey. It is commonly served as a dessert during Ramadan, religious holidays, and family celebrations, often accompanied by tea or coffee.

Boussou la tmessou is an Algerian semolina-based cake prepared by mixing fine semolina with butter, sugar, eggs, and flavorings such as vanilla or lemon zest, then shaping the dough and baking until golden. It is typically served in small pieces alongside tea or coffee, particularly during religious celebrations and family gatherings.

Sochnik is a Russian baked pastry consisting of two layers of dough with a filling of tvorog (curd cheese), typically sweetened with sugar and sometimes flavored with vanilla or jam. The pastry is baked until golden and often served warm as a dessert or breakfast item, sometimes with sour cream or jam on the side.

Börek is a filled pastry made by wrapping a thin dough around savory fillings such as cheese, meat, or vegetables, then baking or frying until crispy. It is commonly served as an appetizer or snack in Turkish and broader Middle Eastern and Mediterranean cuisines.

Stock is a flavored liquid made by simmering water with bones, meat, vegetables, and aromatics for extended periods to extract nutrients and develop depth of flavor. It serves as a foundational ingredient in cooking, used as a base for soups, sauces, and risottos in professional and home kitchens across many cuisines.

Mesclun is a salad composed of a mixture of young, tender salad leaves and greens, typically including lettuce, arugula, chicory, and other regional vegetables, dressed simply with vinaigrette. This traditional Niçoise dish is commonly served as a light starter or side course in French cuisine.

Usban is a traditional Iraqi dish made from seasoned ground meat and rice stuffed into cleaned intestines, which are then coiled and boiled or grilled. It is typically served hot as a main course, often accompanied by flatbread and tomato sauce.

Kurnik is a traditional Russian enclosed pie made with layered dough filled with chicken, rice, mushrooms, and hard-boiled eggs, typically baked until the exterior is golden brown. The dish is served hot as a main course or appetizer, often cut into wedges to reveal the distinct layers of filling inside.

Babelutte is a hard caramel candy originating from the Flanders region of Belgium, made by cooking sugar, butter, and syrup (typically dark corn or molasses-based syrup) to a firm crack stage and allowing it to set into small rectangular or individually wrapped pieces. It is traditionally served as a confection on its own and is closely associated with the city of Dunkirk and the broader Flemish coastal area.

Mtewem is a North African dish consisting of meatballs cooked with whole or crushed garlic, onions, and spices in a braised or simmered sauce. It is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by bread or couscous.

Odontol is typically a traditional fermented beverage, commonly made from palm sap or a similar plant-based base that is left to ferment naturally, resulting in a mildly alcoholic and slightly tangy drink. It is often served chilled in small glasses or cups as a refreshment alongside meals or during social gatherings.

Șodou is a Romanian beverage made by whisking egg yolks with sugar until pale and frothy, then blending in sweet wine, sometimes warmed gently. It is typically served as a drink or light dessert, particularly associated with convalescent or restorative contexts in Romanian culinary tradition.

Pôchouse is a traditional freshwater fish stew originating from the Burgundy region of France, typically prepared by poaching pike and other local fish in a white wine and vegetable broth thickened with beurre manié. The dish is traditionally served in bowls with the cooking liquid and accompanied by crusty bread or toast for dipping.

Rougail morue is a Creole dish made with salted cod that is shredded and combined with rougail, a spiced tomato-based sauce containing onions, garlic, and chilies. The dish is typically served as a main course in Reunion Island and other Indian Ocean regions, often accompanied by rice.

Seffa is a Moroccan sweet dish made from fine semolina that is steamed and then mixed with melted butter, almonds, and cinnamon. It is typically served as a festive dessert or celebratory dish, often presented in a cone or mound shape and garnished with almonds and a dusting of cinnamon and sugar.

Espetada is a Portuguese dish consisting of meat threaded onto a skewer and grilled over an open flame or charcoal. It is typically served with garlic, bay leaves, and regional accompaniments such as maize bread or rice, commonly found in Madeira and mainland Portuguese cuisine.

Ghapama is an Armenian dish consisting of a whole pumpkin hollowed out and filled with a mixture of rice, dried fruits (such as apricots and raisins), and sometimes meat, then baked until the pumpkin flesh becomes tender. The dish is traditionally served as a festive main course, with the pumpkin flesh eaten along with the filling.

Garbure is a hearty French vegetable soup from the Béarn region that combines cabbage, beans, potatoes, and various vegetables simmered together in broth, often enriched with confit duck or ham. The dish is traditionally served as a complete meal in a bowl, sometimes with bread added directly to the soup.

Escargots à la provençale are prepared by cooking snails in their shells with a mixture of garlic, parsley, butter, and herbs typical of Provence, often including fennel or anise. The dish is traditionally served as an appetizer, with the snails remaining in their shells and eaten with a small fork to extract the meat.

Negimaki is a Japanese dish consisting of thinly sliced beef rolled around scallions (negi) and grilled or pan-seared until cooked through. The rolls are typically glazed with a soy-based sauce and served as an appetizer or entrée, often accompanied by rice.

Ćwikła is a beet and horseradish condiment made by grating or finely chopping roasted beets and mixing them with grated horseradish, vinegar, and seasonings, then typically served cold. This traditional Eastern European accompaniment is commonly served alongside meat dishes, particularly roasted meats and sausages, or as a condiment with other main courses.

Zeeuws spek is a cured and smoked pork product from the Zeeland region of the Netherlands, traditionally prepared by salt-curing pork belly or loin followed by smoking. It is typically served sliced as a cold cut or appetizer, often accompanied by bread or cheese.

Bottarga de Martigues is a cured fish roe product from the Martigues region in Provence, France, made by salting and drying the roe sacs of gray mullet. It is typically served sliced thinly as an appetizer, grated over pasta dishes, or accompanied by bread and olive oil.

Klappertaart is an Indonesian coconut cake made with a filling of grated coconut, eggs, and sugar baked inside a pastry shell, resulting in a custard-like texture. The dish is typically served as a dessert or sweet snack, often accompanying coffee or tea.

Romazava is a Malagasy beef stew prepared by simmering beef with tomatoes, onions, garlic, and leafy greens in a savory broth. The dish is traditionally served over rice and is a staple comfort food in Madagascar.

Figa albardada is a Catalan preparation of liver, typically pork or calf liver, that is coated in a flour-based batter and pan-fried until golden. It is commonly served as a main course or tapa, often accompanied by onions or simple pan sauces.

Roasted marshmallow is prepared by skewering a marshmallow on a stick or skewer and holding it over an open flame or heat source until the exterior caramelizes and turns golden to dark brown while the interior softens. It is commonly served as a standalone snack or used as a component in desserts such as s'mores, typically in outdoor or campfire settings.

Salamata is a traditional Russian porridge made by cooking rye or wheat flour in water or milk, stirred continuously until it reaches a thick, smooth consistency, then finished with butter or fat. It is a simple peasant dish historically consumed as an everyday meal, typically served hot as a staple food in rural Russian households.

AnBataSan is a Japanese-style open sandwich made by spreading butter and sweet azuki red bean paste (anko) between or atop slices of bread, typically shokupan (milk bread). It is commonly served as a snack or light breakfast item, often accompanied by coffee or tea in cafés and kissaten.

İzmir bomb (İzmir bombası) is a deep-fried potato dish originating from İzmir, Turkey, made by hollowing out a boiled or parboiled potato, filling it with a seasoned mixture typically containing ground meat, onions, and spices, then coating it in batter and frying until golden. It is commonly served as a street food or snack, often accompanied by sauces such as ketchup or yogurt-based dips.

Gilda is a Basque pintxo consisting of a skewer layered with European anchovies, olives, and chili peppers, typically bound together with a toothpick. The components are dressed with vinegar and olive oil, and the dish is served cold as an appetizer or bar snack.

Qingtuan is a traditional Chinese glutinous rice ball filled with sweet or savory ingredients, prepared by wrapping the filling in a bright green dough made from glutinous rice flour mixed with juice or powder from mugwort or other green plants. The balls are typically steamed and served as a snack or dessert, particularly during the Qingming Festival in spring.

Dibi is a grilled or roasted mutton dish prepared with allium vegetables such as onions and garlic, originating from Somali cuisine. The meat is typically cooked over charcoal or an open flame until well-roasted, and is commonly served as a main course at celebrations and gatherings.

Frita is an Algerian sandwich made with fried potato slices, typically seasoned with harissa and other spices, served in bread with various accompaniments such as tomatoes, onions, and sometimes meat. It is a popular street food and casual meal in Algeria, often consumed for lunch or as a quick snack.

Crozets de Savoie are small, square pasta shapes made from wheat flour and eggs, originating from the Savoy region of France. They are traditionally served in broths or paired with cheese and potatoes in hearty alpine dishes.

Motichur Laddu is an Indian sweet made from fine gram flour (besan) that is fried into thin strands, combined with ghee, sugar, and cardamom, then shaped into round balls. The laddu is typically served as a dessert or sweet confection during festivals and celebrations.

Pralines are confections made by coating nuts, typically almonds or hazelnuts, with caramelized sugar or chocolate, then often roasting or further processing to achieve a crunchy texture. These sweets are traditionally served as petit fours or boxed confections, commonly associated with French and Belgian confectionery traditions.

Dakos is a traditional Greek appetizer consisting of twice-baked barley or wheat rusks that are moistened with water or olive oil and topped with tomatoes, feta cheese, olives, and oregano. The dish is typically served as a light snack or meze and originated in Crete as a way to use stale bread.

A po' boy is a submarine sandwich served on French bread that typically contains fried seafood such as shrimp or oysters, or alternatively roast beef, along with lettuce, tomatoes, pickles, and mayonnaise-based sauce. The sandwich originated in New Orleans and is traditionally served as a casual lunch or dinner item, often accompanied by a side of fries.

Scarpinocc is a traditional Italian pasta from Lombardy, consisting of small filled pasta parcels made with wheat flour dough and typically stuffed with cheese, breadcrumbs, and herbs. The parcels are boiled in salted water until they float to the surface, then served dressed simply with melted butter and grated cheese or in a light broth.

Kömbe is a traditional Turkish pastry made from a dough of flour, butter or clarified butter, sugar, and eggs, sometimes flavored with mahleb or other spices, then shaped into flat rounds or rings and baked in an oven or under hot coals. It is commonly prepared during religious holidays and festive occasions in various regions of Turkey, where it is served as a sweet snack or accompaniment to tea.

Kojak is a Hungarian lollipop consisting of a hard boiled sugar candy formed around a stick, traditionally produced in fruit flavors. It is sold as a packaged confection in shops and kiosks and consumed as a handheld sweet snack.

Sì is a traditional Fuzhou dessert made by rolling small balls of glutinous rice flour dough, which are boiled and then coated in a mixture of ground soybean powder and sugar. It is customarily prepared and eaten during the Winter Solstice festival as a symbol of family reunion and celebration.

Khoresht mast is an Iranian dish made by cooking lamb or veal with strained yogurt, saffron, turmeric, and dried rose petals, resulting in a thick, pale stew. It is traditionally served with saffron-infused white rice and is particularly associated with Isfahani cuisine, where it is often prepared for festive occasions.

Sandapao is a steamed bun made primarily from glutinous rice flour, resulting in a soft, chewy texture distinct from wheat-based buns. It is typically filled with sweet or savory ingredients and served as a snack or light meal in various Asian culinary contexts.

Viennese is a Sicilian dessert, typically prepared as a layered ice cream or semifreddo cake that commonly includes combinations of pistachio, chocolate, and vanilla along with candied fruits or nuts. It is often served chilled in sliced portions as a refreshing finish to a meal, particularly during warmer months.

Tiler Naru is a Bengali sweet made by cooking grated coconut with jaggery or sugar and coating or binding the mixture with sesame seeds (til), then shaping it into small balls or discs. It is typically prepared during winter festivals such as Poush Sankranti and served as a traditional confection alongside other homemade sweets.

Culeca is typically a Spanish-style preparation, likely featuring a base of seasonal vegetables, legumes, or seafood slow-cooked with olive oil, garlic, and regional herbs. It is commonly served as a shared starter or light main course, often accompanied by crusty bread to soak up the flavorful juices.

Labangalatika is a traditional Bengali sweet consisting of a deep-fried pastry parcel made from maida (refined wheat flour) and stuffed with a filling of khoya (reduced milk solids), grated coconut, sugar, and cardamom, then sealed and secured with a clove. The finished parcels are typically soaked or dipped in sugar syrup and served as a festive or ceremonial confection in Bengali cuisine.

Sarbhaja is a Bengali sweet prepared by deep-frying a dough made from maida flour, water, and vegetable oil until golden, then soaking the fried pieces in a cardamom-flavored sugar syrup. It is typically served as a dessert during festivals and special occasions in Bengali cuisine.

A custard tart consists of a pastry shell filled with a smooth custard made from eggs, milk or cream, and sugar, typically baked until the filling sets with a slight wobble. It is traditionally served at room temperature or chilled as a dessert, often dusted with nutmeg on top.

Riz à l'impératrice is a classic French dessert made with rice cooked in milk and sweetened, then mixed with Bavarian cream and candied fruits before being set in a mold. The chilled molded dessert is typically unmolded and served cold as a formal plated dessert, often accompanied by a fruit sauce or coulis.

Glutinous rice is cooked and then mixed with sweetened coconut milk, while fresh mango slices are arranged alongside or on top of the rice. The dish is typically served as a dessert, with the creamy coconut-infused rice complementing the sweet and slightly tart flavor of the mango.

Un helvası is a Turkish halva made by cooking flour in butter or oil until deeply browned, then mixing with hot sugar syrup and pine nuts to create a dense, crumbly paste. It is typically served as a dessert or sweet treat, often portioned into small pieces and consumed with tea or coffee.

Chocolate covered fruit consists of fresh or sometimes dried fruit pieces that are dipped or coated in melted chocolate, which hardens to form a shell. This combination is commonly served as a dessert, snack, or part of a dessert course in restaurants and casual dining settings.

Bouyiourdi is a Greek cheese dish made by baking feta cheese, typically with tomatoes, peppers, and olives, until the cheese softens and begins to melt. It is traditionally served hot in a small baking dish as an appetizer or meze, often accompanied by bread for scooping.

Posolanka is a traditional Slovenian sour soup made from fermented or pickled vegetables, typically including sauerkraut or pickled turnip, along with smoked meats and sometimes potatoes, simmered together in a seasoned broth. It is commonly served as a warming first course or main dish, particularly during colder months and festive occasions in Slovenian households.

Yuasa is a traditional Bashkir dish prepared by stuffing a sheep's or cow's intestine casing with a mixture of liver, fat, grains such as millet or barley, and onions, then boiling or simmering it until cooked through. It is typically served sliced as part of festive meals and celebrations, often accompanied by broth or other meat dishes.

Ban machh bhaja is a Bengali dish of small freshwater fish that are cleaned, coated in spiced flour or batter, and deep-fried until crispy. The fish are typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by rice and curry at Bengali meals.

canonicalName: satsumaimo do sweet potato gooey mitsu imo sweet potato sausce
displayName: Satsumaimo Do Sweet Potato Gooey Mitsu Imo Sweet Potato Sausce
aliases: satsumaimo do sweet potato gooey mitsu imo sweet potato sausce
sweet potato gooey mitsu imo sweet potato sausce
description: Mitsu Imo is a Japanese preparation featuring satsumaimo (Japanese sweet potato) cooked until tender and coated in a thick, sticky mitsu (sweet syrup or honey-based glaze) that gives the dish its characteristic gooey texture. It is typically served as a side dish or snack, showcasing the natural sweetness of the satsumaimo enhanced by the caramelized sauce.

Crespillos are a traditional Aragonese fritter made by dipping borage leaves in a sweetened egg and flour batter, then deep-frying them until golden. They are commonly served as a dessert or snack, often dusted with sugar, and are particularly associated with the cuisine of the Aragon region of Spain.

Knafeh Nabulsiyeh is a Palestinian dessert made with shredded pastry layers filled with white cheese, typically akawi or similar mild cheese, and topped with a clarified butter or ghee-soaked shredded pastry crust that is baked until golden and crispy. The warm pastry is traditionally finished with a drizzle of simple sugar syrup or honey and served immediately as a sweet treat, often enjoyed with tea or coffee.

Honey toast is a Japanese-style open-faced sandwich made by spreading butter and honey on thick-cut white bread, sometimes with added ingredients such as whipped cream, condensed milk, or fruit, then toasting until golden. It is typically served warm as a breakfast or dessert item, often cut into triangles and accompanied by ice cream or eaten on its own.

Duckanoo is a traditional Bahamian sweet dumpling made from grated corn, coconut, brown sugar, and spices wrapped in banana leaves and boiled until firm. The dish is typically served as a dessert or breakfast item during holidays and special occasions in the Bahamas and Caribbean regions.

Chajá is a traditional Uruguayan dessert composed of layers of choux pastry, whipped cream, and fresh fruit, typically peaches or strawberries, topped with meringue. The dish is commonly served as a celebratory dessert and is particularly popular during summer months in Uruguay.

Filloas are thin Galician crêpes made from a batter of flour, eggs, and milk, cooked on a griddle until golden and typically filled with meat, seafood, or cream. They are traditionally served as a dessert or appetizer, often accompanied by a sauce or folded into quarters for presentation.

Mazapán de Toledo is a traditional Spanish confection made from ground almonds and sugar, often bound with egg white and formed into dense, sweet blocks or shaped figures. It originated in Toledo and is typically served as a dessert or sweet treat, sometimes wrapped in rice paper or decorated with royal icing.

Aletria is a Portuguese sweet vermicelli dessert made by toasting thin noodles in butter or oil until golden, then combining them with sugar, cinnamon, and sometimes eggs or milk to create a creamy or firm consistency. It is typically served as a warm dessert or traditional treat, often at Christmas or special occasions.

Biscotto salute is an Italian twice-baked almond biscuit made by baking a dough log and then slicing and re-baking the pieces until crisp. The hard, crunchy cookies are traditionally served as a dunking biscuit with coffee or wine.

Ovdukh is a cold soup made from yogurt, cucumbers, and herbs, typically prepared by mixing yogurt with diced vegetables and seasoning with salt and spices. It is traditionally served chilled as a refreshing starter or side dish, particularly during hot weather in Azerbaijani cuisine.

Sauté is a cooking technique in which food is cooked quickly in a small amount of fat over relatively high heat in a wide, shallow pan, allowing ingredients to brown while remaining tender. This method is commonly applied to vegetables, meat, or seafood and is typically served as a side dish, main course component, or standalone entrée across various cuisines.

Tahu petis is an Indonesian dish made from tofu that is deep-fried until golden and crispy, then served with petis (a dark, pungent shrimp or fish paste) as a dipping sauce or topping. The dish is typically served as a side dish or appetizer, often accompanied by rice or as part of a larger meal.

South African street food encompasses a variety of dishes sold by vendors, including bunny chow (a hollowed-out loaf of bread filled with curry), boerewors rolls (grilled sausage in a bun), vetkoek (deep-fried dough often stuffed with mince or jam), and gatsbys (long submarine-style sandwiches filled with meat, chips, and sauces). These items are typically prepared at roadside stalls, township eateries, and markets, and are commonly eaten as quick, affordable meals on the go.

Grechanyky are Ukrainian patties made from cooked buckwheat combined with ground meat, onion, and egg, shaped into oval or round forms and pan-fried or baked until a crust forms. They are typically served as a main course alongside sour cream, vegetables, or a simple sauce.

Älme is a traditional Kazakh dish typically prepared using ingredients common to Central Asian cuisine, which often include meat such as lamb, horse, or beef, along with simple staples like flour, onions, and broth. It is commonly served as part of a shared meal during family gatherings or celebrations, reflecting the hospitality customs central to Kazakh food culture.

Buffalo satay from Kudus is prepared by marinating sliced or cubed water buffalo meat in a spiced mixture, then skewering and grilling the pieces over charcoal. It is typically served with a peanut sauce or sweet soy sauce alongside compressed rice (lontong or ketupat), and is a common street food and ceremonial dish in the Kudus region of Central Java.

Penyu satay is a Balinese dish consisting of sea turtle meat that is minced, mixed with grated coconut and a spice paste of shallots, garlic, chili, turmeric, and other seasonings, then wrapped around bamboo skewers and grilled over charcoal. It has traditionally been served during Hindu religious ceremonies and festive gatherings in Bali, though its preparation is now heavily restricted due to sea turtle conservation laws.

Əzmə is an Azerbaijani dish made from finely chopped or minced liver, typically combined with herbs, onions, and seasonings, then cooked until the mixture forms a dense, paste-like consistency. It is commonly served as a spread or side dish, often accompanied by flatbread or as part of a larger spread of appetizers.

Pangkong squid is an Indonesian dish in which squid is pounded or beaten (pangkong) to tenderize it before being grilled or cooked over charcoal, often seasoned with a spiced marinade including garlic, shallots, and chili. It is commonly served as a street food or casual meal alongside steamed rice and sambal dipping sauce.

Nauryz kozhe is a traditional Kazakh soup prepared by boiling meat (typically horse meat, beef, or lamb) with seven symbolic ingredients including millet, dried apples, raisins, and spices, creating a slightly sweet and savory broth. The dish is traditionally served during Nauryz, the Kazakh spring festival, and is believed to bring health and prosperity to those who consume it.

Jesa is a traditional Korean ceremonial dish prepared as an offering to ancestors, typically featuring seasoned vegetables including kimchi, fresh fruits, and cooked proteins arranged on a table altar. The preparation follows specific ritual guidelines and is served during jesa ceremonies, accompanied by alcoholic beverages such as wine or soju as part of the ancestral veneration ritual.

Soto Kudus is an Indonesian soup from the Kudus region in Central Java, prepared by simmering beef or chicken with turmeric, garlic, galangal, and other spices in a rich broth. The dish is traditionally served in a bowl with the meat, rice cakes, and accompaniments such as shallots, chilies, and lime on the side.

Arnavut ciğeri is a Turkish dish of thinly sliced liver that is coated in flour and seasonings, then pan-fried until crispy on the outside while remaining tender inside. It is typically served as a meze (appetizer) or main course, often accompanied by lemon wedges, onions, and fresh herbs.

Soto Padang is a traditional Indonesian soup from West Sumatra made by simmering beef or chicken with turmeric, garlic, galangal, and other spices, then finished with coconut milk and served with rice, hard-boiled eggs, and potato cubes. The dish is typically eaten as a breakfast or lunch meal and is characterized by its golden color and rich, aromatic broth.

Strapatsada is a Greek dish made by scrambling eggs with tomatoes, onions, and olive oil until they reach a soft, creamy consistency. It is typically served hot as a breakfast or light dinner dish, often accompanied by bread or feta cheese.

Macaroni soup is prepared by cooking macaroni pasta in broth or water, typically combined with vegetables such as tomatoes, onions, and celery, then simmered until the pasta is tender. This Italian-origin dish is traditionally served hot as a first course or light meal, often finished with grated cheese and olive oil.

Tengkleng is an Indonesian soup dish made by simmering goat or lamb meat with bones in broth, typically seasoned with turmeric, garlic, ginger, and other spices until the meat becomes tender. The dish is traditionally served hot in bowls as a main course, often accompanied by rice or as part of everyday meals in Central Java.

Čokolešnik is a Slovenian chocolate-flavored corn flour porridge made by cooking corn semolina with milk and mixing in cocoa powder or chocolate, resulting in a thick, smooth consistency. It is commonly served as a warm breakfast dish or dessert, often eaten by children, and is sometimes topped with butter or additional sugar.

Flatliner Fries are deep-fried potato strips cooked in pure lard, served salted as a side dish at the Heart Attack Grill restaurant chain. They are typically offered alongside the establishment's high-calorie burgers and milkshakes as part of its themed menu.

Yaohon is a Congolese hot pot dish in which assorted meats, fish, and vegetables are simmered together in a seasoned broth, often prepared communally in a single large pot. It is typically served at gatherings and celebrations, with diners sharing directly from the cooking vessel alongside accompaniments such as fufu or rice.

Kaolaolao is typically prepared as a savory dish featuring slow-cooked or simmered ingredients, often combining meats, vegetables, and aromatic seasonings in a flavorful broth or sauce. It is commonly served hot as a main course, frequently accompanied by rice, flatbread, or noodles depending on regional preferences.

Prune porridge is made by cooking dried plums (prunes) with a grain base such as oats or another cereal, often simmered until the prunes soften and release their natural sweetness into the mixture. It is typically served as a warm breakfast dish, sometimes topped with cream, butter, or additional dried fruit.

Mashed chickpea is prepared by cooking chickpeas until soft and then mashing or blending them, typically with ingredients such as olive oil, garlic, lemon juice, and salt. It is served as a side dish, spread, or dip across a wide range of international cuisines, often accompanied by flatbread, vegetables, or as a component of larger plates.

Smetannik is a Russian layered cake made with thin sponge cake layers brushed with coffee or tea syrup and filled with a sour cream-based cream filling. The cake is typically finished with a sour cream frosting on the exterior and served as a dessert.

Cullen skink is a Scottish soup made by simmering smoked haddock with potatoes and onions in a fish or chicken stock base, typically finished with cream or milk. The dish is traditionally served hot as a starter or light main course, particularly in Scottish cuisine.

Potato soup is made by simmering peeled potatoes with broth or water, typically combined with onions and other aromatics, then blended or left chunky depending on the preparation style. The soup is commonly finished with cream or milk, seasoned with salt and pepper, and often served hot as a starter or main course, sometimes garnished with bacon, cheese, or fresh herbs.

Semolina halva is prepared by toasting semolina in butter or oil, then combining it with sugar or honey and hot water or milk to create a thick, pudding-like consistency. The mixture is typically enriched with nuts, such as pine nuts or almonds, and served warm as a dessert or sweet treat, often garnished with cinnamon or coconut.

Pâté Hénaff is a traditional French pâté made from pork liver and meat, seasoned with spices and salt, then cooked and potted in its own fat for preservation. The product is typically served cold as an appetizer or charcuterie component, spread on bread or crackers.

Rybnik is a Russian fish pie made with a yeast dough crust filled with layers of fish, typically sturgeon or other white fish, combined with buckwheat groats and rice. The pie is baked until golden and traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by melted butter or sour cream.

Tacos gobernador are prepared by filling wheat tortillas with shrimp, cheese, onion, tomato, and roasted poblano peppers, then typically pan-fried or griddled until the exterior becomes crispy. This dish originates from Mexico and is commonly served as a main course or appetizer, often accompanied by lime and salsa.

Újházi is a Hungarian chicken soup made by simmering chicken with vegetables including carrots, parsnips, and celery, typically served with noodles or other pasta additions. The dish is traditionally presented as a complete meal in a bowl, with the broth, tender chicken pieces, and vegetables combined together.

Hausa koko is a millet-based porridge made by grinding millet flour and cooking it with water or milk until thick, often sweetened with sugar or honey. It is traditionally served as a breakfast dish or light meal in Hausa communities across West Africa, typically eaten warm with a spoon.

Huevos al albañil is a Mexican dish of eggs cooked with tomatoes, onions, and chiles, typically prepared by scrambling or frying eggs together with a fresh salsa or chile-based sauce. The dish is commonly served for breakfast or brunch, often accompanied by warm tortillas and as part of traditional Mexican morning meals.

Yaihai cake is a Guinean filled doughnut in the beignet family, made from a dough that is wrapped around a fish filling and deep-fried until golden. It is typically served as a street food snack or appetizer.

Kandu Kukulhu is a Maldivian dish consisting of chicken cooked in a spiced curry sauce, typically incorporating ingredients such as coconut milk, chili, turmeric, and other aromatic spices common to South Asian cooking traditions. It is commonly served with rice or roshi (flatbread) as part of a main meal.

Abuttekamo is a Japanese regional dish consisting of damselfish that is grilled whole, typically over an open flame to crisp the skin while keeping the flesh tender. It is a traditional preparation associated with coastal areas of Japan and is commonly served as a main fish course alongside rice and other accompaniments.

Malé gâteau is a Guinean rice donut made from a batter of rice flour that is shaped into small rounds and deep-fried until golden. It is commonly sold as a street food snack and eaten at breakfast or as an accompaniment to tea and coffee.

Rui Machher Kalia is a Bengali fish curry prepared by frying pieces of rohu fish and then simmering them in a rich, spiced gravy made with onions, ginger, garlic, tomatoes, and a blend of whole and ground spices including turmeric, cumin, coriander, and garam masala. It is commonly served with steamed white rice as a main course in Bengali households and restaurants across West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Moje is a traditional Spanish preparation, typically consisting of bread soaked or moistened in a flavorful mixture that commonly includes olive oil, garlic, tomato, and regional seasonings. It is often served as a rustic side dish or light meal, sometimes accompanied by cured meats, fish, or vegetables depending on the local variation.

A koeksister is a fried pastry made from plaited or twisted dough that is deep-fried until golden and crispy. It is typically served warm, often coated with cinnamon sugar or dipped in sugar syrup, and eaten as a sweet snack or dessert.

Buridda is a Ligurian fish stew traditionally prepared by layering dried stockfish or salt cod with vegetables such as onions, tomatoes, and olives, then braising the mixture in white wine and broth. The dish is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by toasted bread for soaking up the cooking liquid.

Pescado a la talla is a grilled fish dish where the whole fish is split lengthwise, marinated in a mixture of dried chiles, vinegar, and spices, then grilled over an open flame until the skin crisps and the flesh becomes tender. The dish is typically served with lime wedges and accompanied by rice, beans, or tortillas as part of Mexican coastal cuisine.

Kohaku-namasu is a Japanese pickled vegetable dish made by combining shredded carrot and daikon radish that are salted, drained, and dressed with a sweet vinegar sauce. It is typically served as a side dish or appetizer, often during festive occasions and New Year celebrations.

Balıq kebab is a grilled fish dish where fish is marinated and cooked on skewers over an open flame or charcoal, often seasoned with spices, herbs, and oil. It is typically served hot with bread, lemon, and vegetables as a main course in Mediterranean and Middle Eastern cuisine.

Sardella is a traditional Italian condiment made from finely minced or pounded fresh sardines mixed with olive oil, garlic, and chili peppers, often produced in Calabria. It is typically served as a spread on bread, pasta dressing, or accompaniment to vegetables and seafood dishes.

Beccafico sardines are whole sardines stuffed with a breadcrumb mixture containing pine nuts and sultanas, then rolled and typically baked or fried until golden. This Sicilian dish is traditionally served as a second course or antipasto, often accompanied by lemon or a light tomato sauce.

Pasta 'ncasciata is a Sicilian baked pasta dish made by layering cooked pasta with a meat ragù sauce, béchamel, and cheese, then baking until golden. The dish is traditionally served as a main course and is characteristic of Palermo's culinary heritage.

Salted herring is prepared by curing fresh herring with salt, a preservation method that draws out moisture and extends shelf life. The dish is typically served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by bread, potatoes, or onions, and is common in Northern European and Scandinavian cuisines.

Bhetki macher khichudi is a Bengali dish combining rice and lentils cooked together with pieces of bhetki fish, typically seasoned with turmeric and spices. The ingredients are cooked in a single pot until soft and tender, creating a comforting one-dish meal often served with ghee and accompaniments like pickles or fried chilies.

Koi machher tok jhal is a Bengali fish curry made with koi fish, typically cooked with tamarind or other souring agents and green chilies to create a tangy, spicy gravy. The dish is commonly served with steamed rice as part of traditional Bengali meals.

Australian street food encompasses portable dishes sold from food trucks, markets, and festival stalls, with preparations ranging from grilled and fried to baked items such as meat pies, sausage rolls, fish and chips, and sausage sizzles served on white bread with onions. Multicultural influences have expanded offerings to include items like halal snack packs, souvlaki, bánh mì, and dim sims, typically served in hand-held or takeaway packaging for immediate consumption.

Pona macher alugoppa is a Bengali fish curry made with pona fish (Systomus sarana) and cauliflower (alugoppa referring to the combination of potato and cauliflower in some regional usages), cooked in a spiced gravy typically featuring mustard oil, turmeric, cumin, and chili. It is commonly served as a main course alongside steamed rice in traditional Bengali household meals.

Acwica is a traditional Northern Ugandan vegetable dish prepared by boiling or stewing leafy greens, often combined with groundnut (peanut) paste or simsim (sesame) to enrich the sauce. It is typically served as a side accompaniment to staples such as millet bread (kwon kal), posho, or cassava in Acholi and Lango households.

Hongtak samhap is a traditional Korean combination dish consisting of fermented skate (hongeohoe), boiled pork belly (bossam), and kimchi, eaten together in a single bite. It is commonly served at gatherings and celebratory meals in the Jeolla region of South Korea, often accompanied by makgeolli (rice wine).

Bonbons piment are small, deep-fried fritters made from a batter of ground split peas or lentils seasoned with chili peppers, garlic, and spices, a preparation common in Mauritius and Réunion. They are typically sold as street food or served as a snack, often accompanied by chutneys or eaten alongside bread.

Tel koi is a Bengali preparation of koi fish (climbing perch) cooked in mustard oil with spices such as turmeric, chili, and mustard paste, resulting in a pungent, oil-based sauce. It is typically served as a main course alongside steamed rice in traditional Bengali meals.

Mashanza is a traditional Congolese dish from the Kivu region made by curdling fresh cow's milk and combining it with mashed cooked bananas or plantains. It is typically served as a side or main course alongside cassava, beans, or grilled meat.

Ilish machher korma is a Bengali curry prepared by cooking hilsa fish in a creamy sauce made with yogurt, coconut milk, and ground spices including cumin, coriander, and turmeric. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is a popular preparation in Bengali cuisine that balances the fish's distinctive flavor with the richness of the korma base.

Chueo-tang is a Korean soup made by boiling pond loach with vegetables and seasonings until the fish breaks down into the broth, creating a thick and murky consistency. The dish is typically served hot as a comfort food or hangover remedy, often accompanied by rice and side dishes.

Chanakhi is a Georgian stew traditionally prepared by layering meat (typically beef or lamb), potatoes, and onions in a clay pot with spices and broth, then sealed and slow-cooked until the ingredients merge into a tender dish. It is commonly served as a main course in Georgian cuisine, often accompanied by bread to soak up the rich sauce.

Obe ata is a Nigerian pepper sauce made by blending fresh red peppers, tomatoes, and onions with palm oil, then simmering with meat or fish stock until thickened. The sauce is typically served as an accompaniment to rice, swallows, or starchy staples in West African cuisine.

Boudin blanc de Rethel is a traditional French white sausage made from pork meat, fat, and breadcrumbs or other binders, seasoned with spices and traditionally produced in the Rethel region of northern France. It is typically poached or grilled and served hot, often accompanied by mustard or with mashed potatoes and sautéed apples.

Kepta duona is a Lithuanian fried bread dish made from sliced rye or wheat bread that is deep-fried until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a side dish or appetizer, often accompanied by garlic sauce or sour cream for dipping.

Farcement is a traditional Savoyard dish consisting of potatoes, cabbage, and pork (typically bacon or sausage) mixed together and cooked in a single pot until the ingredients are tender and well-combined. It is served as a hearty main course, often eaten as comfort food in the Savoy region of France and surrounding Alpine areas.

Carcamusa is a Spanish fried pastry typically filled with ground meat, onions, and spices, then deep-fried until golden and crispy. It is commonly served as a tapa or appetizer, often accompanied by a dipping sauce such as aioli or tomato sauce.
