It is typically served with steamed white rice as a main course in Bengali households.

Punti machh bhaja is a Bengali fried fish dish made by coating small punti fish (Puntius sophore) with spices such as turmeric and salt before shallow- or deep-frying them in oil until crisp. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice and lentils in everyday Bengali meals.

Sorpunti machh bhaja is a Bengali fried fish preparation in which fish pieces are marinated with spices such as turmeric and chili, then shallow- or deep-fried until crisp. It is commonly served as a side dish or starter alongside rice and dal in traditional Bengali meals.

Sorse diye dhela machher jhal is a Bengali preparation made with dhela fish (a small freshwater catfish) cooked in a pungent mustard-based sauce, typically incorporating mustard paste, green chilies, turmeric, and mustard oil. The dish is prepared by simmering the fish in the spiced mustard gravy and is commonly served with steamed white rice as a main course in Bengali households.

Khoyra paturi is a Bengali dish in which small khoyra fish are marinated with mustard paste, green chilies, turmeric, and mustard oil, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed or lightly pan-cooked until the leaf imparts its aroma to the fish. It is typically served as a main course with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Cooked rice is prepared by boiling or steaming rice grains in water until the liquid is absorbed and the grains become tender. It is a staple carbohydrate served worldwide as an accompaniment to various main dishes, curries, stir-fries, and other cuisines.

Bhola machh bhaja is a Bengali fried fish dish prepared by coating bhola fish (a type of croaker or drum fish) in spices such as turmeric and chili, then shallow- or deep-frying until crisp. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice and dal in traditional Bengali meals.

Chela machh bhaja is a Bengali fried fish dish made from chela (a small freshwater fish common in the rivers of Bengal), typically seasoned with turmeric and salt before being shallow- or deep-fried in mustard oil until crisp. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside rice and dal in traditional Bengali meals.

Eel soup is a traditional German dish made by simmering eel pieces with vegetables such as potatoes, onions, and celery in a broth, often seasoned with herbs and sometimes finished with cream or egg yolk. It is typically served hot as a main course soup, particularly popular in regions along German rivers and waterways.

Manti are small dumplings made from thin dough sheets filled with seasoned ground meat, typically beef or lamb, and sometimes potatoes or cheese, then boiled or steamed. They are traditionally served in a bowl topped with yogurt, melted butter or oil, and often dried mint or a tomato-based sauce, common across Central Asian and Middle Eastern cuisines.

Ayran is a savory yogurt-based beverage made by blending yogurt with cold water and salt until frothy. It is commonly served as a refreshing drink in Caucasian cuisine, particularly in Turkey and surrounding regions, often accompanying meals or consumed on its own.

Niçard salad is a composed salad from Nice, France, made with mixed greens, hard-boiled eggs, anchovies, olives, tomatoes, and potatoes, typically dressed with olive oil and vinegar. The salad is traditionally served as a light lunch or appetizer and may also include tuna or other local vegetables depending on regional variations.

Pizza Margherita is prepared by topping a thin or thick pizza base with tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella cheese, basil, and olive oil, then baking in a high-temperature oven until the crust is crisp and the cheese melts. The dish is traditionally served whole or in slices as a main course or casual meal, originating from Naples, Italy.

Dek Akeo is a traditional Northern Ugandan vegetable dish prepared by boiling leafy greens in hot water and mixing them with groundnut (peanut) paste to form a thick, savory stew. It is typically served as a side dish alongside staple starches such as millet bread, posho, or cassava.

Spätzle are small egg noodles made from a simple dough of flour, eggs, and salt that is pressed or scraped directly into boiling water to cook. This Swabian and Alpine specialty is typically served as a side dish, often topped with melted cheese and crispy fried onions, or mixed into savory preparations like meat stews.

Žganci is a traditional Slovenian and Croatian dish made from a simple dough of flour, eggs, and salt that is torn or grated into small pieces and cooked in boiling salted water or broth until tender. It is typically served as a side dish or main course, often accompanied by sautéed onions, gravy, or meat dishes.

Kkakdugi is a Korean radish kimchi made by cutting Korean radish into small cubes and fermenting them with salt, garlic, ginger, and chili peppers. It is typically served as a banchan (side dish) alongside rice and main courses in Korean meals.

Caragols a la llauna is a Catalan dish of snails cooked in their shells on a flat metal sheet or tin (llauna), typically seasoned with garlic, parsley, and olive oil. The snails are traditionally served hot as an appetizer or casual dish, often accompanied by bread and eaten with a small fork or pin to extract the meat from the shell.

Schläsches Himmelreich is a Silesian stew made from pork ribs or shoulder meat combined with dried pears, prunes, and spices, simmered slowly until the meat is tender and the fruit partially dissolves into the cooking liquid. The dish is traditionally served warm with bread or potatoes as a hearty main course in Silesian cuisine.

Coronation chicken is a cold dish consisting of cooked chicken mixed with a curry-flavoured mayonnaise sauce, typically made with curry powder, apricot preserve, and cream. The dish is traditionally served with rice or salad and was created in Britain in 1953 for the coronation of Queen Elizabeth II.

Chocolate cake is prepared by mixing cocoa powder or melted chocolate with flour, eggs, sugar, and butter, then baking the batter until set. It is typically served sliced as a dessert, often accompanied by frosting, ganache, or whipped cream.

A burrito is a flour tortilla wrapped around a filling of seasoned meat, beans, rice, and other ingredients such as cheese, salsa, and vegetables. It is typically served as a handheld meal, often accompanied by sour cream, guacamole, or additional salsa on the side.

Pemmican is a concentrated food made by mixing dried meat (typically beef or game) with rendered fat and often berries or other dried fruits, then formed into compact cakes or bars. Traditionally prepared by Indigenous peoples of North America, it was designed as a shelf-stable, nutrient-dense provisions for travel and storage, requiring no cooking or refrigeration.

Aşure is a thick, sweet pudding made by cooking a mixture of grains, legumes, and dried fruits together until they soften and blend into a creamy consistency, typically sweetened with sugar and flavored with cinnamon and other spices. It is traditionally served as a dessert or religious offering, particularly during Islamic holidays and special occasions, often garnished with nuts and pomegranate seeds.

Gado-gado is an Indonesian salad composed of blanched or lightly cooked vegetables such as cabbage, bean sprouts, green beans, and potatoes, combined with hard-boiled eggs and often tofu or tempeh, then dressed with a peanut sauce made from ground peanuts, garlic, chilies, and palm sugar. The dish is typically served at room temperature as a light meal or side dish, commonly found as street food and in casual dining settings throughout Indonesia.

Hujiao bing is a Chinese flatbread made from a simple dough of flour, water, and salt that is rolled with a filling of ground black pepper, salt, and sometimes sesame seeds before being pan-fried until crispy. The bread is typically served as a snack or breakfast item and is eaten warm, often accompanied by soy milk or other beverages.

Chingri machh diye chholar dal is a Bengali lentil curry made with split chickpeas (chana dal) cooked with prawns and aromatic spices including turmeric, cumin, and bay leaves. The dish is typically served with rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Aam diye chingri curry is a Bengali shrimp curry prepared with mango as a primary flavoring ingredient, creating a sweet and tangy sauce. The dish is traditionally served with rice and is common in Bengali cuisine, particularly in West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Caldo de piranha is a Brazilian fish broth prepared by simmering whole or filleted piranha with water, garlic, onion, tomato, and herbs until a rich, clear soup is produced. It is commonly served in riverside communities of the Amazon and Pantanal regions, often accompanied by rice or bread, and is traditionally consumed as a warming meal or restorative dish.

Cream shrimp is a dish in which shrimp are cooked in a sauce made primarily from heavy cream, often combined with butter, garlic, and seasonings such as black pepper or herbs. It is typically served over rice, pasta, or with bread as a main course.

Shrimp balls are made by combining minced or ground shrimp with binders such as starch, egg white, and seasonings, then shaping the mixture into small spheres that are typically deep-fried or boiled until cooked through. They are served as appetizers, dim sum items, or added to soups, hot pots, and noodle dishes across various Asian cuisines.

Bhadal chingri is a Bengali preparation in which prawns are cooked with mustard paste, green chilies, and mustard oil, resulting in a pungent, spiced dish. It is typically served with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Azara is a Japanese stew-like soup (shirumono) traditionally prepared in the Tohoku region, made by simmering ingredients such as salted salmon, tofu, daikon, carrot, and other root vegetables in a seasoned broth. It is typically served hot as a warming dish during colder months, often accompanying rice as part of a home-style meal.

Katla Komola is a Bengali fish dish prepared by cooking katla (a type of carp) in a tangy citrus-based gravy made with orange (komola) juice, along with spices, ginger, and mustard oil. It is typically served as a main course with steamed rice.

Ruhi bhuna is a Bengali fish curry made from rohu (ruhi) pieces that are marinated with turmeric, lightly fried, and then slow-cooked in a reduced masala of onions, garlic, ginger, green chilies, and ground spices until the gravy thickens and coats the fish. It is typically served with steamed rice or plain paratha as a main course in Bangladeshi and Eastern Indian meals.

Pepernoot is a small, round spiced cookie made from a mixture of flour, sugar, and warm spices including pepper, nutmeg, and cinnamon, bound together and baked until firm. These treats are traditionally served during Dutch winter holidays, particularly around Christmas and Sinterklaas celebrations, and are commonly enjoyed with hot chocolate or coffee.

Muesli is a breakfast cereal made from a mixture of rolled oats, nuts, dried fruits, and sometimes grains or seeds, typically combined without cooking. It is traditionally served with milk, yogurt, or fruit juice, often eaten cold as a breakfast or light meal.

Rijstevlaai is a Dutch dessert consisting of a pastry crust filled with a creamy rice pudding mixture, typically made with cooked rice, custard, and sometimes raisins or other dried fruits. It is traditionally baked until the pastry is golden and served as a sweet treat, often enjoyed with coffee or as a dessert course.

canonicalName: tengra machh alu korola diye jhol
displayName: Tengra machh alu korola diye jhol
aliases: tengra machh alu korola diye jhol
description: Tengra machh alu korola diye jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with tengra fish (a freshwater catfish), potatoes, and bitter gourd simmered in a light, spiced broth. The dish is typically seasoned with turmeric, cumin, and other traditional Bengali spices, and is commonly served with steamed rice as a main course.

Chingri malai curry is a Bengali prawn dish prepared by cooking shrimp in a creamy sauce made with coconut milk, onions, garlic, ginger, and spices including turmeric and cumin. The dish is typically served with steamed rice or flatbread as part of Bengali cuisine.

Daab chingri is a Bengali dish where prawns are cooked inside a hollowed coconut with coconut milk, spices, and aromatics, then steamed or slow-cooked until tender. The dish is traditionally served in the coconut shell itself as a single-portion presentation, commonly found in Bengali cuisine across West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Chingri machher polau is a Bengali rice dish where prawns are cooked with basmati rice along with spices, ghee, and aromatics such as onions and bay leaves. The dish is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by raita or a light curry.

Chingri machher cutlet is a Bengali appetizer made from shrimp and fish that are minced, mixed with spices and binding ingredients, then formed into patties and shallow-fried until golden. The cutlets are typically served as a starter or snack, often accompanied by lemon wedges and chutney.

Kochu patai Chingri vapa is a Bengali steamed dish in which prawns (chingri) are marinated with mustard paste, green chili, turmeric, and mustard oil, then wrapped in colocasia (kochu) leaves and steamed until cooked through. It is a traditional preparation from the Bengal region, commonly served with steamed rice as part of a main meal.

Tomato diye chingrir patla jhol is a Bengali light prawn curry prepared by cooking shrimp with tomatoes, turmeric, green chilies, and mustard oil in a thin, watery broth seasoned with cumin, coriander, and ginger-garlic paste. It is typically served as a main course alongside steamed white rice, with the thin gravy (jhol) intended to be mixed into the rice before eating.

Ol chingri is a Bengali dish made by cooking taro root (ol) with shrimp (chingri) in a spiced preparation that typically includes mustard paste, green chilies, turmeric, and mustard oil. It is commonly served as a side dish with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Smocha is a Kenyan street food wrap made by rolling a chapati around a sausage (smokie) along with a kachumbari-style mixture of chopped tomatoes, onions, and chili, often seasoned with salt, lemon, and chili sauce. It is typically sold by street vendors and kiosks as a quick, handheld snack eaten on the go.

Chingrir pur vora chhanar kofta is a Bengali dish consisting of spherical chhana (fresh curd cheese) dumplings stuffed with a spiced shrimp (chingri) filling, which are then deep-fried until golden. The koftas are typically served as a starter or side dish in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by a light sauce or as part of a larger meal.

Gondhoraj Ol Chingri is a Bengali preparation combining ol (elephant foot yam, Amorphophallus paeoniifolius) and chingri (prawns or shrimp), cooked together with mustard oil, green chilies, and the aromatic Gondhoraj lime (Citrus × limon 'Gondhoraj'), a variety prized for its distinctive fragrance. The dish is typically prepared by sautéing or simmering the yam and seafood with spices, finished with a squeeze or zest of Gondhoraj lime, and served as a side dish alongside steamed rice in a traditional Bengali meal.

Lever gryta is a Swedish liver stew made by simmering sliced or cubed liver, typically from pork or beef, with onions, broth, and seasonings such as thyme and bay leaf until tender. It is commonly served as a weekday meal accompanied by boiled or mashed potatoes and lingonberry jam.

Chingri machher kalia is a Bengali curry featuring prawns cooked in a rich, spiced gravy made with onions, ginger-garlic paste, tomatoes, and aromatic spices including bay leaves and cardamom. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice or bread as part of Bengali cuisine.

Potol chingri bharta is a Bengali dish made by hollowing pointed gourds (potol) and stuffing them with a spiced mixture of shrimp (chingri) and other seasonings, then cooking until tender. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread and represents a traditional preparation from Bengali cuisine.

Chingri begun er tel jhol is a Bengali curry made with prawns and eggplant simmered in a light oil-based gravy seasoned with mustard seeds, nigella seeds, and spices. The dish is typically served as a main course with steamed rice or flatbread in Bengali cuisine.

Kumro patay chingrir bora is a Bengali dish consisting of shrimp fritters wrapped in pumpkin leaves, typically made by coating shrimp in a spiced batter and deep-frying until golden. The dish is commonly served as an appetizer or snack, with the tender pumpkin leaves providing a subtle flavor that complements the seasoned shrimp.

Kosha chingri is a Bengali preparation of prawns stir-fried with onions, ginger, garlic, and spices including turmeric and chili, cooked until the prawns are well-coated and the onions caramelize. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Gondhoraj chingri malaikari is a Bengali shrimp curry made by cooking prawns in a creamy sauce of coconut milk, yogurt, and spices, with gondhoraj limes providing a distinctive aromatic citrus flavor. The dish is typically served with rice or bread as part of Bengali cuisine.

Prawn Kabiraji is a Bengali cutlet made by coating marinated prawns in a spiced gram flour batter and deep-frying until golden, typically served with tamarind and mint chutneys. The dish is commonly prepared as an appetizer or street food in Bengali cuisine and is often accompanied by sliced onions and lemon wedges.

Tacos de lengua are made with beef tongue that is simmered until tender, then sliced or shredded and served in warm tortillas. The dish is typically topped with onions, cilantro, and lime, and is commonly served as a traditional Mexican street food or casual meal.

Quiaude is a traditional Québécois fish soup prepared by simmering freshwater or saltwater fish with potatoes, onions, salt pork, and milk or cream to create a hearty broth. It is typically served hot as a main course, often accompanied by bread, in home and regional dining settings in Quebec.

Achi Soup is a traditional Eastern Nigerian soup thickened with ground achi seeds (from the Brachystegia eurycoma tree), typically prepared with assorted meats, fish, stockfish, palm oil, leafy vegetables such as ugu or uziza, and seasonings like crayfish, pepper, and stock cubes. It is commonly served hot alongside swallow staples such as fufu, pounded yam, eba, or semolina as a main course.

Capoun is a medieval French preparation of a capon—a castrated rooster prized for its tender, flavorful meat—typically roasted or boiled and dressed with sauces made from ingredients such as verjuice, ginger, and other spices common in late medieval European cookery. It appears in historical French culinary manuscripts and was served as a prestigious dish at noble or wealthy tables, reflecting the high status of capon as a luxury poultry ingredient.

Abak atama soup is a traditional soup from the Efik and Ibibio peoples of southeastern Nigeria, prepared by simmering periwinkles, crayfish, stockfish, and assorted meats in a base of palm oil along with atama leaves (Heinsia crinita), which give the soup its distinctive bitter, aromatic flavor. It is typically served with fufu, pounded yam, or other starchy accompaniments as a main meal.

Peeled beans porridge is prepared by cooking skinned beans with palm oil, crayfish, onion, peppercorns, and salt until the mixture reaches a soft, thick consistency. It is a common West African dish served as a main meal, often eaten on its own or accompanied by plantain, yam, or other staple foods.

Oil bean soup is a West African dish, particularly associated with Nigerian cuisine, prepared by cooking African oil beans (Pentaclethra macrophylla) together with proteins such as beef, stockfish, and snail, seasoned with local spices and often enriched with palm oil. It is typically served as a main course accompanied by starchy sides such as boiled yam, plantain, or rice.

Umm Rgaigah is a traditional Gulf Arab dish made from thin, crepe-like flatbreads (rgaig) layered or torn and combined with honey, butter, and sometimes date syrup or eggs. It is typically served as a breakfast or celebratory dish, often presented warm with the sweet toppings poured over the delicate bread.

Kola kanda is a traditional Sri Lankan herbal porridge made by cooking rice with coconut milk and a blend of medicinal green herbs, commonly including gotukola (Centella asiatica), karapincha (curry leaves), and other locally sourced plants. It is typically consumed as a morning meal or health tonic, often served warm and accompanied by jaggery or treacle to balance the herbal bitterness.

Tjvjik is a traditional Armenian dish made by quickly sautéing liver, heart, lungs, and kidneys—typically from lamb or beef—with onions, tomatoes, and spices such as salt and pepper. It is commonly served as a main course, often accompanied by lavash or bread, and is considered a characteristic offal preparation in Armenian home cooking.

Breadfruit Porridge is prepared by cooking peeled and cubed breadfruit with water or milk until soft, then mashing or blending to create a thick, creamy consistency, typically enriched with palm oil for flavor and richness. This dish is commonly served as a breakfast or comfort food in Caribbean and West African cuisines, often accompanied by additional protein or enjoyed as a standalone meal.

Ofe Oha is a Nigerian soup made with cocoyam leaves, palm oil, and various proteins such as meat, fish, or seafood, simmered together with aromatics and spices until thickened. The dish is traditionally served hot with fufu, cassava, or other starch-based sides as a complete meal.

Egusi soup is a West African dish prepared by cooking ground melon seeds with vegetables, meat or fish, and seasonings in a broth base, typically thickened to a stew-like consistency. It is traditionally served with fufu, pounded yam, or cassava products as an accompaniment.

Pempek panggang is an Indonesian dish consisting of grilled fish cake made from fish paste and tapioca flour, typically served with a tangy vinegar-based sauce. The dish is traditionally from Palembang, South Sumatra, and is eaten as a snack or light meal, often accompanied by rice or as street food.

Kadyos, manok, kag ubad is a Filipino stew made with pigeon peas (kadyos), chicken (manok), and coconut palm shoots (ubad) simmered together with onions, garlic, and tomatoes. The dish is traditionally served as a main course with rice and is characteristic of Visayan cuisine.

Porcupine meatballs are meatballs made from ground meat mixed with uncooked rice, which creates a spiky appearance as the rice grains protrude during cooking. The meatballs are typically baked or simmered in tomato sauce until the rice is cooked through, resulting in a tender interior with a distinctive textured surface.

Cara isi is an Indonesian stuffed pancake made from a thin, slightly crispy rice flour or wheat flour batter cooked in a special mold, filled with a savory or sweet mixture that commonly includes grated coconut and palm sugar. It is typically sold as a street food snack and served warm, often found at traditional markets and roadside stalls across Indonesia.

Tipatipa is a traditional Batak dish from North Sumatra, Indonesia, typically prepared from young, unripe rice grains that are pounded and processed into a flattened, flaky form, sometimes mixed with grated coconut and palm sugar. It is commonly served as a snack or ceremonial food during Batak cultural gatherings and is often enjoyed alongside coffee or tea.

Areias are small Portuguese shortbread-style pastries made from a dough of flour, butter, sugar, and sometimes egg yolk or lemon zest, shaped into small rounds or crescents and baked until pale, then rolled in granulated sugar while still warm. They are typically served as a sweet accompaniment to coffee or tea and are common offerings in Portuguese bakeries, particularly around festive occasions.

Jopara is a traditional Paraguayan dish made by cooking together Phaseolus vulgaris (common beans) and locro (a type of hominy or dried corn), typically simmered until both components are tender and the stew reaches a thick consistency. It is commonly consumed as a main meal, particularly associated with the Day of the Dead celebrations on November 2nd, when it is traditionally prepared and eaten in Paraguay.

Crispy tortilla chips are served with salsa, a sauce typically made from tomatoes, onions, cilantro, and chili peppers that may be prepared fresh or cooked. This appetizer is commonly served at the beginning of meals in Mexican and Tex-Mex restaurants, with diners using the chips to scoop and consume the salsa.

Bola Ubi is an Indonesian sweet snack made from grated cassava (ubi) mixed with grated coconut, sugar, and sometimes eggs, then formed into balls and deep-fried until golden. The resulting fritters are crispy on the outside and soft inside, typically served as a dessert or between-meal snack.

Kue leker is a thin, crispy Indonesian crepe made from a batter of rice flour, coconut milk, and eggs, cooked on a flat griddle until lightly golden. The crepe is typically filled with grated coconut mixed with brown sugar and folded or rolled, commonly served as a sweet snack or dessert.

Ekuru is a Nigerian steamed bean pudding made by grinding beans into a paste, mixing with sautéed onions, tomato soup, bouillon cube, nutmeg, salt, and vegetable oil, then steaming the mixture until set. It is typically served as a main dish or side at meals, often accompanied by other Nigerian staple foods.

Fenek is a Maltese rabbit stew traditionally prepared by braising rabbit meat with tomatoes, garlic, red wine, and herbs until tender. The dish is typically served as a main course with crusty bread or pasta to accompany the rich sauce.

Iscas is a Portuguese dish consisting of liver, typically pork or beef, that is marinated in wine, garlic, and spices before being pan-fried until browned. It is commonly served as a main course with bread or potatoes, often accompanied by the reduced cooking sauce.

Tirokafteri is a Greek cheese dip made by blending feta cheese with roasted red peppers and olive oil until smooth and creamy, sometimes with the addition of garlic or chili for heat. It is typically served cold or at room temperature as an appetizer with bread, crackers, or vegetables for dipping.

Macun şekeri is a traditional Turkish paste candy made by cooking fruit juice or fruit pulp with sugar and sometimes spices until thickened, then cut into small pieces and dusted with powdered sugar or starch. It is typically served as a sweet confection or dessert, often accompanying Turkish coffee or tea.

Xerém is a traditional Portuguese cornmeal dish made by cooking maize flour with water or broth until it reaches a thick, porridge-like consistency, often enriched with butter or lard. It is typically served as a side dish or breakfast food, sometimes accompanied by fried fish or other proteins.

A Parmo is a breaded and deep-fried meat cutlet, typically pork or chicken, topped with béchamel sauce and melted cheese, then finished under a grill. It is traditionally served with chips and peas, originating from Middlesbrough in northern England.

Efó is a Bahian vegetable stew made with leafy greens such as spinach or amaranth, onions, garlic, and dendê (palm) oil, often combined with dried shrimp and spices. The dish is traditionally served as an accompaniment to rice and beans or with grilled meats as part of Bahian cuisine.

Tunde ke kabab are minced meat kebabs from Lucknow, India, made by grinding mutton or beef with spices, onions, and herbs, then shaping and pan-frying or grilling until cooked through. The kababs are typically served hot as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by lemon wedges and mint chutney.

Silverside is a cut of beef taken from the hindquarter, specifically the rear section of the animal just above the leg, and is traditionally prepared by slow cooking methods such as braising, boiling, or salt-curing to tenderize the lean meat. It is commonly served sliced with vegetables, gravy, or mustard sauce, and is a staple of British and Commonwealth cuisine, often featured in Sunday roasts or as corned silverside.

Shark chutney is a Mauritian dish made from boiled shark flesh that is deboned, flaked, and mixed with lime juice, salt, and green chillies, sometimes combined with raw onion and fresh coriander to form a thick paste. It is commonly served as a side condiment or accompaniment to street food dishes such as dholl puri and roti in Mauritius.

Vaho is a traditional Nicaraguan dish consisting of salted beef, green and ripe plantains, and cassava (yuca), layered over plantain leaves and steamed together in a covered pot. It is typically served on a plantain leaf accompanied by a cabbage and tomato salad (ensalada de repollo), most commonly as a Sunday family meal.

Roti buaya is a traditional Betawi bread shaped to resemble a crocodile, made from wheat flour dough and baked until firm. It is a ceremonial food typically presented as part of wedding customs in Jakarta, where the crocodile shape symbolizes fidelity, and it is not primarily intended for eating but rather as a ritual offering or display item.

Nasi Jamblang is a traditional dish from Cirebon, West Java, in which steamed rice is individually wrapped in teak leaves, which impart a subtle aroma to the rice. It is served as a set meal accompanied by a variety of side dishes such as fried tofu, tempeh, sambal goreng, salted fish, and offal-based preparations, with diners selecting their preferred accompaniments from an array of pre-cooked options.

Thoran is a South Indian vegetable stir-fry made by cooking finely chopped or grated vegetables with coconut, cumin seeds, and dried chilies until tender and lightly caramelized. It is typically served as a side dish with rice and curry in Kerala and other regions of South India.

Carrilleras are braised pork jowls that are slowly cooked in red wine or broth until tender, often with aromatics such as onions, garlic, and herbs. The dish is typically served as a main course in Spanish and Latin American cuisine, with the meat becoming gelatinous and fork-tender after the long cooking process.

Piemontese rice is a traditional Italian dish from the Piedmont region made by cooking risotto rice with broth, butter, and cheese, typically enriched with meat ragù and sometimes truffles. The dish is served as a main course or primo course, characterized by its creamy texture achieved through the gradual addition of liquid and constant stirring during cooking.

Pilaki is a dish of vegetables, typically white beans or legumes, cooked slowly with tomatoes, onions, and olive oil until tender. It is served as a cold appetizer or side dish in Mediterranean and Middle Eastern cuisines, often garnished with fresh herbs.

Quince dessert is prepared by cooking quinces with sugar and spices until softened, often resulting in a paste-like or jam consistency that may be set into a solid form. It is typically served as a sweet course, sometimes accompanied by cheese or nuts, and is common in Turkish and broader Eastern Mediterranean cuisine.

Roti john is a stuffed flatbread sandwich made by filling a flattened roti or paratha with seasoned ground meat (typically beef or mutton), onions, and eggs, then pan-frying until golden. It is commonly served as a street food or casual meal, often accompanied by curry sauce or chili sauce for dipping.

Tekwan is an Indonesian soup from Palembang made by combining fish paste, tapioca flour, and spices shaped into small balls or dumplings that are cooked in a clear broth with vegetables and sometimes shrimp. The dish is traditionally served hot as a street food or light meal, often garnished with fried shallots, cilantro, and served with sambal or chili paste on the side.

Shrimp Creole is prepared by cooking shrimp in a tomato-based sauce seasoned with the Creole holy trinity of onions, celery, and bell peppers, along with garlic, spices, and stock. The dish is typically served over white rice as a main course.

Petit salé is a French charcuterie preparation consisting of pork belly or shoulder that has been cured in salt brine, then typically boiled or simmered until tender. The dish is traditionally served hot with vegetables such as cabbage, carrots, and potatoes, and is common in French bistro cuisine.

Skirlie is a Scottish side dish made by frying diced onions in butter or fat, then mixing in toasted oatmeal until the mixture is bound and crumbly. It is traditionally served alongside meat dishes, particularly game or beef, and is a common accompaniment to Scottish meals.

Mersu is an ancient Mesopotamian confection prepared by combining dates with ingredients such as butter, flour, and nuts or dried fruits, then shaping the mixture into small cakes or balls. It was typically served as a sweet offering in religious rituals or as a festive treat at banquets and special occasions.

Bok machh bhaja is a Cambodian fried fish dish in which whole or portioned freshwater fish is marinated with salt and aromatics, then deep-fried or pan-fried until crispy. It is typically served with steamed rice, fresh vegetables, and dipping sauces such as fish sauce or fermented condiments as part of a shared meal.

Darkina machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared by cooking darkina fish (a small freshwater fish common in Bangladesh and West Bengal) in a pungent, spiced gravy based on mustard paste, green chilies, and mustard oil. It is typically served with steamed white rice as a main course meal.

Chanda machher vorta is a Bengali preparation made by mashing or roughly flaking cooked chanda fish (Chanda ranga, a small freshwater fish) with mustard oil, green chilies, onion, and sometimes roasted garlic or dried red chili. It is typically served as a side dish alongside plain steamed rice, functioning as a simple, rustic accompaniment common in Bangladeshi and West Bengali home cooking.

Foli machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared by cooking foli fish (Notopterus notopterus, also known as bronze featherback) in a spiced gravy made with mustard paste, green chilies, turmeric, and mustard oil. It is a traditional dish from the Bengal region, typically served with steamed white rice as a main course.

Desi tengrar tel jhal is a Bengali preparation made with small freshwater tengra catfish cooked in a spiced mustard oil base, typically featuring mustard seeds, green chilies, turmeric, and nigella seeds. It is a dry or semi-dry dish served as a side with steamed rice, common in everyday Bengali household meals.

Lau patay kachki machher paturi is a Bengali preparation in which small kachki fish (Corica soborna) are wrapped with bottle gourd strips or leaves along with mustard paste, green chili, turmeric, and mustard oil, then steamed or slow-cooked in the parcel until the fish absorbs the spiced coating. It is typically served as a side dish with plain steamed rice in a traditional Bengali meal.

Shingi machh bhaja is a Bengali preparation in which stinging catfish (Heteropneustes fossilis, known as shingi) are cleaned, marinated with turmeric and salt, then shallow- or deep-fried in mustard oil until crisp. It is commonly served as a standalone side dish or alongside steamed rice in traditional Bengali meals.

Bacha machher jhal bhuna is a Bengali dry-style spiced fish dish prepared by frying small bacha fish (Eutropiichthys vacha) and then cooking them with mustard oil, turmeric, red chili, onion, and ginger-garlic paste until the masala is well-reduced and coats the fish. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice in Bengali households across Bangladesh and West Bengal.

Begun diye bata machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry where fish is cooked in a spiced broth with eggplant (begun) that is mashed or ground into the sauce base. The dish is typically served with steamed rice and features a balance of spices, eggplant, and fish in a medium-consistency gravy.

Ban machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry made by cooking fish in a spiced sauce containing mustard oil, onions, and aromatic spices such as turmeric and chili. The dish is traditionally served as a main course with rice or flatbread as part of Bengali cuisine.

Chitol machh bhaja is a Bengali fried fish dish made from chitol (featherback fish), seasoned with spices and deep-fried until crispy. It is traditionally served as a main course or side dish in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by rice and curry.

Foli machh bhaja is a Bengali fish preparation where fish is coated in a spiced batter and deep-fried until crispy. The dish is typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanying rice and curries in Bengali cuisine.

Kachki machh bhaja is a Bengali dish made from raw or partially cooked fish that is marinated and then deep-fried until crispy. The dish is typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by rice and curry in Bengali cuisine.

Koi machh bhaja is a Bengali preparation of carp fish that is marinated and deep-fried until crispy. The dish is typically served as an appetizer or main course accompaniment in Bengali cuisine, often garnished with onions and green chilies.

Pangas machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry made with pangas (swai) fish cooked in a spiced tomato-based gravy with onions, garlic, ginger, and green chilies. The dish is typically served with steamed rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Chelsea is a Japanese hard candy produced by Meiji, made by cooking sugar, butter, and flavorings such as yogurt scotch or butter scotch into small rounded lozenges that are individually wrapped in patterned paper. It is typically sold in boxed or bagged portions and consumed as a snack or with tea or coffee.

White bonde of Kamarpukur is a traditional Bengali sweet made from chhana (fresh curdled milk solids) that is shaped into small balls and soaked in sugar syrup, giving it a pale, whitish appearance. Originating from Kamarpukur in West Bengal, India, it is typically served as a dessert or offered as prasad in religious contexts, particularly in association with the birthplace of Sri Ramakrishna.

Dewliser is a Kurdish dish prepared by cooking rice with sautéed onions and lamb chops, allowing the meat's flavor to infuse the rice during simmering. It is typically served as a main course, often at family meals or communal gatherings.

Naiboa is a traditional preparation made by soaking or cooking cazabe (cassava flatbread) with panela (unrefined whole cane sugar), softening the bread and infusing it with sweetness. It is typically served as a simple dessert or snack, particularly associated with Dominican and Caribbean culinary traditions.

Bûş is a traditional dish of Kurdish cuisine, typically prepared as a hearty preparation that commonly includes grains, dairy, or legumes cooked slowly to develop a rich, comforting texture. It is often served as a home-style main course, frequently accompanied by flatbread or yogurt, and enjoyed in communal family settings.

Khushka is a plain steamed or boiled rice dish prepared without added spices, vegetables, or meat, resulting in simple white rice cooked in water or light broth. It is commonly served as a side dish in South Indian and Sri Lankan cuisine, typically accompanied by curries, gravies, or other sauce-based dishes that provide additional flavor.

Tirit is a dish made by tearing or crumbling stale flatbread and soaking it in broth, typically prepared with sheep meat and fat to produce a hearty, porridge-like consistency. It is served as a main meal across parts of the Middle East, Central Asia, and North Africa, often presented in communal bowls and eaten by hand or with bread.

Chraime is a North African Jewish dish in which fish is poached or braised in a spiced tomato-based sauce seasoned with harissa (pilpelchuma), garlic, and paprika. The dish is typically served hot with bread or over rice as a main course, particularly during Shabbat meals and Jewish holidays.

Pegao is a crispy, caramelized rice dish made by cooking rice in broth and then allowing the bottom layer to brown and stick to the pot, creating a golden crust. The dish is typically served as a side or main course in Latin American and Filipino cuisines, often accompanied by beans, meat, or vegetables.

The Monte Cristo is a sandwich made with sliced turkey, ham, and Swiss cheese between three slices of bread, typically French bread or white bread, that is battered and deep-fried until golden. It is traditionally served with powdered sugar and jam on the side, and often accompanied by a cherry or other garnish.

Sopas is a Spanish soup typically made by simmering bread, garlic, paprika, and broth together until the bread softens and breaks down into the liquid. The dish is traditionally served hot in bowls and is a common preparation in Spanish cuisine, particularly in regions like Extremadura and Andalusia.

Fetîr is a traditional Turkish pastry made by layering dough with milk, eggs, and baker's yeast, then baking until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a breakfast or dessert item, often accompanied by syrup or honey.

Nasi tim is an Indonesian steamed rice dish prepared by cooking rice with broth, typically chicken or pork, along with vegetables and seasonings in a covered pot or clay vessel. The dish is commonly served as a complete meal in Indonesian cuisine, often accompanied by proteins such as chicken, eggs, or preserved meats mixed directly into the rice.

Pölsa is a Swedish hash made by chopping and mixing beef, pork, and potatoes, then pan-frying the mixture until browned and crispy. It is traditionally served with pickled beetroot, raw onion, and a fried egg on top.

Devilled sauce is prepared by combining mayonnaise or a cream base with mustard, cayenne pepper, Worcestershire sauce, and other spices to create a pungent condiment. It is typically served as an accompaniment to cold meats, seafood, eggs, or vegetables in British and international cuisine.

Minchee is a Macanese dish made from minced meat (traditionally pork or beef) stir-fried with diced potatoes, onions, and soy sauce, often finished with a fried egg on top. It is typically served as a casual meal over rice or as a filling for sandwiches and pastries.

Maque choux is a Creole corn dish made by cooking corn kernels with onions, peppers, and tomatoes until the vegetables break down and the mixture becomes creamy and thickened. The dish is typically served as a side dish or vegetable accompaniment in Louisiana Creole cuisine.

Konbini sweets are pre-packaged desserts sold in Japanese convenience stores, typically mass-produced and including items such as cream-filled pastries, chilled cakes, pudding, daifuku, and parfaits made with ingredients like wheat flour, sugar, dairy cream, eggs, matcha, and red bean paste. They are generally refrigerated, served chilled directly from the packaging, and consumed as an on-the-go snack or quick dessert.

Hebnîsk is a lentil-based dish typically prepared by simmering lentils with aromatic spices and vegetables until they reach a thick, stew-like consistency. It is commonly served warm as a main course or hearty side, often accompanied by flatbread or rice.

Maşik is a traditional Kurdish dish prepared by cooking rice with butter until tender and rich in flavor. It is typically served as a warm, hearty staple, often accompanying meat dishes or stews in home-style meals.

Pîçik is a traditional Kurdish dish typically prepared with simple, rustic ingredients common to the region, often featuring seasoned meat, grains, or vegetables cooked slowly to develop flavor. It is commonly served as part of a shared meal, accompanied by flatbread, yogurt, or fresh herbs in the style of home-style Kurdish cuisine.

Bomiwô is a Beninese dish consisting of a thick porridge or dough made from corn flour cooked with water until it reaches a smooth, firm consistency. It is typically served as a starchy staple alongside sauces, stews, or grilled meats and fish in traditional Beninese meals.

Douida is a traditional dish from Rabat, Morocco, typically prepared as a hearty soup or stew featuring fine vermicelli or thin pasta simmered in a seasoned broth, often enriched with warming spices common to Moroccan cuisine such as cinnamon, pepper, and saffron. It is commonly served warm as a comforting starter or light main course, particularly during cooler months or festive family gatherings.

Postor kalia is a Bengali dish made by cooking poppy seed paste (posto) with potatoes and sometimes other vegetables or fish in a gravy seasoned with mustard oil, green chilies, and spices. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Hirçkî is a Kurdish cold dish made from diced cucumber and onion, typically combined with yogurt to form a refreshing salad or side preparation. It is commonly served alongside main courses in Kurdish cuisine, functioning as a cooling accompaniment to heavier meat or grain-based dishes.

Ifisashi is a Zambian dish made by simmering vegetables such as pumpkin leaves and tomatoes with onion in peanut oil and water until tender, creating a thick, protein-rich stew. It is typically served as a side dish or main course alongside a starchy staple such as nshima (maize porridge).

Kumspî is a Luxembourgish potato dish in which boiled or steamed potatoes are mashed or crushed and combined with melted cheese until smooth and creamy. It is typically served hot as a main course or side dish, often accompanied by sausages, bacon, or cured meats.

Mafrum is a Tunisian dish of vegetables, typically potatoes and tomatoes, stuffed with ground meat and spices, then fried or baked until golden. It is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by bread and harissa sauce.

Konro is an Indonesian grilled meat dish, typically made with beef ribs or other cuts that are marinated in a mixture of spices, coconut milk, and aromatics before being grilled over charcoal. The dish is commonly served as a main course at informal dining establishments and is often accompanied by rice and sambal.

Khichdi is a one-pot dish made by cooking rice and lentils together with ghee, spices, and vegetables until soft and well-blended. It is commonly served as comfort food, often accompanied by yogurt, pickles, or curry, and is frequently eaten during illness or as a light meal in Indian cuisine.

Ladob is a Mauritian dessert prepared by cooking sliced cooking bananas and sweet potatoes in a sauce made from sugar and cow's milk until tender and caramelized. The dish is typically served warm as a sweet course or dessert, often enjoyed during family meals and special occasions.

Pitepalt is a traditional Swedish dumpling made from grated potatoes and barley flour mixed with onions, then boiled in salted water or broth. It is typically served hot with melted butter, lingonberry jam, and pickled cucumber as accompaniments.

A flour tortilla is filled with seasoned cooked chicken and rolled tightly into a cylindrical shape. The burrito is typically served as a handheld meal, often accompanied by salsa, sour cream, or other condiments on the side.

Garri is made by grating cassava root, fermenting it, and then frying or toasting the resulting granules until dry and light brown. It is typically served as a staple carbohydrate in West African cuisine, often eaten with soups, stews, or prepared as a porridge-like dish by mixing with hot water or broth.

Gowi nitutu is a traditional dish from Nias Island, Indonesia, made from steamed or boiled cassava that is pounded or mashed until smooth and often mixed with grated coconut. It is typically served as a staple accompaniment to meat dishes or consumed as a filling everyday food in Niasan households.

Xîlok is a traditional Kurdish dessert prepared by combining eggs with ground walnuts and cinnamon to form a sweet, spiced mixture that is cooked until set. It is typically served as a dessert or sweet course, often accompanying tea during family gatherings and seasonal celebrations.

Mylta is a traditional Finnish dessert made from cloudberries that are mashed or whipped together with sugar and semolina or cream until the mixture becomes light and airy. It is typically served chilled as a dessert, often accompanied by milk or cream.

Dudh parse is a Bengali sweet preparation that typically features milk (dudh) as a central ingredient, likely simmered and reduced with sugar and flavorings such as cardamom to create a rich, creamy base. It is commonly served as a dessert at the end of a meal or during festive occasions, often enjoyed chilled or at room temperature.

Lonka parse is a dish from Bengali cuisine, typically prepared as a spiced preparation featuring chilies or a chili-based component, as the name suggests a relation to 'lonka,' the Bengali word for chili pepper. It is commonly served as an accompaniment alongside steamed rice or flatbreads, often forming part of a traditional Bengali meal with other curries and side dishes.

Bata machher horogouri is a Bengali dish made by grinding small freshwater fish (bata fish) into a paste, which is then mixed with spices such as mustard, green chili, and turmeric before being cooked, often steamed or pan-fried. It is traditionally served as a side dish with steamed rice in Bengali households, particularly in the regions of West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Bata machher tok is a Bengali sour fish dish made with bata fish (Labeo bata) cooked in a tamarind-based sauce, typically combined with mustard, green chilies, and spices to produce a tangy, pungent flavor profile. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice in traditional Bengali households.

Niku-dofu is a Japanese simmered dish made by cooking sliced beef and firm tofu together in a seasoned broth of dashi, soy sauce, mirin, and sugar, sometimes with the addition of green onions or other vegetables. It is typically served as a main dish alongside steamed rice in home-style Japanese cooking.

Aam diya bata machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry in which small freshwater fish, typically bata (Labeo bata), are cooked with raw or semi-ripe mango pieces in a spiced mustard-based gravy, often seasoned with mustard oil, green chilies, turmeric, and nigella seeds. The dish is commonly served with steamed rice as part of an everyday Bengali meal, particularly during the summer months when raw mangoes are in season.

Doi bata is a Bengali preparation in which mustard seeds are ground into a paste and combined with yogurt (doi) to create a tangy, pungent sauce or marinade. It is commonly used with fish or vegetables and served as part of a larger meal alongside rice.

Amiwô is a Beninese dish made from corn flour cooked with tomato paste, onions, and peppers into a reddish dough-like porridge, often prepared with the broth of a meat such as chicken, guinea fowl, or fish. It is typically served hot alongside grilled or stewed meat or fish and a spicy tomato sauce.

Tfaya is a Moroccan sweet and savory dish featuring caramelized onions combined with raisins, honey, and cinnamon, traditionally prepared by slow-cooking these ingredients together until the onions become soft and deeply browned. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to meat dishes, particularly tagines or roasted meats, where the sweet glaze complements the savory protein.

Bata machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering freshwater fish (typically catfish) in a spiced gravy made with onions, tomatoes, and mustard oil, seasoned with turmeric, chili, and fenugreek leaves. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as part of Bengali cuisine in West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Aar machher doi sorse is a Bengali fish curry made with catfish cooked in a yogurt-based sauce flavored with mustard seeds and spices. The dish is traditionally prepared by simmering the fish in a blend of yogurt, mustard paste, and seasonings, and is typically served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Aar machher sorse jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared with catfish cooked in a spiced mustard and chili-based sauce. The dish is traditionally served with rice and features a balance of pungent mustard flavor with heat from red chilies and other Bengali spices.

Yam porridge is a West African dish made by boiling peeled and cubed yams with vegetables, spices, and protein such as fish, meat, or beans until soft, then mashing or blending to create a thick, creamy consistency. It is typically served hot as a main course or comfort food, often accompanied by bread or eaten as a standalone meal.

Xohol is a traditional Mexican dessert prepared by cooking cornmeal with grated coconut and cinnamon until it reaches a thick, pudding-like consistency. It is typically served as a sweet course at the end of a meal or during regional festivities.

Hib is a traditional Croatian dessert made by combining dried figs and almonds with brandy, shaped into a firm loaf or roll and left to mature before slicing. It is typically served in thin slices as a sweet course or alongside coffee and digestifs, particularly during the winter holiday season.

Lebu pabda is a Bengali fish dish prepared by cooking pabda (butter catfish, Ompok bimaculatus) with lemon (lebu) juice, mustard, green chilies, and turmeric, typically in a light, tangy sauce. It is commonly served with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Tel pabda is a Bengali fish dish prepared by cooking pabda (butter catfish, Ompok pabda) in a mustard oil-based sauce along with spices such as turmeric, green chilies, and nigella seeds. It is typically served as a main course alongside steamed rice and is a common preparation in the cuisines of West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Tomato pabda is a Bengali fish curry prepared by cooking pabda (butterfish, Ompok pabda) in a tangy sauce made with tomatoes, mustard oil, green chilies, turmeric, and spices such as cumin and coriander. It is typically served with steamed white rice as part of a multi-course Bengali meal.

Pabda machh bhaja is a Bengali preparation of pabda fish (a freshwater catfish) that is cleaned, coated in spices and flour, then deep-fried until crispy. The dish is typically served as a main course or side dish with rice and curry in Bengali cuisine, particularly in West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Katla kalia is a Bengali fish curry prepared by frying pieces of katla (rohu carp) and then simmering them in a rich, spiced gravy made with onion, ginger, garlic, tomato, and whole spices such as cumin, cardamom, and bay leaves. It is typically served with steamed white rice as a main course and is a common dish in Bengali Hindu households, particularly during festive occasions.

Zhimazao is a traditional Chinese sweet soup made by dissolving sesame paste or ground black sesame seeds in hot water, sometimes thickened with glutinous rice flour or starch to achieve a smooth, porridge-like consistency. It is typically served warm as a breakfast item, dessert, or snack, and is commonly found at street food stalls and dim sum establishments across southern China.

Parse machh bhaja is a Bengali-style fried fish dish in which fish pieces, typically river fish, are marinated with turmeric and salt before being shallow- or deep-fried in mustard oil until golden and crisp. It is commonly served as a starter or side dish alongside rice and dal in traditional Bengali meals.

Gondhoraj Pabda is a Bengali fish dish prepared by cooking pabda (Ompok pabda), a small freshwater catfish, in a mustard-based gravy infused with gondhoraj lime (a fragrant lime variety native to Bengal), green chilies, and mustard oil. It is typically served with steamed white rice and represents a traditional preparation from the Bengal region of the Indian subcontinent.

Pabda sorse posto is a Bengali fish curry made with pabda fish (a freshwater catfish) cooked in a sauce of ground mustard seeds and poppy seeds tempered with spices. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as part of Bengali cuisine.

Sorshe diye bhola machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry where bhola fish (a freshwater Indian carp) is cooked in a spiced mustard-based gravy made with mustard seeds, mustard paste, turmeric, and green chilies. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as a main course component of Bengali cuisine.

Katla machh bhaja is a Bengali fried fish dish made by coating pieces of katla (a freshwater carp) in seasoned batter and deep-frying until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a main course or appetizer, often accompanied by rice, lentils, or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Roast beef is prepared by cooking a large cut of beef in an oven, typically at high heat initially to develop a brown crust, then at lower temperature until the desired internal doneness is reached. It is traditionally served sliced, often with gravy made from the pan drippings, and commonly accompanied by roasted vegetables and potatoes.

Bulz is a Romanian appetizer made from smoked cheese wrapped in cornmeal dough, typically formed into a cylindrical or oval shape, and then deep-fried until golden brown. The dish is traditionally served hot as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by sour cream or garlic sauce for dipping.

Avial is a vegetable curry from Kerala, India, prepared by cooking mixed vegetables such as carrots, beans, and cucumber with coconut paste, turmeric, and green chilies until tender. It is traditionally served as a side dish with rice or flatbreads, typically at lunch or dinner meals.

Corn salad is a cold dish made primarily from cooked or raw maize kernels, often combined with vegetables, herbs, and a dressing such as vinegar or lime juice. It is typically served as a side dish or light meal in a variety of international cuisines, particularly during summer months when fresh corn is in season.

Lassi is a yogurt-based beverage from India made by blending yogurt (dahi) with water, spices, and sometimes fruit or sugar to achieve a smooth, drinkable consistency. It is typically served chilled as a refreshing drink with meals or as a standalone beverage, particularly in South Asian cuisine.

Cendol is a cold dessert made from rice flour jelly that is pressed through a special mold to create thin green noodles, typically flavored with pandan leaf extract, then combined with shaved ice, brown sugar syrup, and coconut milk. It is served chilled as a popular street dessert and refreshment throughout Indonesia, particularly in warm climates.

Bakalca is a slow-cooked dish made primarily from mutton and seasonal vegetables, simmered together until the meat becomes tender and absorbs the flavors of the broth. It is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by bread or rice.

Buckwheat dumplings are made from a dough prepared with buckwheat flour, typically filled with savory ingredients such as potatoes, cheese, or mushrooms, then boiled or pan-fried until cooked through. These dumplings are commonly served as a main course or side dish, often accompanied by sour cream, caramelized onions, or melted butter.

Chwee kueh is a steamed rice cake made from rice flour batter poured into small molds and cooked with steam until set. It is typically served warm topped with preserved radish (chwee) and crispy fried shallots, often accompanied by a thin soy-based sauce, and eaten as a breakfast or light snack in Southeast Asian cuisine.

Fleckerl is a traditional Austrian and Central European pasta dish made with small, square egg noodles that are typically fried or sautéed with onions, often incorporating ingredients such as cabbage, potatoes, or meat. It is commonly served as a main course or side dish, with variations found throughout the region including sweet versions with poppy seeds or savory preparations with sauerkraut.

Peixinhos da horta consists of green beans and other vegetables coated in a light batter and deep-fried until crispy. The dish is traditionally served as an appetizer or side dish in Portuguese cuisine, often accompanied by a simple aioli or tomato-based sauce for dipping.

Bobotie is a South African dish of minced meat cooked with onions, garlic, and spices, then topped with a savory egg custard and baked until golden. It is traditionally served with yellow rice, chutney, and sliced banana on the side.

Mohr im Hemd is an Austrian chocolate dessert consisting of a chocolate sponge cake or pudding base topped with whipped cream, traditionally served warm or at room temperature. The dish's name translates to 'Moor in a shirt,' referring to the dark chocolate cake covered with the white cream topping, and is commonly served as a dessert in Austrian and Central European cuisine.

Torpedo dessert is a Turkish pastry made from a flaky, buttery viennoiserie-style dough that is shaped into an elongated, torpedo-like form and baked until golden. It is typically filled or topped with cream, chocolate, or custard and served as a sweet treat in bakeries and patisseries alongside tea or coffee.

Svele are Norwegian fried pastries made from a batter of flour, eggs, kefir, sugar, and leavening agents (sodium bicarbonate and ammonium hydrogen carbonate), which create a light, airy texture when cooked. Traditionally served as a warm snack or dessert, often dusted with sugar or filled with jam and cream.

Spundekäs is a traditional German cheese spread made by mixing soft cheese (typically quark or cream cheese) with butter, onions, and seasonings until creamy. It is typically served cold as an appetizer or snack with bread, pretzels, or crackers.

Bayrisch Kraut is a Bavarian cabbage dish made by braising shredded or chopped cabbage with bacon, onions, and caraway seeds, often finished with a touch of vinegar and sugar for a sweet-sour balance. It is typically served as a side dish alongside roasted meats such as pork or poultry in traditional Bavarian cuisine.

A galantine is a French charcuterie preparation consisting of boned, stuffed poultry or meat that is rolled, wrapped, and poached or braised until cooked through, then typically chilled and sliced for serving. It is traditionally served cold as a terrine or pâté-style appetizer, often garnished with aspic or its own cooking jelly.

Cheese on toast is prepared by toasting bread and topping it with cheese, typically cheddar, which is then melted under a grill or broiler until golden. It is commonly served as a light meal, snack, or appetizer in British cuisine.

Jawzia Mousse is an Algerian dessert prepared by whipping a mixture featuring ground walnuts, eggs, sugar, and cream into a light, aerated mousse, which is then chilled until set. It is typically served in small glasses or ramekins as a sweet course at the end of a meal or during festive occasions.

Babute is a South African baked dish of spiced ground beef combined with curry powder, dried apricots, and a custard topping made from eggs and milk. It is closely related to bobotie and is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by yellow rice.

Xarró is typically a rustic dish associated with Mediterranean culinary traditions, commonly prepared as a hearty salad or stew-like preparation featuring salted or cured fish such as cod or tuna, combined with ingredients like tomatoes, onions, olives, and olive oil. It is often served as a starter or light main course, accompanied by crusty bread to soak up the flavorful juices.

Seokbakji is a type of Korean kimchi made primarily from whole or halved radishes (mu) that are salted and fermented together with napa cabbage, seasoned with chili pepper, garlic, ginger, and salted seafood. It is traditionally prepared in the autumn during kimjang (kimchi-making season) and served as a banchan (side dish) alongside rice and other Korean dishes.

Enchiladas consist of corn tortillas filled with meat and rolled, then topped with hot sauce and typically cheese before baking. They are served as a main course in Mexican cuisine, often accompanied by rice and beans.

Bocconotto is a small Italian pastry consisting of a short pastry shell filled with a cream or custard base topped with royal icing and candied fruit. It is a traditional Calabrian sweet typically served as a dessert or petit four, often enjoyed during festive occasions and holidays.

Tajine is a North African stew traditionally cooked in a cone-shaped clay vessel of the same name, where ingredients are layered and slow-cooked together with spices such as cinnamon, cumin, and ginger. The dish typically combines meat or vegetables with dried fruits like apricots or prunes, and is served over couscous or bread to absorb the aromatic sauce.

Potjevleesch is a traditional Belgian terrine made from seasoned pork, veal, and rabbit that is cooked slowly, cooled, and set in its own aspic jelly. It is typically served cold, sliced, and eaten as a charcuterie item or appetizer, often with pickles and bread.

Yaksik is a Korean sweet rice dish made by cooking glutinous rice with soy sauce, sesame oil, and sugar, then mixed with jujubes, chestnuts, and pine nuts before being pressed and sliced. It is traditionally served as a dessert or side dish during special occasions and holidays.

Arepas are round, flat cakes made from cornmeal dough that is kneaded, shaped, and cooked on a griddle or fried until golden and crispy on the outside while remaining soft inside. They are typically served as a staple bread or snack in Colombian cuisine, often split and filled with cheese, meat, beans, or other savory ingredients, or eaten plain as an accompaniment to meals.

Ogi is a fermented cereal pudding made from grains such as corn, millet, or sorghum that are soaked, ground, and left to ferment before being cooked into a smooth, pourable paste. It is commonly served as a breakfast dish or light meal in West African cuisine, typically eaten with savory accompaniments or consumed as a standalone porridge.

Chocolate crinkles are soft, fudgy chocolate cookies made from melted chocolate, butter, eggs, and flour, typically rolled in powdered sugar before baking, which creates their characteristic cracked surface. They are served as a dessert or sweet treat, often at room temperature, and are common in international baking traditions.

Neera is a sweet beverage made from the fermented sap of coconut or palm trees, collected from the flower buds and allowed to naturally ferment over time. It is typically served as a refreshing drink, either chilled or at room temperature, and is consumed throughout Indonesia as a traditional beverage.

Saumagen is a traditional German dish consisting of a pork stomach stuffed with a forcemeat mixture made from pork, liver, and spices, then braised or boiled. It is typically served sliced with sauerkraut and potatoes as a main course, particularly in the Palatinate region.

Shkedei marak are almond-shaped dumplings made from flour dough that are boiled in broth or soup. They are traditionally served in chicken soup or other broths as a side dish or component of the main course in Israeli cuisine.

Cream of crab soup is prepared by making a roux-based stock with crab meat, seafood broth, and heavy cream or milk, then seasoned with spices such as Old Bay or cayenne pepper. The soup is typically served hot as a first course or light meal, often garnished with crab roe, fresh herbs, or croutons.

Zucchini muffins are baked quick breads made by folding grated zucchini into a batter of flour, eggs, oil, sugar, and leavening agents such as baking powder or baking soda, which produces a moist crumb as the vegetable releases moisture during baking. They are commonly served as a breakfast item, snack, or light accompaniment and may include optional additions such as cinnamon, nuts, or chocolate chips.

Ashoka Halwa is a Tamil sweet preparation made by cooking soaked and ground green moong dal (Vigna radiata) with generous amounts of ghee and sugar until the mixture thickens into a dense, smooth confection, often flavored with cardamom and garnished with cashews or raisins. It is a traditional dessert associated with festive occasions and temple offerings, particularly in Tamil Nadu, and is served warm as a sweet dish or prasad.

Tomato bhetki is a Bengali preparation in which bhetki (barramundi or Asian sea bass) fillets are cooked in a tangy tomato-based sauce seasoned with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and green chilies. It is typically served as a main course alongside steamed rice.

Koko is a Ghanaian porridge prepared by cooking fermented cereal flour—typically millet, corn, or sorghum—with water until thickened, often seasoned with spices such as ginger, cloves, and pepper. It is commonly served hot for breakfast, accompanied by sugar, milk, or fried bean cakes known as koose or bread.

Bulle Cake is a Swiss pastry from Bulle in the canton of Fribourg, made of a walnut and caramel filling enclosed in dough and coated with chocolate. It is typically sold in local bakeries and served as a sweet treat alongside coffee or tea.

Serbizêr is a Kurdish dish prepared by cooking rice and bulgur together, typically simmered until the grains are tender and the mixture is evenly combined. It is commonly served as a staple side or main component alongside stews, yogurt, or vegetable dishes in traditional Kurdish meals.

Maribruni is a traditional dessert from Cartagena, Spain, typically prepared as a sweet pastry or confection using simple ingredients such as flour, sugar, eggs, and almonds, reflecting the Mediterranean baking traditions of the Murcia region. It is commonly served in small portions as an after-meal treat or alongside coffee, and often appears during local festivities and family gatherings.

Smegiassa is a traditional dessert from the Veneto region of Italy, typically prepared as a dense, rustic cake using humble pantry ingredients such as cornmeal or wheat flour, sugar, dried fruits, and nuts, reflecting the area's peasant baking heritage. It is commonly served in small slices as an afternoon treat or after meals, often accompanied by a glass of sweet wine or coffee.

Amudi machh vaja is a Bengali fried fish dish made by coating amudi fish (mullet) in spices such as turmeric and chili, then shallow or deep frying until crisp. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside rice and dal in traditional Bengali meals.

Adghess is a traditional Berber dish typically prepared from slow-cooked semolina or barley porridge enriched with rendered animal fat, butter, or olive oil, sometimes flavored with honey or local herbs. It is commonly served warm in a communal bowl as a hearty breakfast or celebratory meal, often eaten by hand or with flatbread in rural Amazigh households.

Brem is a traditional Indonesian sweet rice wine made by fermenting glutinous rice with koji mold and yeast, resulting in a low-alcohol beverage with a thick, syrupy consistency. It is typically served as a dessert drink or sweet beverage, often chilled or at room temperature, and is particularly popular in Bali and Java.

Kalakukko is a traditional Finnish fish pie made by wrapping fish (typically pike or perch) and pork belly in a rye bread crust and baking it until golden. The dish is served warm, often sliced, and is a distinctive specialty from the Savonia region of Finland.

Choucroute de la mer is a seafood variation of the classic French choucroute garnie, combining sauerkraut with various fish and seafood instead of traditional pork. The sauerkraut is typically braised with white wine, broth, and aromatics, then layered or served alongside the cooked fish, and is commonly presented as a main course in French coastal regions.

Kemalpaşa dessert is a Turkish sweet made by wrapping cheese (typically white cheese or kashar) in thin pastry sheets, which are then fried until golden and crispy. The fried pastry parcels are served warm, often drizzled with sugar syrup or honey, making them a popular dessert in Turkish cuisine.

Chicken Fries are breaded and deep-fried strips of chicken breast, typically seasoned and served as a side dish or light entrée. They are commonly accompanied by dipping sauces such as ketchup, ranch, or barbecue sauce.

Ampyang is an Indonesian peanut brittle candy made by cooking roasted peanuts with palm sugar (gula jawa) and ginger, then allowing the mixture to harden into flat, irregular pieces. It is a traditional snack commonly associated with Central Java, often sold at markets and served as a sweet confection or souvenir.

Grybukai are Lithuanian mushroom-shaped cookies made from a spiced dough containing flour, honey, sugar, butter, eggs, and seasonings such as cinnamon, nutmeg, and cloves, baked in special molds that form distinct caps and stems. After baking, the caps are glazed with colored icing and the stems are often dipped in chocolate or dusted with poppy seeds, and the cookies are traditionally served during Christmas and other festive occasions.

Kapsalon is a Dutch-Turkish layered dish consisting of fries topped with döner meat, melted cheese, and salad, typically finished with garlic and yogurt sauces. It is traditionally served in a disposable aluminum container and eaten with a fork as a casual street food or late-night meal.

Hawaiian pizza is prepared by topping a pizza base with tomato sauce, cheese, ham, and pineapple, then baking until the crust is crisp and the cheese is melted. It is typically served sliced as a casual meal or appetizer, with the sweet pineapple contrasting against the savory ham and cheese.

Civet de chevreuil is a French stew made from venison (young deer) that is marinated and braised in red wine with pearl onions, mushrooms, and lardons until tender. The sauce is typically thickened with the animal's blood and served with potatoes or egg noodles as a classic game dish.

Tomato salad is prepared by slicing or cutting fresh tomatoes and combining them with simple seasonings such as salt, pepper, and oil, often with the addition of herbs like basil or onions. It is typically served as a fresh side dish or light appetizer, commonly found in Mediterranean and international cuisines.

The Luther Burger consists of a beef patty served between two glazed donuts instead of traditional bread, typically topped with cheese, bacon, and lettuce. This sandwich originated in the Southern United States and is served as a novelty item, combining savory and sweet flavors in a single serving.

Rogan josh is a curry dish prepared by braising meat, traditionally lamb or goat, in a sauce made from tomatoes, yogurt, and aromatic spices including cardamom, cinnamon, and cloves. The dish is typically served with rice or bread as an accompanying starch.

Balti is a curry dish originating from Birmingham, England, prepared by cooking meat or vegetables with onions, peppers, and tomatoes in a spiced sauce, typically served in a small steel balti bowl. The dish is commonly eaten with naan bread or rice and is a popular main course in British Indian restaurants.

Toum is a thick, creamy emulsion made by blending garlic, lemon juice, vegetable oil, and salt into a pale, uniform paste, similar to aioli. It is commonly served as a condiment or dip in Levantine cuisine, particularly alongside grilled meats and sandwiches.

A frikandel is a Dutch fried snack made from a seasoned mixture of minced meat, typically pork, that is formed into an elongated cylindrical shape and deep-fried until golden brown. It is commonly served as a street food or casual meal with condiments such as ketchup, mustard, or mayonnaise.

Klenät are a traditional Swedish fried pastry made from a dough of flour, eggs, butter, and sugar, typically flavored with cardamom and shaped into diamond or twisted forms before deep frying. They are commonly served as a dessert or sweet treat during festive occasions and holidays, often dusted with powdered sugar or served alongside coffee and tea.

Makkaraperunat is a Finnish dish consisting of sliced sausage and fried potatoes that are combined and cooked together, typically in a skillet. This hearty preparation is commonly served as a casual main course, often accompanied by pickled cucumber and served with bread or other side dishes.

Ndolé is a Cameroonian dish made with ndolé leaves (a bitter green) combined with ground peanuts, fish or shrimp, and sometimes meat, cooked together into a thick stew. The dish is traditionally served with boiled plantains or cassava as an accompaniment.

Farsu magru is a Sicilian rolled meat dish made by butterflying a thick slice of beef or pork, filling it with breadcrumbs, cheese, eggs, and sometimes ground meat or vegetables, then rolling and securing it before braising or frying. It is traditionally served as a main course, often with sauce or gravy, and represents a classic preparation from southern Italian cuisine.

Restaurationsbrot is a German whole grain bread made from a mixture of rye and wheat flours, typically with added seeds or grains, and prepared using a long fermentation process. It is commonly served as a staple bread accompaniment to meals in German and Central European cuisine.

Caribé porridge is an Amazonian dish prepared by cooking caribé, a coarse flour made from peach palm fruit (Bactris gasipaes), in water or milk until it reaches a thick, porridge-like consistency. It is typically served warm as a breakfast or snack in regions of the Amazon basin, sometimes sweetened with sugar or accompanied by additional ingredients such as milk.

Okinawa soba is a noodle dish featuring wheat noodles served in a rich broth typically made from pork bones and broth, often topped with sliced pork belly (rafute), fish cake (kamaboko), and green onions. The dish is traditionally served hot in a bowl and is a regional specialty of Okinawa Prefecture, Japan.

Polonaise is a preparation method in which cooked vegetables, particularly cauliflower or asparagus, are topped with a mixture of browned butter, breadcrumbs, and hard-boiled egg yolks or whites. It is commonly served as a side dish, with the topping added just before serving to provide a crisp, golden finish.

Porkkanalaatikko is a Finnish carrot casserole made by layering grated carrots with a creamy custard or béchamel sauce, then baking until set. Traditionally served as a side dish during Finnish holiday meals, particularly Christmas and Easter, often accompanied by lingonberry jam.

Sarde in saor is a Venetian dish of fresh sardines that are pan-fried and then marinated in a sweet and sour sauce made with onions, vinegar, and pine nuts or raisins. The dish is traditionally served at room temperature as an appetizer or part of a cicchetti selection in Venetian cuisine.

Crostini are small slices of bread, typically baguette or ciabatta, that are toasted until crisp and golden. They are served as an appetizer or snack, often topped with various spreads, cheeses, cured meats, or vegetable preparations.

Sujebi is a Korean noodle soup made by hand-tearing sheets of dough and cooking them in a seasoned broth, typically with vegetables such as zucchini, potatoes, and onions, along with seafood or meat. The dish is traditionally served hot in a bowl with the soft, irregularly-shaped noodles and broth, commonly eaten as a casual meal or comfort food.
