Kuftesîr is a traditional Kurdish dish that typically features kufte-style meatballs or dumplings, commonly prepared with seasoned ground meat such as lamb or beef combined with bulgur, rice, or cracked wheat and flavored with onions, garlic, and regional spices. It is often simmered in a broth or sauce and served hot alongside flatbread, yogurt, or rice as part of a family-style meal.

Tamales are made by spreading masa (corn dough) onto corn husks, filling them with ingredients such as meat, cheese, or vegetables, then wrapping and steaming until cooked through. They are typically served warm as a main course or breakfast dish, often accompanied by salsa, sour cream, or broth.

Red velvet cake is a layered cake made with buttermilk, cocoa powder, and red food coloring, typically topped with cream cheese frosting. The cake is traditionally served as a dessert at celebrations and special occasions, often cut into slices.

A club sandwich is typically constructed with three slices of toasted bread layered with sliced chicken or turkey, bacon, lettuce, tomato, and mayonnaise, often cut diagonally and secured with toothpicks. It is served as a lunch or light dinner item, frequently accompanied by french fries or potato chips.

Mohnzelten is an Austrian pastry consisting of a yeast dough base filled with ground poppy seeds mixed with sugar, nuts, and dried fruit, then rolled and baked until golden brown. It is typically served as a sweet bread or dessert, often sliced and eaten plain or with coffee.

Duxelles is a French preparation of finely chopped mushrooms cooked down with shallots and herbs in butter until reduced to a paste-like consistency. It is commonly used as a filling, garnish, or flavor base for other dishes such as beef Wellington, omelets, and sauces.

Loco moco is a Hawaiian dish consisting of a bed of white rice topped with a hamburger patty, a fried egg, and brown gravy. It is typically served as a casual meal or comfort food, often found in diners and plate lunch establishments throughout Hawaii.

Unadon is a Japanese bowl dish consisting of grilled eel (unagi) glazed with a sweet and savory tare sauce, served over steamed white rice. The eel is typically filleted, grilled over charcoal or flame, brushed with the sauce multiple times during cooking, and arranged atop the rice in a donburi bowl as a complete meal.

Gu lou juk is a Cantonese dish made from pork ribs that are marinated and deep-fried until crispy, then braised with a sweet and sour sauce typically containing vinegar, sugar, and tomato. It is commonly served as a main course in Cantonese cuisine, often accompanied by steamed rice.

Kakioko is a savory okonomiyaki-style pancake originating from Onomichi, Hiroshima Prefecture, made with a batter of flour and water mixed with oysters (kaki) and green onions, then cooked on a griddle. It is a regional specialty typically served as a casual meal or street food, reflecting the area's abundance of locally cultivated oysters.

Åland pancake is a baked Finnish-Swedish dish originating from the Åland Islands, made from a batter of semolina or rice porridge combined with eggs, milk, cream, and cardamom, then oven-baked until set. It is traditionally served warm, cut into squares or portions, and accompanied by stewed prunes and whipped cream.

Akindo Sushiro is a Japanese conveyor belt sushi (kaiten-zushi) chain that serves nigiri and maki sushi prepared with vinegared rice topped with fish, seafood, or other ingredients, alongside side items such as onigiri rice balls. Plates circulate on a conveyor belt past diners or are delivered via express lanes ordered from a touchscreen, and dishes are typically eaten with soy sauce, wasabi, and pickled ginger in a casual sit-down setting.

Moriguksu is a Korean noodle soup made by boiling thin wheat noodles in a hot broth typically flavored with anchovy, kelp, or other seafood stock, then serving in a bowl with the hot liquid. The dish is commonly topped with vegetables, egg, meat, and served as a light meal or side dish in Korean cuisine.

aliases: riz au poulet en cocotte d argile
poulet et saucisse en cocotte d argile
description: Claypot chicken rice is prepared by cooking marinated chicken pieces with rice in a traditional clay vessel over direct heat, which creates a caramelized crust on the bottom layer. The dish is typically served in the same claypot at the table and is commonly found in Chinese and Southeast Asian cuisines, often accompanied by soy sauce or other condiments.

Kuba is a Cuban dish consisting of seasoned ground meat, typically beef or pork, mixed with rice and other ingredients such as olives, capers, and sofrito, then baked or cooked together. It is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by black beans and plantains.

Num banhchok is a Cambodian noodle soup made by layering rice vermicelli in a bowl and topping it with a fish-based broth made from prahok (fermented fish paste) and kroeung (curry paste). The dish is traditionally served with freshwater fish and various garnishes, typically eaten as a breakfast or light meal.

Punjena paprika is a dish of bell peppers stuffed with a mixture of ground meat, rice, and seasonings, then baked or simmered until the peppers are tender. It is a traditional Balkan dish commonly served as a main course, often accompanied by sour cream or tomato-based sauce.

Nalisniki are thin Russian pancakes (blini) filled with a savory or sweet filling, typically cheese curds mixed with egg and sometimes potato, then rolled and pan-fried until golden. They are traditionally served hot with sour cream as a side dish or light main course.

Christmas carp is a whole carp that is typically cleaned, scaled, and prepared through baking, poaching, or frying, often stuffed with herbs, breadcrumbs, or vegetables. The dish is traditionally served during Christmas celebrations in Central and Eastern European cuisines, particularly in Czech, Polish, and German culinary traditions.

Hormone yaki udon is a Japanese noodle dish combining grilled offal (hormone) with thick udon noodles, typically cooked together on a flat griddle with vegetables, soy-based sauce, and aromatics. The dish is traditionally served as a casual meal at yaki udon restaurants and street food stalls in Japan.

Sorshe ilish is a Bengali fish preparation featuring hilsa (Tenualosa ilisha) cooked with a pungent mustard-based sauce made from mustard oil and mustard paste, seasoned with salt. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice and represents a traditional preparation method common in Bangladeshi cuisine.

Laoyou rice noodles are prepared by coating fresh or dried rice noodles with aged soy sauce (laoyou), typically combined with aromatics such as garlic, ginger, and chili oil, then tossed together until the noodles are evenly coated. The dish is served at room temperature or slightly warm as a quick noodle course, common in Cantonese cuisine and often enjoyed as a casual meal or snack.

Ardei umpluţi are bell peppers hollowed and filled with a mixture of ground meat, rice, and spices, then baked until tender. This Romanian dish is typically served hot as a main course, often accompanied by sour cream or tomato sauce.

Rollò is a swiss roll-style dessert from Caltanissetta, Sicily, prepared by spreading a filling over a thin sheet of sponge cake and rolling it into a cylinder before slicing. It is typically served as a dessert or sweet snack, often accompanied by coffee.

Kanthal bij er vorta is a Bangladeshi dish made by mashing or crushing jackfruit seeds, which are boiled or roasted until tender and then combined with mustard oil, green chilies, onion, and salt. It is typically served as a side dish alongside rice and other accompaniments in a traditional Bengali meal.

Ika shūmai are steamed dumplings made with a filling of minced squid (ika) enclosed in a thin wheat-based wrapper, prepared using the same technique as traditional Chinese shumai. They are typically served in bamboo steamers as part of a dim sum-style meal or as an appetizer in Japanese and Japanese-influenced restaurants.

Patriotic soup (also known as 'zupa patriotyczna' in Polish tradition) is a clear beet broth-based soup prepared by simmering beetroots with vegetables and sometimes meat stock, resulting in a deep red color associated with national symbolism. It is typically served as a first course during festive or ceremonial occasions, often accompanied by small dumplings or croutons.

Itoko-ni is a Japanese simmered dish in which ingredients such as kabocha squash and adzuki beans are cooked together in a seasoned broth of dashi, soy sauce, and sugar until tender. The name references the Japanese word for 'cousin,' alluding to the pairing of related or complementary ingredients, and it is typically served as a side dish in home-style or traditional Japanese meals.

Grytbit is a Swedish preparation of meat cut into small cubes or pieces intended for braising or stewing in a pot, often browned first and then simmered with liquid and seasonings. It is typically served as the base for dishes such as beef stew or kalops, accompanied by potatoes, root vegetables, or rice.

Ilish machh vaja is a Bengali preparation of hilsa fish (Tenualosa ilisha) that is shallow-fried or pan-fried in mustard oil after being lightly seasoned with turmeric and salt. It is typically served as a standalone fish course alongside steamed rice, and the crisp-fried exterior contrasts with the rich, oily flesh characteristic of the hilsa.

Beguner vorta is a Bengali dish made by mashing roasted or fried eggplant with onions, green chilies, and spices until it reaches a coarse paste consistency. It is typically served as a side dish or starter with rice, bread, or as part of a meal in Bengali cuisine.

Ilish fish is steamed inside a hollowed pumpkin with mustard paste, turmeric, and green chili, creating a sealed cooking vessel that infuses the fish with subtle vegetable flavors. This Bengali preparation is traditionally served as a main course during special occasions and festivals, often accompanied by rice.

Blueberry pancakes are made from a basic pancake batter mixed with fresh or frozen blueberries, then cooked on a griddle until golden brown on both sides. They are typically served warm for breakfast or brunch, often accompanied by maple syrup, butter, and whipped cream.

Chow mein is prepared by stir-frying noodles with vegetables such as onion and celery, typically combined with chicken and a savory sauce. This dish is commonly served as a main course in Chinese-American cuisine and international restaurants.

Noisette refers to a small, round piece of meat, typically lamb or beef, that is boned, rolled, and tied before cooking. It is commonly roasted or pan-seared and served as an individual portion, often accompanied by vegetables and sauce in French cuisine.

Cơm bình dân is a Vietnamese working-class rice dish typically composed of steamed white rice served with simple, affordable protein and vegetable accompaniments such as grilled meat, fried eggs, or stir-fried vegetables. The dish is commonly eaten as an everyday lunch or dinner meal and represents practical, budget-friendly home cooking in Vietnam.

Bingka is an Indonesian baked dish traditionally made by layering coconut milk-based custard with grated coconut, eggs, and sugar in a clay or metal pan, then baked until the top becomes golden and slightly crispy. It is typically served as a dessert or sweet snack, often cut into squares, and is particularly popular in regions of Indonesia such as Palembang and Banjarmasin.

Shoyumame are soybeans boiled and seasoned with soy sauce, salt, and sometimes kombu seaweed, resulting in a savory legume dish. Typically served as a cold appetizer or snack, often enjoyed with beer or sake in Japanese cuisine.

Fukagawa-meshi is a Japanese rice bowl dish topped with a miso-based broth containing clams (asari) and vegetables such as green onions and sometimes ginger. The dish originates from the Fukagawa district of Tokyo and is traditionally served as a hearty, one-bowl meal.

Niku sui is a Japanese soup dish consisting of thinly sliced beef simmered in a light, clear dashi-based broth seasoned with soy sauce, mirin, and sake, without the addition of tofu or other typical sukiyaki accompaniments. It is commonly served in the Osaka and Kansai region as a warm dish, often accompanied by a bowl of rice, and is found in restaurants specializing in beef cuisine such as sukiyaki and shabu-shabu establishments.

Seruit is a traditional Lampungese dish from Sumatra, Indonesia, consisting of grilled or fried fish—commonly patin, baung, or snakehead—mixed with sambal terasi (shrimp paste chili sauce), tempoyak (fermented durian paste), and sliced vegetables such as cucumber and eggplant. It is typically served with steamed rice and additional raw or cooked vegetables, and is considered a customary dish often prepared for communal gatherings in Lampung culture.

Kwikwi is a Surinamese dish made from armored catfish, which is typically cleaned, seasoned, and stewed in a spiced tomato-based sauce with onions, garlic, and peppers. It is commonly served with rice or cassava as part of a traditional Surinamese meal.

Tajine Lham-Lahlou is a slow-cooked Moroccan and North African stew combining lamb with dried fruits such as prunes or apricots, honey, and warm spices including cinnamon and ginger, resulting in a sweet-savory preparation. It is traditionally served during festive occasions and family gatherings, typically accompanied by bread or couscous.

Miso kanpura is typically a fusion dish combining Japanese miso paste with tempura-style battered and fried ingredients, often featuring vegetables or seafood coated in a light, crisp batter and glazed or accompanied by a savory-sweet miso sauce. It is commonly served as an appetizer or small plate, often garnished with sesame seeds or scallions and paired with steamed rice or a dipping broth.

Dam curry is a slow-cooked curry preparation in which meat or vegetables are sealed in a pot and cooked over low heat using steam trapped inside, a technique known as 'dum' cooking originating from South Asian cuisine. The dish typically uses spiced meat such as mutton or chicken along with aromatics including onions, garlic, ginger, and whole spices, and is commonly served with rice or flatbread.

Gabacai is a traditional snack from Tianjin, China, made by cutting thin mung bean flour pancakes into small diamond-shaped pieces and simmering them in a seasoned broth. It is typically served hot for breakfast, garnished with sesame paste, fermented tofu, chili oil, pickled vegetables, and coriander.

Boiled blood curd is prepared by cooking animal blood (typically pork) that has been mixed with other ingredients and set into a firm cake, then sliced and boiled. It is commonly served in soups or as a cold appetizer in Chinese cuisine, particularly in regions of southern China.

Sesame oil chicken is prepared by cooking chicken pieces and tossing them with sesame oil, soy sauce, and aromatics such as garlic and ginger, often finished with sesame seeds. The dish is typically served hot as a main course, either over rice or as part of a multi-course meal.

Huitlacoche is a Mexican delicacy made from corn smut, a fungus that grows on corn kernels, which is harvested and typically cooked with onions, garlic, and chiles. It is commonly served as a filling for quesadillas, tacos, and tamales, or used in soups and other traditional Mexican dishes.

Äggost is a Swedish cheese made by heating a mixture of milk curds and whey, then adding eggs and cream to create a custard-like texture before setting and slicing. It is traditionally served as a dessert or sweet course, often accompanied by fruit preserves or a light sauce.

Khô cá lóc is a Vietnamese dried snakehead fish preparation made by salting and sun-drying fresh snakehead fish until it reaches a firm, preserved state. The dish is typically served as a side dish or snack with rice and vegetables, and is valued for its concentrated savory flavor and long shelf life.

Jalangkote is a deep-fried pastry from South Sulawesi, Indonesia, made with a thin dough wrapper filled with a spiced mixture of grated coconut, eggs, and seasonings. The pastry is typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanying coffee or tea.

Fenkata is a traditional Maltese stew made by braising rabbit meat with onions, garlic, and oregano in olive oil and wine until tender. The dish is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by bread to absorb the aromatic sauce.

Bột chiên is a Vietnamese fried flour cake made by deep-frying a batter of rice flour, tapioca starch, and water until golden and crispy, then typically cut into pieces. It is commonly served as a street food or snack, often accompanied by dipping sauces such as fish sauce or sweet chili sauce.

Nyama choma is meat that is marinated and grilled over an open flame or charcoal, traditionally prepared with mutton, beef, or goat. The dish is typically served with accompaniments such as ugali, sukuma wiki, and lime, and is common in East African cuisine, particularly in Kenya and Tanzania.

Boeber is a sweet, warm South African Cape Malay beverage made by simmering vermicelli and sago in milk with butter, sugar, cardamom, cinnamon, and rose water, often enriched with beaten eggs. It is traditionally prepared and consumed on the 15th night of Ramadan as a celebratory drink within Cape Malay communities.

Kololak is an Armenian meatball dish made from ground lamb or beef mixed with rice, onions, and herbs such as parsley, formed into balls and simmered in a broth or tomato-based sauce. It is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by the cooking broth as a light soup or alongside vegetables.

Supersize typically refers to an extra-large portion option rather than a specific dish, commonly applied to international fast-food staples such as burgers, fries, or soft drinks. It is often served as an upsized version of a standard combo meal, featuring larger quantities of the same ingredients and accompaniments.

Legendar is an Indonesian sauce prepared by drying cooked rice and then frying it before processing it into a condiment. It is typically served as an accompaniment to rice-based meals and traditional Indonesian dishes.

Efo riro is a Nigerian leafy vegetable soup made by sautéing spinach or other leafy greens with tomatoes, peppers, and palm oil, then simmering with meat or fish stock and seasonings. It is typically served with fufu, pounded yam, or rice as an accompaniment.

Afghan salad is prepared by combining chopped fresh vegetables such as tomatoes, cucumbers, and onions with herbs and a vinegar-based dressing. It is typically served as a side dish or accompaniment to Afghan main courses, providing a fresh and acidic contrast to rich meat dishes.

Sooji toast is made by coating semolina (sooji) on bread slices, sometimes mixed with spices and herbs, which are then fried until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a breakfast item or snack, often accompanied by chutneys or used as a base for toppings.

Mandryky are Ukrainian pastries made from dough filled with a mixture of cheese, egg, and smetana (sour cream), then baked until golden. These savory or slightly sweet pastries are typically served as a snack or appetizer in Ukrainian cuisine.

Tuo Zaafi is a West African porridge made by cooking maize and cassava flour with water until thick and smooth. The dish is typically served as a staple breakfast or lunch item, often accompanied by soups or stews.

Spillonga is an Italian purée made from grapes, typically prepared by cooking and mashing the fruit into a thick, smooth consistency. It is traditionally served as a sweet accompaniment or dessert component within regional Italian cuisine.

Tajine el bey is a North African dish featuring minced meat cooked with spinach, eggs, onions, and rigouta cheese, seasoned with spices and traditionally prepared in a conical clay vessel. The ingredients are layered and slow-cooked together, resulting in a savory combination typically served as a main course.

Relleno Negro is a traditional Mexican dish from the Yucatán Peninsula made with chili peppers stuffed with seasoned pork or turkey meat and cooked in a rich, dark mole sauce. The dish is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by rice or tortillas to absorb the flavorful sauce.

Medfouna is a Moroccan filled pancake made from thin layers of dough that are stacked with a savory filling of spiced meat (typically ground beef or lamb), onions, and herbs, then cooked on a griddle until golden. It is traditionally served as a breakfast or brunch dish, often accompanied by butter and honey or eaten plain as street food.

Purslane stew is a traditional Turkish dish made by simmering fresh purslane leaves with tomatoes, onions, and garlic, typically seasoned with salt and pepper. The stew is served hot as a main course or side dish, often accompanied by bread for soaking up the broth.

Lomo saltado is a Peruvian stir-fry dish made by quickly cooking strips of beef over high heat with onions, tomatoes, and aji amarillo peppers, then seasoning with soy sauce and vinegar. The dish is traditionally served with rice and French fried potatoes on the side.

Ham and eggs is prepared by frying or cooking ham slices alongside eggs that are typically fried, scrambled, or poached. The dish is commonly served for breakfast or brunch, often accompanied by bread, toast, or other breakfast sides.

Kadu bouranee is an Afghan pumpkin dish where pumpkin is roasted or fried until tender, then topped with a garlicky yogurt sauce and a spiced meat or lentil mixture. It is traditionally served as a main course or side dish, often accompanied by rice or flatbread.

Parêv is a Kurdish rice dish prepared by cooking rice until tender, often seasoned and served as a staple accompaniment. It is typically presented alongside stews, meat dishes, or vegetables as part of a traditional Kurdish meal.

Azuki Bar is a frozen confection made from sweetened red bean (azuki) paste and water, shaped into a rectangular bar around a wooden stick. It is a widely consumed Japanese popsicle, typically eaten as a cold snack and known for its firm texture compared to other ice bars.

Nem Lai Vung is a fermented pork roll originating from Lai Vung district in Đồng Tháp Province, Vietnam, made from ground pork, pork skin, garlic, and chili wrapped tightly in banana leaves and left to ferment for several days. It is typically served as a snack or appetizer, eaten with raw vegetables, fresh herbs, and accompanying dipping sauces.

Wôkoli is a traditional Beninese preparation based on wɔ̌, a dough-like staple typically made from cooked and pounded maize or cassava flour that is shaped into soft, smooth mounds. It is commonly served alongside flavorful sauces or stews featuring vegetables, fish, or meat, and is often eaten by hand as part of a shared meal.

Menemen is a Turkish dish made by scrambling eggs with diced tomatoes, peppers, and onions, typically cooked together in olive oil or butter until the eggs are set. It is commonly served for breakfast or brunch, often accompanied by bread for dipping or scooping.

Spare ribs are cut from the rib section of pork and typically prepared by removing the membrane, seasoning with dry rubs or marinades, and cooking slowly through smoking, braising, or roasting until the meat is tender and pulls away from the bone. They are commonly served as a main course or appetizer, often accompanied by barbecue sauce for dipping.

Sajoer is an Indonesian vegetable soup or stew made by simmering mixed vegetables with onions and aromatics in a light broth, often seasoned with garlic, ginger, and spices. It is typically served as an accompaniment to rice and other main dishes in Indonesian meals.

Malakoff is a Swiss appetizer consisting of fried cheese cubes, typically made from Gruyère or similar hard cheese, breaded and deep-fried until golden. It is traditionally served hot as a starter, often accompanied by a fruit preserve such as redcurrant jelly or apricot jam.

Syrniki are Russian pancakes made from tvorog (curd cheese), eggs, and flour, pan-fried until golden brown on both sides. They are typically served warm with sour cream, jam, or sweetened condensed milk as accompaniments.

Blåbärssoppa is a Swedish berry soup made by simmering blueberries with sugar, water, and often a thickening agent such as cornstarch or potato starch, sometimes with the addition of spices like cinnamon or cloves. The soup is typically served chilled as a dessert or light course, often accompanied by whipped cream or vanilla ice cream.

aliases: musa acuminata aaa group mutika lujugira subgroup
description: Matoke is a dish of steamed or boiled green bananas that are peeled, mashed, and cooked with ingredients such as onions, tomatoes, and sometimes meat or fish in a savory sauce. It is a staple carbohydrate dish in East African cuisine, particularly in Uganda, Kenya, and Tanzania, typically served as a main course or side dish.

Piperade is a Basque dish made by sautéing peppers, tomatoes, and onions together, typically finished with eggs that are scrambled or lightly set into the mixture. It is traditionally served as a main course or side dish, often accompanied by cured ham or other Basque proteins.

Ribollita is a thick Tuscan vegetable soup made by simmering beans, tomatoes, celery, carrots, and leafy greens like kale or chard, then thickened with day-old bread that is either stirred into the soup or layered at the bottom of the bowl. The dish is traditionally served at room temperature or slightly warm, often drizzled with olive oil and served as a hearty first course or main course.

Red curry is prepared by simmering meat or vegetables in a paste made from red chili peppers, garlic, galangal, lemongrass, and shrimp paste, combined with coconut milk to create a creamy sauce. It is typically served over jasmine rice and garnished with fresh basil and lime.

Fried bread is prepared by deep-frying slices or pieces of bread in oil until golden and crispy on the outside. It is typically served as a side dish, appetizer, or snack, often accompanied by dips, spreads, or eaten plain.

Pissaladière is a Provençal tart consisting of a bread or pastry base topped with caramelized onions, anchovies, and black olives. It is typically served as an appetizer or light main course, either warm or at room temperature, and originates from the Nice region of southeastern France.

Komanmelna is a traditional Mari pancake made from a batter of flour, eggs, and milk, cooked in layers on a heated pan to produce a thick, multi-layered texture. It is typically served warm as part of everyday or festive meals in Mari cuisine, often accompanied by butter, sour cream, or honey.

Timus is a traditional Indonesian cake made from sweet potatoes that are mashed and mixed with sugar, flour, and sometimes grated coconut before being steamed, fried, or baked. It is commonly served as a snack or dessert, often accompanying tea or coffee.

Mollete melenero is a typical street food from Cuba, prepared by frying or baking a dough made from flour, sugar, and fat until it forms a dense, sweet bread-like pastry. It is commonly sold by street vendors and consumed as a snack or accompaniment to coffee.

Supakatsu is a Japanese dish consisting of a breaded and deep-fried pork cutlet (tonkatsu) served over spaghetti, typically with a tomato-based or demi-glace sauce. It is a yoshoku-style dish, representing the Japanese adaptation of Western cuisine, commonly found in family restaurants and specialty katsu establishments in Japan.

Kissuto rombo is a traditional Angolan goat meat dish, typically prepared by slow-cooking the meat with onions, garlic, tomatoes, and regional spices until tender. It is commonly served with staple accompaniments such as funge (a cassava or cornmeal porridge) or rice, often alongside sautéed greens.

Opok-opok is a traditional Malaysian dish in which fish, commonly mackerel or tenggiri, is mixed with spiced coconut milk, chillies, lemongrass, and other aromatics, then wrapped in banana leaves and grilled over charcoal or open flame. It is typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice, and is common in coastal communities of Peninsular Malaysia.

Okra soup is prepared by simmering fresh or dried okra with a liquid base such as water, broth, or palm nut liquid, typically combined with proteins such as meat, fish, or shellfish, along with seasonings like onions, peppers, and locust beans depending on the regional variation. It is commonly served as a main dish alongside starchy accompaniments such as fufu, pounded yam, or eba in West African culinary traditions.

Botok yuyu is a Javanese dish made by wrapping freshwater crab (yuyu) mixed with grated coconut, spices such as shallots, garlic, galangal, and chilies in banana leaves, which are then steamed until cooked. It is traditionally served as a side dish in Indonesian cuisine, often accompanied by rice.

Bungong Kayee is an Acehnese dish from the province of Aceh in Indonesia, consisting of assorted mixed vegetables cooked with a spiced coconut milk broth. It is typically served as a side dish alongside rice and other Acehnese dishes, featuring local vegetables simmered with aromatics such as turmeric, galangal, and lemongrass.

Asado de puerco is a Mexican braised pork dish where pork meat is slow-cooked with dried chiles, spices, and broth until tender, creating a rich sauce. The dish is typically served with flour tortillas, rice, and beans as a main course.

Bánh chay is a Vietnamese vegetarian cake made from glutinous rice and mung bean, typically prepared by steaming a batter of these ingredients until set. It is commonly served as a snack or dessert, often cut into squares and eaten plain or with a light syrup.

Daging masak hitam is an Indonesian dish of meat braised in a dark sauce made from roasted spices, garlic, shallots, and soy sauce, which gives the dish its characteristic dark brown color. The meat is typically beef or sometimes chicken, slow-cooked until tender, and served with rice as a main course.

Soto Medan is a traditional Indonesian soup from North Sumatra made with beef or chicken broth combined with turmeric, garlic, ginger, and chilies, typically thickened with ground nuts or coconut milk and served with rice, hard-boiled eggs, and vegetables. The dish is traditionally eaten for breakfast or lunch and is often garnished with fried shallots, fresh herbs, and lime wedges.

A venison burger consists of ground venison meat formed into a patty and cooked by grilling, pan-frying, or broiling, typically served on a bread roll with condiments and toppings. Venison, being leaner than beef, is often prepared with added fat or binders to maintain moisture and is commonly accompanied by classic burger accompaniments such as lettuce, tomato, onion, and cheese.

Escargots à la bourguignonne are snails cooked in their shells with a mixture of butter, garlic, parsley, and shallots, then typically finished with a red wine sauce inspired by Burgundy cooking traditions. The dish is served hot as an appetizer, with the snails remaining in their shells and eaten with a small fork.

Vegan kebab consists of plant-based ingredients such as vegetables, legumes, or meat substitutes threaded onto skewers and grilled or roasted over direct heat. The cooked skewers are typically served with flatbread, fresh vegetables, and plant-based sauces as a complete meal.

Badak kubang is a traditional Malaysian kueh, typically prepared as a sweet snack using glutinous rice flour, coconut milk, and palm sugar or sweet fillings, often steamed or shaped by hand. It is commonly served as a teatime treat or festive dessert, frequently accompanied by grated coconut or a drizzle of coconut-based sauce.

Palua ladu is a traditional sweet from Bhadrak, Odisha, prepared by roasting rice flour and combining it with jaggery or sugar syrup, then shaping the mixture into small round balls. It is commonly served as a dessert or festive treat, particularly during religious occasions and family celebrations.

Totché is a traditional savory tart from the Canton of Jura in Switzerland, prepared by topping a bread or pastry dough base with a mixture of cream, eggs, and seasoning before baking until golden. It is typically served warm as a starter or light main course, often accompanied by a green salad.

Bolle are Nordic sweet buns made from a yeasted wheat dough enriched with milk, butter, sugar, and often cardamom, shaped into small rounds and baked until golden. They are commonly served at breakfast, with coffee, or as an afternoon snack, sometimes containing raisins or chocolate chips.

Puteri ayu is a Malaysian steamed mini cake made from a pandan-flavored coconut milk and rice flour batter, which gives it a green color and soft, moist texture. It is typically served in small fluted molds topped or filled with freshly grated coconut mixed with palm sugar, and is commonly found as a traditional kuih at breakfast or as a snack.

Agepan is a Japanese fried bread roll, typically made by deep-frying a soft bread bun until golden and crispy on the outside, then coating it in sugar and kinako (roasted soybean flour). It originated as a school lunch item in Japan and is commonly served as a snack or light meal, often accompanied by milk.

Cream Box is a regional bread-based confection originating in Fukushima City, Japan, consisting of a thick slice of soft white bread topped with a generous layer of sweetened whipped cream or butter cream. It is commonly sold at local bakeries and consumed as a snack or light dessert, often served at room temperature or chilled.

Jhinuk pitho is a Bengali steamed rice-flour dumpling shaped to resemble small clams or cockles (jhinuk), made by pinching a dough of rice flour and hot water into curved shell-like forms before steaming. It is typically served as a breakfast or snack item, often accompanied by jaggery, grated coconut, or a light curry.

A galette-saucisse consists of a savory buckwheat pancake (galette) wrapped around a grilled sausage, typically a French pork sausage. This Breton specialty is commonly served as a casual street food or quick meal, often accompanied by mustard or other condiments.

Wachauer Laberl is an Austrian bread roll from the Wachau region, traditionally made with a simple yeast dough and baked until golden brown. The rolls are typically served as a breakfast or light meal accompaniment, often split and filled with butter, cheese, or cured meats.

The Bombay sandwich is prepared by layering buttered white bread with sliced beets, tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and capsicum, topped with grated cheese and sev (crispy chickpea noodles), then finished with chaat masala seasoning. This street food sandwich is typically served as a quick lunch or snack, often cut diagonally and eaten immediately while the sev remains crispy.

aliases: sandwich au beurre d arachide et aux pickles
description: A sandwich made by spreading peanut butter on sliced bread and adding pickled cucumber slices. Typically served cold as a light meal or snack, combining savory and tangy flavors.

An alligator po' boy is a sandwich served on French bread that is typically filled with fried alligator meat, lettuce, tomato, pickles, and mayonnaise. This dish is traditionally served as a casual meal, particularly in Louisiana cuisine, where alligator is a regional protein.

Terang bulan is a Indonesian street food dessert consisting of a thin, crispy crepe-like pancake filled with a sweet mixture of peanuts, chocolate, and condensed milk. The crepe is cooked on a flat griddle until golden, then folded or rolled and typically served warm as a casual snack or dessert.

Ensaymada is a sweet spiral pastry made from soft dough rolled with butter, sugar, and cheese, then baked until golden brown. It is typically served as a breakfast pastry or dessert, often with a cup of coffee or hot chocolate.

The Wonder Burger is a vegan burger patty product sold by the Aldi supermarket chain, made from plant-based ingredients as a meat substitute. The patty is typically pan-fried or grilled and served in a bun with accompaniments such as lettuce, tomato, onion, and sauces, in the style of a conventional hamburger.

Goralı is a Turkish sandwich typically made with a toasted bun filled with seasoned minced meat, melted cheese, pickles, tomatoes, and special sauces. It is served as fast food, most commonly associated with the Goralı restaurant chain that originated in Turkey.

Toros salad is a Turkish eggplant-based salad made from roasted or grilled eggplants that are peeled and mashed or chopped, then combined with ingredients such as garlic, yogurt or tahini, lemon juice, and olive oil. It is typically served cold as a meze alongside grilled meats and bread.

Tapak kuda is a traditional cake from Brunei, typically prepared as a soft, sweet baked or steamed confection whose name, meaning 'horseshoe,' refers to its characteristic shape. It commonly includes ingredients such as flour, eggs, sugar, and butter, and is often served as a snack or accompaniment to tea.

Cicerata is a traditional Italian sweet made by frying small balls of dough prepared from flour, eggs, and lard, then coating them in honey and sometimes sprinkled with colored sugar sprinkles or cinnamon. It is a festive confection particularly associated with Carnival and Christmas celebrations in southern Italy, typically served as a dessert or sweet snack.

A lunch pack is a pre-portioned meal assembled from items such as sandwiches, bread rolls, or rice paired with sides like boiled eggs, fruit, juice, or snacks, and packaged together for convenience. It is commonly prepared in advance and served as a ready-to-eat midday meal in school, workplace, or travel contexts across many cultures.

Bolla de chicharrones is a Spanish dish consisting of seasoned pork meat and offal that is cooked and formed into a ball or loaf, typically bound together with fat and spices. It is sliced and served cold or at room temperature as part of charcuterie boards or as a traditional appetizer in Spanish cuisine.

Schäufele is a roasted pork shoulder dish from Franconia, Germany, prepared by slow-roasting a bone-in cut until the skin becomes crispy and the meat tender. It is traditionally served with bread, sauerkraut, and gravy, commonly appearing in regional German cuisine and beer halls.

Christmas cake is a rich, dense fruitcake made with dried fruits (such as currants, raisins, and sultanas), spices (typically cinnamon, nutmeg, and cloves), butter, sugar, eggs, and flour, often soaked in alcohol like brandy or rum. The cake is traditionally baked slowly, aged for several weeks to develop flavor, and served sliced in small portions during the Christmas holiday season, frequently topped with marzipan and royal icing.

A sno-ball is a shaved ice dessert coated with flavored syrup, typically served in a paper cone or cup. The preparation involves freezing water into a block, shaving it into fine ice, and coating it with sweet colored syrup in flavors such as cherry, grape, or tropical fruit.

Dace fish balls are made by mincing fresh dace fish, mixing it with seasonings and binders such as tapioca starch or potato starch, and forming the mixture into spherical shapes before boiling or pan-frying until cooked through. These balls are typically served in soups, broths, or as a dim sum appetizer, often accompanied by dipping sauces.

Scouse is a British stew made by slow-cooking diced beef or lamb with potatoes, onions, and carrots in a savory broth until tender. This traditional working-class dish is typically served hot in bowls and is particularly associated with Liverpool and the surrounding Merseyside region.

Chocobanano is prepared by blending or processing frozen banana with chocolate, typically resulting in a creamy, frozen dessert with a consistency similar to ice cream. It is commonly served as a cold dessert or snack, either on its own or as a component of other dishes.

Kakigōri is a frozen dessert made by shaving blocks of ice and coating them with flavored syrups, typically fruit-based or melon flavors. It is commonly served as a refreshing summer treat at Japanese festivals and casual food stalls, often consumed quickly before the ice melts.

Panino con porchetta is an Italian sandwich made with thinly sliced porchetta, a roasted whole pig or pork belly seasoned with herbs and spices, typically served on crusty bread. It is traditionally eaten as a casual meal or street food, often accompanied by rosemary and served warm or at room temperature.

A shrimp po' boy consists of fried shrimp served on a long French bread roll, typically dressed with lettuce, tomato, pickles, and a remoulade or tartar sauce-based condiment. The sandwich is a traditional Louisiana dish, particularly associated with New Orleans, and is commonly served as a lunch or casual dinner item.

Shirunashi Tantan-men is a broth-less (dry) version of tantanmen served by the Japanese chain Sugakiya, consisting of noodles coated in a spicy sesame-based sauce with ground meat, chili oil, and Sichuan-influenced seasonings. It is typically served as a bowl of dressed noodles meant to be mixed together before eating, distinguishing it from the soup-based original.

Shavla is a Central Asian rice porridge made by cooking rice with meat (typically lamb or beef), carrots, onions, and oil or fat, resulting in a softer, more porridge-like consistency than the related dish plov. It is commonly prepared in large quantities for communal gatherings and served as a main course in Uzbek and broader Central Asian culinary traditions.

Treasure Chest is a Heartbrand ice cream product consisting of a chocolate-coated shell filled with vanilla ice cream and a fruit or caramel sauce center. It is sold as an individually wrapped frozen novelty, typically served directly from the wrapper as a single-portion dessert.

Prima is typically a frozen dessert made from a sweetened dairy base such as milk, cream, and sugar, often churned to achieve a smooth, creamy texture. It is commonly served in scoops, cones, or cups, and may be offered in a variety of flavors ranging from classic vanilla and chocolate to fruit-based options.

Carmen salad is a chicken salad typically prepared with strips of cooked chicken breast, bell peppers, lettuce, and a seasoned dressing, tossed together and served chilled. It is commonly offered as a light main course or starter in international menu settings.

A Dutch sandwich made with sliced whole wheat or white bread filled with fresh vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, cucumber, and sprouts, typically topped with cheese and a spread like mayonnaise or hummus. The sandwich is served cold and commonly eaten as a quick lunch or light meal in the Netherlands.

Yosenabe is a Japanese hot pot dish in which various ingredients such as seafood, chicken, vegetables, and tofu are simmered together in a broth flavored with soy sauce, sake, and dashi stock. The dish is traditionally served in a communal pot at the table, with diners cooking and eating the ingredients throughout the meal.

A guajolota is a Mexican street food consisting of a freshly fried churro or piece of fried dough dipped in or served alongside hot chocolate or atole, a traditional corn-based beverage. It is typically consumed as a breakfast or early morning snack, often sold by street vendors in Mexico.

Scotch broth is a thick soup made by simmering beef or lamb with pearl barley, split peas, and root vegetables such as carrots, turnips, onions, and celery. The dish is traditionally served as a hearty main course, particularly during colder months in Scotland.

Rogel is an Argentine dessert made of thin layers of puff pastry alternated with dulce de leche and topped with Italian meringue, then baked until golden. The dish is typically served chilled or at room temperature as a sweet treat, often presented in rectangular portions.

Taktouka is a Moroccan salad made by charring tomatoes and bell peppers over an open flame, then peeling and mashing them together with garlic, olive oil, and spices. The dish is typically served cold as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by bread for scooping.

Beyran is a traditional Turkish soup made by simmering lamb or beef meat with chickpeas, onions, and spices until tender, then blended or left chunky depending on regional variation. The soup is typically served hot in bowls, often garnished with sumac and fresh herbs, as a hearty breakfast or light meal dish.

Milanesa is a thin, breaded cutlet made by pounding meat (typically beef, chicken, or pork) until very thin, coating it with breadcrumbs, and frying until golden. It is traditionally served with lemon wedges and accompanied by sides such as rice, potatoes, or salad.

Ayam goreng Jepara is a deep-fried chicken dish from the Jepara region in Central Java, Indonesia, prepared by marinating chicken pieces in spices and then frying them until golden and crispy. The dish is typically served with rice and sambal, and is characterized by its seasoned, crunchy exterior and tender meat inside.

Adobong Goto is a Filipino stew made with goat meat (goto) braised in a sauce of vinegar, soy sauce, garlic, and spices until tender. The dish is typically served with rice and is common in Filipino cuisine as a main course.

Croûte au fromage is a toasted bread slice topped with melted cheese, typically served as an appetizer or light main course in French cuisine. The dish consists of bread (often baguette) layered with cheese such as Gruyère or Emmental, then baked or broiled until the cheese is golden and bubbling.

Hotsi is a Finnish dish typically prepared as a hearty stew or baked casserole, commonly featuring seasoned meat and onions slow-cooked until tender. It is often served warm as a main course, accompanied by potatoes, root vegetables, or rye bread in keeping with traditional Finnish home cooking.

Bêdirav is a Kurdish bean salad prepared by combining cooked beans with chopped onions, tomatoes, parsley, and seasonings, typically dressed with olive oil and lemon juice or vinegar. It is commonly served chilled or at room temperature as a side dish or mezze alongside bread and other small plates.

Getuk kicak is a Javanese steamed cake made from grated cassava mixed with grated coconut and palm sugar or jackfruit, giving it a soft, slightly sticky texture. It is a traditional street food and snack commonly found in Yogyakarta, typically sold at traditional markets and often wrapped in banana leaves.

Lampet is a traditional Batak snack from North Sumatra made from a mixture of glutinous rice flour and grated coconut, wrapped in banana leaves and then steamed or grilled until cooked. It is commonly served as a snack or accompaniment during ceremonial occasions and everyday meals in Batak communities.

Dushenyna is a Ukrainian braised meat dish, traditionally prepared by slow-cooking pork or beef with onions, carrots, and seasonings until tender in its own juices or a small amount of liquid. It is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by potatoes, buckwheat, or bread.

Doganitang is a Korean soup made by simmering beef knee cartilage and bones for an extended period until the collagen-rich cartilage becomes soft and gelatinous, resulting in a milky, rich broth. It is typically seasoned with garlic, green onions, and salt, and served hot as a meal accompanied by rice and various banchan (side dishes).

Itak Gurgur is a traditional Batak cake made from coarsely ground rice flour mixed with grated coconut, palm sugar, and ginger, which together give it a warming, slightly spicy flavor. It is typically shaped by hand and served during Batak ceremonial occasions and cultural gatherings as a customary food offering.

Khuruma is a deep-fried sweet made from wheat flour dough that is kneaded with ghee, shaped into small pieces, fried until crisp, and then coated or soaked in sugar syrup. It is traditionally prepared as one of the items in the Chhapan Bhog offered to Lord Jagannath at the Puri temple and is served as a dessert or festive sweet.

Potée savoyarde is a traditional French Alpine stew made by simmering pork, potatoes, and other vegetables in broth until tender. The dish is typically served hot as a hearty one-pot meal, particularly during winter months in the Savoie region.

Eiernockerl is an Austrian sweet soufflé-like dish made from eggs, sugar, and flour, typically folded to create a light, airy texture and baked until golden. It is traditionally served as a dessert, often accompanied by plum compote or fruit sauce.

Babi kecap is an Indonesian dish of pork braised in sweet soy sauce (kecap manis), often with the addition of garlic, shallots, and spices until the meat becomes tender and deeply caramelized. The dish is typically served with steamed rice and is a common preparation in Indonesian cuisine, particularly in regions with significant pork consumption.

Afang is a Nigerian soup made with afang leaves (a leafy vegetable similar to spinach), combined with stock fish, dried shrimp, and palm oil, then thickened with ground melon seeds. The soup is traditionally served hot with fufu, cassava, or other starchy accompaniments as a main course.

Clam pie is a savory pie made with clams and other ingredients encased in a pastry shell, typically prepared by combining chopped or minced clams with a binding sauce or broth before baking. The dish is traditionally served as a main course or hearty appetizer and may include additional ingredients such as potatoes, onions, or cream depending on regional variations.

Getuk Goreng is a Javanese fried snack made from cassava that is steamed, mashed, and shaped into cylinders before being deep-fried until golden and crispy on the outside. It is typically served as a street food or breakfast item, often accompanied by sweet soy sauce or sambal for dipping.

Smaženice are small fried patties or fritters made from mushrooms and eggs, typically bound together with flour and seasonings before being pan-fried until golden. They are commonly served as an appetizer or side dish in Central and Eastern European cuisine, often accompanied by sour cream or a simple sauce.

Sukuma wiki is a collard greens dish prepared by sautéing or braising finely chopped collard greens (Brassica oleracea var. viridis) with onions and tomatoes until tender. It is commonly served as a side vegetable dish in East African cuisine, particularly in Kenya and Uganda, alongside staple foods like ugali or rice.

Bongko mento is an Indonesian steamed dessert made from a mixture of coconut milk, rice flour, and banana, wrapped in banana leaves before being cooked. It is typically served as a sweet snack or dessert, particularly associated with Javanese culinary tradition.

Tempong is a Balinese dish consisting of steamed or grilled fish, typically mackerel or other local fish, served with a sambal made from chili, shrimp paste, tomato, and lime. It is commonly accompanied by raw or blanched vegetables such as cassava leaves, long beans, and eggplant, and is typically eaten with steamed rice.

Egbo is a West African dish made from whole dried maize kernels that are soaked and boiled until soft, then seasoned with peppers (Capsicum), onions, and typically palm oil or other local condiments. It is commonly served as a snack or light meal, often accompanied by coconut, groundnuts, or fried fish depending on regional variation.

Muvi is a traditional dish of the Nivkh people of the Russian Far East, typically prepared from fish, berries, and seal or fish oil blended together into a thick, mousse-like consistency. It is commonly served cold as a festive or ceremonial dish, reflecting the indigenous reliance on local marine and foraged ingredients.

Mute is a hearty Colombian soup traditionally prepared by slow-simmering goat meat or other meats with hominy corn, chickpeas, potatoes, fava beans, and regional vegetables until thick and stew-like. It is commonly served as a main course at family gatherings and festive occasions, particularly in the Santander region, accompanied by rice or arepas.

Khenagi is typically prepared as a savory dish that commonly includes a base of seasoned meat or vegetables, often simmered or slow-cooked with aromatic spices and herbs to develop a rich flavor. It is usually served warm as a main course, frequently accompanied by bread, rice, or a simple side salad depending on regional preferences.

Ombus ombus is a Batak steamed rice cake from North Sumatra, made from glutinous rice flour mixed with grated coconut and palm sugar, then wrapped in banana leaves and cooked by steaming. It is commonly sold as a street food or market snack and is traditionally associated with the Batak community, often consumed in the morning alongside hot beverages.

Lêvenî is a Kurdish soup prepared by sautéing onion in sunflower oil, then simmering it with minced meat and black pepper. It is typically served hot as a starter or a light main course in traditional Kurdish meals.

Bara is a deep-fried savory cake made from a batter of urad dal (black gram lentils) that is soaked and ground to a fluffy consistency, often with added onions, green chilies, and spices. It is typically served as a breakfast or snack item, frequently accompanied by sambar or chutney for dipping.

Ayam tangkap is an Indonesian dish consisting of chicken that is marinated in a mixture of spices including turmeric, garlic, and chilies before being fried until golden. The dish is typically served with sambal and rice, and originates from the Sundanese cuisine of West Java.

Mish-mash is a Bulgarian breakfast dish made from diced tomatoes, peppers, onions, and eggs cooked together in a pan, often with added meat such as sausage or bacon. It is typically served hot with bread as a traditional morning meal.

Colenak is an Indonesian street snack made from glutinous rice flour dough that is flattened, grilled on a hot plate, and topped with sweet spreads such as chocolate, cheese, or condensed milk. It is typically served warm as a casual snack or dessert, often eaten with the hands or folded for easy consumption.

Getuk pero is an Indonesian steamed cassava cake made by grating cassava root, mixing it with grated coconut, brown sugar, and salt, then steaming the mixture in banana leaves until set. The resulting dense, slightly sweet cake is cut into portions and typically served as a traditional snack or breakfast item, often topped with grated coconut or palm sugar syrup.

Prażucha is a traditional Polish pancake made from a batter combining wheat flour and buckwheat flour, typically cooked on a griddle until golden. The dish is commonly served as a breakfast or snack item, often topped with or incorporating crackling for added texture and flavor.

Beet kvass is a fermented beverage made by fermenting beets with water, salt, and sometimes spices or additional vegetables, resulting in a tangy, slightly sweet drink with a deep red color. It is traditionally served chilled as a refreshing drink or light appetizer in Eastern European and Russian cuisine.

Xis is a Brazilian sandwich made with two slices of buttered bread filled with layers of ingredients such as ham, cheese, eggs, and various cold cuts, then typically pan-fried until golden. It is commonly served as a quick meal or snack, often accompanied by french fries or other side dishes.

Doi rui is a Bengali fish dish made by cooking rohu (rui) in a yogurt-based gravy seasoned with spices such as turmeric, cumin, ginger, and green chilies. It is typically served hot with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Chanasan makh is a Mongolian dish consisting of lamb pieces boiled in salted water, often with the bones in, until tender. It is typically served hot as a main course, accompanied by the cooking broth and sometimes flatbread or other simple sides.

Umxhanxa is a traditional Xhosa dish from South Africa made by cooking dried corn (maize) kernels together with dried beans until both are soft and well combined. It is typically served as a staple side dish or main course, often eaten plain or with a small amount of fat, and is a common food associated with rural and ceremonial contexts in the Eastern Cape region.

Yam pepper soup is a West African dish made by boiling chunks of yam in a spiced broth containing hot peppers, crayfish, and aromatic herbs such as uziza leaves or scent leaves, along with seasonings like stock cubes and salt. It is commonly served as a light meal or appetizer, often consumed warm and regarded in Nigerian cuisine as a comfort food or remedy during illness.

Cuisine gauloise typically refers to traditional French-style country cooking, often featuring hearty preparations with ingredients such as pork, poultry, root vegetables, herbs, and wine-based sauces. It is commonly served as a main course, sometimes accompanied by crusty bread or potatoes, and may be presented family-style in a rustic setting.

Chiratta puttu is a South Indian steamed dish made by layering rice flour and grated coconut inside a coconut shell, which acts as the mold and gives the dish its name ('chiratta' meaning coconut shell in Malayalam). It is typically served as a breakfast or tiffin item, commonly accompanied by kadala curry (black chickpea curry), banana, or palm sugar.

Tinaktak is a Chamorro dish from Guam made by simmering ground meat, typically beef or pork, with coconut milk, long beans, tomatoes, and onions until the sauce is reduced and the ingredients are cooked through. It is commonly served over white rice as a everyday household meal.

Vitamin salad is a Soviet-era dish made from finely shredded raw cabbage, carrots, and sometimes bell peppers or other fresh vegetables, dressed with vegetable oil, vinegar, and salt. It was commonly served as a light side salad in Soviet canteens and home kitchens, valued for its year-round availability using inexpensive, shelf-stable vegetables.

Sylheti rice pudding is a traditional dessert from the Sylhet region of Bangladesh, prepared by slowly cooking rice in coconut milk until the mixture thickens to a creamy consistency. It is typically served chilled or at room temperature as a sweet course during festivals, family gatherings, or as an everyday dessert.

Pudina Khara Pongal is a savory rice and lentil dish prepared by cooking rice and moong dal together with freshly ground mint (pudina), cumin seeds, black pepper, and ghee until the grains are tender and well-combined. The dish is traditionally served hot as a main course during breakfast or lunch, often accompanied by yogurt and pickles.

Carbonade is a beef stew braised in beer or ale, typically cooked slowly until the meat becomes tender and the sauce reduces to a rich glaze. The dish is traditionally served with bread, potatoes, or egg noodles to accompany the savory sauce.

Ham rice is a fried rice dish where cooked rice is stir-fried with diced ham, vegetables, and seasonings until heated through and well combined. It is typically served as a main course or side dish in Japanese international cuisine, often accompanied by soy sauce or other condiments.

Sauce chateaubriand is a classic French sauce made from a demi-glace base combined with béarnaise sauce, typically enriched with butter, shallots, and fresh tarragon. The sauce is traditionally served alongside grilled or roasted beef cuts, particularly chateaubriand steak, as an accompaniment to enhance the meat's flavor.

Saag gosht is a meat curry where lamb or mutton is braised in a spinach-based sauce (saag) seasoned with spices such as garlic, ginger, and garam masala. The dish is typically served with rice or bread as part of South Asian cuisine.

Escargots à la bordelaise are snails prepared in the Bordeaux style, typically cooked in their shells with a mixture of shallots, garlic, parsley, and red wine or red wine reduction, often finished with butter. The dish is traditionally served hot as an appetizer, with bread used to soak up the flavorful sauce.

canonicalName: cookbook śląsko modro kapusta red cabbage with onion and bacon
displayName: Cookbook:Śląsko Modro Kapusta (Red Cabbage with Onion and Bacon)
aliases: cookbook śląsko modro kapusta red cabbage with onion and bacon
description: Red cabbage is braised with onions and bacon, creating a sweet and savory side dish characteristic of Silesian cuisine. This dish is typically served warm alongside roasted meats and traditional Polish main courses.

Kancha tomator jhal is a Bengali preparation made by cooking raw (unripe) green tomatoes with mustard paste, green chilies, mustard oil, and spices such as nigella seeds, resulting in a pungent, tangy dish. It is typically served as a side accompaniment with steamed rice in Bengali household meals.

Steak au four is a German preparation in which a beef steak is oven-roasted, typically seasoned with salt, pepper, and herbs, often accompanied by vegetables or potatoes cooked alongside it in the same pan. It is commonly served as a main course, with the oven method producing a uniformly cooked steak that retains its juices.

Quick N' Toast is typically a fast-prepared toasted sandwich, commonly made by grilling or pressing bread with fillings such as cheese, ham, vegetables, or eggs until warm and crisp. It is often served as a casual breakfast or snack item, suitable for on-the-go dining in international quick-service settings.

Leg soup is a Yemenite dish prepared by slowly simmering beef or lamb legs with bones, along with onions, garlic, tomatoes, and spices such as hawaij, cumin, and turmeric, until the meat becomes tender and the broth rich. It is typically served hot as a hearty main course, often accompanied by flatbread and traditional condiments like zhug or hilbeh.

Margi special is a Nigerian soup indigenous to the Margi people of the North Eastern region, prepared by simmering fish with tomato, garlic, and spinach. It is typically served as a main dish accompanied by a starchy staple such as rice or a local swallow.

A chicken fillet roll consists of a breaded and deep-fried or griddled chicken breast fillet served inside a sliced bread roll, typically a soft white or baguette-style roll. It is a common lunchtime staple in Ireland, often accompanied by butter, sauces such as ketchup or mayonnaise, and optional additions like cheese or salad leaves.

Roust is a Bengali dish typically prepared as a slow-cooked, richly spiced preparation of meat or poultry, often marinated in yogurt and aromatic spices such as ginger, garlic, cardamom, and cinnamon before being braised until tender. It is commonly served with steamed rice, pulao, or Indian breads like luchi or paratha, often as part of a festive or celebratory meal.

A caprese sandwich is assembled with sliced fresh mozzarella, tomato, and basil leaves layered between bread, typically dressed with olive oil and salt. The sandwich is commonly served cold or at room temperature as a light lunch or appetizer.

A frikandel sandwich consists of a fried Dutch sausage (frikandel) placed inside a bread roll and topped with special sauce, typically a creamy or spicy condiment. This quick snack is commonly served at street food vendors and casual eateries across the Netherlands and Belgium.
Pigs in a blanket are small sausages wrapped in dough, typically baked until golden. They are commonly served as an appetizer or snack at parties and social gatherings.

Scallion pancakes are made by layering a simple dough with oil and chopped scallions, then pan-frying until crispy and golden brown. They are typically served hot as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by a dipping sauce such as soy sauce or vinegar-based condiments.

Appelbeignet is a Dutch fried pastry made by coating apple slices or apple-filled dough in a light batter and deep-frying until golden brown. It is typically served as a dessert or snack, often dusted with powdered sugar and sometimes accompanied by whipped cream or custard sauce.

Sambal shrimp is prepared by cooking shrimp in a spiced paste made from chilies, garlic, shallots, and other aromatics, creating a dish with significant heat and depth of flavor. It is typically served as a main course with rice, accompanying the shrimp with its rich, savory sauce.

Whole artichokes are trimmed, steamed until partially tender, then filled with a seasoned breadcrumb mixture often combined with garlic, herbs, cheese, and sometimes meat before being roasted or braised until fully cooked. This dish is typically served as a vegetable course or appetizer, with the leaves pulled away individually to eat the filling and tender leaf base.

Jengkol stew is an Indonesian dish made by simmering jengkol (stink beans) with spices, typically including garlic, shallots, chilies, and coconut milk or broth until tender. The stew is traditionally served as a main dish with rice and is popular in Indonesian cuisine, particularly in Java and Sumatra.

Düsseldorfer Senfrostbraten is a roasted beef dish prepared with a distinctive mustard crust, characteristic of Düsseldorf cuisine. The meat is typically seared and roasted with mustard coating, often served with potato side dishes and gravy in traditional German restaurants.

Mfarakeh is a Levantine dish made by scrambling eggs together with diced or sliced cooked potatoes, typically fried in oil or butter until set. It is commonly served as a breakfast or light meal, often accompanied by bread, olives, or fresh vegetables.

Omebe is an indigenous soup of the Esan people of Edo State, Nigeria, prepared by cooking dried fish, meat, and seasonings with locally sourced vegetables and thickeners into a hearty broth. It is traditionally served with swallows such as pounded yam, starch, or fufu as part of everyday meals and cultural gatherings.

Tauto Pekalongan is a beef or buffalo soup from Pekalongan, Central Java, distinguished by the use of tauco (fermented soybean paste) as a primary flavoring ingredient, which sets it apart from other Indonesian soto varieties. The dish is typically prepared with meat, vermicelli, boiled eggs, and aromatic spices, served in a savory broth and commonly accompanied by steamed rice or lontong (compressed rice cake).

Würzfleisch is a German dish consisting of diced veal or beef cooked in a creamy, spiced white sauce typically seasoned with lemon, capers, and anchovy, resulting in a rich, savory preparation. It is commonly served warm over toast or bread, and was historically a popular cold-buffet or supper dish in German cuisine.

KafBikh is a traditional Iranian sweet, typically prepared with ingredients common to Persian confectionery such as sugar, rosewater, and aromatic spices like cardamom or saffron. It is commonly served in small portions alongside tea as part of a dessert course or during festive gatherings and celebrations.

Potage à la Marcus Hill is a veal-based soup prepared by simmering veal with aromatic vegetables and seasonings to produce a rich, savory broth. It is typically served hot as a starter course in an international dining context.

Satay Tuna is an Indonesian-influenced dish in which tuna pieces are marinated and grilled or charcoal-cooked on skewers, typically brushed with a spiced peanut or sweet soy-based sauce. It is commonly served as a main course or appetizer alongside steamed rice, sliced cucumber, and additional peanut sauce for dipping.

Beggar's Purse is a small appetizer consisting of a thin crêpe gathered and tied at the top with a strip of chive or blanched leek to form a pouch, enclosing a filling typically made of crème fraîche and caviar, though variations may include smoked salmon, mushrooms, or other savory ingredients. It is commonly served as an hors d'oeuvre or first course in fine dining contexts.

Puruvesi vendace is a freshwater fish dish featuring Coregonus albula (vendace), a small whitefish native to Finnish lakes. The fish is typically prepared through pan-frying or baking and served as a main course, often with simple accompaniments that highlight the delicate flavor of the freshwater fish.

Malabar biryani is a rice dish prepared by layering parboiled rice with marinated meat (typically chicken, mutton, or fish) and aromatics, then slow-cooked together in a sealed pot. The dish is traditionally served as a complete meal, often accompanied by raita, pickle, and shorba (broth).

Fegatelli are Italian meatballs made from pork liver, breadcrumbs, and seasonings, typically wrapped in caul fat or bay leaves and grilled or pan-fried. This Tuscan specialty is traditionally served as a second course or appetizer, often accompanied by bread or vegetables.

Pumpernickelsuppe is a German soup made from pumpernickel bread, typically dark rye bread, combined with broth and seasonings to create a thick, hearty preparation. The dish is traditionally served warm as a main course or substantial starter, often garnished with sour cream or accompanied by bread.

Sikbāj is an Arab stew made by slow-cooking meat with chickpeas, dried fruits such as apricots or raisins, and aromatic spices including cinnamon and coriander, creating a sweet and savory combination. The dish is traditionally served over rice or with bread and represents a common preparation in Levantine and broader Arab cuisine.

Rendang Itiak is a traditional Indonesian dish featuring duck meat slow-cooked in a rich spice paste made from coconut milk, galangal, garlic, shallots, chilies, and turmeric until the sauce reduces and coats the meat. The dish is typically served with steamed rice and is common in Minangkabau cuisine from West Sumatra.

Danish pastry dough is a laminated dough made by folding butter into a yeast-based dough multiple times to create distinct layers, then rolled thin and cut into various shapes before baking until golden and crispy. It is used as the base for Danish pastries, which are typically filled with jam, custard, almond paste, or cheese and served as breakfast items or desserts.

Panku Streetfood is a United Kingdom-based sushi counter brand that prepares ready-to-eat sushi items such as maki rolls, nigiri, and rice-based bowls, typically assembled on-site with ingredients like vinegared rice, seaweed, raw or cooked fish, and vegetables. The products are sold through retail counters in a fast-food format, intended for takeaway consumption as a quick meal or snack.

Krupky are small Ukrainian dumplings made from a dough of flour and egg, filled or combined with millet and chicken, then boiled until cooked through. They are typically served as a main dish or hearty soup component in traditional Ukrainian cuisine.

Katt Bafla is a Rajasthani dish consisting of wheat flour dough balls (bafla) that are first boiled in water and then baked or roasted over a fire or in an oven, similar to baati but softer in texture due to the boiling step. The bafla is served with katt, a tangy and spiced gravy typically made from gram flour (besan) and tamarind, along with dal and ghee, and is commonly eaten as a main meal in Rajasthan.

Rock-Hard Food is prepared by cooking stones and ores together in a pot, resulting in an inedible dish originating from The Legend of Zelda series. It is typically served as a novelty or joke item, referencing the in-game outcome of combining mineral ingredients rather than standard cooking components.

Garigit is a traditional Banjarese dish from South Kalimantan, Indonesia, typically prepared using local ingredients and cooking techniques characteristic of the region's cuisine. It is commonly served as part of everyday meals or during communal gatherings, often accompanied by steamed rice and traditional side dishes.

Sunduk lawang is a traditional Banjarese dish from South Kalimantan, Indonesia, typically prepared using local ingredients and seasonings characteristic of the region's cuisine, such as aromatic spices, coconut, and freshwater fish or poultry. It is commonly served as part of a family meal alongside steamed rice and accompanying side dishes, reflecting the culinary heritage of the Banjar people.

Hatuhūn sogi is typically a meat-based dish, with 'sogi' suggesting beef or similar cuts that are often sliced and prepared through grilling, braising, or stir-frying with seasonings. It is commonly served as a main course alongside rice or flatbread, and may be accompanied by vegetables or a light sauce depending on regional preparation.

Gajitsuae (가지쓰아에) is a Korean dish made by blanching or steaming eggplant, then dressing it with a seasoned mixture typically including soy sauce, sesame oil, garlic, green onions, and chili. It is commonly served as a banchan (side dish) at room temperature alongside steamed rice and other accompaniments.

Jyu cheung fun is a Cantonese dim sum dish consisting of glutinous rice flour dough formed into a long tube or coil shape and filled with Chinese sausage (lap cheong) before steaming. The dish is typically served as part of a dim sum meal, often accompanied by soy sauce or chili sauce for dipping.

cuisine: cuisine of the southern united states
tags: cuisine of the southern united states
description: Lemon pepper wings are chicken wings coated in a seasoning blend of lemon zest, cracked black pepper, and spices, then typically fried until crispy. This Southern preparation is commonly served as an appetizer or entrée with lemon wedges and is known for its bright citrus and peppery flavor profile.

Salt and pepper chips are a British Chinese dish consisting of thick-cut potato chips coated with a seasoning mixture of salt, white pepper, and typically garlic and chili, then deep-fried until crispy. The dish is commonly served as an appetizer or side in Chinese takeaway restaurants throughout the United Kingdom.

Cookies are baked confections made from a dough of flour, butter, sugar, and eggs, often with additional ingredients such as chocolate chips, nuts, or dried fruit. They are typically served as a dessert or snack, either warm or at room temperature, and are common in many cuisines worldwide.

Ginger cake is a spiced cake made with flour, butter, sugar, and ground ginger, often enriched with molasses or treacle for depth and moisture. It is typically baked in a tin, served in slices at room temperature or warmed, and commonly enjoyed as a dessert or afternoon treat with tea or coffee.

Pais is an Indonesian preparation in which ingredients such as fish, meat, tofu, or vegetables are mixed with a spice paste of shallots, garlic, chili, turmeric, and other aromatics, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed or grilled. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice and sambal as part of a larger meal.

Almond chicken is an American Chinese dish featuring chicken pieces stir-fried with a savory sauce and garnished with sliced or slivered almonds for texture and flavor. The dish is typically served over steamed rice or noodles as a main course in Chinese-American restaurants.

Ofe Ujuju is an Igbo soup from southeastern Nigeria, prepared by boiling ujuju leaves with ingredients such as assorted meats or fish, palm oil, crayfish, and seasonings until thickened. It is typically served hot alongside swallows like fufu, pounded yam, or garri as a main course.

Massini is a Uruguayan dessert made of layers of sponge cake soaked in syrup, filled with pastry cream, and topped with a layer of meringue that is typically caramelized or lightly browned. It is commonly served in slices as a sweet course at celebrations, family gatherings, and traditional bakeries throughout Uruguay.

Morich bharta is a Bangladeshi preparation made by roasting or charring green or dried chilies, then mashing them with mustard oil, onion, garlic, and salt to form a coarse paste. It is typically served as a side condiment alongside steamed rice and lentils or other main dishes.

Davelaar Jodenkoeken are thin, crispy Dutch butter cookies made from a simple dough of flour, sugar, butter, and a small amount of leavening, rolled flat and baked until golden. They are a well-known commercial variety of the traditional Dutch 'jodenkoek' (Jewish cookie), typically sold as a packaged snack and eaten plain as an everyday biscuit.

Broeder is a traditional steamed or boiled pudding from South African cuisine, made from a batter of flour, sugar, eggs, fat, and a leavening agent, cooked in a cloth or mold submerged in boiling water. It is typically served warm as a dessert, often accompanied by a sweet sauce or custard.

Schissotto is a traditional flatbread from the Padua area of Veneto, Italy, made from a simple dough of flour, water, salt, and lard or olive oil, cooked directly on a hot griddle or flat stone surface. It is typically served warm as an accompaniment to cured meats, cheeses, and vegetables, and is a common feature of local rustic meals and festivals in the Paduan tradition.

Pulut kuning is an Indonesian glutinous rice dish prepared by cooking sticky rice with coconut milk, turmeric, and spices until the rice absorbs the golden-yellow liquid and becomes fragrant. It is traditionally served as a festive accompaniment to main dishes or as part of celebratory meals, particularly during special occasions and ceremonies.

Salmon carpaccio is prepared by thinly slicing raw salmon that has been cured or marinated, typically with citrus juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper. It is served chilled as an appetizer, often garnished with capers, red onion, or fresh herbs.

Fiskekaker are Norwegian fish cakes made from ground fish (typically cod or similar white fish), potatoes, onions, and seasonings, formed into patties and pan-fried until golden. They are traditionally served with melted butter, lemon wedges, and creamy sauce, often accompanied by boiled potatoes or rice.

Kongnamul-bap is a Korean rice bowl topped with seasoned soybean sprouts and typically garnished with sesame seeds and soy sauce. The soybean sprouts are blanched and dressed with garlic, sesame oil, and salt before being served over steamed rice as a simple, everyday meal.

Bean goulash is a Hungarian stew made by braising meat and beans together with potatoes and traditional goulash seasonings including paprika, onions, and garlic until tender. The dish is typically served hot in bowls as a main course, often accompanied by bread or sour cream.

Ouarka is a Moroccan pastry made from thin sheets of dough that are filled with ingredients such as meat, vegetables, or seafood, then rolled or folded and typically fried until crispy. It is commonly served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by a sweet and spicy dipping sauce.

Butterschnitzel is a breaded and fried thin cutlet, typically made from veal or pork, that is finished with a generous knob of butter placed on top while still hot so it melts over the meat. The dish is traditionally served with lemon wedges and accompaniments such as potatoes, vegetables, or salad, and is common in Central European cuisine, particularly in Austria and Germany.

Calçotada is a Catalan dish featuring calçots, a type of spring onion, that are roasted over an open flame until charred and tender. The roasted onions are typically served with romesco sauce or a simple oil and vinegar dressing, accompanied by grilled meats such as sausage or chicken, and is traditionally eaten during winter and early spring months.
