Pølse is a Scandinavian sausage typically made from pork, beef, or a mixture of meats combined with spices and fat, then stuffed into casings and either boiled, grilled, or fried. It is commonly served in a bread roll with condiments such as mustard, ketchup, and crispy onions, or eaten as a standalone item with potatoes and pickled vegetables.

A huarache is an elongated Mexican fried masa cake topped with refried beans, meat (typically carnitas or chorizo), and garnishes such as lettuce, tomato, cheese, and crema. It is served as a street food or casual meal, often eaten by hand or with utensils.

King's Hand is a surrealist dessert presented in the shape of a human hand, assembled from components including Greek salad, M&M's, and cookie dough. It is served as a creative, sculptural dish where the ingredients are arranged to form the hand's structure rather than prepared through conventional cooking techniques.

Panzaje is a traditional Mexican dish typically prepared with masa-based dough or tortillas that are filled or layered with ingredients such as beans, cheese, meat, and salsa, then cooked on a griddle or fried until golden. It is commonly served warm as a main course or hearty snack, often accompanied by fresh salsa, crema, and pickled vegetables.

Larb pa is a Lao-style minced or chopped fish salad prepared with raw or cooked freshwater fish combined with toasted rice powder, fish sauce, lime juice, fresh herbs such as mint and cilantro, dried chili, and shallots. It is typically served at room temperature as part of a shared meal alongside sticky rice and other dishes.

Pindang Serani is an Indonesian sour and spicy fish soup originating from the coastal communities of Java, prepared by simmering fish—commonly freshwater or sea fish—in a broth seasoned with turmeric, galangal, lemongrass, shallots, chilies, and tamarind or vinegar to achieve its characteristic tangy flavor. The dish is typically served hot with steamed rice and is a common everyday meal in households and small eateries in regions such as Kudus and other parts of Central Java.

Nasi gandul is a dish from Pati, Central Java, consisting of steamed rice served with a rich, brown beef broth-based sauce made from coconut milk, spices, and slow-cooked buffalo or beef offal and meat. It is traditionally served on a plate lined with banana leaf, with the meat and broth ladled over the rice, and is commonly accompanied by empal (fried spiced beef) and sambal.

Corn rice, known in Indonesian as nasi jagung, is prepared by cooking cracked or ground dried corn kernels, sometimes mixed with rice, typically by steaming or boiling until tender. It is a staple dish associated with regions such as East Java and Madura, commonly served as the carbohydrate base of a meal alongside vegetables, salted fish, or other side dishes.

Dakgaejang is a Korean spicy chicken stew made by simmering chicken with gochugaru (red chili pepper flakes), vegetables such as perilla leaves and green onions, and aromatic seasonings including garlic and ginger. The dish is typically served hot in a communal pot or individual bowls as a main course, often accompanied by rice and side dishes.

Mendol is an Indonesian fritter made by wrapping seasoned fish or shrimp paste around banana or cassava, then coating it in batter and deep-frying until golden brown. It is typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by a sweet and spicy dipping sauce.

Ossobuco is a Milanese braise of veal shank cut crosswise to expose the marrow bone, braised slowly in white wine with vegetables and broth until tender. It is traditionally served with risotto alla milanese and finished with gremolata, a mixture of lemon zest, garlic, and parsley.

Consommé is a clear soup made by simmering stock with a mixture of ground meat, vegetables, and egg whites, which are strained out to produce a transparent, refined broth. It is typically served hot in small portions as a first course, often garnished minimally with julienned vegetables, pasta, or other delicate garnishes.

Sōki is an Okinawan dish featuring spare ribs braised until tender in a savory broth seasoned with soy sauce, awamori (local liquor), and dashi stock. The dish is typically served over rice with the cooking liquid spooned over top, often accompanied by pickled vegetables or as part of a mixed plate lunch.

Rica-rica is an Indonesian dish where meat or seafood is cooked in a spiced paste made from red chilies, shallots, garlic, ginger, and turmeric, creating a rich and intensely flavored sauce. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice and is characteristic of Indonesian, particularly Manado, cuisine.

Lahpet thohk is a Burmese salad made from fermented tea leaves mixed with cabbage, tomatoes, peanuts, sesame seeds, and dried shrimp, dressed with oil and lime juice. The dish is typically served as a side dish or appetizer and is known for its distinctive bitter and tangy flavor profile from the fermented tea leaves.

Pastırma is a cured and dried meat product made from beef, veal, or camel meat that is salted, dried, and then coated with a spice paste containing fenugreek, garlic, and paprika before final curing. It is typically sliced thin and served as a cold appetizer, in sandwiches, or as part of a meze platter in Turkish and Eastern Mediterranean cuisine.

Keichan is a Japanese grilled chicken dish made with chicken offal, particularly hearts and other internal organs, marinated and cooked on a griddle or skewers at the table. It is typically served with dipping sauces and consumed as an izakaya appetizer or main course, often accompanied by beer or sake.

Voressen is a Swiss braised meat dish, typically made from veal or beef offcuts such as shoulder or neck, slow-cooked with onions, root vegetables, and white wine or broth until tender. It is commonly served with Rösti, pasta, or bread, and is a traditional home-cooked meal found particularly in German-speaking Switzerland.

Riebel is an Austrian cornmeal dish made by cooking polenta with butter and milk until thick, then typically served in a mound or patty form. It is commonly accompanied by sauerkraut, mushrooms, or gravy, and traditionally eaten as a hearty side dish or main course in Austrian cuisine.

A taquito is a rolled tortilla typically filled with shredded chicken, beef, or cheese, then fried until crispy. It is commonly served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by sour cream, guacamole, or salsa for dipping.

Forshmak is a Russian appetizer made from finely chopped or minced herring mixed with onions, apples, and sometimes potatoes, bound together with sour cream or mayonnaise. It is typically served cold as a starter or snack, often garnished with hard-boiled eggs and accompanied by bread or crackers.

Buckwheat cake is made from buckwheat flour mixed with eggs, milk, and leavening agents, then baked until golden. It is traditionally served as a breakfast or dessert item, often topped with butter, sour cream, or jam.

Aboukir is a French layered dessert composed of génoise or sponge cake layered with chestnut cream and buttercream, then coated with fondant icing. It is typically served chilled as a pâtisserie-style dessert, often presented in individual portions or as a small cake.

Croquignoles are small, crispy fried pastries made from choux dough that is piped into various shapes and deep-fried until golden. Traditionally associated with French and Creole cuisines, they are typically dusted with powdered sugar or cinnamon sugar and served as a dessert or pastry accompaniment.

Macaroni casserole is prepared by combining cooked macaroni pasta with a binding sauce, typically a cheese or cream-based mixture, then baking the combined ingredients in a casserole dish until set. The dish is commonly served as a main course or side dish at family meals and gatherings.

Hochepot is a traditional stew made by slowly braising meat with root vegetables and sometimes dried fruit, creating a rich, savory dish. It is typically served hot as a main course, often accompanied by bread or potatoes to soak up the cooking liquid.

Chè trôi nước is a Vietnamese dessert consisting of glutinous rice flour balls filled with brown sugar that are boiled in a ginger-infused sugar syrup. The dish is traditionally served warm as a sweet soup, typically enjoyed as a dessert or snack in Vietnamese cuisine.

Buddha's delight is a vegetarian stir-fried dish typically prepared with a mix of vegetables such as broccoli, mushrooms, bell peppers, and snap peas in a light sauce, often served over rice or noodles. The dish is commonly found in Asian restaurants and reflects Buddhist vegetarian dietary principles.

Undhiyu is a traditional Gujarati dish made by layering vegetables such as sweet potato, banana, and coconut with spices, then cooking the mixture in an earthen pot buried in hot coals or baked in an oven until tender. It is typically served as a festive side dish during winter celebrations, particularly around Makar Sankranti.

Ema datshi is a Bhutanese dish made by cooking fresh chili peppers with datshi, a cheese sauce typically prepared from yak or cow cheese mixed with butter and spices. The dish is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by white rice, and represents a staple of Bhutanese cuisine.

Lazanki is a traditional Polish noodle dish made from thin, flat pasta strips that are typically boiled and then combined with melted butter, sometimes with the addition of poppy seeds or caramelized onions. The dish is commonly served as a side accompaniment to meat courses or as a main course, particularly during festive occasions and family meals.

Sate kambing is a grilled meat skewer dish made from marinated goat meat threaded onto bamboo sticks and cooked over charcoal, typically seasoned with spices such as garlic, shallots, and chilies. It is commonly served with peanut sauce, sliced onions, and cucumber, and is eaten as a street food or appetizer in Indonesian cuisine.

Murukku is a savory spiral-shaped snack made from rice flour mixed with spices and other ingredients, then deep-fried until crispy. It is a traditional Indian snack commonly served as an appetizer or eaten with tea and coffee.

Flake is a species of shark, primarily gummy shark (Mustelus antarcticus), commonly sold in the United Kingdom and Australia as a white-fleshed fish fillet with a mild flavour and firm texture. It is typically battered and deep-fried, and served as part of the traditional fish and chips meal, often accompanied by chips, mushy peas, and vinegar.

Cadbury Tempo is a chocolate bar consisting of a caramel and wafer center coated in milk chocolate, manufactured by Cadbury. It is typically sold as a individually wrapped confectionery snack and consumed as a ready-to-eat treat without any further preparation.

Mostillo de mel is a traditional Spanish dessert prepared by cooking honey with flour and water until it thickens into a dense, pudding-like paste, sometimes flavored with spices such as anise or cinnamon. It is typically served in small portions at the end of a meal or during festive occasions, often at room temperature.

Kudos is a brand of granola bar made from rolled oats and other grains bound together and coated or dipped in milk chocolate, often incorporating mix-ins such as chocolate chips, peanut butter, or candy pieces. The bars are pre-packaged and typically eaten as a handheld snack or lunchbox item rather than as part of a plated meal.

Bottle Caps are a hard candy produced by Ferrara Candy Company, shaped and sized to resemble metal bottle caps, and made primarily from sugar with fruit-flavored additives including cola, root beer, grape, orange, and cherry. They are sold as a standalone snack candy, commonly found in movie theaters and convenience stores, and are consumed directly without any preparation.

Bournville is a dark chocolate bar produced by Cadbury, made from cocoa mass, sugar, and cocoa butter, formed by conching and tempering the chocolate before molding it into segmented bars. It is typically eaten as a confection on its own or used as an ingredient in baking and desserts.

Cadbury Snowflake is a chocolate bar manufactured by Cadbury, consisting of a flaky, layered milk chocolate centre coated in an outer shell of milk chocolate. It is typically sold as a individually wrapped confectionery product and consumed as a snack.

Choco Bat is a Japanese snack consisting of a small chocolate-coated ice cream bar shaped like a baseball bat, produced by Lotte. It is sold individually at convenience stores and snack shops across Japan, commonly consumed as a handheld frozen treat.

Tumpeng is a cone-shaped rice dish prepared by pressing cooked yellow rice (seasoned with turmeric and coconut milk) into a mold, then unmolded and surrounded by various side dishes such as vegetables, eggs, and meat or fish. It is traditionally served at celebrations and gatherings in Indonesian cuisine, with the cone shape symbolizing a mountain and often cut and shared among multiple people.

Albanian stew is a slow-cooked dish typically made with meat (usually beef or lamb), potatoes, onions, and tomatoes, seasoned with garlic and spices. It is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by bread to soak up the rich broth.

The Bacon Explosion is a grilled meat dish consisting of a layer of fresh sausage wrapped in woven strips of bacon, then cooked over heat until the bacon crisps and the sausage cooks through. The finished dish is typically sliced into rounds and served with barbecue sauce for dipping or drizzling.

Hopje is a small, hard coffee-flavored candy made with coffee extract and sugar, traditionally produced in the Netherlands. It is typically consumed as a confection or after-dinner sweet, often enjoyed with coffee or tea.

A thin, crispy wafer cookie rolled into a cylindrical shape while still warm, typically made from a simple batter of flour, sugar, butter, and eggs. Cocoa cigarettes are flavored with cocoa powder and often served as a petit four or accompaniment to coffee and desserts.

Yōguru is a fermented milk beverage prepared by culturing milk with specific bacterial strains to create a tangy, creamy product. It is typically served chilled as a drink or dessert, often consumed plain or with added fruits, honey, or granola.

KFC Original Recipe consists of chicken pieces seasoned with a proprietary blend of herbs and spices, then breaded and deep-fried until golden brown. The dish is typically served with sides such as mashed potatoes, coleslaw, or biscuits, and is a signature item at KFC restaurants worldwide.
Cadbury Dairy Milk Fruit & Nut is a chocolate bar made from milk chocolate blended with raisins and roasted almonds. It is typically consumed as a ready-to-eat confectionery snack or dessert item.

Chicken nanban is a Japanese dish consisting of fried chicken coated in a sweet and tangy vinegar-based sauce, typically made with soy sauce, vinegar, and mirin, often garnished with pickled vegetables and served over rice. The dish originated in Miyazaki Prefecture and is traditionally served as a casual meal or side dish in Japanese restaurants and home cooking.

Chef Cookie's Deep Dish is a dessert served at Disneyland, consisting of a warm baked cookie topped with ice cream. It is typically presented in an individual dish and eaten as a shareable or single-serving sweet course.

# Alu Potala Rasa

Alu Potala Rasa is a South Indian vegetable curry made by sautéing diced potatoes and sliced potala (a climbing vine vegetable) with onions, tomatoes, and spices including turmeric, chili powder, and cumin, then simmering in coconut milk or water until tender. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or flatbread in everyday meals and special occasions throughout Karnataka and other southern Indian regions.

Nerikuri is a Japanese confection made by kneading together various ingredients such as bean paste, sugar, and sometimes mochi or other starches until thoroughly combined into a smooth, pliable dough. It is typically served as a small sweet treat or wagashi during tea ceremonies and formal occasions, often molded into decorative shapes and sometimes dusted with kinako (roasted soybean powder) or other coatings.

Kaishui baicai is a Chinese dish prepared by blanching Napa cabbage in boiling water and then serving it with a simple chicken soup-based sauce. The dish is typically served as a light appetizer or side course in Chinese meals, valued for its delicate flavor and minimal preparation.

Namul is a Korean side dish made by blanching or lightly steaming various vegetables such as spinach, bean sprouts, zucchini, and mushrooms, then seasoning them with sesame oil, garlic, soy sauce, and salt. It is typically served as a banchan (side dish) alongside rice and main courses in Korean meals.

Minced meat pie is prepared by baking a filling of ground meat mixed with onions, seasonings, and often vegetables or sauce within a pastry crust, either as an enclosed pie or with a top crust only. It is typically served as a main course for lunch or dinner in various cuisines including British, Australian, and Middle Eastern traditions.

Vegetable stock is prepared by simmering a combination of vegetables such as onions, carrots, celery, leeks, and garlic along with herbs like thyme and bay leaves in water for approximately one to two hours, then straining to remove solids. It is used as a base for soups, sauces, and other dishes in French cuisine rather than served as a standalone dish.

Onion rings are made by slicing onions into rings, dipping them in a batter typically containing flour, egg, and milk or water, and then deep-frying them until golden brown. They are commonly served as a side dish or appetizer in casual dining establishments, often accompanied by dipping sauces such as ketchup or ranch dressing.

Mzhaveuli is a Georgian soup traditionally prepared by simmering beef or lamb with onions, tomatoes, garlic, and herbs like coriander and dill in a meat broth until the ingredients are tender and flavorful. The dish is typically served hot as a main course during family meals or festive occasions, often accompanied by bread.

# Aam diye Motor Dal

Aam diye motor dal is a Bengali dish made by cooking dried green peas (motor dal) with raw or unripe mango, turmeric, and spices, typically prepared through a simmering method in a pot or pressure cooker. The dish is traditionally served as an accompaniment to rice and curries during meals, particularly during the summer season when raw mangoes are in season.

Chholar dal is a Bengali sweet lentil curry prepared by cooking split Bengal gram (chholar dal) with jaggery, coconut, and spices like cinnamon and cardamom, traditionally tempered with ghee and served as a festive dessert or accompaniment during celebrations like Durga Puja. The dish is typically served warm alongside puris or other fried breads during Bengali Hindu festivals and special occasions.

# Anaroser Polau

Anaroser polau is an Afghan rice dish prepared by cooking rice with carrots, raisins, and meat (typically lamb or beef) in a pilaf method, often layered and steamed together. It is traditionally served as a main course during special occasions and family meals in Afghan cuisine.

Chhanar dalna is an Indian dessert made by curdling milk to create paneer (cheese curds), which are then shaped into balls, fried until golden, and simmered in a light sugar syrup flavored with cardamom and sometimes rose water or nuts. This sweet dish is typically served as a dessert during festivals and special occasions, particularly in Bengali cuisine.

Chingri machher bati chorchori is a Bengali dish combining prawns (chingri) and fish (machher) with vegetables like pumpkin, potatoes, and beans, prepared together in a single pot with spices and coconut milk. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice, representing a traditional Bengali approach to one-pot cooking that balances protein, vegetables, and aromatic seasoning.

Kêpayî is a traditional Kurdish dish typically prepared with rice as a central ingredient, often cooked slowly and seasoned with regional spices to develop a rich, savory flavor. It is commonly served as a main course, sometimes accompanied by stewed meat, yogurt, or fresh vegetables in the style of home-cooked Kurdish meals.

Shutki chingri bhorta is a Bengali preparation made by mashing dried shrimp (shutki) together with fresh shrimp (chingri) and aromatics such as onions, garlic, and green chilies, typically finished with mustard oil and salt. The dish is commonly served as a side condiment or appetizer with rice and lentils in Bengali cuisine.

# Enchorer Dalna

Enchorer dalna is a Bengali vegetable curry prepared by simmering pointed gourd (enchorer), potatoes, and other vegetables in a mustard oil-based gravy flavored with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and fenugreek seeds. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to steamed rice or flatbread during everyday meals in West Bengal and other Bengali-speaking regions.

Chhanar chop is a Bengali sweet prepared by mixing crumbled paneer (Indian cottage cheese) with semolina, cardamom, and sometimes nuts, then shaping the mixture into balls or patties and deep-frying until golden brown. It is typically served as a dessert or sweet snack at festivals, celebrations, and special occasions in Bengali cuisine.

# Dhonkar Kaliya

Dhonkar kaliya is a traditional Bengali curry made by slow-cooking mutton or goat meat with onions, ginger, garlic, yogurt, and spices such as cumin, coriander, and bay leaves until the meat becomes tender and the flavors meld together. This rich, aromatic dish is typically served as a main course during festive occasions and family gatherings in Bengal, accompanied by rice or bread.

# Alur rosa

Alur rosa is a Peruvian potato dish prepared by boiling potatoes with peanuts, hard-boiled eggs, and cheese, then mixing them with a sauce made from fresh herbs, spices, and occasionally ají peppers. It is typically served cold or at room temperature as a side dish or light main course during lunch meals, particularly in Andean regions of Peru.

# Chholar Shami Kebab

Chholar shami kebab is a Bengali appetizer made by grinding boiled chickpeas with spices, onions, and ginger-garlic paste, then shallow-frying the formed patties until golden brown. The kebabs are typically served as a starter during festive occasions and meals, often accompanied by mint chutney or served with bread.

Chhanar koptakari is a Bengali vegetable curry made by stir-frying paneer cheese cubes with potatoes, peas, and cauliflower in a tempering of mustard seeds and nigella seeds, then simmering them in a light spiced sauce. It is typically served as a side dish with rice or Indian breads during lunch or dinner meals in Bengali households.

# Aamer Morobba

Aamer morobba is a sweet mango preserve made by cooking raw mangoes with sugar, spices such as cardamom and cloves, and sometimes ginger, until the fruit breaks down into a thick, jam-like consistency. It is typically served as a condiment or spread with bread and dairy products like yogurt and cheese during breakfast or as a dessert accompaniment in South Asian cuisine.

Doi fulkopi is a Bengali dish made by cooking cauliflower with yogurt, onions, ginger, garlic, and spices such as turmeric and cumin, typically prepared through a slow-cooking or braising method. It is commonly served as a vegetable curry accompaniment to rice or bread in Bengali cuisine, particularly for family meals and special occasions.

Jhinge diye muger dal is a Bengali dish made by cooking mung beans with ridge gourd (jhinge), onions, garlic, ginger, and spices including turmeric and cumin, typically prepared through sautéing aromatics followed by simmering until the dal reaches a thick, curry-like consistency. The dish is commonly served as an accompaniment to rice and other curries during everyday family meals in Bengali cuisine.

Gondhoraj chingri is a Bengali shrimp curry made with large prawns cooked in a spiced gravy featuring gondhoraj lime, a small citrus fruit native to Bengal known for its distinctive aromatic quality. The dish is typically prepared by sautéing shrimp with onions, garlic, ginger, and spices, then simmered in a coconut milk or yogurt-based sauce finished with gondhoraj lime juice, and is commonly served with steamed rice or bread.

# Dumurer Kopta

Dumurer kopta is a traditional Bengali dish made by simmering potato and lentil dumplings in a spiced yogurt-based gravy, with key ingredients including potatoes, lentils, yogurt, onions, garlic, ginger, and various spices such as turmeric and cumin. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread during everyday meals and special occasions in Bengal and other regions of South Asia.

Gota seddho is a Gujarati vegetable dish prepared by boiling or steaming a mixture of vegetables such as potatoes, carrots, beans, and peas along with spices like turmeric and salt until tender. It is typically served as a simple side dish or light meal, often accompanied by bread or rice in Gujarati cuisine.

Alur dal is a Bengali lentil curry made by simmering dried lentils with potatoes, onions, garlic, ginger, and spices such as turmeric, cumin, and chili powder until the ingredients break down into a thick, savory paste. The dish is typically served as a main course or side dish alongside rice or bread in Bengali households and restaurants.

Jafrani fulkopi is a cauliflower dish prepared by sautéing florets with saffron, turmeric, cumin, and other spices, typically finished with ghee or oil and served as a vegetable side dish in Indian cuisine. The dish is commonly accompanied by rice or bread at family meals and formal dining occasions throughout India.

Guguta's hat is a Moldovan layered cake made from baked cocoa sponge rounds assembled into a tall conical shape resembling a hat, filled and coated with sour cream and whipped cream, and studded throughout with fresh or preserved cherries. It is typically served chilled as a dessert at family gatherings and celebrations, often sliced into wedges to reveal the cherry-filled cream layers inside.

Kashmiri begun is an eggplant dish prepared by frying sliced or cubed eggplant until tender and then simmering it in a yogurt-based sauce seasoned with spices such as asafetida, fennel seeds, and ginger. It is typically served as a vegetable curry accompanying rice or bread in Kashmiri cuisine and Indian meals.

Kancha aamer sorbot is a Bengali summer beverage prepared by blending raw mango pulp with water, sugar, and spices such as cumin and black salt to create a refreshing drink. It is typically served chilled as a cooling refreshment during hot weather in West Bengal and Bangladesh, often at meals or as a street beverage.

Peper dalna is a lentil curry from Bengali cuisine prepared by cooking split peas or lentils with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and mustard seeds, along with onions and garlic, until the mixture reaches a thick, porridge-like consistency. It is traditionally served as a comforting side dish or main course alongside rice or bread at everyday meals and special occasions in Bengali households.

# Malai Kopta

Malai kopta is a Bengali appetizer made by combining paneer cheese, potatoes, and spices into a mixture that is shaped into balls or patties, coated with breadcrumbs, and deep-fried until golden brown. The dish is typically served as a starter or snack, often accompanied by chutneys or sauces, and features a creamy interior contrasting with a crispy fried exterior.

Sobji Dal is an Indian lentil curry made by cooking dried lentils with onions, tomatoes, garlic, ginger, and spices such as turmeric, cumin, and coriander until tender and blended into a thick, creamy consistency. It is typically served as a main dish alongside rice or flatbread at lunch or dinner in Indian households and restaurants.

# Mochar Kopta

Mochar kopta is a Bengali fritter made by combining banana flower with spices, herbs, and sometimes lentil flour, then deep-frying the mixture until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by chutney, and is common in Bengali cuisine across West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Niramish korma is a vegetarian curry prepared by simmering mixed vegetables such as potatoes, carrots, peas, and cauliflower in a spiced yogurt or coconut milk-based sauce, typically without onions or garlic. This dish is commonly served as part of Bengali or Indian vegetarian meals, often accompanied by rice or bread.

# Postor Kaliya

Postor kaliya is a Bengali curry made by simmering poppy seeds ground into a paste with onions, tomatoes, and spices, then combined with vegetables or meat cooked until tender. It is typically served as a main dish with rice or bread at family meals and special occasions in Bengali cuisine.

# Alu dal vate

Alu dal vate is an Indian dish made by cooking split lentils with potatoes, onions, and spices such as turmeric and cumin, typically prepared through boiling or simmering until the ingredients reach a soft, porridge-like consistency. The dish is commonly served as a simple, everyday meal or side dish in Maharashtrian cuisine, often accompanied by rice, bread, or eaten on its own as a comfort food.

Nabaratna korma is an Indian vegetarian curry prepared by simmering nine different vegetables along with paneer cheese, nuts, and cream in a mild, spice-infused yogurt-based sauce. The dish is typically served as a main course at formal meals and special occasions, accompanied by rice or bread.

Rold Gold Sticks are thin, baked pretzel sticks made from wheat flour, water, yeast, and salt, shaped into narrow cylindrical rods and coated with a salted exterior. They are a shelf-stable snack product commonly served as a standalone snack, in snack mixes, or alongside dips.

Chingri mathar kalia is a Bengali curry made with prawns and green peas cooked in a spiced gravy base of onions, tomatoes, and aromatic spices including cumin and coriander. The dish is typically served with rice or flatbread as part of Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: chingri diye pui shaag er chorchori
displayName: Chingri diye pui shaag er chorchori
aliases: chingri diye pui shaag er chorchori
description: Chingri diye pui shaag er chorchori is a Bengali stir-fried vegetable dish featuring pui shaak (Indian spinach) cooked with shrimp (chingri) and a blend of spices. The dish is typically prepared by tempering mustard oil with cumin and dried red chilies, then adding vegetables and shrimp until cooked through, and is commonly served as a side dish with rice and curries in Bengali cuisine.

Chingri diye lau ghonto is a Bengali vegetable stir-fry where bottle gourd (lau) is cooked with shrimp (chingri), onions, and spices including turmeric and chili powder. The dish is typically served as a side curry alongside rice or flatbread in Bengali cuisine.

Wedding cabbage (svadbarski kupus) is a traditional Serbian dish prepared by slow-cooking whole sour (fermented) cabbage leaves layered with large chunks of various meats, typically pork, beef, and smoked cuts, along with onions, paprika, and spices in an earthenware pot for several hours. It is customarily served at weddings, slavas, and other large celebrations, traditionally accompanied by bread and rakija.

Jhinge shukto is a Bengali vegetable curry made by cooking ridged gourd (jhinge) with other vegetables such as potatoes, carrots, and peas, seasoned with mustard oil, cumin seeds, and a paste of ground spices. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali meals.

# Chhanchi kumror shukto

Chhanchi kumror shukto is a Bengali vegetable curry prepared by tempering mustard seeds and dried chilies in hot oil before adding pumpkin and other vegetables like potatoes, beans, and eggplant, which are then cooked with a paste of ginger and garlic along with spices such as turmeric and cumin. The dish is typically served as part of a Bengali meal alongside rice or bread, functioning as a vegetable accompaniment to main protein dishes.

Chholar dal is a Bengali sweet lentil dish made by cooking split chickpeas with jaggery, coconut, and ginger, typically tempered with ghee and spices like cinnamon and cardamom. It is traditionally served as a dessert or sweet course during Bengali festivals and special occasions, particularly Durga Puja.

# Chalkumro Narkel

Chalkumro narkel is a traditional Bengali dessert made by slow-cooking rice flour, semolina, and grated coconut with ghee until golden brown, then binding the mixture with jaggery or sugar syrup and shaping it into balls or rounds. This sweet confection is typically served during festivals and special occasions in West Bengal and Bangladesh, often accompanied by milk or served as part of festive meal spreads.

Chhanar payesh is a Bengali dessert made by simmering small cubes of paneer (Indian cottage cheese) in a sweetened milk reduction flavored with cardamom, raisins, and cashews. It is typically served as a festive dessert during religious ceremonies and special occasions in Bengali households.

Peper shukto is a Bengali vegetable stir-fry dish made by cooking mixed vegetables such as potatoes, carrots, beans, and cabbage with spices including mustard seeds, cumin, and dried chilies. The dish is typically served as a side accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Ovos moles are a traditional Portuguese confection made by combining egg yolks with sugar to create a smooth, sweet paste that is often shaped into small portions and sometimes coated or decorated. These treats are typically served as desserts or petit fours, particularly during festive occasions and holidays in Portugal.

Oyakodon is a Japanese rice bowl dish where chicken and chicken eggs are simmered together in a savory broth and served over steamed rice. The name derives from the Japanese words for parent (oya) and child (ko), referring to the chicken and eggs respectively, and the dish is typically served as a quick, satisfying meal in Japanese cuisine.

Panzanella is a Tuscan salad made by combining stale bread cubes with fresh tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and basil, then dressed with olive oil and vinegar. The dish is typically served at room temperature as a light summer course or side dish, with the bread absorbing the vegetable juices and vinaigrette.

Shakshuka is a dish of eggs poached in a sauce of tomatoes, onions, and chili peppers, typically simmered until the egg whites are set while the yolks remain runny. It is traditionally served in the same pan with bread for dipping, commonly eaten for breakfast or brunch across Middle Eastern and North African cuisines.

Quesabirria consists of flour or corn tortillas filled with shredded beef that has been braised in a rich consommé seasoned with dried chiles, and cheese, then typically pan-fried until crispy. The dish is traditionally served with the reserved consommé on the side as a dipping sauce.

Gulai tauco is an Indonesian curry dish made with meat or seafood simmered in a sauce of fermented soybean paste (tauco), coconut milk, and spices including turmeric, garlic, and chilies. The dish is typically served over rice as a main course and is common in Indonesian cuisine, particularly in regions of Sumatra and Java.

Bubur sada is an Indonesian rice porridge made by boiling rice with water until it reaches a soft, congee-like consistency, typically seasoned minimally with salt. It is commonly served as a breakfast dish or comfort food, often accompanied by side dishes such as fried shallots, eggs, or savory toppings.

Sorse begun is a Bengali dish in which eggplant (begun) is cooked in a mustard seed (sorse) paste, typically combined with green chilies, turmeric, and mustard oil to create a pungent, spiced preparation. It is commonly served as a side dish with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Tong bat lat is a Cantonese dessert consisting of small rice flour balls cooked in a sweet syrup, typically flavored with ginger or brown sugar. It is traditionally served warm as a concluding course in Cantonese meals or as a street-food snack.

Adrem is a traditional Indonesian kue made from a batter of grated coconut, rice flour, and palm sugar, shaped into small rounds or cones and deep-fried until crisp on the outside and chewy within. It is commonly served as a sweet snack alongside tea or coffee, and is often found at traditional markets and during festive occasions in regions such as Yogyakarta.

Kumro Chhenchki is a Bengali vegetable dish made with pumpkin or squash that is cut into pieces and cooked with mustard oil, nigella seeds, and green chilies until tender. The dish is typically served as a side curry alongside rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Chhanchi kumror ghonto is a Bengali vegetable dish made by stir-frying pumpkin or squash with onions, ginger, garlic, and spices until the vegetables break down into a thick, cohesive mixture. The dish is typically served as a side accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Mochar ghonto is a Bengali vegetable curry made primarily from banana flower buds combined with potatoes and other vegetables, cooked with onions, garlic, ginger, and spices. The dish is typically served as a side preparation with rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Aalu Chhenchki is a Bengali potato dish where potatoes are cut into small pieces and cooked with mustard oil, nigella seeds, and dried red chilies until golden and crispy on the edges. It is typically served as a side dish or accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Koi machher ghonto is a Bengali fish curry made by cooking shredded or minced koi fish with onions, tomatoes, and spices including turmeric, cumin, and coriander, resulting in a dry or semi-dry textured dish. It is traditionally served as a main course with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Doi diye kumror ghonto is a Bengali vegetable curry made with bottle gourd (kumro) cooked in a yogurt-based sauce seasoned with mustard oil, cumin, and spices. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: ilish machher matha diye lau ghonto
displayName: Ilish machher matha diye lau ghonto
aliases: ilish machher matha diye lau ghonto
description: This Bengali dish combines bottle gourd (lau) with ilish fish head (machher matha) in a preparation known as ghonto, which involves cooking vegetables and fish together with spices until well-integrated. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice and is common in Bengali cuisine, particularly during ilish fish season.

Moong phulkopir ghonto is a Bengali vegetable stir-fry combining sprouted mung beans with cauliflower, typically cooked with onions, garlic, ginger, and spices until the vegetables are tender. The dish is commonly served as a side accompaniment to rice and curry in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: katla machher matha diye lau ghonto
displayName: Katla machher matha diye lau ghonto
aliases: katla machher matha diye lau ghonto
description: This Bengali dish combines bottle gourd (lau) with catfish head (machher matha) and katla fish, slow-cooked together with spices to create a savory curry. It is traditionally served with steamed rice as part of Bengali cuisine and represents a common preparation method for utilizing fish heads in South Asian cooking.

Seddho dimer bhorta is a Bengali egg dish made by mashing boiled eggs with mustard oil, green chilies, and spices to create a coarse paste or mixture. It is typically served as a side dish or appetizer with rice or bread, particularly in Bengali cuisine.

A bun made from flour dough is filled with cooked ham and egg, typically prepared by baking or pan-frying the assembled pastry. This Brazilian sandwich is commonly served as a breakfast or light meal item, often consumed warm.

Pasta alla gricia is a Roman pasta dish made by tossing hot pasta with rendered guanciale (cured pork jowl), Pecorino Romano cheese, and black pepper, creating a creamy sauce from the fat and cheese without the addition of eggs or cream. It is traditionally served as a first course in Italian cuisine and is considered a precursor to carbonara.

Käsespätzle is a southern German dish made from spätzle noodles mixed with melted cheese, typically Emmental or similar Alpine varieties. The dish is traditionally topped with crispy fried onions and served as a main course or side dish in Bavarian and Swabian cuisine.

Spaghetti bolognese consists of spaghetti pasta served with bolognese sauce, a meat-based sauce traditionally made from ground beef, tomatoes, onions, and garlic, simmered to develop flavor. The dish is typically plated with the sauce distributed over the cooked spaghetti and garnished with parmesan cheese, commonly served as a main course.

Lalat satay is a Madurese variety of satay from Indonesia, made from small pieces of beef or mutton skewered on bamboo sticks and grilled over charcoal. It is typically served with sweet soy sauce, sliced shallots, and chili, accompanied by rice cake (lontong) or steamed rice.

Clot soup is a traditional Finnish dish prepared by simmering fresh animal blood, typically from freshly slaughtered livestock, until it coagulates into soft clots, which are then cooked in a broth seasoned with onions, salt, and spices. It is customarily served hot as a rustic main course, often accompanied by boiled potatoes or rye bread, and is associated with slaughtering traditions in rural Finland.

Spaghetti alla carrettiera is a Roman pasta dish made with spaghetti tossed with garlic, chili pepper, tomatoes, and sometimes anchovies or tuna, finished with olive oil. The dish is typically served as a main course and originated as a simple meal for cart drivers (carrettieri) in Rome.

Payesh is a rice pudding dessert made by cooking rice with milk and sweetening it with date palm jaggery, a traditional sweetener from India. The dish is typically served warm or chilled as a festive dessert, particularly during celebrations and special occasions in Indian cuisine.

Bagara khana is a rice dish from Hyderabadi cuisine where rice is cooked with meat (typically mutton or chicken), yogurt, and spices including saffron, cardamom, and bay leaves, with the meat often marinated before cooking. The dish is traditionally served as a complete meal, with the meat and rice cooked together in a sealed pot using the dum pukht (slow-cooking) method.

Mutabbaq Samak is a stuffed pastry made by folding thin dough around a filling of fish, herbs, and spices, then pan-frying until golden and crispy. It is typically served as an appetizer or light main course in Middle Eastern cuisine, often accompanied by lemon wedges or a yogurt-based sauce.

Instant rice is rice that has been pre-cooked and dehydrated, then rehydrated quickly by adding hot water or broth and allowing it to stand for a few minutes. It is commonly served as a side dish or base for grain bowls across various cuisines worldwide, offering convenience with minimal preparation time.

Barley rice is prepared by cooking pearl barley grains, which have been polished to remove the outer hull, using a cooking method similar to that of white rice. This grain dish is commonly served as a side or main course base in various international cuisines, offering a chewy texture and mild, slightly nutty flavor.

Rice and curry is a dish consisting of cooked rice served alongside or mixed with curry, a spiced sauce or stew made from vegetables, legumes, meat, or seafood. It is commonly prepared by cooking rice separately and serving it as an accompaniment to curry, which may be simmered with aromatic spices such as turmeric, cumin, and coriander, and is eaten as a main course in many cuisines worldwide.

Kashiwameshi is a Japanese rice dish made by cooking rice with chicken, vegetables, and dashi broth, typically seasoned with soy sauce and mirin. The dish is traditionally served in a clay pot or ceramic bowl, often as a main course at lunch or dinner.

canonicalName: pasta with mozzarella tomato and basil
displayName: pasta with mozzarella, tomato and basil
aliases: pasta with mozzarella tomato and basil
description: Fresh pasta is combined with melted mozzarella cheese, tomato sauce or fresh tomatoes, and basil leaves, typically finished with olive oil. This dish is served hot as a main course and represents a classic preparation from Italian cuisine emphasizing simple, quality ingredients.

Älplermagronen is a Swiss Alpine pasta dish made with egg noodles, potatoes, and cheese, typically layered or mixed together and baked until golden. It is traditionally served with a topping of crispy fried onions and a side of applesauce or tomato sauce, and originates from the Appenzell Alps region.

Arroz de marisco is a Portuguese rice dish prepared by cooking short-grain rice with seafood stock and a variety of shellfish such as clams, mussels, shrimp, and sometimes squid or crab, typically finished with white wine and garlic. The dish is traditionally served in individual portions as a main course, often garnished with fresh coriander and accompanied by lemon wedges.

Mianwo is a Hubei noodle dish made by mixing wheat noodles with a savory broth typically containing pork or other proteins, along with vegetables and seasonings. It is commonly served as a casual meal or street food in Hubei province, often enjoyed for breakfast or lunch.

Vangibath is a Karnataka rice dish prepared by cooking rice with roasted eggplant, coconut, and spices, then mixing them together to create a flavorful one-pot meal. It is typically served as a lunch or dinner dish, often accompanied by yogurt or pickle.

Basanti pulao is a Bengali rice dish prepared by cooking basmati rice with ghee, whole spices such as bay leaves and cinnamon, and typically includes potatoes and peas. The dish is traditionally served as a main course or side dish, often accompanied by meat curries or vegetable accompaniments in Bengali cuisine.

Hansakeli is a traditional Odia sweet prepared from chhena (fresh curdled milk cheese) kneaded with sugar and shaped into small dumplings, which are then simmered in thickened, cardamom-scented milk until soft. It is offered to Lord Jagannath as part of the Chhapan Bhog at the Jagannath Temple in Puri and is served as a dessert during religious and festive occasions.

Dolmades are grape leaves stuffed with a mixture of rice, herbs, and sometimes ground meat, then rolled tightly and cooked by steaming or simmering. They are typically served as an appetizer or meze dish, either warm or at room temperature, often accompanied by lemon wedges or a yogurt-based sauce.

Gajor er payesh is a Bengali rice pudding dessert made by cooking grated carrots with rice, milk, and jaggery or sugar until the mixture reaches a creamy consistency, often finished with ghee and cardamom. It is traditionally served warm as a festive dessert during celebrations and special occasions in Bengali cuisine.

Bhendi posto is an Indian vegetable dish made with okra (bhindi) cooked with a paste of poppy seeds (posto), onions, and spices. The dish is typically served as a side accompaniment to rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Alur chop is a Bengali potato fritter made by mashing boiled potatoes with spices, shaping the mixture into balls or patties, coating with breadcrumbs, and deep-frying until golden. It is typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by chutney or sauce.

Kumro fuler bora is a Bengali fritter made by dipping pumpkin flowers in a spiced chickpea flour batter and deep-frying until golden and crispy. It is typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by chutney or as part of a Bengali meal.

Kashmiri aloo chop is a fried potato cake prepared by mixing boiled and mashed potatoes with spices, formed into patties, coated in breadcrumbs, and deep-fried until golden. It is typically served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by tamarind chutney or mint sauce.

Potol er dolma is a Bengali dish made from pointed gourds (potol) that are hollowed out and stuffed with a spiced mixture of ground meat, lentils, or vegetables, then cooked in a light curry sauce. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread and represents a common preparation in Bengali cuisine where the vegetable serves as the primary vessel for the filling.

Misti kumror chop is a Bengali fritter made by mixing grated sweet pumpkin with spices and gram flour, then deep-frying until golden brown. It is typically served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanying tea or as part of a larger meal.

Potol alur torkari is a Bengali vegetable curry made by cooking pointed gourd (potol) and potatoes with onions, tomatoes, and spices including turmeric and cumin. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or flatbread as part of a Bengali meal.

Aloo korma is a vegetarian curry dish made with potatoes cooked in a spiced yogurt-based sauce, typically including onions, tomatoes, and aromatic spices such as cumin, coriander, and garam masala. It is commonly served as a main course with rice or bread in South Asian cuisine.

Bandhakopir dalna is a Bengali vegetable curry made primarily with cabbage that is cooked with spices, onions, and tomatoes until tender. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Dal dhokali is a Gujarati dish made by cooking steamed gram flour dumplings (dhokali) in a seasoned lentil curry base. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread, combining soft, dumpling-like pieces with a spiced lentil sauce.

Kumro bij er dalna is a Bengali lentil curry featuring roasted pumpkin seeds as the primary ingredient, cooked with lentils and spices to create a savory dal. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or flatbread as part of a main meal.

Kochu patar dalna is a Bengali curry made with colocasia leaves (kochu pata) cooked with lentils, onions, and spices including turmeric and chili. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or flatbread as part of a Bengali meal.

Bhaja muger dal is a Bengali dry lentil preparation made by roasting dried mung beans until they are crispy and golden, then typically seasoned with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and chili. It is commonly served as a crunchy side dish or snack alongside rice and curries in Bengali cuisine.

Chhana potol dolma is a Bengali dish made by stuffing pointed gourd (potol) with a mixture of paneer (chhana), spices, and sometimes lentils, then cooking it in a light curry or dry preparation. The dish is typically served as a side course or main vegetable preparation alongside rice or flatbread in Bengali cuisine.

Narkel diye chholar dal is a Bengali lentil curry made with split chickpeas (chhole) cooked with fresh coconut, onions, and spices until tender. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or flatbread in Bengali meals.

Cheypa sutki bhuna is a Bangladeshi fish dish prepared by slow-cooking dried shrimp (sutki) with onions, garlic, ginger, green chilies, and a blend of spices until the mixture reduces to a thick, aromatic paste. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice, often accompanied by lentils or vegetables.

Beondegi is a Korean street food made from silkworm pupae (Bombyx mori) that are typically boiled or steamed and seasoned with soy sauce, garlic, and spices. The dish is commonly served as a snack or side dish, either hot or at room temperature, and is known for its chewy texture and savory flavor profile.

Borscht with uszka is a traditional Ukrainian soup made with beets, vegetables, and beef or vegetable broth, typically served hot with sour cream and dark rye bread. Uszka are small filled dumplings with mushroom and onion stuffing that are boiled separately and added to the soup just before serving.

Thai street food encompasses a variety of quick, handheld dishes typically prepared fresh at outdoor stalls and markets, including items such as satay skewers, spring rolls, pad thai, and mango sticky rice, utilizing ingredients like rice noodles, fish sauce, chilies, lime, and grilled or fried proteins. These dishes are commonly eaten as casual meals, snacks, or light dinners and are served at room temperature or hot, often with accompanying dipping sauces and fresh herbs.

Bánh tráng nướng is a Vietnamese snack made from rice paper that is grilled or roasted over charcoal until crispy and slightly charred. It is typically seasoned with salt, sesame seeds, and dried herbs, then torn into pieces and served as a standalone snack or appetizer.

Magiritsa is a traditional Greek offal soup made from lamb or kid organs—typically liver, heart, lungs, and intestines—simmered with onions, herbs, and rice or orzo in a light broth. It is customarily served as a first course during Easter celebrations, particularly after the midnight liturgy, and is seasoned with lemon juice and fresh dill.

South Korean street food encompasses a variety of quick, handheld items prepared fresh at outdoor vendors and markets, featuring ingredients such as rice cakes, vegetables, seafood, and meat cooked through grilling, frying, or boiling. These foods are typically served hot and eaten casually while walking or standing, representing popular casual dining options throughout South Korea's streets and neighborhoods.

Pancotto is a rustic Italian soup made by simmering stale bread with water or broth, garlic, and olive oil until the bread breaks down into a porridge-like consistency. The dish is traditionally served hot as a humble, economical meal typical of cucina povera, often finished with a drizzle of quality olive oil and sometimes topped with grated cheese or fresh herbs.

Namaribushi is a fermented and dried fish product made from skipjack tuna that has been treated with koji mold and aged, resulting in a hard, shelf-stable block used as a flavoring ingredient. It is typically shaved into thin flakes and used to make dashi broth, sprinkled over dishes, or incorporated into sauces and seasonings in Japanese cuisine.

Crocchè are Sicilian fried croquettes made from mashed potatoes mixed with cheese, sometimes meat or vegetables, shaped into cylindrical or oval forms, and coated in breadcrumbs before deep frying until golden. They are typically served as a street food snack or appetizer, often eaten warm and sometimes accompanied by sauce for dipping.

Panelle are fritters made from chickpea flour mixed with water to form a batter, which is then fried until golden and crispy. Originating from Sicilian cuisine, they are typically served as a street food or appetizer, often seasoned with salt and sometimes served in a sandwich with bread.

Aarh macher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering fish in a light, aromatic broth made with onions, ginger, garlic, and spices including turmeric and cumin. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is a staple preparation in Bengali cuisine, valued for its mild, flavorful broth and tender fish pieces.

Koi machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made by simmering koi fish in a spiced broth with onions, tomatoes, and aromatic spices including turmeric and cumin. The dish is typically served as a main course with steamed rice or flatbread.

Jyanto rui machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with rohu fish cooked in a light, spiced broth typically prepared with onions, ginger, garlic, and turmeric. The dish is traditionally served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Bhetki macher dimer jhol is a Bengali curry made with Indian threadfish (bhetki) and fish eggs combined in a savory gravy base typically prepared with onions, tomatoes, and spices. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Bori diye boyal machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry in which fish is cooked with bori (sun-dried lentil balls) that have been fried until golden, along with onions, ginger-garlic paste, and spices in a light gravy. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Qirîs is a traditional Kurdish dish typically prepared with meat that is slow-cooked and often combined with nuts and dried or fresh fruit to create a rich, savory-sweet flavor profile common in the region. Machher dimer borar jhol is a Bengali curry made with fish eggs (roe) cooked in a spiced gravy base that typically includes onions, tomatoes, and aromatic spices such as turmeric, cumin, and mustard. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Tilapia machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering tilapia in a spiced broth made with onions, tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and Bengali spices including turmeric and chili. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is a staple preparation in Bengali cuisine.

Arayes are grilled or fried pita bread sandwiches filled with seasoned ground meat, onions, and spices, typically cooked until the exterior is crispy and the filling is heated through. Pona machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering fish in a light, aromatic broth made with onions, ginger, garlic, and spices, often finished with mustard oil and green chilies. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is characterized by its subtle flavors and soupy consistency.

This Bengali dish features ridge gourd and split lentils combined in a savory gravy, with the vegetables and lentils cooked together until tender and infused with spices. It is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or bread as part of a main meal.

Thankuni patar bora is a fried appetizer made by coating thankuni leaves (water hyssop) in a seasoned gram flour batter and deep-frying until crispy. The dish is commonly served as a snack or starter in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by chutneys or eaten on its own.

Kumror bhorta is a Bengali vegetable dish made by mashing roasted or cooked pumpkin with spices, onions, and green chilies until it reaches a coarse, uniform consistency. It is typically served as a side dish or accompaniment to rice and curry in Bengali cuisine.

Bobozee, also known as Abacha Mmiri or akpu mmiri, is a Nigerian snack made from cassava root that is sliced into chips and typically combined with coconut pieces and roasted peanuts. It is commonly served as a street food or casual snack, eaten by hand as a mix of the three ingredients.

Pagit-pagit is a traditional North Sumatran dish from the Batak Karo people, prepared by cooking the partially digested grass from the first stomach of a cow or buffalo with spices such as lemongrass, garlic, shallots, chilies, and andaliman pepper. It is typically served with rice at ceremonial gatherings and family feasts in the Karo Highlands region.

Phulkopi posto is a Bengali vegetable curry made with cauliflower cooked in a paste of ground poppy seeds, onions, and spices, creating a creamy sauce. The dish is typically served as a side course with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Jhinge narkoler torkari is a Bengali vegetable curry made with ridge gourd (jhinge) and grated coconut (narkol), cooked together with mustard oil, onions, and spices until tender. The dish is typically served as an accompaniment to rice or bread as part of a Bengali meal.

Koraishuntir dhokar dalna is a Bengali vegetarian curry made with lentil cakes (dhokar) made from ground lentils and spices, which are steamed and then cut into pieces before being cooked in a tomato-based gravy with peas (korai). The dish is traditionally served as a main course with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Chhana phulkopi is a Bengali dish made from fresh paneer (chhana) and cauliflower that are typically sautéed with spices and aromatics such as onions, ginger, and garlic. It is served as a vegetarian curry or side dish, often accompanied by rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Kumro phuler pur is a Bengali stuffed squash flower dish where pumpkin or squash blossoms are filled with a spiced mixture of vegetables, paneer, or lentils, then either fried or steamed. The dish is typically served as a vegetarian curry accompaniment to rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Kacha aamer chutney is a tangy condiment made from raw (unripe) green mangoes that are grated or chopped and mixed with spices such as mustard seeds, turmeric, and chili peppers, often with salt and sometimes jaggery. The chutney is typically served as an accompaniment to rice dishes, curries, and Indian breads to add sour and spicy flavors to meals.

Doi begun is a Bengali dish consisting of eggplant (begun) cooked in a yogurt-based sauce with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and fenugreek seeds. The eggplant is typically fried or roasted before being simmered in sweetened yogurt, and the dish is served as a side curry with rice or bread.

Bandhakopir bora is a Bengali fritter made by coating cabbage pieces in a spiced gram flour batter and deep-frying until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by chutney or served alongside meals in Indian cuisine.

Chholar daler bora is a fried appetizer made from roasted Bengal gram lentils (chholar dal) that are ground into a paste, mixed with spices, shaped into balls or patties, and deep-fried until golden. It is typically served as a snack or starter in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by tamarind chutney or other condiments.

Musur daler bora are fritters made from ground red lentils mixed with spices and seasonings, then deep-fried until golden and crispy. They are commonly served as a snack or appetizer in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by chutney or eaten on their own.

Alu Bhorta is a Bengali mashed potato dish prepared by boiling and mashing potatoes, then mixing with mustard oil, green chilies, onions, and spices. It is typically served as a side dish or condiment with rice and curry in Bengali cuisine.

Tamale pie is a casserole dish consisting of a savory filling topped with cornmeal batter or polenta that is baked until golden. The filling typically contains seasoned meat, vegetables, and chiles, with the cornmeal topping cooking in the oven to form a crust; it is served directly from the baking dish as a main course.

Khasta bora is a crispy fried snack from Bengali cuisine made with a pastry shell filled with spiced lentils or split peas. It is typically served as an appetizer or street food, often accompanying tea or as part of a meal spread.

Jhinge posto chorchori is a Bengali vegetable stir-fry featuring ridge gourd (jhinge) and a poppy seed paste (posto) base, typically cooked with mustard oil and spices. The dish is commonly served as a side accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Moong daler bora are fritters made from split yellow mung beans (moong dal) that are soaked, ground into a paste, mixed with spices and sometimes onions, then deep-fried until golden and crispy. These savory snacks are commonly served as appetizers or side dishes in Bengali and other South Asian cuisines, often accompanied by chutneys or eaten as street food.

Potol alu chorchori is a Bengali vegetable curry made by stir-frying pointed gourd (potol), potatoes, and other vegetables with spices including turmeric, cumin, and mustard seeds. The dish is typically served as a side accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Snow White salad is a Bulgarian dish prepared by combining strained yogurt with diced cucumbers, garlic, dill, and sometimes walnuts or vegetable oil. It is typically served chilled as a cold appetizer or side dish, often accompanying grilled meats or rakia.

Muchmuche amudi fry is a Bengali bhaja preparation in which small amudi fish are marinated with turmeric and salt, then shallow- or deep-fried until crisp. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice and dal as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Kumro shaker chorchori is a Bengali vegetable stir-fry dish made primarily with pumpkin (kumro) cut into thin slices or shreds, cooked with mustard oil, onions, and spices including turmeric and green chilies. The dish is typically served as a side vegetable accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Tangra machher chorchori is a Bengali stir-fried dish made with Tangra fish (a small catfish variety) combined with vegetables such as potatoes, eggplant, and beans, cooked with mustard oil and spices. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Bori diye kumro dantar chorchori is a Bengali vegetable curry prepared by cooking pumpkin and drumstick with dried lentil dumplings (bori) in a spiced onion and tomato base. The dish is traditionally served as a side accompaniment to rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: macher tel diye lal note shaager chorchori

Mola machher chorchori is a Bengali stir-fried preparation made with mola fish (a small freshwater fish) combined with various vegetables such as potatoes, eggplant, and leafy greens, seasoned with mustard oil and spices. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: ilish machher matha diye pui shaker chorchori
displayName: Ilish machher matha diye pui shaker chorchori

The fish head is typically sautéed with the leafy greens, onions, and basic seasonings, resulting in a humble, lightly cooked preparation commonly served as a side dish or main course with rice in Bengali households.

Panipuri consists of small hollow fried flour shells filled with boiled potatoes, chickpeas, and spices, then served with tamarind water and chili water for dipping. The dish is typically eaten as a street food snack or appetizer, with diners consuming each puri in one bite after dunking it in the flavored waters.

Falafel is prepared by grinding dried chickpeas and broad beans with spices including cumin, coriander, and mahleb, then forming the mixture into balls or patties that are deep-fried until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a popular street food and appetizer in Middle Eastern and international cuisine, often accompanied by tahini sauce, hummus, or served within pita bread with vegetables.

Tororo kombu is a Japanese seasoning made from kombu (kelp) that has been shredded or grated into fine, thread-like strips, often prepared by boiling and then drying. It is typically used as a garnish, flavoring agent, or ingredient in soups, rice dishes, and other preparations in Japanese and Asian cuisines.

Lokša is a traditional Central European potato pancake made from grated potatoes, flour, and salt, formed into a flat cake and fried until golden. It is typically served as a side dish or main course, often accompanied by sour cream, mushroom sauce, or other savory toppings.

Žemaičių blynai are potato pancakes from the Žemaitija region of Lithuania, made by mixing grated potato with minced meat and onion, then pan-frying until golden. These savory pancakes are typically served hot as a main course or appetizer, often accompanied by sour cream.

Bakso is an Indonesian soup dish made with seasoned ground meat (typically beef) formed into balls and simmered in a savory broth flavored with garlic, shallots, and spices. It is traditionally served hot in bowls with the meatballs, broth, and accompaniments such as noodles, tofu, or vegetables.

aliases: pommes de terre ridees des canaries
description: Canarian wrinkly potatoes are small potatoes boiled in salted water until tender, then served in their skins which develop a characteristic wrinkled appearance as they dry. The dish is typically accompanied by mojo sauce, a traditional Canarian condiment made from garlic, peppers, and oil, and is commonly served as a side dish or appetizer in Canarian cuisine.

Kun is a traditional Kurdish dish prepared by preserving goat meat, which is typically salted and dried to allow for long-term storage. It is commonly served as a protein staple in home-cooked meals, often incorporated into stews or rice-based dishes during colder months.

Mataz are small filled dumplings from Circassian cuisine, made by wrapping thin rounds of unleavened dough around a filling of minced meat, often beef or lamb seasoned with onion and spices, and then boiled until tender. They are typically served with a garlic-yogurt sauce or melted butter, often accompanied by the broth in which they were cooked.

Erdäpfelknödel are Austrian potato dumplings made from grated or mashed potatoes mixed with flour, eggs, and seasonings, then boiled until they float to the surface. They are typically served as a side dish with meat stews, sauerkraut, or other hearty main courses in Austrian cuisine.

Potato chips are thin slices of potato that are deep-fried in oil until crispy and golden brown, then typically seasoned with salt. They are commonly served as a snack or side dish, either on their own or with various flavor seasonings.

Fries are prepared by cutting potatoes into thin strips and deep-frying them in oil until golden and crispy on the outside while remaining soft inside. They are typically served hot as a side dish or snack, often seasoned with salt and accompanied by condiments such as ketchup, mayonnaise, or vinegar.

Bombay potato is an Indian dry curry dish made with potatoes that are diced and cooked with onions, tomatoes, and spices including turmeric, cumin, and chili powder until crispy and well-coated. It is typically served as a vegetable side dish alongside bread or rice in Indian cuisine.

Pataniscas are Portuguese fritters made with dried and salted cod that is desalted, shredded, and combined with a batter typically seasoned with parsley and peppercorn, then deep-fried until golden. These savory fritters are traditionally served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by lemon or a simple sauce.

Papa a la Huancaína is a Peruvian dish consisting of boiled potatoes covered with a creamy sauce made from huancaína peppers, queso fresco, evaporated milk, and ground peanuts or sesame seeds. The dish is typically served cold or at room temperature as an appetizer or light entrée, garnished with hard-boiled eggs, olives, and lettuce.

Bacalhau à Narcisa is a Portuguese dish made with desalted dried cod combined with sautéed onions and parsley, typically bound together and served with fried potatoes and olives. The preparation involves rehydrating and cooking the salt cod, then mixing it with the aromatic vegetables before plating alongside the crispy fries and garnish of olives.

Zeytinli açma is a Turkish pastry made from thin, layered dough that is rolled or folded and filled with olives, typically black olives, along with herbs and sometimes cheese. It is baked until golden and crispy, then served as a savory snack or appetizer, often accompanied by tea or as part of a Turkish breakfast spread.

Mlawi is a Moroccan pastry made by layering thin sheets of dough with butter or oil, then folding and rolling the layers to create a flaky, crispy texture. It is typically filled with savory ingredients such as meat, cheese, or vegetables, and is commonly served as a breakfast item or appetizer, often accompanied by honey for dipping.

Taboon bread is a round, flatbread traditionally baked in a taboon oven, a clay or stone barrel-shaped oven used throughout the Levantine region. The dough is typically made from flour, water, salt, and yeast, then pressed onto the hot interior walls of the oven where it bakes quickly until puffed and lightly charred, resulting in a bread with a soft interior and crispy exterior that is commonly served with mezze, grilled meats, or used for wrapping.

Focaccia novese is a Ligurian flatbread from Novi Ligure made with wheat flour, water, salt, and olive oil, characterized by a dimpled surface created by pressing fingers into the dough before baking. It is traditionally served as an appetizer or snack, often eaten plain or with savory toppings, and represents a regional variation of the broader focaccia tradition found throughout northern Italy.

Somarro is a grilled roast prepared from lamb or pork, typically seasoned and cooked over an open flame or grill until the exterior is charred and the interior remains tender. It is commonly served as a main course, often accompanied by bread, vegetables, or regional side dishes.

Bêran is a traditional Kurdish dish prepared by cooking meat together with rice, typically simmered until the flavors combine and the rice absorbs the juices from the meat. It is commonly served as a main course at family meals and on festive or communal occasions.

Cemita is a traditional Mexican sandwich made with a round, seeded bread roll from Puebla that is baked until golden and crispy. It is typically filled with ingredients such as breaded meat (usually breaded steak or chicken), avocado, cheese, onions, and jalapeños, served as a popular street food and casual meal throughout Mexico.

Korean mochi breads are soft, chewy baked goods made from glutinous rice flour (mochiko) mixed with wheat flour, sugar, and often filled with sweet ingredients such as red bean paste, custard, or fruit. These breads are typically served as a snack or dessert and are characterized by their light, pillowy texture that results from the combination of rice flour and standard bread-making techniques.

Malaxlimpa is a Swedish rye bread made with rye flour, traditionally kneaded (malax) to develop its characteristic dense texture and dark color. The bread is typically served sliced as part of Swedish smörgåsbord spreads or alongside cheese and cured meats.

Papad is a thin, crispy Indian wafer made from lentil flour (typically urad dal), rice flour, or potato starch mixed with spices, then dried in the sun or mechanically. It is typically deep-fried or roasted over an open flame until it puffs and crisps, then served as a crunchy appetizer or accompaniment to main courses.

Tabouna bread is a Moroccan flatbread traditionally baked in a clay oven called a tabouna, resulting in a soft, pillowy texture with a slightly charred exterior. The dough is made from flour, water, and salt, and the bread is commonly served warm as an accompaniment to meals or used to scoop up tagines and other dishes.

Festival is a fried cornmeal dumpling made from a batter of cornmeal, wheat flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar, mixed with water and deep-fried until golden. It is commonly served as a breakfast or snack item, often alongside savory dishes or eaten on its own as a sweet treat in Caribbean cuisine.

aliases: pizza all interno di un tegame
description: Deep-dish pizza is prepared by pressing dough into a deep, oiled pan to create a thick, cake-like crust, then layering cheese, toppings, and tomato sauce, typically baked at high temperature until the crust is golden and crispy. It is traditionally served cut into squares or wedges and eaten with a fork and knife due to its substantial depth and filling.

Bolo do caco is a traditional Portuguese flatbread from Madeira made with wheat flour, sweet potato, and butter, cooked on a griddle or hot stone until golden. It is typically served warm, often split and filled with garlic butter or cheese, as a side dish or snack.

A slice of bread is a single portion cut from a loaf of bread, typically made from flour, water, salt, and yeast that are mixed, kneaded, and baked until golden. It is commonly served as an accompaniment to meals, used as a base for sandwiches, or eaten on its own, often with butter, spreads, or dips.

Mosbolletjies are small, sweet yeast buns from South Africa that are prepared by baking a lightly spiced dough containing flour, butter, sugar, eggs, and often aniseed or other flavoring agents. These buns are typically served warm as a breakfast item or tea-time treat, traditionally accompanied by butter or jam.

Bica de azeite is a Portuguese pastry made from choux dough that is piped into elongated shapes and deep-fried until golden, then drizzled with olive oil and typically dusted with sugar or cinnamon. It is commonly served as a sweet snack or dessert, often enjoyed with coffee or as part of traditional Portuguese bakery offerings.

Hallulla is a Chilean sweet bread made with wheat flour, butter, and sugar, typically shaped into a flat oval loaf with a characteristic split or crease down the center. It is commonly served warm for breakfast or as an afternoon snack, often accompanied by cheese or eaten plain.

Pain aux noix is a French bread made with a basic dough enriched with walnuts mixed throughout or folded into the dough before baking. It is typically served as a breakfast bread or accompaniment to meals, sliced and eaten plain or with butter and cheese.

Pão de Mistura de Seia is a traditional Portuguese bread made from a mixture of rye and wheat flours, water, salt, and yeast, typically baked until golden. This rustic loaf originates from the Seia region and is commonly served as an accompaniment to meals or used for traditional Portuguese sandwiches.
Doxave is a Kurdish dish prepared by cooking meat with chickpeas (Cicer arietinum) in a seasoned broth until tender. It is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by rice or bread.

Derf is a traditional Kurdish dish prepared with goat meat, typically slow-cooked to tenderize the meat and develop flavor. It is commonly served as a main course in communal meals, often accompanied by bread or rice.

Alentejano bread is a traditional Portuguese bread made from wheat flour, water, salt, and sourdough starter, characterized by its dense crumb and thick crust resulting from slow fermentation and baking in wood-fired or traditional ovens. This rustic bread is commonly served as an accompaniment to meals throughout the Alentejo region and pairs particularly well with soups, stews, and local cheeses.

A circular loaf of bread is made by shaping dough into a round form and baking it until the crust is golden brown. It is typically served as a staple bread item, sliced and eaten with meals, or used as a base for sandwich preparations.

Nazook is an Armenian pastry made by rolling sheets of phyllo dough with a filling of ground nuts (typically walnuts or pistachios) mixed with sugar and spices, then baking until golden and crispy. The baked pastry is traditionally cut into diamond or square pieces and often brushed with simple syrup or honey after baking, creating a sweet, flaky dessert served at celebrations and special occasions.

Pastel de feijão is a baked pastry made with a filling of seasoned beans, typically black beans or kidney beans, wrapped in a pastry dough or bread crust. This Portuguese dish is commonly served as a savory appetizer, snack, or side dish, either hot or at room temperature.

Viennese nougat is made by whipping egg whites with powdered sugar, then folding in chopped nuts (typically hazelnuts or almonds) and sometimes chocolate, creating a light, airy confection that is set between wafer layers. The candy is traditionally sliced into bars and served as a sweet treat or dessert, often enjoyed with coffee or as a gift.

Pastissets menorquins are small fried pastries from Menorca filled with meat, typically pork or beef, mixed with spices and sometimes pine nuts or raisins. They are traditionally served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by alioli sauce.
