Gujia is a crescent-shaped pastry made by filling a dough with khoya (reduced milk solids), dry fruits, and coconut, then deep-frying until golden brown. It is traditionally prepared and served during the festival of Holi in North India, often enjoyed with milk or rabri (thickened sweetened milk).

Sitabhog is a Bengali sweet made by combining chhena (fresh cheese curds) with sugar and rice flour, then shaping the mixture into small balls or cylindrical forms. It is typically served as a dessert or offered during festivals and special occasions in Bengali cuisine.

Musk sticks are cylindrical sweets made from a hard candy base flavored with musk-scented compounds, typically colored pink or red, and coated with a fine sugar dust. They are traditionally served as confectionery candies and have been produced since the early 20th century, particularly popular in Australia and New Zealand.

Ledikeni is an Indian sweet made by cooking chhena (fresh cheese curds) with milk and sugar until it reaches a fudgy consistency, then formed into round balls or logs. The confection is typically served as a dessert or sweet treat, often presented plain or occasionally finished with a coating of dry fruits or condensed milk.

Cucur cake is an Indonesian snack made from rice flour and palm sugar, typically formed into small, flat cakes and deep-fried until golden brown. It is commonly served as a sweet treat or breakfast item, often enjoyed with tea or coffee.

Narkeler naru is a Bengali confection made from freshly grated coconut mixed with jaggery or sugar, then shaped into balls or cylindrical forms. The mixture is typically cooked gently to bind the ingredients together and is commonly served as a sweet treat during festivals and special occasions in Bengal.

Bjawia is a North African confection made by combining roasted nuts—typically almonds, walnuts, hazelnuts, and pistachios—which are then bound together, often with honey or other sweeteners, and sometimes formed into pressed blocks or balls. The mixture is traditionally served as a sweet treat or dessert, commonly enjoyed during celebrations and special occasions in Moroccan and Algerian cuisines.

Rold Gold Tiny Twists are small pretzel snacks made from wheat flour, salt, and vegetable oil, baked until crispy. These bite-sized twisted pretzels are typically served as a standalone snack or accompaniment to beverages.

Rold Gold Tiny Twists Cheddar are small pretzel snacks made from twisted wheat flour dough that is baked and coated with cheddar cheese flavoring. These bite-sized pretzels are typically consumed as a ready-to-eat snack or appetizer.

Gulûl is a Kurdish dish prepared by cooking rice, maize, or bulgur and combining it with yogurt or ayran to create a porridge-like consistency. It is typically served as a rustic home-style meal, often eaten warm as part of traditional Kurdish cuisine.

Mannheimer Dreck is a traditional German dessert from Mannheim consisting of a pastry base layered with chocolate cream and topped with chocolate shavings or cocoa powder. The dish is typically served as a sweet treat or dessert in cafes and bakeries throughout the Baden-Württemberg region.

Die Plinse is a traditional German pancake made from a batter of flour, eggs, and milk, typically cooked on a griddle or frying pan until golden brown on both sides. It is commonly served as a breakfast or dessert dish, often topped with jam, powdered sugar, or other sweet accompaniments.

A puddingbroodje is a Dutch pastry consisting of a soft bread roll filled with pastry cream and topped with powdered sugar. It is typically served as a sweet snack or dessert, often eaten fresh from a bakery.

Chebakia is a Moroccan pastry made from dough that is shaped into a flower-like form, deep-fried, and then coated with honey and sesame seeds. The dish is traditionally served during Ramadan as a festive sweet treat, often accompanied by hot beverages.

Basler Läckerli is a traditional Swiss spiced gingerbread cookie made from a dough containing honey, flour, spices such as cinnamon and cloves, and candied fruits and nuts, then baked until firm. The baked cookies are typically glazed with a thin icing and cut into rectangular pieces, traditionally served as a festive treat or gift during the Christmas season in Basel and surrounding regions.

Julekake is a sweet yeast bread made with flour, butter, milk, sugar, and yeast, enriched with cardamom, raisins, and candied citrus peel (succade). This traditional Scandinavian bread is typically served sliced during the Christmas season, often toasted and buttered.

Julienne is a Russian appetizer consisting of mushrooms, chicken, or seafood mixed with a creamy béchamel or sour cream sauce, typically topped with melted cheese and breadcrumbs, then baked in individual scallop shells or small dishes. It is served hot as a first course or light appetizer, often accompanied by bread for scooping.

Devils on horseback are dried fruits, typically prunes or dates, wrapped in bacon and then roasted or grilled until the bacon is crisp. This British appetizer is traditionally served hot as a starter or party snack, often with the bacon providing a savory contrast to the sweet fruit.

Mlabes are a North African confection made by whipping egg whites with sugar and almonds into a light, airy mixture that is then baked or dried. These sweet treats are traditionally served as a dessert or petit four, often enjoyed with tea or coffee.

Samosas are triangular pastries made from wheat flour dough that is filled with spiced meat, peas, and potatoes, then deep-fried until golden and crispy. They are commonly served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by chutneys or tamarind sauce for dipping.

Spanakopita is a Greek savory pie made with phyllo pastry layers alternating with a filling of spinach, feta cheese, and onions, then baked until the pastry turns golden and crisp. It is typically served as an appetizer, main course, or part of a mezze spread, either warm or at room temperature.

Vatrushka is a Russian pastry consisting of a yeast dough base topped with a filling of tvorog (curd cheese), typically sweetened with sugar and sometimes enriched with eggs or sour cream. The pastry is baked until golden and is commonly served as a breakfast dish or sweet snack, often enjoyed warm with tea or sour cream.

Szarlotka is a Polish apple cake made with a yeast or shortcrust pastry base filled with spiced apples, typically cinnamon and sugar, and often topped with streusel or additional pastry layers. It is traditionally served as a dessert or with coffee and tea, either warm or at room temperature.

Echaude is a French pastry made from choux dough that is poached in water or broth before being baked, resulting in a light and airy texture. Traditionally served as a sweet pastry, often dusted with sugar or filled with cream, and typically enjoyed as a dessert or breakfast item.

Navoşk is a Kurdish dish prepared by combining minced meat with finely chopped onion and garlic, typically shaped and cooked together to form a savory preparation. It is commonly served as a main course, often accompanied by bread or rice in traditional Kurdish meals.

Hêkerûn is a Kurdish omelette prepared by frying beaten eggs in oil or clarified butter, often combined with ingredients such as onions, tomatoes, or herbs. It is typically served as a breakfast dish alongside flatbread, cheese, and tea.

Bamiyeh is a stewed dish made with okra pods that are typically blanched before being combined with tomatoes, onions, and garlic in a savory broth, often with lamb or beef. The dish is served hot as a main course, typically accompanied by rice or bread to absorb the sauce.

Coulibiac is a Russian pastry dish consisting of a filleted fish (typically salmon) combined with duxelles, hard-boiled eggs, and sometimes kasha or rice, all encased in brioche dough and baked until golden. It is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by a rich sauce such as beurre blanc or hollandaise.

A Swedish chocolate confection made by mixing butter, sugar, cocoa powder, rolled oats, and grated coconut into a paste, which is then formed into balls and typically coated with chocolate. Served as a petit four or candy, often at coffee time or as a dessert accompaniment.

Polvoron is a Spanish shortbread cookie made by combining flour, sugar, milk, and ground almonds into a crumbly dough, then baking until lightly golden. The cookies are typically served as a teatime treat or dessert, often dusted with powdered sugar and known for their delicate, crumbly texture.

Kogel mogel is a dessert made by beating egg yolks with powdered sugar until thick and creamy, sometimes with added flavorings such as cocoa, rum, or fruit. The dish is typically served chilled as a sweet treat or light dessert, with origins in Jewish cuisine and popular across Central and Eastern Europe.

Gulgula is a deep-fried Indian sweet made from wheat flour mixed with jaggery and other spices, shaped into small round balls or knots. These are typically served as a traditional dessert or snack during festivals and special occasions.

Mazamorra is a thick porridge made from cornmeal that is cooked with water or broth until it reaches a dense, spoonable consistency, often finished with cheese, butter, or meat. The dish is traditionally served as a savory side dish or main course in Spanish cuisine, particularly in regional preparations where it accompanies stews or is eaten on its own.

Chichinga is a grilled meat skewer dish made by marinating meat in suya spice blend—a mixture of ground peanuts, chili peppers, and spices—then threading it onto skewers and grilling over charcoal. The dish is typically served as a street food appetizer or snack, often accompanied by sliced onions and tomatoes.

Fried ice cream is prepared by coating a ball of ice cream with a crispy batter or breading, then deep frying it briefly at high temperature until the exterior becomes golden and crisp while the interior remains frozen. It is typically served immediately while still warm on the outside and cold on the inside, often accompanied by sweet toppings such as cinnamon sugar, chocolate sauce, or fruit compote.

Bingsu is a Korean shaved ice dessert prepared by topping finely shaved ice with sweetened condensed milk, fruit, and various toppings such as red beans, mochi, or cereal. It is typically served in a bowl and consumed as a cold dessert, especially during warm weather.

Zwetschkenröster is an Austrian dish made from plums that are roasted or baked, typically with sugar and spices such as cinnamon. It is traditionally served as a dessert or accompaniment to meat dishes, often with a dollop of sour cream or vanilla sauce.

Suji ka halwa is a semolina-based dessert made by roasting semolina in ghee, then cooking it with sugar, water or milk, and aromatics like cardamom until it reaches a thick, pudding-like consistency. It is traditionally served warm as a sweet dish during festivals, celebrations, and special occasions across India and neighboring regions.

Thirteen desserts is a traditional Provençal French Christmas selection consisting of thirteen different sweet items, typically including nougat, candied fruits, nuts, dried fruits, and local specialties like calissons and panettone. The assortment is served as a festive finale to Christmas Eve dinner, with the specific thirteen items varying by family tradition and region.

Café gourmand is a French dessert course consisting of multiple small sweet preparations served together, typically including miniature pastries, chocolates, macarons, and other confections. It is traditionally served with coffee at the end of a meal as an alternative to a single large dessert.

Köje is a traditional Uyghur, Kyrgyz, and Kazakh congee prepared by slowly simmering grains such as wheat, millet, barley, or rice in water or broth until they break down into a thick porridge, often with the addition of meat, beans, or dairy depending on regional variation. It is typically served hot as a warming communal dish, especially during colder months, holidays, and family gatherings.

A cobbler is a baked dessert made with fruit filling topped with a thick batter or biscuit topping that cooks to a golden crust. It is typically served warm, often with custard or cream, and is common in British home cooking and traditional restaurants.

Roti jala is a Malaysian crepe made from a batter of flour, eggs, and coconut milk that is poured through a special perforated ladle onto a hot griddle, creating an intricate lacy net-like pattern. It is typically served warm with curry, sambal, or condensed milk as an accompaniment, commonly eaten for breakfast or as a light meal.

Turrón de Agramunt is a nougat confection made from honey, sugar, egg whites, and roasted almonds, traditionally produced in Agramunt, Catalonia. The mixture is beaten until light and airy, then set and cut into bars, typically served as a sweet treat or dessert.

Sebada is a Sardinian fried pastry dessert consisting of two layers of thin dough enclosing a filling of melted cheese (typically pecorino romano or a similar hard cheese) mixed with honey or lemon zest. It is served warm, typically dusted with powdered sugar and drizzled with additional honey.

Bananas Foster is prepared by cooking sliced bananas in a sauce of butter, brown sugar, cinnamon, and rum or banana liqueur until caramelized, then typically flambéed tableside. The dish is traditionally served warm over vanilla ice cream, where the hot caramelized sauce creates a contrast with the cold ice cream.

Ciğer kebap is a Turkish grilled liver dish where seasoned liver pieces are threaded onto skewers and cooked over an open flame until the exterior develops a charred crust while the interior remains tender. The kebap is typically served hot with grilled onions, tomatoes, and peppers, accompanied by flatbread and herbs like parsley.

Givré is a frozen dessert made by freezing a mixture of fruit puree or juice with sugar and sometimes egg white, then serving it in the hollowed-out fruit skin from which it was made. The preparation creates a sorbet-like frozen confection traditionally presented in its original fruit shell, commonly served as a palate cleanser or dessert course.

Mantecadas de Astorga are small, individual sponge cakes from the Castilian city of Astorga, made with flour, butter, eggs, and sugar, traditionally baked in paper molds that give them their characteristic ridged appearance. These sweet pastries are typically served as a dessert or afternoon treat, often consumed with coffee or chocolate.

Cranachan is a Scottish dessert made by layering whipped cream with toasted oatmeal, fresh raspberries, and honey, often finished with a drizzle of whisky. It is traditionally served chilled as a dessert course, particularly popular during summer months when raspberries are in season.

Cartellate are fried pastry ribbons from Puglia, Italy, made by rolling thin dough into spiral or rose-like shapes and deep-frying until golden and crispy. They are traditionally finished with honey or vincotto (cooked grape must) and served as a dessert or sweet treat, particularly during festive occasions.

Quindim is a Brazilian custard dessert made from egg yolks, sugar, and grated coconut, typically baked in a water bath until set with a caramelized top and creamy interior. The dessert is traditionally served at room temperature as a sweet course, often presented in individual portions or as part of a dessert spread.

Baked Alaska consists of a layer of ice cream placed on top of a cake base, covered with meringue, and briefly baked or torched at high temperature until the meringue is browned while the ice cream remains frozen inside. This dessert is typically served cold and whole, often flambéed tableside in restaurant settings.

Zabaione is an Italian custard dessert made by whisking egg yolks with sugar over gentle heat, typically with the addition of Marsala wine, until it reaches a light and fluffy consistency. It is traditionally served warm as a dessert on its own, or used as an accompaniment to fresh fruit, pastries, and other desserts.

Natas do Céu is a Portuguese pastry dessert made with layers of crispy cookie or pastry filled with a rich cream made from doce de ovos (a traditional egg yolk and sugar confection). It is typically served as an individual cake or small pastry, often enjoyed with coffee or as a dessert course in Portuguese restaurants.

Orelletes are fried pastry treats from Valencian cuisine, made by deep-frying thin strips of dough that are often twisted or shaped into decorative forms, then dusted with powdered sugar. Traditionally served as a dessert or sweet snack, particularly during festive occasions and celebrations in Valencia.

Ora (Oha) soup is a traditional Nigerian dish from the South Eastern region, prepared by cooking Ora leaves with ingredients such as palm oil, assorted meats or fish, cocoyam paste as a thickener, and seasonings like crayfish and pepper. It is typically served hot alongside swallows such as fufu, pounded yam, or garri as part of a main meal.

Baci di dama are small Italian hazelnut cookies made from flour, butter, sugar, and ground hazelnuts, formed into balls and typically dipped in chocolate on one end. These Piedmont-origin treats are traditionally served as petit fours or with coffee and are characterized by their delicate, crumbly texture.

Chingrir kebab is a Bengali dish made from shrimp that is marinated in spices and then grilled or pan-fried on skewers. It is typically served as an appetizer or main course accompaniment with rice or bread, common in Bengali cuisine and restaurants serving South Asian food.

Chicken reshmi kebab is prepared by marinating boneless chicken pieces in a creamy mixture of yogurt, cream, and spices, then threading them onto skewers and grilling or cooking in a tandoor until tender. The dish is typically served with sliced onions, lemon wedges, and mint chutney as accompaniments.
A completo is a Chilean sandwich made by layering mayonnaise, Vienna sausage, avocado, and tomato on bread. It is typically served as a casual street food or light meal throughout Chile.

White Christmas is a no-bake confection made by combining condensed milk, coconut, and dried fruits such as raisins, then setting the mixture in a tray before cutting into squares. This Australian treat is typically served as a festive sweet during the Christmas season and can be eaten plain or coated with chocolate.

Semiya payasam is a South Indian dessert made by roasting vermicelli in ghee until golden brown, then simmering it with milk, jaggery or sugar, and cardamom until the noodles soften and absorb the sweet liquid. The dish is typically served warm as a festive dessert during celebrations and religious occasions, often garnished with cashews and raisins that have been fried in ghee.

Quesada Pasiega is a traditional Spanish cheese made from cow's milk, originating from the Picos de Europa region in Asturias. The cheese is typically aged and served as a table cheese or used in regional dishes, characterized by its creamy texture and mild flavor.

Certosino is an Italian cake made with honey, dried fruits, and nuts that are bound together and typically topped with a dark chocolate or icing glaze. The mixture is baked into a dense, cake-like loaf and traditionally served sliced as a dessert or sweet treat, particularly during festive occasions and Christmas.

Suspiro de limeña is a traditional Peruvian dessert consisting of a layer of dulce de leche topped with a meringue made from egg whites and sugar, sometimes finished with a sprinkle of cinnamon. The dish is typically served chilled or at room temperature as a sweet course or accompaniment to coffee and tea.

Neccio is a traditional Tuscan crepe made from chestnut flour batter cooked on a hot griddle or specialized pan until golden and crispy. It is typically served warm, either plain, folded with cheese and herbs, or filled with sweet or savory ingredients, and represents a staple preparation from the Lunigiana region of Italy.

Quaresimali are traditional Italian almond cookies made with ground almonds, sugar, and egg whites, formed into elongated shapes and baked until firm. These biscuits are typically served as a sweet treat during Lent or as a pastry accompaniment to coffee or hot beverages.

Schiaccia briaca is a traditional Tuscan flatbread made from a simple dough of flour, water, salt, and olive oil, typically studded with rosemary and coarse salt before baking. It is served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by cured meats or cheese, and is characterized by its crispy exterior and soft, oil-rich interior.

Testa di moro is a Sicilian pastry consisting of a chocolate-covered shell made from marzipan (almond paste) that is typically filled with ricotta cheese and candied fruits. The dessert is traditionally served as a sweet treat or gift, particularly during festive occasions and is recognizable by its round shape designed to resemble a Moorish head, which is the origin of its name.

Zelten is a traditional Italian fruit bread from the Trentino-Alto Adige region, made with a yeast dough studded with dried fruits, nuts, and spices including anise, then baked until golden. It is typically sliced and served as a festive dessert or breakfast bread, particularly during the Christmas season.

Peanut pie is a dessert made with a pastry crust filled with a mixture of peanuts, sugar, and butter, typically baked until the filling is set. The pie is served at room temperature or chilled and is common in American and Southern cuisine.

Sanga is a traditional Cameroonian dish prepared by combining fresh corn kernels with leafy vegetables, typically cassava or huckleberry leaves, and simmered with palm oil until the mixture thickens. It is commonly served as a main course at family gatherings and communal meals.

Martino is a Belgian sandwich made with raw minced beef (filet américain) spread on a baguette or soft roll, typically seasoned with Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco, anchovies, capers, onions, pickles, and mustard. It is commonly served cold as a quick lunch or snack in Belgian sandwich shops and cafés.

Cassatelle are deep-fried Sicilian pastries typically filled with ricotta cheese, candied fruits, and chocolate pieces, then dusted with powdered sugar. These tube-shaped or rolled pastries are traditionally served as a dessert or sweet treat, particularly during festive occasions and celebrations.

Ciambellino is a ring-shaped Italian baked good made from a simple dough of flour, eggs, sugar, and butter, often flavored with anise or lemon zest. It is typically served as a breakfast pastry or snack, sometimes dunked in coffee or hot chocolate.

Ravioli dolci are Italian sweet ravioli made with a pasta dough filled with ingredients such as ricotta cheese, candied fruits, nuts, and sugar, then typically sealed and boiled or fried. They are traditionally served as a dessert, often dusted with powdered sugar or accompanied by a light sauce.

Cupeta is an Italian nougat confection made by combining toasted nuts, honey, and egg whites, then typically setting the mixture in molds or on flat surfaces to harden. This traditional sweet is served as a dessert or confectionery treat, often cut into pieces and sometimes dusted with powdered sugar or cocoa.

Rice Krispies is a breakfast cereal made from rice grains that are cooked, dried, and toasted to create crispy, light pieces. It is typically served cold in a bowl with milk and is commonly eaten as a breakfast dish or light snack.

Krówki are Polish fudge candies made by boiling sweetened condensed milk with butter and sugar until thickened, then cooled and cut into small cubes. These milk fudge sweets are typically served as a dessert or confection, often enjoyed with coffee or tea.

Flaki is a traditional Polish tripe soup made from beef stomach lining cut into strips and simmered in a rich broth seasoned with marjoram, garlic, and other spices. It is typically served hot as a main course or hearty appetizer, often accompanied by bread or served with sour cream and fresh dill.

Hueso de santo is a Spanish pastry consisting of a cylindrical tube of crispy fried dough filled with sweet cream or custard, typically dusted with powdered sugar. This traditional dessert is commonly served during All Saints' Day (Día de Todos los Santos) and is eaten as a standalone sweet or with hot chocolate.

Boondi is made by pouring besan (chickpea flour) batter through a perforated ladle into hot oil, creating small spherical droplets that are fried until golden. The fried beads are then typically coated with a sugar syrup and served as a sweet snack or dessert in Indian cuisine.

A mimosa sugar ball is a confection made by coating a ball of sponge cake or similar base with a layer of finely shredded coconut and sugar mixture. It is typically served as a dessert or petit four, often accompanying coffee or tea.

Yanagawa nabe is a Japanese hot pot dish that combines diced eel, burdock root, and egg simmered together in a dashi-based broth, traditionally prepared in a clay pot at the table. The dish is typically served as a warming one-pot meal with rice and is named after the Yanagawa region in Fukuoka Prefecture, where it originated.

Maritozzo is an Italian choux pastry bun filled with sweetened whipped cream, traditionally prepared by splitting the light, airy pastry and generously filling it with freshly whipped cream flavored with sugar and sometimes vanilla. It is typically served as a dessert or snack, often enjoyed with coffee or as a sweet treat in Italian bakeries and cafés.

Mustacciuoli are Italian almond-based cookies made from flour, almonds, honey, and spices, shaped into elongated forms and typically glazed or decorated. These confections are traditionally prepared during festive occasions and the Christmas season, particularly in Southern Italy.

Cadbury Roses is a assorted chocolate confectionery product containing multiple chocolate pieces with various fillings including caramel, nougat, and fondant, individually wrapped in colored foils. The selection is typically served as a shared chocolate assortment for gifting or special occasions.

Chhena jalebi is an Indian sweet made from chhena (fresh cheese curds) that is shaped into coils, deep-fried until golden, and then soaked in sugar syrup infused with cardamom and saffron. It is typically served as a dessert or festive sweet, often during celebrations and special occasions.

Meulemeester eggs is a gratin made with gray shrimps marinated in beer and hard-boiled eggs, bound in a sauce and baked until the top is golden. It is typically served as a warm starter or light main course in Belgian cuisine.

Doi mouri parse is a Bengali fish dish in which parse (mullet) fish is cooked in a yogurt-based (doi) gravy, typically seasoned with mustard oil, ginger, green chilies, and a blend of spices. It is generally served as a main course alongside steamed rice in traditional Bengali meals.

The Jos. Louis is a Canadian snack cake consisting of two sponge cake layers with a creamy filling sandwiched between them, typically coated in chocolate. It is commonly eaten as a dessert or snack and is traditionally served at room temperature.

Mishti doi is a sweetened yogurt dessert from Bengal made by fermenting milk that has been reduced and sweetened with caramelized sugar or jaggery, then set in traditional clay pots. The dish is typically served chilled as a dessert after meals or as a standalone sweet treat.

Castagnole are small fried pastry balls made from a simple dough of flour, eggs, butter, and sugar, often flavored with rum or marsala wine and lemon zest. Traditionally served during Carnival season in Italy, they are dusted with powdered sugar and eaten as a festive dessert or pastry.

Malban is a Lebanese confection made by mixing nougat with pistachios and then rolling or pressing the mixture into thin sheets. The preparation is typically finished by cutting into small pieces or rolling into logs, and it is commonly served as a sweet treat or dessert.

Hornazo is a Spanish filled pastry made from bread dough that encloses a savory filling of cured meats, typically chorizo and ham, along with hard-boiled eggs and sometimes cheese. It is traditionally prepared for Easter and other celebrations, often served sliced either warm or at room temperature as a substantial snack or light meal.

Fudge is a confection made by cooking sugar, butter, and milk or cream to the soft-ball stage, then beating the mixture as it cools to create a smooth, dense candy with a crystalline structure. It is typically cut into small squares and served as a sweet treat or dessert, often flavored with chocolate, vanilla, or nuts.

Barfi is a dense, fudge-like confection made by reducing milk solids with sugar and ghee, then setting the mixture in a pan and cutting it into squares or diamonds. It is typically flavored with cardamom, nuts, or coconut, and served as a sweet at festivals, celebrations, and as a general dessert across Indian cuisine.

Sapin-sapin is a Filipino layered cake made from glutinous rice flour, coconut milk, and brown sugar, with each layer traditionally colored and flavored differently using ingredients like pandan, ube, or jackfruit. The cake is steamed in banana leaves and typically served as a dessert or merienda (afternoon snack), often cut into small squares and topped with grated coconut.

Misto-quente is a Portuguese grilled sandwich made with ham, cheese, and sometimes other fillings placed between sliced bread, then pressed and heated on a griddle or panini press until the bread is toasted and the cheese melts. It is typically served as a quick lunch or casual meal, often accompanied by a beverage.

Tlačenka is a traditional Central European meat dish made by cooking pork offal, meat scraps, and sometimes blood with spices, then pressing the mixture into a loaf form to set. It is typically served cold or at room temperature, sliced thin, and eaten as an appetizer, snack, or part of a charcuterie board.

Bisciola is a traditional Italian sweet bread from Lombardy made with a yeasted dough mixed with dried fruits, nuts, and sometimes candied citrus peel. It is typically baked into a loaf and served sliced as a breakfast bread or dessert, often accompanied by coffee or wine.

Menudo is a traditional Mexican stew made with beef tripe and hominy, simmered in a rich broth seasoned with dried chiles, garlic, and spices until the tripe is tender. It is typically served hot in bowls with lime wedges, onion, and oregano on the side, commonly consumed as a breakfast dish or as a remedy for hangovers.

Chaogan is a Chinese stir-fried noodle dish made with fried wheat noodles tossed with vegetables, eggs, and protein such as shrimp or chicken in a savory sauce. It is typically served as a main course in Chinese cuisine and street food contexts.

Tulumba is a fried pastry dessert made by piping elongated strips of choux dough into hot oil until golden and crispy. The fried pastry is then soaked in or drizzled with sugar syrup and typically served at room temperature, often garnished with chopped pistachios or walnuts.

Buttermilk koldskål is a Danish chilled dessert soup made by whisking together buttermilk, sugar, and vanilla, creating a smooth, tangy beverage with a custard-like consistency. It is traditionally served cold as a summer dessert, often accompanied by crispy biscuits or cookies for dunking.

Parser sorse jhal is a Bengali fish dish prepared by cooking parshe (mullet) fish in a spicy mustard-based gravy, typically made with ground mustard seeds, green chilies, turmeric, and mustard oil. It is served hot with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Shol machher kalia is a Bengali fish curry made with shol fish (a freshwater fish), potatoes, and onions, cooked in a spiced gravy of turmeric, ginger, garlic, and other aromatic spices. The dish is traditionally served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Ritha machher kalia is a Bengali fish curry where fish is marinated and cooked with yogurt, spices, and onions to create a rich, aromatic gravy. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Nasi is an Indonesian rice dish prepared by frying pre-cooked rice with vegetables, proteins such as chicken or shrimp, and seasonings including soy sauce and garlic. It is commonly served as a complete meal, often accompanied by a fried egg and fresh vegetables on the side.

Farofa is a toasted cassava flour mixture prepared by sautéing the flour in butter or oil with various ingredients such as vegetables, meat, eggs, or herbs until golden and fragrant. It is typically served as a side dish or condiment at Brazilian tables, alongside grilled meats, rice, and beans.

Konowata is a Japanese seasoning paste made from fermented sea cucumber intestines, salt, and koji mold, aged to develop its characteristic umami-rich flavor. It is typically served as a small side dish or condiment to accompany alcoholic beverages, particularly sake and shochu, in traditional Japanese dining.

Hae mee is a Malaysian noodle dish made with a combination of egg noodles and rice vermicelli, typically stir-fried together with shrimp, squid, and Chinese sausage in a savory gravy or sauce. The dish is commonly served with a lime wedge and sliced red chili on the side, and is popular as a casual meal in Malaysian hawker stalls and restaurants.

canonicalName: costco 1 50 hot dog and soda combo
displayName: Costco $1.50 hot dog and soda combo
aliases: costco 1 50 hot dog and soda combo
description: A grilled or steamed beef hot dog served in a bread bun, accompanied by a fountain soda beverage, typically offered as a fixed-price combo meal. The hot dog is commonly topped with mustard, onions, and relish, and is served as a quick casual meal at Costco food courts.

Herring soup is a traditional Nordic and Eastern European dish made by simmering herring fillets with vegetables such as potatoes, onions, and beets in a broth base, often seasoned with dill and other spices. The soup is typically served hot as a main course or light meal, particularly in Scandinavian and Russian cuisines.

Mulhoe is a Korean raw fish dish prepared by marinating thinly sliced raw fish, typically flounder or other white fish, in a sauce made with gochugaru (red chili pepper), vinegar, and sesame oil, often combined with vegetables and served over rice or noodles. It is traditionally enjoyed as a refreshing summer dish and is sometimes served with a spicy broth as part of a complete meal.

Grilled eggplant is prepared by slicing eggplant lengthwise or into rounds, brushing with oil, and cooking over direct heat until softened and marked with charring. The dish is typically served as a side or appetizer, often finished with garlic, herbs, vinegar, or olive oil.

Kuai is a Chinese noodle dish made from wheat flour dough that is kneaded, stretched, and cut into thick strands, then boiled or stir-fried with vegetables, meat, or sauce. It is typically served as a main course in casual dining settings and is common in northern Chinese cuisine.

Ulmyeon is a Korean-Chinese noodle dish consisting of thick, chewy wheat noodles served in a savory black bean sauce made from fermented soybean paste, typically topped with diced vegetables, potato, and sometimes seafood or meat. The dish is commonly eaten as a casual meal or takeout food and is often served with pickled radish and vinegar on the side for seasoning adjustment.

Churrasca is a grilled meat dish consisting of thin slices of beef, typically sirloin or other tender cuts, that are marinated and cooked over an open flame or on a grill. The meat is traditionally served sliced with chimichurri sauce, grilled vegetables, and bread, commonly presented as part of a Brazilian churrascaria dining experience where servers bring skewered meats directly to diners' tables.

Ayran soup is a cold soup prepared by combining ayran (a yogurt-based beverage) with vegetables such as cucumbers, tomatoes, and sometimes fresh herbs like dill and mint. It is typically served chilled as a refreshing starter or light meal, particularly in Turkish and broader Middle Eastern cuisine.

Homard à l'américaine is a French lobster dish in which the crustacean is cut into pieces, sautéed, and finished in a sauce made with tomatoes, cognac, white wine, and aromatics including shallots and garlic. The dish is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by rice or toast to absorb the flavorful sauce.

Bap is a Korean dish of steamed white rice served in a bowl, often mixed with various ingredients such as vegetables, meat, or seafood and seasoned with sesame oil and salt. It is a staple accompaniment to Korean meals and is commonly eaten as a main dish when combined with other ingredients like eggs, kimchi, or leftover vegetables in bibimbap variations.

Cethot is an Indonesian dish from the Central Java region, typically made from boiled cassava that is mashed or pounded and mixed with grated coconut and palm sugar. It is commonly served as a traditional snack or light meal, often accompanied by a sprinkle of salt to balance its sweet flavor.

Aluek trei ngeat is a Cambodian salad that combines shredded dried fish with fresh watermelon, balancing salty and sweet flavors in a simple toss. It is typically served as a side dish or accompaniment to rice-based meals.

Köttsoppa is a Swedish beef soup made by simmering beef chunks with root vegetables such as carrots, onions, and potatoes in a seasoned broth. The dish is traditionally served hot as a main course, often accompanied by bread or with sour cream and lingonberry jam on the side.

Chicken seekh kabab is prepared by mincing chicken meat and mixing it with spices, herbs, and binding ingredients, then molding the mixture onto metal skewers and grilling or roasting over charcoal or an open flame. The kababs are typically served hot as an appetizer or main course, often accompanied by sliced onions, lemon wedges, and chutney.

Baked zucchini slices are prepared by slicing zucchini lengthwise or crosswise, typically seasoning with salt, pepper, and oil, then baking until tender and lightly browned. This dish is commonly served as a side vegetable or appetizer in Mediterranean and international cuisines.

Kaszotto is a Polish risotto-style dish made by cooking groat (pearl barley or other grains) with broth and butter, stirred constantly until creamy. It is typically served as a side dish or main course, often accompanied by meat or mushrooms.

Kofteh berenj is an Iranian meatball made from ground meat mixed with rice, herbs, and spices, then shaped into large oval patties and either fried or baked. It is typically served as a main course with rice, yogurt, and fresh herbs, often accompanied by grilled tomatoes and onions.

Chhanar kaliya is a Bengali sweet curry made by simmering homemade cheese (chhanar) cubes in a spiced yogurt-based gravy with ginger, turmeric, and sometimes raisins and nuts. The dish is typically served warm with rice or bread as a main course or special occasion preparation in Bengali cuisine.

Chitol macher kopta is a Bengali preparation of the head and upper body portions of chitol fish (a freshwater fish native to South Asia), typically cooked with mustard oil, nigella seeds, and spices. The dish is commonly prepared by curry cooking methods and served with rice as part of Bengali cuisine meals.

Macher dim er kalia is a Bengali fish curry where fish is cooked with eggs in a spiced yogurt-based gravy, typically featuring onions, ginger, garlic, and traditional Bengali spices. The dish is traditionally served with rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Ritha machher kalia is a Bengali fish curry made by simmering fish pieces in a spiced gravy typically containing onions, ginger, garlic, and aromatic spices like turmeric and cumin. The dish is traditionally served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Alu beguner kalia is a Bengali curry made by cooking potatoes and eggplant in a spiced onion and tomato-based gravy with ginger, garlic, and traditional Bengali spices. The dish is typically served as a vegetarian main course with rice or bread and is common in Bengali cuisine across India and Bangladesh.

Gajor er moshola kalia is a Bengali curry made with carrots cooked in a spiced yogurt-based gravy, typically prepared with onions, ginger-garlic paste, and a blend of spices including cumin and coriander. The dish is traditionally served as a side dish or main course accompaniment with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Mohrenkopf is a German confectionery consisting of a round sponge cake base topped with vanilla pudding or cream filling, all encased in a chocolate coating. This dome-shaped treat is typically served as a dessert or sweet snack and is popular in German-speaking regions.

Tsoureki is a sweet braided bread made with eggs, butter, and milk, flavored with mahleb (cherry kernel) and mastic, then topped with sesame seeds or whole eggs before baking. Traditionally prepared for Easter celebrations in Greece and Turkey, it is served sliced as a festive breakfast or dessert bread.

Kartoffelkager are Danish potato pancakes made from grated or finely chopped potatoes mixed with eggs, onions, and seasonings, then pan-fried until golden and crispy on both sides. They are typically served as a side dish or light meal, often accompanied by sour cream, applesauce, or bacon.

Pavê is a Brazilian layered dessert made by stacking thin sponge cake or biscuit layers with cream, chocolate, or fruit fillings, then refrigerated until the layers meld together. It is typically served chilled as a dessert at celebrations and informal meals, often cut into squares or rectangles.

Soneberî is a Kurdish dish prepared by cooking meat together with onions and capsicum, typically simmered or stewed until the flavors combine. It is commonly served as a main course, often accompanied by rice or flatbread in traditional Kurdish meals.

Sovanî is a Kurdish dish prepared by sautéing onions in cooking oil and combining them with minced meat, producing a simple stew-like preparation centered on the sweetness of cooked onions. It is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by bread or rice.

A dinner roll is a small, soft bread made from yeast dough containing flour, water, butter, milk, and salt, typically baked until golden brown. It is commonly served warm alongside main courses at dinner meals, often accompanied by butter for spreading.

M'hancha is a Moroccan pastry made by rolling almond paste in sheets of puff pastry, then coiling the roll into a snake-like shape before baking until golden. The finished pastry is traditionally drizzled with honey and served as a dessert or special occasion treat.

A barquette is a small, boat-shaped pastry shell typically made from shortcrust or puff pastry dough, baked until crispy in a fluted mold. The pastry case is filled with savory or sweet ingredients such as seafood, vegetables, custard, or fruit, and served as an appetizer, side dish, or petit four depending on the filling and occasion.

Krokan is a Norwegian confection made by combining caramelized sugar with crushed roasted almonds or hazelnuts, then breaking the cooled mixture into irregular pieces. It is typically served as a standalone candy or used as a crunchy topping and flavoring ingredient in desserts such as ice cream and chocolate confections.

Qatlama is an Azerbaijani pastry made by layering thin sheets of dough with butter and sometimes herbs or meat fillings, then baking until golden and crispy. The pastry is typically cut into squares or diamond shapes and served as a savory snack or appetizer, often accompanied by tea or as part of a traditional spread.

Nasi kuning is an Indonesian rice dish prepared by cooking rice with turmeric, coconut milk, and spices such as garlic, ginger, and bay leaves, which gives it a distinctive golden-yellow color. It is typically served as an accompaniment to main dishes or as part of a festive meal, particularly during celebrations and special occasions.

Bird's nest is a traditional Chinese delicacy made from the hardened saliva of swiftlet birds, which is harvested from their nests and typically prepared by simmering in broth or sweet liquid. The dish is commonly served as a soup or dessert, prized for its delicate texture and high nutritional value in East Asian cuisine.

Esqueixada is a Catalan salad made with shredded salt cod that has been soaked and desalted, combined with tomatoes, onions, olives, and peppers, then dressed with olive oil and vinegar. It is typically served cold as an appetizer or light dish, particularly popular during summer months in Spain.

Kalamay is a Filipino sweet confection made by cooking coconut milk with brown sugar and glutinous rice flour until it reaches a thick, fudgy consistency, often flavored with vanilla or ginger. The mixture is typically poured into banana leaves or wax paper and served as a dessert or snack, eaten plain or sliced into portions.

Fouée is a traditional French bread from the Anjou region, made from a light, airy dough that is baked until it forms a hollow interior with a crispy crust. It is typically served warm and split open to be filled with savory or sweet toppings, commonly including rillettes, butter, or jam.

Llapingacho is an Ecuadorian potato cake made by mixing mashed potatoes with cheese, forming patties, and pan-frying them until golden brown. It is typically served as a side dish or main course, often accompanied by fried eggs, avocado, and a peanut-based sauce.

Merluza en salsa verde is a hake fillet poached or baked in a sauce made from parsley, garlic, white wine, and olive oil, often with the addition of clams or mussels and peas. This Spanish dish is traditionally served hot with the cooking liquid as sauce, typically accompanied by boiled potatoes or crusty bread to absorb the green sauce.

aliases: thon et rascasse crus a la tahitienne
description: 'Ota 'ika is a Polynesian raw fish dish prepared by marinating diced fish in coconut milk, citrus juice, and seasonings such as onions, tomatoes, and peppers. It is typically served as an appetizer or light meal, often accompanied by vegetables or bread for dipping.

Ravitoto is a traditional Malagasy dish made from cassava or taro leaves that are shredded and cooked with coconut milk, garlic, and often pork or beef. The mixture is simmered until the leaves become tender and the flavors meld together, typically served as a main course alongside rice.

Arròs a banda is a Valencian rice dish prepared by cooking short-grain rice in a flavorful seafood or fish broth, traditionally made with local ingredients like rockfish or other Mediterranean fish. The dish is typically served with the rice and broth together, accompanied by aioli and often finished with a drizzle of olive oil, representing a regional variation of paella originating from the Albufera lagoon area near Valencia, Spain.

Christmas sweets are confections prepared seasonally around the Christmas holiday, typically made from ingredients such as sugar, butter, flour, chocolate, dried fruits, nuts, and warming spices like cinnamon, nutmeg, and cloves. They are commonly served as desserts, gifts, or accompaniments to festive meals and gatherings throughout the Christmas season.

Panisse is a fried chickpea flour cake from Nice, made by cooking besan (chickpea flour) with water and salt into a thick paste, then pouring it into a pan to set before cutting into slices or sticks and deep frying until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a street food snack or appetizer, often eaten plain or with salt.

Gulyásleves is a Hungarian beef stew soup made by simmering beef, onions, and potatoes in a broth seasoned with paprika, caraway seeds, and garlic. Traditionally served hot as a main course, often with bread or egg noodles on the side.

Gardiane de taureau is a Provençal stew made with bull meat, red wine, garlic, and tomatoes, traditionally prepared in a heavy pot and simmered for several hours until the meat becomes tender. The dish is typically served with rice, pasta, or polenta and originates from the Camargue region in southern France.

Otak-otak is a Southeast Asian dish made from fish paste mixed with spices, coconut milk, and herbs, wrapped in banana leaves and grilled or steamed until cooked through. It is typically served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by sambal or peanut sauce for dipping.

Nasi campur is an Indonesian rice dish prepared by mixing steamed rice with various accompaniments including vegetables, proteins such as chicken or eggs, and condiments like soy sauce and sambal. The dish is typically served as a single plate combining multiple components and is common throughout Indonesia as a casual meal option.

Nasi uduk is Indonesian rice cooked in coconut milk with aromatics such as garlic, shallots, ginger, and bay leaves, resulting in fragrant, creamy grains. It is traditionally served as a breakfast or light meal, often accompanied by fried chicken, salted fish, hard-boiled eggs, and sambal.

Kefteji is a Tunisian dish consisting of fried potato croquettes mixed with meat, typically beef or lamb, along with spices and binders. The mixture is shaped into balls or patties and deep-fried until golden, traditionally served as an appetizer or street food.

Bitter almond cookies are made from a dough containing bitter almond extract or ground bitter almonds, sugar, and eggs, then shaped and baked until firm. These cookies are typically served as a small dessert or accompaniment to coffee and tea in Turkish and broader Mediterranean cuisines.

Kyaraben is a Japanese lunch box featuring bento-style rice and side dishes arranged to resemble cartoon or character designs, typically created using food coloring, shaped ingredients, and creative plating techniques. It is commonly prepared for children's meals and special occasions, combining standard bento components like rice, protein, and vegetables into visually themed presentations.

Chả cá Lã Vọng is a Vietnamese dish of turmeric-marinated fish (typically catfish) that is grilled or pan-fried and served with fresh herbs, vegetables, and a savory dipping sauce. The dish is traditionally accompanied by rice noodles or rice and eaten by wrapping the fish and herbs in rice paper or lettuce leaves.

Cromesquis are French fried appetizers consisting of a savory filling, typically ragout or béchamel-based mixtures with meat or seafood, enclosed in a thin crêpe or pastry wrapper, breaded, and deep-fried until golden. They are traditionally served hot as a first course or appetizer, often accompanied by a sauce such as tomato or a reduction.

Nasi kucing is an Indonesian rice dish prepared by mixing cooked rice with small portions of various side dishes such as sambal, fried anchovies, eggs, and vegetables, then served in a small portion on a single plate. This affordable street food and casual meal is typically eaten as breakfast or lunch and is characterized by its variety of flavors combined in one modest serving.

Æbleflæsk is a Danish dish made by layering bacon, sliced apples, and onions in a pan, then cooking them together with sugar, cinnamon, and peppercorn until caramelized and tender. The dish is traditionally served as a main course or side dish, often accompanied by boiled potatoes and pickled cucumber.

Rold Gold Thins are a variety of pretzel snack produced by Frito-Lay, made from baked wheat dough seasoned with salt and shaped into thin, flat pretzel form. They are typically served as a standalone snack or alongside dips and are commonly found packaged for retail sale.

Menmian is a Chinese noodle dish in which fresh noodles are braised together with string beans, typically alongside pork, garlic, and soy sauce, allowing the noodles to absorb the cooking liquid. It is commonly served as a one-pot main course, particularly in northern Chinese home cooking.

Rörost is a Swedish cheese dish prepared by stirring fresh cheese curds with syrup and spices such as cinnamon and cardamom until a smooth, spreadable consistency is achieved. It is typically served as part of a traditional breakfast or coffee table spread, often accompanied by bread or crispbread.

Poularde aux morilles is a classic French dish featuring a young hen braised with morel mushrooms in a cream sauce. The chicken is typically cooked whole or in large pieces and served as an elegant main course, often accompanied by the rich, earthy sauce created from the morels and cooking liquid.

Raviole du Dauphiné are small pasta parcels traditionally filled with meat, cheese, and herbs, then cooked by boiling or pan-frying until golden. They are typically served as a main course, often accompanied by a rich sauce such as cream, tomato, or meat-based preparation, and are characteristic of Dauphiné cuisine in southeastern France.

Poulet DG is a Cameroonian dish consisting of chicken pieces sautéed with vegetables including tomatoes, onions, and peppers, typically cooked in a rich sauce with spices. The dish is commonly served with rice or other starches as a main course in Central African cuisine.

Rougail saucisses is a Réunionnese dish consisting of sausages cooked in a spiced tomato-based sauce called rougail, which typically includes tomatoes, onions, garlic, and chili peppers. The dish is commonly served with rice and is a staple of Creole cuisine from the island of Réunion.

Rougail is a Réunionese condiment made by combining tomatoes, onions, garlic, and chili peppers, typically cooked down into a chunky sauce or relish. It is commonly served as an accompaniment to rice, grilled meats, and fish dishes in Creole cuisine.

Karnıyarık is a Turkish dish made from halved eggplants that are fried and then stuffed with a mixture of seasoned ground meat, onions, and tomatoes before being baked. It is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by rice pilaf or bread.

Stoemp with sausage is a Belgian dish consisting of mashed potatoes mixed with vegetables, typically including carrots or other root vegetables, topped with or served alongside grilled or fried sausage. This hearty, comfort-food preparation is commonly served as a main course in Belgian and Dutch cuisine, often accompanied by mustard or pickles.

Sautéed reindeer is prepared by quickly cooking thin slices or cubes of reindeer meat in a hot pan with butter or oil until browned on the exterior while remaining tender inside. The dish is typically served with lingonberry sauce, sour cream, or mushroom sauce, accompanied by boiled potatoes or other root vegetables, and is common in Nordic and Scandinavian cuisine.

Tunisian tarte is a pastry dish made with thin sheets of malsouka (crispy fried pastry) layered with fillings such as eggs, meat, or vegetables, then cut into triangular pieces. It is typically served as a savory appetizer or light main course, often accompanied by harissa or other traditional condiments.

Mussels marinière is prepared by steaming fresh mussels in a pot with white wine, shallots, garlic, and parsley until the shells open. The dish is typically served in the cooking liquid as a light appetizer or main course, often accompanied by crusty bread for dipping.

Pantxineta is a traditional Basque pastry consisting of two thin layers of puff pastry filled with pastry cream and topped with a light glaze or icing. This sweet treat is typically served as a dessert or with coffee, and is a common offering in Basque bakeries and pastry shops.

A tostada is a crispy tortilla base, typically made from corn or wheat, that is fried until hardened and topped with various ingredients such as beans, meat, cheese, lettuce, and salsa. It is commonly served as an appetizer or light meal in Mexican cuisine and is eaten by hand.

Taro dumplings are prepared by wrapping a filling of mashed or diced taro root, often combined with pork, shrimp, or other ingredients, in wheat or tapioca flour dough, then steaming or frying until cooked through. These dumplings are commonly served as dim sum appetizers or snacks, typically accompanied by dipping sauces such as soy sauce or chili oil.

Akhuni is a fermented soybean product from the North Eastern Region of India, prepared by boiling soybeans and allowing them to ferment, often wrapped in leaves, before being mashed or dried into cakes. It is used as a flavoring base or condiment in stews, chutneys, and curries, typically served alongside rice.

Noonsom is a traditional pickled preparation of the Kadazandusun people of Sabah, made by fermenting fruits or vegetables with salt and sometimes rice to preserve them over extended periods. It is typically served as a condiment or side accompaniment to rice-based meals.

Pate acha is a Northern Nigerian porridge-style dish prepared by cooking fonio (acha) grains with water until soft, then combining them with ingredients such as vegetables, meat or fish, spices, and sometimes groundnut or palm oil. It is typically served hot as a main meal, often eaten communally and valued as a staple among communities in northern Nigeria.

Asida is a sweet paste dessert made by cooking flour in butter or ghee until browned, then mixing it with honey or sugar and sometimes dates to create a thick, creamy consistency. It is traditionally served warm, often during special occasions and religious festivals, particularly in Middle Eastern and North African cuisines.

Dinde aux marrons is a French poultry dish consisting of turkey prepared with chestnuts as the primary accompaniment or ingredient in the sauce. The dish is traditionally served as a main course, particularly during autumn and winter months, with the chestnuts providing a sweet, nutty flavor that complements the meat.

Tocană is a traditional Romanian stew made by slow-cooking beef or pork with onions, garlic, and spices such as paprika and caraway seeds until the meat becomes tender. The dish is typically served hot with polenta, bread, or sour cream as an accompaniment.

Polenta e osei is a traditional Lombard dish consisting of a polenta base topped with small game birds, typically larks or other songbirds, which are roasted and arranged on top. The dish is served as a main course and represents a classic preparation from the Brescia region of Italy, though modern versions often substitute the birds with marzipan or other ingredients due to hunting restrictions.

Gambas con gabardina is a Spanish dish of shrimp coated in a light batter and deep-fried until crispy. The dish is typically served hot as a tapa or appetizer, often accompanied by lemon wedges for squeezing over the fried shrimp.

Phutu is a South African maize meal dish made by mixing coarsely ground maize with boiling water and salt to create a crumbly, porridge-like consistency. It is typically served as a breakfast or side dish, often paired with beans, vegetables, or relish.

Kopanisti Mykonou is a traditional Greek cheese from the Cycladic island of Mykonos, made from cow's milk and aged through a fermentation process that develops its characteristic pungent flavor and creamy texture. The cheese is typically served as part of a meze platter or crumbled over salads and pasta dishes, and is known for its sharp, tangy taste that intensifies with age.

Pelamushi is a Georgian pudding made by cooking grape juice with maize flour and water until thickened, resulting in a smooth, custard-like dessert. It is typically served chilled or at room temperature as a traditional sweet course, often during autumn grape harvest season.

Jerk chicken is prepared by marinating chicken in a spice blend typically containing Scotch bonnet peppers, allspice, thyme, garlic, ginger, and other seasonings, then grilling or baking until cooked through. The dish originates from Jamaica and is commonly served with rice and beans, plantains, or bread as part of Caribbean cuisine.

Anolini are small filled pasta parcels from Emilia-Romagna, typically made with a thin egg pasta dough folded around a filling of meat, cheese, and breadcrumbs. They are traditionally served in a rich meat broth as a first course, though they can also be dressed with butter and cheese.

Brioche con gelato is an Italian dessert consisting of a soft brioche bun filled with a generous scoop of ice cream and topped with whipped cream. It is typically served as a cold dessert or sweet snack, popular in Italian gelaterias and cafes.

Lakerda is a Turkish dish of raw fish, typically bonito or similar white fish, cured in salt and citrus juice (usually lemon or vinegar) for several hours until the fish becomes opaque and firm. It is traditionally served as a cold appetizer or meze, often accompanied by olive oil, herbs, and bread.

Kheer payesh is a rice pudding dessert made by slowly cooking rice with milk, ghee, and sweetener until the rice softens and the mixture thickens. The dish is traditionally served warm or chilled, often garnished with nuts such as almonds and cashews, and is popular across South Asia as a festive dessert and offering in religious ceremonies.

Gyoza are thin dough wrappers filled with a mixture of ground pork, vegetables, and seasonings, then pan-fried until the bottoms develop a golden crust before steaming. They are typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by a dipping sauce made from soy sauce, vinegar, and chili oil.

Wèli is a Beninese dish consisting of potatoes that are peeled, cut, and deep-fried until golden. It is typically served as a street food or side dish, often accompanied by a spicy sauce or grilled meat and fish.

Pizza Poro is a Finnish-style pizza topped with smoked reindeer meat, tomato sauce, cheese, chanterelle mushrooms, and red onion, baked in a hot oven until the crust is crisp and the cheese has melted. It is typically served as a main course, showcasing northern Finnish ingredients in an international pizza format.

Bombe glacée is a frozen dessert made by layering different flavors of ice cream or mousse inside a spherical mold, then freezing the assembled structure until solid. It is typically unmolded and served sliced at the table, often accompanied by a sauce or syrup.

Kadayif is a crispy pastry dessert made from thin shredded phyllo dough that is layered with nuts and bound together, then baked until golden and typically soaked in sugar syrup or honey. It is commonly served in Middle Eastern and Mediterranean cuisines as a sweet treat, often cut into portions and eaten as a dessert or with tea and coffee.

Cold sweet soup is prepared by chilling a sweetened broth or fruit-based liquid, often made with berries, stone fruits, or cream and served at a low temperature. It is typically served as a dessert or refreshing course in Nordic and Eastern European cuisines, particularly during warm months.

Hurmašica is a fried pastry dessert from the Balkans made by wrapping thin dough around a filling of nuts, typically walnuts or hazelnuts, mixed with sugar and spices. The rolled pastries are deep-fried until golden and crispy, then typically drizzled with honey or syrup and dusted with powdered sugar, commonly served as a sweet treat or dessert.

Barazek is a Middle Eastern sesame and pistachio seed cake made by combining flour, sugar, and butter into a dough, then topping it with a mixture of sesame seeds and chopped pistachios before baking until golden. It is typically cut into diamond or square shapes and served as a sweet snack or dessert, often accompanied by tea or coffee.

Pea flour cake is made by combining ground dried peas with flour, eggs, and other binding ingredients, then baking the batter until set. This savory or lightly sweet cake is typically served as a side dish, snack, or part of traditional meals in various cuisines.

Champús is a traditional Ecuadorian beverage made by cooking corn flour, cinnamon, and other spices with water or broth, then sweetening with panela (unrefined cane sugar) and sometimes adding fruits like blackberries or naranjilla. It is typically served hot as a breakfast drink or dessert beverage, often accompanied by bread or empanadas.

Layali Lubnan is a Lebanese dessert consisting of layers of crispy phyllo pastry alternated with sweet cream filling, typically made from milk-based custard or mascarpone, and topped with nuts such as pistachios or almonds. The assembled pastry is baked until golden and often finished with a light sugar syrup or honey drizzle, served as a traditional sweet at Lebanese celebrations and special occasions.

Bienmesabe is a Spanish dessert made with ground almonds, egg yolks, sugar, and cinnamon that are mixed into a thick paste, typically served chilled or at room temperature. The dish is traditionally accompanied by sponge cake or wafers for dipping, and is commonly enjoyed as a sweet course in Spanish cuisine.

Kesaria Peda is an Indian milk-based sweet made by reducing khoya (evaporated milk solids) with ghee, sugar, and saffron until it reaches a fudgy consistency, then shaped into small cylindrical or rounded forms. The dish is typically served as a dessert or sweet offering at festivals and special occasions, with saffron providing its distinctive golden-red color and flavor.

Cottage pudding is a steamed sponge cake made from basic ingredients including flour, butter, sugar, and eggs, typically served warm with a sweet sauce poured over or around it. The dish is traditionally served as a dessert, often with custard, chocolate sauce, or fruit sauce as accompaniment.

Persimmon pudding is a steamed or baked dessert made with persimmon puree combined with flour, sugar, spices such as cinnamon and nutmeg, and often includes eggs and butter. It is traditionally served warm, frequently topped with whipped cream or hard sauce, and is particularly popular as a dessert during autumn and winter months.

Pootharekulu is a traditional Andhra Pradesh sweet made by layering thin sheets of rice paper with jaggery and ghee, then rolling and cutting into cylindrical pieces. The dish is typically served as a dessert or festive sweet treat, often given as a gift during special occasions.

Pastafrola is a traditional Argentine pastry consisting of a sweet shortcrust pastry base topped with a thick layer of quince paste (dulce de membrillo) and covered with a lattice of pastry strips. The assembled pastry is baked until golden and is typically served as a dessert or afternoon treat, often accompanied by tea or coffee.

Barrel chicken is a Taiwanese roasted chicken dish in which a whole marinated chicken is cooked inside a cylindrical wood-fired barrel oven, allowing the bird to roast evenly while smoky aromas from the wood infuse the meat. It is typically sold by roadside vendors and specialty shops, served whole or hand-torn into pieces, often accompanied by dipping salt or pepper seasoning.

Shirokuma is a shaved ice dessert topped with sweetened condensed milk, evaporated milk, and various colorful fruit syrups or fruit pieces. It is served cold in a bowl or cup and is popular as a refreshing summer treat in Japan.

Putu piring is a Malaysian steamed cake made from rice flour mixed with grated coconut, brown sugar, and boiling water, then poured into a cylindrical mold and steamed until set. The cake is typically served warm, often accompanied by a dark brown sugar syrup for dipping, and is commonly eaten as a breakfast or snack item.

aliases: gateau de lune en peau de neige
description: Snow skin mooncake is made with a glutinous rice flour-based wrapper that is cooked and cooled before being wrapped around sweet fillings such as lotus paste, red bean paste, or fruit compounds, creating a soft, chewy exterior without baking. This Cantonese dessert is traditionally served during the Mid-Autumn Festival and eaten cold or at room temperature as a celebratory pastry.

Merveilleux is a French dessert consisting of a meringue base topped with whipped cream and chocolate shavings, sometimes garnished with nuts or fruit. It is typically served as an individual pastry or cake at dessert course in French and international cuisine.

Hojaldres de Astorga are a traditional Spanish pastry from the León region made with puff pastry layers that are fried until golden and crispy. The pastries are typically dusted with powdered sugar and served as a sweet treat or dessert, often enjoyed with coffee or hot chocolate.

Alajú is a traditional Spanish confection made by combining almonds, honey, and spices, then pressing the mixture into a dense, fudge-like bar that is typically cut into small pieces. This sweet treat is commonly served as a dessert or gift item, particularly associated with Spanish culinary traditions.

Briosce are Italian pastries made from a sweet yeast dough enriched with butter and eggs, then baked until golden. Typically enjoyed as a breakfast item or snack, they are often served plain or filled with jam, custard, or cream, and are commonly paired with coffee or hot chocolate.

Khanom tan is a Thai dessert made from a batter of rice flour, tapioca flour, and coconut milk that is steamed in small molds or cups until set. The finished cakes have a custard-like texture and are typically served as a sweet snack or dessert, often accompanied by coconut cream or syrup.

Timlo is a Javanese soup prepared by simmering beef or chicken with aromatic ingredients such as garlic, shallots, and galangal, typically served in a clear broth with added vegetables like cabbage and bean sprouts. The dish is traditionally served hot in bowls and commonly eaten as a main course, particularly in Central Java.

Gonggal-ppang is a Korean-Chinese hybrid bread made by deep-frying a sweet dough that is often coated with a crispy exterior and sometimes filled with sweet red bean paste or custard. It is typically served as a dessert or snack, commonly found in Korean-Chinese restaurants and bakeries.

Lauvsteik is a Norwegian grilled or pan-fried beef steak typically cut from the loin, seasoned simply with salt and pepper, and often served medium-rare. It is commonly accompanied by traditional sides such as potatoes, vegetables, and a rich sauce or gravy, and represents a classic preparation in Norwegian cuisine.

Mastava is a traditional Uzbek soup made by cooking rice with meat, vegetables, and aromatics in a seasoned broth. The dish is typically served hot as a main course and is known for its hearty, comforting composition that combines tender rice with savory meat and vegetable elements.

Hasip is a traditional Central Asian dish made by stuffing cleaned animal intestines with a mixture of rice, mutton, liver, onions, cumin seed, and tail fat, then cooking the filled casings until the meat is tender. The dish is typically served as a main course, often sliced into portions, and is common in Kazakh and other regional cuisines.

Slavski kolač is a traditional Balkan sweet bread made with layers of dough, nuts (typically walnuts), sugar, and spices, baked until golden brown. It is commonly served during celebrations and holidays, often accompanied by coffee or tea, and traditionally cut into diamond or square pieces.

Ajo carretero is a Spanish soup traditionally made by heating garlic, paprika, and bread in broth or water, sometimes with the addition of eggs or ham. The dish was historically prepared by muleteers and cart drivers as a simple, economical meal that could be made with minimal ingredients over a fire.

Kaqdoq Minnyaq is a traditional Makassarese rice dish prepared by cooking rice with coconut milk and aromatic spices until rich and fragrant. It is served as a ritual food at nearly every ceremonial celebration in Makassar, Indonesia, typically accompanied by side dishes such as meat or eggs.

Jruek Drien is an Acehnese fermented durian preparation made by salting ripe durian flesh and allowing it to ferment until it develops a sour, pungent flavor. It is typically used as a condiment or cooking ingredient in Acehnese dishes, often incorporated into curries, sambals, or fish stews served with rice.

Tutug oncom is an Indonesian rice dish made by mixing cooked rice with oncom, a fermented soybean cake product, along with vegetables and spices, then pan-fried until the mixture becomes golden and slightly crispy. It is typically served as a main course or side dish in Indonesian cuisine, often accompanied by fresh vegetables or proteins.

Sambal goreng is an Indonesian stir-fried dish prepared by cooking meat, seafood, or vegetables with sambal (a spice paste made from chilies, garlic, and other seasonings) along with complementary ingredients like shallots and spices. The dish is typically served as a main course with steamed rice and is common throughout Indonesia and other Southeast Asian regions.

Buah keluak is an Indonesian dish featuring black nuts from the keluak tree that are cracked open and their toxic flesh removed, leaving a safe dark paste that is mixed with spices, shrimp paste, and other seasonings to create a rich filling. The mixture is typically used to stuff fish or served as a savory condiment alongside rice and other dishes in Peranakan cuisine.

Dwaeji-gukbap is a Korean soup dish made by simmering pork with vegetables and seasonings in a broth, then served over rice in the same bowl. The dish is typically prepared with pork bones or meat, garlic, and gochugaru (red chili pepper), creating a savory and mildly spicy broth that is commonly eaten as a hearty meal or comfort food.

Laksam is a Malaysian noodle dish made from rice flour dough that is steamed and cut into flat strands, then served in a rich curry or gravy typically flavored with coconut milk, spices, and sometimes seafood or meat. The dish is traditionally eaten as a breakfast or light meal, often accompanied by hard-boiled eggs and fresh vegetables.

Soto Jepara is a traditional Indonesian soup from the Jepara region of Central Java, made by simmering chicken or beef with turmeric, galangal, garlic, and other spices in a savory broth, typically served with rice and accompanied by hard-boiled eggs and vegetables. The dish is commonly eaten as a main course for lunch or dinner and is characterized by its warm, aromatic broth and tender meat.

Soto Betawi is a beef soup prepared by simmering beef, offal, and potatoes in a rich broth flavored with garlic, shallots, galangal, turmeric, and coconut milk. The dish is traditionally served hot with rice, fried shallots, and sambal, originating from the Betawi people of Jakarta.

Rujak manis is an Indonesian fruit salad made from chopped fresh fruits such as papaya, pineapple, cucumber, and jicama, dressed with a sweet and spicy peanut sauce made from ground peanuts, palm sugar, and chili peppers. The dish is typically served as a refreshing dessert or snack, often enjoyed in warm climates as a cooling treat.

Tegal satay is an Indonesian grilled meat skewer dish from the Tegal region in Central Java, featuring marinated meat threaded onto bamboo sticks and cooked over charcoal. The dish is typically served with peanut sauce and rice, and is commonly eaten as a street food or casual meal.

Çörek is a filled pastry made by wrapping seasoned ingredients in thin sheets of dough, typically phyllo or yufka, and baking until golden and crispy. The filling commonly consists of cheese, spinach, or meat, and the pastry is traditionally served warm as an appetizer, snack, or part of a meze spread in Turkish cuisine.

Candlenut soup is an Indonesian dish made by grinding candlenuts with spices and coconut milk to create a creamy base, typically combined with vegetables and protein such as chicken or fish. The soup is traditionally served hot as part of an Indonesian meal, with the candlenuts providing both thickening and a distinctive nutty flavor to the broth.

Kue talam is a two-layered steamed cake made with a bottom layer of coconut custard and a top layer of flour-based cake, typically flavored with pandan or vanilla. The dish is prepared by steaming the batter in a shallow pan and is commonly served as a dessert or snack in Indonesian cuisine.

Tahu campur is an Indonesian dish made from fried tofu combined with various vegetables such as cabbage, bean sprouts, and carrots, typically bound together with egg and seasoned with soy sauce and spices. The mixture is usually pan-fried until golden and served as a casual side dish or light main course, often accompanying rice at meals.

Igado is a Filipino stew made by simmering pork offal, typically liver and intestines, with garlic, onions, and chili peppers until tender. The dish is traditionally served as a main course with rice, popular in Filipino cuisine as an economical way to utilize all parts of the animal.

Kelamai is a traditional Malaysian kuih prepared by cooking glutinous rice flour with palm sugar (gula melaka) and coconut milk until it forms a thick, sticky, pudding-like mass. It is typically served as a sweet snack or dessert, often during festive occasions and communal gatherings.

Meat jelly is a Russian aspic dish made by simmering meat bones and connective tissue to extract gelatin, which is then cooled until it sets into a savory jelly, typically containing pieces of meat, vegetables, and sometimes hard-boiled eggs suspended throughout. It is traditionally served cold as an appetizer or first course, often accompanied by mustard or horseradish, and is a common dish at festive meals and celebrations.

Branston Baked Beans are canned beans in a tomato-based sauce made with Branston Pickle, a British condiment containing chopped vegetables and spices. The dish is typically served hot as a side dish or on buttered toast, commonly eaten for breakfast or light meals.

Tomato soup is prepared by simmering tomatoes with aromatics such as onions and garlic, then blending or straining to create a smooth or textured base, often finished with cream or stock. It is typically served hot as a starter or light meal, commonly accompanied by bread, croutons, or a dollop of sour cream.

Waldorf salad is prepared by combining diced apples, celery, and walnuts with a mayonnaise-based dressing, typically finished with grapes and served on a bed of lettuce. This dish originated in the late 19th century and is traditionally served as a side salad or light main course, often at lunch or as part of a buffet.

Greek salad is prepared by combining tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and Kalamata olives with crumbled feta cheese, then dressing with olive oil and oregano. It is typically served as a cold appetizer or light main course, commonly accompanied by bread in Greek cuisine.

Kūčiukai are Lithuanian sweet pastries made from a yeast dough that is rolled thin, filled with a mixture of ground poppy seeds, honey, and sometimes nuts or dried fruits, then rolled and baked until golden brown. These pastries are traditionally served during Christmas Eve celebrations as part of the Kūčios meal, Lithuania's most important festive dinner.

Faworki are traditional Polish fried pastries made from a thin dough that is rolled out, cut into rectangular strips with a slit in the center, and deep-fried until golden and crispy. They are typically dusted with powdered sugar and served as a dessert or sweet treat, often during festive occasions and holidays.

Caesar salad is prepared by tossing romaine lettuce with a dressing made from olive oil, lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, egg, and Parmesan cheese, finished with croutons and black pepper.
