The salad is typically served as a starter or light main course, either as a standalone dish or with added protein.

Taco árabe consists of sliced lamb meat that is cooked on a vertical rotisserie, then wrapped in pita bread or a thin flour tortilla. The dish is typically served with onions, cilantro, and a sauce such as tahini or garlic sauce, originating from Middle Eastern influence in Mexican cuisine.

Harira is a traditional Moroccan soup prepared by simmering tomatoes, legumes (typically chickpeas and lentils), and flour-based broth together with warm spices until thickened. It is commonly served during Ramadan to break the fast at sunset, and is eaten as a hearty starter or main course, often accompanied by dates and bread.

Borscht is a traditional Russian soup made by simmering beets, cabbage, carrots, potatoes, and onions in broth, typically seasoned with salt and vinegar or lemon juice. The dish is commonly served hot as a starter or main course, often with a dollop of sour cream.

Caprese salad is composed of fresh mozzarella cheese, ripe tomatoes, and basil leaves, typically layered or arranged on a plate and dressed with olive oil and salt. It is traditionally served as a cold appetizer or light side dish in Italian cuisine.

Bingka sawit is a Malaysian kuih made from a batter of palm (sawit) ingredients combined with flour, eggs, sugar, and coconut milk, which is baked until set and lightly browned on top. It is typically sliced into squares or wedges and served as a snack or dessert, often accompanying tea during afternoon refreshments or festive gatherings.

Omiki Chōdaishiki is a Shinto ritual offering of sake (omiki), in which rice wine is ceremonially presented to a kami at a shrine altar and subsequently shared among participants after the rite. The sake is typically poured into shallow lacquered cups and consumed following prayers, serving as a symbolic communion with the deity during festivals and formal Shinto ceremonies.

Ramen is a Japanese noodle dish consisting of wheat noodles served in a savory broth, typically flavored with soy sauce, miso, or tonkotsu (pork bone), and topped with ingredients such as sliced pork, soft-boiled eggs, green onions, and nori seaweed. The dish is prepared by cooking the noodles separately and then combining them with the hot broth in a bowl, and is commonly served as a complete meal in specialized ramen restaurants.

A tartlet is a small open-faced tart consisting of a pastry shell filled with savory or sweet ingredients. It is typically served as an appetizer, dessert, or petit four, with the filling and presentation varying by preparation method and occasion.

Israeli salad is prepared by finely dicing tomatoes and cucumbers, then mixing them with olive oil and lemon juice, typically with added parsley and onion. It is commonly served as a fresh side dish or appetizer in Israeli cuisine and Mediterranean meals.

Drunken shrimp is a Chinese dish where live shrimp are immersed in Chinese liquor, typically baijiu or rice wine, until they become intoxicated and immobilized before being consumed. The dish is traditionally served as an appetizer or delicacy, with the shrimp eaten raw or briefly cooked, emphasizing the freshness of the ingredient.

Pescado frito is a whole fish or fish fillets that are cleaned, seasoned, and deep-fried until the exterior is crispy and golden brown. The dish is typically served with lemon wedges and accompanied by rice, potatoes, or fresh vegetables as a main course.

Bryndzové halušky are Slovak potato dumplings made from grated potatoes and flour, boiled until they float to the surface, then topped with bryndza (a sharp sheep's milk cheese) and crispy bacon pieces. This traditional dish is typically served as a main course in Slovak cuisine, often accompanied by sour cream.

Pickled herring is prepared by curing fresh herring fillets in a brine solution of vinegar, salt, and sugar, often with added spices such as bay leaves, peppercorns, and onions. The dish is typically served cold as an appetizer or light meal, commonly accompanied by bread, potatoes, or sour cream.

Soldaditos de Pavía are salt cod fillets that are marinated in wine and lemon, coated in batter, and deep-fried until golden. They are traditionally served as a tapa or appetizer, often accompanied by a simple tomato sauce or aioli for dipping.

Tako wasabi consists of octopus (tako) prepared and served with wasabi, the pungent Japanese horseradish condiment. This dish is typically served as an appetizer or part of a sushi/sashimi course, with the octopus either sliced raw or briefly blanched, accompanied by wasabi for dipping or direct application.

Ikizukuri is a Japanese preparation method in which a fish is filleted and served while still alive, with the body arranged on a plate alongside its still-beating heart and other organs. The dish is typically served as sashimi with soy sauce and wasabi, showcasing the freshness of the ingredients in high-end Japanese restaurants.

Surströmming is a fermented herring prepared by salt-curing small Clupea fish (Baltic herring) for several months, resulting in a pungent product with a distinctive strong odor. It is typically served as an appetizer or light meal, often on crisp bread with potatoes, onions, and sour cream.

Chingrir dalna is a Bengali curry made with prawns cooked in a spiced gravy of onions, tomatoes, and ginger-garlic paste, typically thickened with ground spices like turmeric and cumin. The dish is traditionally served with rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Chingri paturi is a Bengali dish in which prawns are marinated in a paste of mustard seeds, coconut, and spices, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed or baked. The dish is typically served as a main course in Bengali cuisine, with the banana leaf imparting a subtle flavor to the tender, aromatic prawns.

Assyrian Cookie is a traditional spiced pastry from the Mardin region of Turkiye, prepared by blending flour with warming spices such as mahlab, fennel, and nigella seeds before shaping and baking into dense, aromatic rounds. It is typically served with tea or coffee during religious holidays and family gatherings within Assyrian communities.

Bhetki machher fry is a Bengali fish dish made by marinating bhetki (Asian sea bass) fillets in spices and then deep-frying them until golden and crispy. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread, often accompanied by lemon wedges and a side of dal or vegetables.

Koi macher dalna is a Bengali fish curry made with pieces of koi fish cooked in a spiced gravy containing mustard oil, onions, garlic, ginger, and traditional Bengali spices including turmeric and chili. The dish is typically served with steamed rice and is a staple preparation in Bengali cuisine.

Ilish macher dom is a Bengali preparation where hilsa fish is marinated in spices and cooked slowly in a sealed pot with mustard oil, onions, and green chilies. The dish is traditionally served with rice as a main course and is popular during the hilsa fishing season in Bengal.

Macher shukto is a Bengali fish curry prepared by cooking fish with a mixture of spices, mustard oil, and often includes vegetables such as potatoes and onions in a savory gravy. The dish is typically served as a main course with steamed rice in Bengali cuisine, particularly in West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Pona machher vapa is a Bengali steamed fish preparation where fish is wrapped in banana leaves with mustard paste, coconut, and spices, then steamed until cooked through. The dish is traditionally served as part of Bengali cuisine meals, with the banana leaf imparting a subtle flavor to the tender, aromatic fish.

Bacalao al ajoarriero is a Spanish cod dish prepared by cooking salt cod with a sauce of tomatoes, garlic, and peppers, typically finished with a fried egg. The dish is traditionally served as a main course in Spanish cuisine, often accompanied by bread to soak up the sauce.

Bollito misto is a northern Italian dish consisting of multiple cuts of meat—typically beef, chicken, and sometimes pork or tongue—simmered together in broth with aromatic vegetables until tender. The dish is traditionally served hot with the cooking broth and accompanied by condiments such as salsa verde, mostarda, or horseradish sauce, making it a classic comfort food in Piedmont and Lombardy.

Frango na púcara is a Portuguese braised chicken dish where chicken pieces are slowly cooked in a sealed clay pot (púcara) with white wine, garlic, and bay leaves until tender. The dish is traditionally served directly in the clay vessel, creating a rustic presentation typical of Portuguese home cooking and regional restaurants.

Shumai are open-topped dumplings made by wrapping minced meat, typically pork, along with shrimp and seasonings in a thin wheat wrapper, then steaming until cooked through. They are traditionally served as a dim sum dish, often accompanied by soy sauce or chili oil for dipping.

Zucchini flower fritters are made by dipping fresh zucchini blossoms in a light batter and deep-frying them until golden and crispy. This dish from Niçoise cuisine is typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by a simple tomato sauce or aioli for dipping.

Agnolotti are small pasta parcels made from thin sheets of dough that are filled with a mixture of meat and vegetables, then sealed and typically arranged in a square or circular shape. They are commonly served in broth or with butter and sauce as a primo course in Italian cuisine.

Shakriyeh is a Middle Eastern stew prepared by cooking lamb meat in a yogurt-based sauce, typically seasoned with spices and sometimes including chickpeas or other vegetables. The dish is traditionally served over rice or with bread as a main course.

Sirop de Liège is a thick, dark brown syrup made by reducing pear and apple juice concentrate with spices, traditionally produced in the Liège region of Belgium. It is typically served as a spread on bread, waffles, or pastries, or used as an ingredient in baking and confectionery.

Foutou banane is a West African dish made by pounding boiled cassava and cooking bananas together into a smooth, dough-like paste. It is traditionally served as a starchy side dish or main course, often accompanied by a meat or fish sauce.

Bozbash is a traditional Azerbaijani soup made by simmering lamb meat with chickpeas, potatoes, and various spices until tender. The dish is typically served hot as a main course, often accompanied by bread, and represents a staple of Caucasian cuisine.

Nigerian buns are a deep-fried snack made from a dough of flour, sugar, milk, and baker's yeast, shaped into small rounds and fried until golden, resulting in a slightly crusty exterior and a soft, fluffy interior. They are commonly served as a street food or tea-time snack, often accompanied by beverages such as tea, soft drinks, or pap.

Bolus is a Brussels-style brioche made from an enriched yeast dough that is rolled with brown sugar and cinnamon, coiled into a spiral shape, and baked until the sugar caramelizes. It is typically served as a sweet breakfast or teatime pastry alongside coffee.

Chupe Andino is a traditional Peruvian soup prepared by simmering potatoes, corn, beans, and seafood such as fish or shrimp in a broth base, typically finished with cream, cheese, and egg. The dish is traditionally served hot in bowls as a hearty main course in Andean regions of Peru.

Mole poblano is a complex sauce made from a blend of dried chiles, spices, nuts, seeds, and chocolate, slowly simmered together and traditionally ground by hand or stone to create a smooth, rich preparation. The sauce is typically served over chicken or turkey and accompanied by rice, with the dish originating from Puebla, Mexico.

Ốc vú nàng is a Vietnamese snail dish where freshwater snails are typically boiled or steamed and served in their shells, often with a dipping sauce made from fish sauce, lime, and chilies. The dish is commonly eaten as an appetizer or casual snack, with diners using a small pick or needle to extract the meat from each shell.

Ojja is a North African dish, particularly popular in Tunisia, made by cooking eggs with meat (typically lamb or beef), vegetables, and spices in a clay pot or skillet until the eggs are set. The dish is traditionally served hot, often as a main course, and is commonly eaten for breakfast, lunch, or dinner, sometimes accompanied by bread for scooping.

Grilled cheese is prepared by placing cheese between two slices of bread and cooking the sandwich on a griddle or skillet until the bread is golden brown and the cheese is melted. It is typically served hot as a light meal or sandwich, often accompanied by soup or tomato-based condiments.

Saucisson chaud is a French dish consisting of sliced sausage that is heated and typically served warm, often poached or simmered in wine or broth. It is traditionally served as an appetizer or light main course, frequently accompanied by bread, potatoes, or lentils.

Vindaye is a pickled dish originating from Indian cuisine, particularly associated with Goan and Creole preparations, made by marinating meat, fish, or vegetables in a spiced vinegar-based sauce with onions, garlic, and chilies. The dish is typically served cold or at room temperature as an appetizer or side dish, with the preservation method allowing it to be stored for extended periods.

Machher dimer bora is a Bengali fried appetizer made from fish eggs (roe) mixed with spices and gram flour batter, then deep-fried until golden. It is typically served as a starter or snack, often accompanied by chutney or sauce.

Chal kumro patar bora is a Bengali fritter made by coating pumpkin leaves in a spiced gram flour batter and deep-frying until golden and crispy. The dish is typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by chutney or served alongside rice and curry in Bengali cuisine.

Kathal bijer bora is a fritter made from jackfruit seeds that are boiled, mashed, and mixed with spices before being deep-fried until golden. This Bengali snack is typically served as an appetizer or side dish, particularly during monsoon season when fresh jackfruit is in season.

Lote machher bora is a Bengali fried fish preparation made by coating pieces of lote fish (a freshwater fish) in a spiced gram flour batter and deep-frying until golden. The dish is typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Sankar machher tel er bora is a Bengali fritter made by coating pieces of sankar fish (a freshwater fish) in a seasoned batter of flour, spices, and sometimes turmeric, then deep-frying until golden. The dish is typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by rice and curry in Bengali cuisine.

Paella is a rice dish cooked in a wide, shallow pan where short-grain rice is toasted in oil or fat, then simmered with broth, saffron, and ingredients such as seafood, meat, or vegetables until the liquid is absorbed and a flavorful crust forms on the bottom. It originates from Valencia in Spain and is traditionally served directly from the cooking pan as a communal dish, often at midday meals or social gatherings.

Chaku is a Newar sweet prepared by slowly reducing milk until it reaches a thick, condensed consistency, then cooked further with ghee and sugar to form a dense, fudge-like paste. It is typically served as a dessert or sweet preparation during festivals and special occasions in Newari cuisine.

Rodelika is a reproducible, seed-solid carrot variety typically prepared by washing, peeling, and either grating it raw for salads or cooking it through boiling, roasting, or juicing. It is commonly served as a side vegetable, incorporated into soups and stews, or pressed into fresh juice, and is particularly valued in biodynamic and organic cuisine.

Buttamagedon is a Japanese donburi consisting of a bowl of rice topped with pork and a soft-boiled chicken egg, flavored with yuzu for a citrus accent. It is typically served as a single-bowl main dish, with the egg broken over the pork and rice before eating.

Suea rong hai is a Thai stir-fried beef dish where thin slices of beef are cooked quickly over high heat with garlic, chilies, and fish sauce, typically finished with a squeeze of lime juice. The dish is commonly served over jasmine rice as a main course and features a balance of spicy, savory, and acidic flavors characteristic of Thai cuisine.

Sarma is a Serbian dish consisting of seasoned ground meat wrapped in cabbage or vine leaves and cooked in a tomato-based broth. The rolls are typically simmered until tender and served hot, often accompanied by sour cream or kajmak, as a main course or hearty appetizer.

Anar bij is an Iranian pomegranate seed condiment made by drying and sometimes roasting pomegranate arils until they become crisp and concentrated in flavor. It is typically sprinkled over rice dishes, stews, and salads as a garnish to add tartness, texture, and visual appeal.

Gemblong is an Indonesian sweet snack made from glutinous rice flour dough that is deep-fried until golden and crispy, then coated with palm sugar syrup. It is typically served as a dessert or street food snack, often enjoyed warm with its chewy interior and crispy exterior.

Jenang Kudus is a traditional Indonesian sweet made by slowly cooking coconut milk, brown sugar, and rice flour together until the mixture becomes thick and dark brown, with the cooking process requiring constant stirring. The resulting paste is typically served in small portions as a sweet snack or dessert, and originates from Kudus in Central Java.

Lotek is a Sundanese salad from West Java made with blanched vegetables such as cabbage, bean sprouts, and long beans, combined with fried tofu and hard-boiled eggs, then dressed with a peanut sauce flavored with garlic, chilies, shrimp paste, and palm sugar. It is typically served at room temperature as a vegetable side dish or light meal, often accompanied by crackers or fried shallots for texture.

Bakpia is an Indonesian sweet pastry made by wrapping a filling of green beans, sugar, and butter in a thin dough, then baked until golden brown. This Yogyakarta specialty is typically served as a snack or dessert and is commonly given as a gift due to its long shelf life.

A Czech-style hot dog consists of a grilled or fried sausage served in a bread roll, typically topped with mustard, mayonnaise, and a spiced ketchup-vinegar sauce called pálivá omáčka. It is commonly served as a casual street food or snack in the Czech Republic and is often accompanied by pickled vegetables or onions.

Rfiss is a traditional Algerian pastry made by layering thin sheets of dough with butter and filling, then rolling and folding the layers to create a flaky, textured consistency. The dish is typically served as a breakfast item or dessert, often accompanied by honey or eaten plain, and represents a classic preparation method in Maghrebi cuisine.

Century egg pastry combines preserved century eggs (pídàn) with a flaky pastry shell, typically made with wheat flour dough. The dish is commonly served as a dim sum appetizer or snack, either warm or at room temperature.

Chungmu gimbap is a Korean rice roll made by wrapping seasoned rice and vegetables in dried seaweed sheets, typically prepared without meat and featuring ingredients such as pickled radish, cucumber, and spinach. It is traditionally served as a light meal or side dish, often accompanied by spicy squid or other protein options on the side for individual preference.

Zanahorias aliñadas are cooked carrots dressed with a vinaigrette made from vinegar, olive oil, and seasonings such as garlic, cumin, and sometimes paprika. This Spanish dish is typically served cold as a side dish or tapa, either as part of a vegetable spread or on its own.

Stracciatella is an Italian soup made by slowly pouring beaten eggs into hot broth while stirring, creating thin, delicate egg ribbons that give the dish its name, meaning "torn" or "shredded." It is typically served hot as a first course, often with the addition of cheese, pasta, or vegetables, and is a traditional preparation found throughout Italy.

Qurutob is a Tajik bread dish prepared by tearing flatbread into pieces and layering it with a yogurt-based sauce made from kurt (dried yogurt balls) that are dissolved into a tangy liquid, often finished with a meat or oil topping. It is typically served as a main course or hearty breakfast dish in Tajik cuisine.

Milhard is a type of clafoutis made by baking a batter of eggs, milk, flour, and sugar, traditionally poured over fresh fruit such as cherries or pears in a shallow dish. It is typically served as a dessert, either warm or at room temperature, often dusted with sugar.

Hajnowski marcinek is a traditional Polish layered cake from the Hajnówka region, made by baking numerous thin pastry layers and stacking them with a sweet cream filling, typically based on butter, milk, and sugar. It is served sliced as a dessert, often accompanying coffee or tea at family gatherings and regional celebrations.

Ramadan pide is a Turkish boat-shaped pastry filled with minced meat, onions, and spices, then baked until the edges are golden and crispy. Traditionally served during iftar (the meal breaking the daily fast in Ramadan) and often topped with an egg yolk before baking.

Mücver are Turkish fritters made from grated zucchini mixed with fresh herbs, cheese, and flour, then pan-fried until golden and crispy. They are typically served as a meze (appetizer) or side dish, often accompanied by yogurt or a yogurt-based dipping sauce.

Pholourie is a fried Indian snack made from split pea flour batter combined with spices, onions, and sometimes herbs, formed into small balls and deep-fried until golden. It is typically served as an appetizer or street food, often accompanied by chutneys or tamarind sauce for dipping.

Napoleon cake consists of layers of crisp puff pastry alternated with pastry cream (crème pâtissière), topped with a thin layer of icing and often garnished with caramelized lines. It is a classic French dessert typically served as an individual pastry or in slices, requiring careful layering and chilling before serving.

I fu mie is an Indonesian noodle dish made with thin wheat noodles stir-fried with vegetables, protein, and savory seasonings including soy sauce and garlic. The dish is typically served as a quick meal or side dish in casual dining settings and street food contexts throughout Indonesia.

Pork liver noodles is a dish consisting of noodles served with sliced or diced pork liver, typically stir-fried or simmered in a savory sauce or broth. The dish is commonly found in Asian cuisines and is served as a main course or noodle soup, often garnished with vegetables and aromatics.

Mulgikapsad is an Estonian dish consisting of cabbage rolls filled with a mixture of bacon and other ingredients, then braised in salted water until tender. The rolls are typically served as a main course, often accompanied by potatoes or sour cream.

Tiwul is an Indonesian cassava-based dish made from grated cassava that is fermented and then cooked with coconut milk, typically combined with meat or seafood and aromatics such as garlic, shallots, and chilies. It is traditionally served as a main course in Central Javanese cuisine, particularly in the Yogyakarta and Solo regions.

Bazeen is a Libyan dish made by mixing barley flour or wheat flour with a broth of meat or vegetable, then shaped into a mound and topped with a meat or vegetable sauce. It is traditionally served as a communal dish, often for special occasions and family gatherings.

Bülbülyuvası is a Turkish pastry dessert made from thin phyllo dough layers filled with chopped nuts, typically pistachios or walnuts, and bound with butter and spices such as cinnamon. The pastry is baked until golden and then soaked in a light sugar syrup or honey, resulting in a sweet, crispy-textured confection typically served as a dessert or with coffee.

Nokul is a Turkish pastry made from thin sheets of dough that are rolled with a filling of nuts (typically pistachios or walnuts) and spices, then coiled or folded before baking until golden. It is commonly served as a dessert or sweet pastry, often accompanied by syrup or honey and consumed with tea or coffee.

aliases: livre de cuisine œufs en meurette
description: Oeufs en meurette is a Burgundian dish consisting of poached eggs served in a red wine sauce made from burgundy wine, pearl onions, mushrooms, and lardons. The eggs are typically arranged in a shallow dish or bowl and garnished with the braised vegetables and sauce, often accompanied by toasted bread.

Heotjesabap is a Korean mixed rice dish prepared by combining steamed rice with sautéed vegetables, meat, and a fried egg, typically seasoned with gochujang (red chili paste) and sesame oil. The dish is traditionally served in a heated stone or metal bowl, which crisps the rice at the bottom, and is commonly eaten as a casual meal or quick lunch.

Cuchifritos are a traditional Spanish and Latin American dish consisting of pork offal and meat parts, typically including liver, heart, and other organ meats, which are seasoned and fried until crispy. The dish is commonly served as a breakfast or lunch item, often accompanied by rice, beans, or fried plantains, and is popular in Spanish and Caribbean cuisines.

Erkuai is a Yunnan province dish made from rice that is ground into a paste, steamed, and then cut into strips or slices. It is typically served cold or at room temperature, often topped with a spicy sauce made from chili peppers, soy sauce, and other seasonings, and commonly eaten as a breakfast or snack food.

Sugakiya Curry is a Japanese-style curry rice dish served by the Sugakiya restaurant chain, consisting of steamed white rice topped with a thick curry sauce typically made from a roux of onions, meat, and Japanese curry spices. It is commonly served as a casual single-plate meal in the chain's outlets, often accompanied by pickles or offered alongside the restaurant's signature ramen.

Nagasari is an Indonesian steamed cake made from rice flour, coconut milk, and sugar, with fillings of banana and chocolate or other ingredients wrapped in banana leaves. It is typically served as a sweet snack or dessert at room temperature, often sold as street food or in local bakeries.

Papeda is an Indonesian staple made by cooking sago flour with water or broth into a thick, sticky paste, typically served as a base starch in place of rice. It is traditionally accompanied by fish-based dishes and savory broths, particularly in the cuisine of Eastern Indonesia, especially Papua and Maluku regions.

Tongseng is an Indonesian stew made by simmering meat (typically goat or lamb) with aromatic spices including galangal, garlic, shallots, and chilies, often finished with sweet soy sauce and coconut milk. The dish is traditionally served with rice and is popular in Central Java, particularly as a casual meal or street food.

Oncom is a fermented food made from peanut or soy pulp that is inoculated with Neurospora mold and left to ferment, resulting in a cake-like product with a reddish-orange color. It is typically sliced and fried until crispy, then served as a side dish or main protein component in Indonesian meals, often accompanied by rice and sambal.

Sformato is an Italian savory custard or mousse typically made with eggs, cream, and pureed vegetables or meat, baked in a mold until set. It is traditionally served as a primo or contorno (side dish) in Italian cuisine, often unmolded and accompanied by a light sauce.

Stroganina is a traditional Russian Arctic dish consisting of raw fish or reindeer meat that is thinly sliced or shaved and typically served frozen. The dish is prepared by partially freezing the protein, then slicing it into thin strips, and is commonly served with salt, pepper, and mustard as condiments.

Khychyn is a Caucasian filled pastry made from dough that is stuffed with cheese and herbs, then either baked or fried until golden. It is traditionally served warm as a savory snack or appetizer in Circassian and North Caucasian cuisines.

Olive all'ascolana are large green olives from Ascoli Piceno that are pitted, filled with a mixture of seasoned ground meat, cheese, and breadcrumbs, then breaded and deep-fried until golden. They are typically served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by lemon wedges.

Botok is an Indonesian dish where a mixture of grated coconut, spices, and fish or seafood is wrapped in banana leaves and steamed until cooked through. It is traditionally served as a main dish or side dish in Indonesian cuisine, particularly in Java, and is often accompanied by rice.

Bubur kacang hijau is an Indonesian sweet porridge made by cooking mung beans with water or coconut milk until soft and creamy, typically sweetened with palm sugar and flavored with pandan leaves or vanilla. This dessert is commonly served warm or at room temperature as a sweet snack or light meal, particularly in Southeast Asia.

Sangrecita is a Peruvian appetizer made from coagulated blood, typically seasoned with cumin, garlic, and spices, then pan-fried until the exterior is crispy while the interior remains tender. It is traditionally served as a tapa or starter, often accompanied by lime wedges and served hot.

Pempek is an Indonesian dish from South Sumatra consisting of fish cake pieces made from ground fish and tapioca flour, shaped into various forms and either fried or boiled. It is typically served with a sweet and savory vinegar-based sauce called kuah cuko and accompanied by rice or as a street food snack.

Zuppa gallurese is a Sardinian bread and cheese soup made by layering toasted bread with beef or vegetable broth, grated cheese (typically Pecorino Romano), and sometimes meat or bone marrow, then baked until the bread absorbs the liquid and forms a cohesive dish. It is traditionally served as a rustic first course or main dish in Sardinian cuisine, particularly in the Gallura region.

cuisine: cuisine of the province of modena
tags: cuisine of the province of modena
description: Tigella is a traditional flatbread from the province of Modena, made from a simple dough of flour, water, salt, and lard, then cooked between two heated terracotta or metal discs. It is typically served warm, often filled with cured meats, cheese, or topped with garlic and herbs, as a street food or appetizer.

Pohulpohul is a traditional Batak snack from North Sumatra, Indonesia, made from roasted rice flour mixed with grated coconut and palm sugar, shaped by hand-pressing the mixture in the fist to form oblong pieces marked with finger impressions. It is commonly served at Batak customary ceremonies and gatherings, often presented alongside other traditional offerings.

aliases: сала т мимо за salat mimoza
description: Mimosa salad is a layered Russian appetizer made with grated hard-boiled eggs, processed cheese or mayo, carrots, and canned fish or meat, traditionally arranged in alternating colored rows and topped with a creamy dressing. The dish is typically served chilled as a cold appetizer or side dish at Russian celebrations and holiday meals.

Bakwan is an Indonesian fritter made by binding vegetables such as cabbage, carrots, and bean sprouts with a flour batter, then deep-frying until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by a sweet and spicy dipping sauce.

Coniglio alla sanremese is a rabbit dish from the Liguria region of Italy, prepared by braising rabbit pieces with tomatoes, olives, pine nuts, and local herbs. The dish is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by polenta or crusty bread to absorb the flavorful cooking liquid.

Pangiallo is a traditional Italian Christmas bread from Rome made with flour, honey, dried fruits (raisins and candied citrus peel), and nuts (pine nuts and walnuts), bound together with a sticky mixture of honey and sometimes spices. The dough is shaped into a flat round loaf and baked until golden, then traditionally served sliced during the Christmas season.

Tempoyak is an Indonesian condiment made from fermented durian flesh, prepared by salting and fermenting durian pulp to develop a pungent, umami-rich paste. It is traditionally served as a flavoring agent in curries, soups, and sambal dishes, particularly in Malay and Indonesian cuisine.

Arroz con costra is a Spanish rice dish where rice is cooked with broth and vegetables, then topped with a mixture of eggs that forms a crispy, golden crust when baked or fried. The dish is traditionally served as a main course in Spanish cuisine, with the contrasting textures of creamy rice beneath a crisp egg coating.

Pav bhaji is an Indian street food consisting of a spiced vegetable curry (bhaji) served with buttered bread rolls (pav). The bhaji is made by mashing boiled vegetables such as potatoes, peas, and cauliflower, then cooking them with onions, tomatoes, and a spice blend including pav bhaji masala, typically finished with a knob of butter.

Çılbır is a Turkish dish consisting of poached eggs served in a bed of strained yogurt, typically seasoned with garlic and salt, and topped with melted butter infused with Aleppo pepper or paprika. It is traditionally eaten for breakfast or as a light meal, often accompanied by bread for dipping.

Lumpia Semarang is a fried spring roll filled with a mixture of bamboo shoots, shrimp, and meat, wrapped in thin wheat flour pastry and deep-fried until crispy. It is typically served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by a sweet and tangy dipping sauce.

Agliata is a traditional Italian sauce made by grinding garlic with bread, vinegar, and olive oil into a smooth paste. It is typically served as a condiment with meat, fish, or vegetables, particularly in the cuisine of Southern Italy and Sicily.

Serabi is a sweet pancake made from a batter of rice flour, coconut milk, and sugar, cooked in a special two-sided mold over charcoal or gas flame until golden and slightly crispy on the edges. It is typically served warm with palm sugar syrup and grated coconut, often consumed as a breakfast or afternoon snack throughout Indonesia.

Unleavened bread is made from a simple dough of flour and water mixed together without leavening agents such as yeast or baking powder, then flattened and cooked on a griddle, oven, or open flame. It is a staple across many cultures worldwide, commonly served as an accompaniment to meals or used to wrap other foods.

Celery Victor is a cold appetizer consisting of blanched celery hearts dressed with a vinaigrette made from olive oil, vinegar, and anchovies, typically garnished with pimientos, hard-boiled eggs, and olives. The dish is served chilled as a starter and represents classic California cuisine from the early 20th century.

Boodog is a traditional Mongolian dish in which meat (typically goat or mutton) and vegetables are cooked inside a sealed animal hide or stomach using heated stones placed directly on the ingredients. The dish is served communally, with diners tearing open the cooked hide to access the tender, steamed meat and vegetables within.

Bāṭarsh is an eggplant-based dish originating from Hama, Syria, prepared by roasting or grilling eggplants until tender and blending the pulp with tehina (sesame paste) along with seasonings to form a smooth, savory purée. It is typically served cold or at room temperature as a mezze or appetizer, accompanied by flatbread.

Gurullos are a traditional Spanish pasta dish made from wheat flour dough that is rolled into irregular, thick strands or pellets, then boiled until tender. The cooked pasta is typically served in a savory broth or stew, often as part of rustic Spanish cuisine from the Castilla-La Mancha region.

Cacio e pepe is a Roman pasta dish made by tossing hot spaghetti with a creamy sauce created from Pecorino Romano cheese and coarsely ground black pepper, using pasta water to emulsify the ingredients. The dish is typically served immediately as a first course in Roman cuisine, with no additional ingredients beyond pasta, cheese, pepper, and salt.

Rengginang is an Indonesian snack made from sliced rice cakes that are deep-fried until crispy and golden brown. It is typically served as a savory accompaniment to meals or eaten as a standalone snack, often with sambal or other condiments for dipping.

Indonesian corn fritters are made by mixing corn kernels with a batter of flour, eggs, and seasonings, then deep-frying until golden brown. They are commonly served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by sambal or sweet soy sauce for dipping.

A Dagwood sandwich is a tall, multilayered sandwich constructed with multiple types of deli meats, cheeses, and vegetables stacked between slices of bread, typically served cut diagonally. The sandwich is eaten as a substantial meal, often requiring the diner to unhinge their jaw or use utensils to manage its height and contents.

Nasi ulam is an Indonesian rice dish made by mixing cooked rice with fresh herbs, vegetables, and sometimes grated coconut, typically seasoned with salt and served at room temperature. It is commonly eaten as a side dish or light meal, particularly in Malaysian and Indonesian cuisine, and can be accompanied by proteins such as fish or chicken.

Cốm is made from young, immature rice grains that are harvested before fully ripening, then roasted and ground into a fine, fragrant powder with a pale green color. This ingredient is typically used to flavor desserts, drinks, and ice cream, or eaten as a sweet snack mixed with sugar, and is primarily available during the brief rice harvest season in Vietnam.

Cabbage casserole is a baked dish made with shredded or chopped cabbage combined with a binding sauce, typically containing dairy products such as cream or cheese, and often layered with proteins like ground meat or bacon. The assembled ingredients are baked in a casserole dish until the cabbage is tender and the top is lightly browned, resulting in a hearty side dish or light main course commonly served in Central and Northern European cuisines.

Pecel is an Indonesian vegetable salad made by blanching or boiling vegetables such as cabbage, bean sprouts, and green beans, then mixing them with a peanut-based sauce made from ground peanuts, garlic, chilies, and spices. The dish is typically served at room temperature or warm as a side dish or light meal, often accompanied by rice and fried shallots.

Bánh cáy is a Vietnamese fried pastry made from a simple dough of flour, water, and salt that is rolled thin, cut into strips, and deep-fried until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a snack or breakfast item, often accompanied by sweetened condensed milk for dipping or consumed plain as a crispy accompaniment to savory dishes.

Eba is a West African staple made by mixing grated cassava with hot water or broth to form a smooth, dough-like consistency. It is typically served as an accompaniment to soups and stews, where it is eaten by breaking off small portions and dipping them into the accompanying sauce.

Khoresh karafs is an Iranian stew made by braising celery with lamb or beef, onions, and dried limes, creating a tangy and herbaceous sauce. The dish is typically served over steamed rice as a main course.

Cağ kebabı is a Turkish grilled meat dish where marinated strips of lamb or beef are threaded onto a flat metal skewer and cooked over charcoal, then sliced and typically served with flatbread, onions, and tomatoes. The meat is traditionally seasoned with spices and sometimes yogurt marinade before cooking, and is often accompanied by grilled vegetables and served as a main course.

Charleston red rice is a one-pot dish made by cooking rice with tomatoes, onions, and bacon or other cured pork, creating a savory tomato-based rice. It is a traditional Lowcountry dish from Charleston, South Carolina, typically served as a side dish or main course component in Southern cuisine.

Cơm nắm is a Vietnamese dish made by mixing cooked rice with grilled or fried meat, typically pork, along with herbs, vegetables, and a fish sauce-based dressing. The ingredients are combined and often shaped into a ball or mound, served as a casual meal or street food that can be eaten with bare hands or utensils.

Goyang is a traditional fermented vegetable preparation of the Sherpa people, made by packing leafy greens such as radish or mustard leaves into containers and allowing them to ferment over an extended period. It is typically served as a side dish or ingredient in soups and stews, providing a sour, preserved accompaniment to staple meals.

Snail liver is a dish made from the hepatopancreas of edible land snails, typically cleaned and then sautéed, grilled, or incorporated into sauces and pâtés with ingredients such as butter, garlic, herbs, and wine. It is served as an appetizer or delicacy in specialty preparations, often accompanying the snail meat itself or spread on bread.

Albóndigas de bacalao are meatballs made from salt cod that is desalted, shredded, and combined with breadcrumbs, egg, and seasonings before being formed into balls and fried or baked. These are typically served as a tapas or appetizer, often accompanied by a tomato-based sauce or aioli for dipping.

Palourdes marinières is a French shellfish dish consisting of clams cooked in their shells with white wine, garlic, shallots, and parsley in a shallow pan until they open. The dish is traditionally served in the cooking liquid as a starter or light main course, typically accompanied by crusty bread for soaking up the broth.

Chorizo a la sidra is a Spanish dish in which chorizo sausage is cooked in cider, typically with onions and sometimes apples or other ingredients. The dish is traditionally served as a tapa or appetizer, often accompanied by bread to soak up the flavorful cider sauce.

Lomo en adobo is a pork loin dish prepared by marinating and cooking the meat in an adobo sauce made with vinegar, paprika, garlic, oregano, and salt. The dish is typically served as a main course, with the meat becoming tender through the braising process in the flavorful sauce.

Rice with hare is a Castilian dish in which hare meat is browned and cooked together with rice, typically in a cazuela (earthenware pot), along with broth, garlic, and regional spices. The dish is traditionally served as a main course in rustic Castilian cuisine and reflects the region's hunting heritage.

Crawfish Monica is a pasta dish combining fettuccine or similar pasta with a cream and butter sauce enriched with cheese, typically served with sautéed crawfish and aromatic vegetables including garlic, onion, and tomato. The dish is seasoned with salt and peppercorn, creating a rich seafood pasta preparation commonly served as a main course in Creole and international cuisine.

Duelos y quebrantos is a traditional Castilian-Manchego dish made by frying diced ham, lard, and sometimes chorizo together with scrambled eggs, creating a hearty mixture. The dish is typically served as a breakfast or light lunch preparation, reflecting its origins as peasant fare from the La Mancha region.

A flauta is a flour tortilla tightly rolled around a savory filling, typically shredded chicken, beef, or cheese, then deep-fried until crispy. It is commonly served as an appetizer or main course, often accompanied by sour cream, guacamole, salsa, or other Mexican condiments.

Betutu is a traditional Balinese dish where poultry (typically chicken or duck) is marinated in a complex spice paste made from garlic, shallots, galangal, turmeric, and other aromatics, then wrapped in banana leaves and slow-cooked underground or in an oven for several hours until the meat is tender and infused with spice flavors. The dish is commonly served during ceremonial occasions and family gatherings in Bali, often accompanied by steamed rice and sambal.

Doodhpak is a sweet pudding made by simmering rice and milk together with ghee and sugar until the mixture thickens into a creamy consistency, typically flavored with cardamom and garnished with nuts. This dessert is traditionally served warm or at room temperature during festivals and special occasions in Indian cuisine.

aliases: poulet a l ecorce de mandarine
description: Orange chicken consists of bite-sized chicken pieces that are battered, deep-fried until crispy, and coated in a tangy-sweet sauce made with orange juice or orange zest, sugar, vinegar, and soy sauce. The dish is typically served over steamed rice or noodles as a main course in Chinese-American cuisine.

Empal gentong is an Indonesian beef stew made by slow-cooking beef chunks in a clay pot (gentong) with coconut milk, spices, and aromatics such as garlic, shallots, galangal, and chilies until tender. The dish is traditionally served hot in its cooking vessel and typically accompanied by rice, with origins in the Cirebon region of West Java.

Garlic fingers are bread strips brushed with garlic butter or garlic oil and baked until golden and crispy. They are typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by dipping sauce such as marinara or garlic aioli.

Gavvalu is a crispy, spiral-shaped snack made from rice flour dough that is deep-fried until golden and crunchy. It is typically seasoned with salt and spices, and commonly served as a savory appetizer or accompaniment to meals in Indian cuisine.

Confetti square is a no-bake dessert bar prepared by melting butter and peanut butter, combining the mixture with colorful miniature marshmallows, and pressing it into a pan to set before slicing into squares. It is typically served as a sweet treat at holiday gatherings, potlucks, and bake sales, particularly in Canadian home baking traditions.

Githeri is a traditional East African dish made by boiling maize and beans together until tender, often with the addition of onions and tomatoes. The mixture is typically served as a main course or side dish, particularly in Kenya and other regions of East Africa.

Bika Ambon is an Indonesian sponge cake made with eggs, sugar, and coconut milk, featuring a distinctive honeycomb texture created by baking soda and the specific mixing technique used during preparation. The cake is traditionally baked in a mold and served as a sweet snack or dessert, with a golden exterior and airy, slightly chewy interior characteristic of this Medan specialty.

Étouffée is a cooking method where shellfish is smothered in a rich, heavily seasoned sauce made from a roux base, onions, celery, bell peppers, and Creole spices. The dish is typically served over rice as a main course in Louisiana Creole cuisine.

Kolak is a traditional Indonesian sweet soup made by simmering vegetables and fruits, typically including pumpkin, sweet potato, and palm sugar, in coconut milk until tender. This dessert or light snack is commonly served warm during Ramadan or as a cooling beverage in other seasons.

Zeytoon parvardeh is an Iranian dish of olives that are cured and preserved through fermentation or brining, often combined with herbs and spices. It is typically served as a condiment or appetizer alongside bread, cheese, and other mezze items.

Sugared cranberries are made by coating fresh or dried cranberries with powdered sugar, creating a candied confection with a crisp sweet exterior. They are typically served as a garnish for desserts and beverages, or consumed as a sweet snack.

Sheki halva is an Azerbaijani confection made by cooking hazelnuts with white sugar and cardamom until the mixture reaches a thick, paste-like consistency. The preparation is traditionally served in small pieces as a sweet treat, often offered with tea or coffee.

Poke is a Hawaiian dish of cubed raw fish, typically ahi tuna, marinated in soy sauce, sesame oil, and other seasonings such as ginger and scallions. It is traditionally served as an appetizer or light meal, often over rice or as a standalone bowl with vegetables and seaweed.

Poi is a Hawaiian staple made by cooking taro root until soft, then mashing and mixing it with water to achieve a smooth, paste-like consistency. It is traditionally served as a side dish or staple carbohydrate at Hawaiian meals, often eaten with the fingers or as an accompaniment to fish and other main dishes.

Apfelküchle are deep-fried pastry fritters made with sliced apples that are coated in batter and fried until golden brown. They are traditionally served as a dessert or snack, often dusted with powdered sugar and sometimes accompanied by vanilla sauce or applesauce.

Bubur cha cha is a Malaysian dessert made by cooking taro, sweet potato, and other root vegetables in a sweet coconut broth thickened with tapioca flour, typically garnished with crushed ice and evaporated milk. This warm or chilled pudding is commonly served as a popular street dessert or refreshment throughout Malaysia and Singapore.

Fresh salmon fillet is cooked using a broiling method, which exposes the fish to direct heat from above until the flesh becomes opaque and flakes easily. The dish is typically seasoned simply with salt, pepper, and lemon, then served as a main course, often accompanied by vegetables and a starch.

Mango float is a Filipino dessert made by layering graham crackers soaked in condensed milk and evaporated milk with whipped cream and fresh mango pieces. The assembled dessert is refrigerated until chilled and served as a no-bake treat, typically at Filipino celebrations and family gatherings.

Lingonberry pie is a traditional Nordic dessert made with a pastry crust filled with tart lingonberry jam or compote, typically baked until the crust is golden. The pie is commonly served warm or at room temperature with whipped cream or vanilla sauce as an accompaniment.

An appelflap is a Dutch pastry made by wrapping spiced apple filling in puff pastry and deep-frying or baking until golden. It is typically served warm as a snack or dessert, often dusted with powdered sugar.

Salčići are small, thin sausages made from minced meat (typically pork or beef) mixed with spices and seasonings, then stuffed into casings and grilled or fried until browned. They are commonly served as a main dish or street food, often accompanied by bread, onions, and condiments such as mustard or ajvar.

Zozulia is a traditional Ukrainian sweet porridge prepared by cooking millet until tender and combining it with ground poppy seeds, typically sweetened with honey or sugar. It is commonly served as a dessert or festive dish, particularly in the context of Ukrainian holiday and ritual cuisine.

Apfeltaschen are pastry parcels made by encasing spiced apple filling in puff pastry or yeast dough, then baking until golden. These hand-sized pastries are typically served as a dessert or sweet snack, either warm or at room temperature, often dusted with powdered sugar.

Osmanthus cake is a traditional Chinese pastry made with glutinous rice flour dough filled with a sweet osmanthus flower jam or paste, often combined with sugar and lard. The cake is typically steamed or baked until cooked through and served as a dessert or dim sum item, particularly popular during autumn when osmanthus flowers are in season.

Macaroni salad is prepared by cooking elbow macaroni pasta and combining it with a creamy dressing typically made from mayonnaise, vinegar, and seasonings, along with diced vegetables such as celery, onions, and bell peppers. The salad is chilled and served as a cold side dish, commonly found in Hawaiian, Asian, and international cuisines.

Pfeffernusse are small, round German spiced cookies made from flour, sugar, and spices including black pepper, typically mixed with nuts and dried fruits, then rolled in powdered sugar. These cookies are traditionally served during Christmas and other festive occasions, often paired with coffee or hot beverages.

Koovappodi payasam is a traditional Kerala dessert made by cooking rice flour (or roasted rice powder) with jaggery, ghee, and coconut milk until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. The dish is typically served warm as a sweet course during festive occasions and religious ceremonies in Kerala cuisine.

Ohaw is a traditional Ainu soup made by simmering vegetables, grains, and sometimes meat or fish in a broth base, reflecting the indigenous food practices of Hokkaido. The dish is typically served as a warming staple meal, often prepared with seasonal vegetables and regional ingredients available in northern Japan.

Garlic soup is prepared by simmering garlic cloves with stock, often adding potatoes, bread, and cream or olive oil to create a smooth or rustic texture. The soup is typically served hot as a starter or light main course, with garlic as the dominant flavor and sometimes finished with croutons or fresh herbs.

Dosirac is a Korean one-pot rice dish prepared by cooking rice with various ingredients such as vegetables, meat, and seasoning in a heated stone or ceramic bowl, which creates a crispy bottom layer. It is typically served hot in the cooking vessel and eaten by mixing the contents together with gochujang (red chili paste) or soy sauce before consumption.

Octopus salad is prepared by cooking octopus until tender, then cutting it into pieces and combining it with vegetables such as tomatoes, onions, and celery, dressed with olive oil, lemon juice, and seasonings. The dish is typically served cold as an appetizer or light main course in Mediterranean and international cuisine.

Sopa Juliana is a vegetable soup made by julienning (cutting into thin matchstick strips) vegetables such as carrots, celery, and potatoes, then simmering them in a meat or vegetable broth. The soup is typically served hot as a light first course or light meal, common in Spanish and Latin American cuisines.

Ogbono soup is a West African dish made by grinding ogbono (African mango) seeds into a powder, which is then cooked with vegetables, meat or fish, and spices to create a thick, mucilaginous broth. The soup is typically served hot with fufu, cassava, or rice as an accompaniment.

Watercress soup is prepared by simmering fresh watercress (Nasturtium officinale) with stock and aromatics, then blending until smooth, often with added potatoes or cream for body. It is typically served hot as a starter or light course in European cuisine.

Kak'ik is a traditional Guatemalan soup made with chicken or turkey cooked in a broth flavored with cilantro, garlic, and dried chiles, typically thickened with ground pumpkin seeds. The dish is commonly served with corn tortillas and is particularly associated with K'iche' Maya cuisine and special occasions.

Vori vori is a Paraguayan soup consisting of small dumplings made from cornmeal, cheese, and eggs that are boiled in a meat or vegetable broth until they float to the surface. The dish is traditionally served hot as a comforting main course, often garnished with fresh herbs and accompanied by bread or rice.

Tibok-tibok is a Filipino dessert made from freshly grated young coconut meat mixed with brown sugar and cooked in coconut milk until it reaches a custard-like consistency, traditionally served warm in bowls. The dish is typically garnished with toasted grated coconut or latik (coconut curds) and eaten as a sweet snack or dessert, particularly popular in the Bicol region.

Ol vapa is a Bengali steamed preparation made from elephant foot yam (ol), which is grated or mashed and combined with mustard paste, green chilies, salt, and mustard oil before being steamed until set. It is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Seven-layer salad is assembled in a clear bowl or dish with distinct horizontal layers of vegetables, typically including lettuce, tomatoes, cucumbers, bell peppers, onions, peas, and cheese, often topped with mayonnaise or dressing and bacon bits. The ingredients are left unmixed and served chilled, with diners combining layers as desired when eating.

Pazhapradhaman is a sweet payasam dessert from Kerala made with ripe bananas, jaggery, and ghee, traditionally prepared by mashing bananas and cooking them with melted jaggery and clarified butter until caramelized. The dish is typically served warm as a festive dessert during Hindu festivals and special occasions, often garnished with fried banana chips or nuts.

Huzarensalade is a Dutch salad traditionally composed of cooked potatoes, pickled gherkins, onions, and mayonnaise, mixed together and served chilled. The dish is typically garnished with hard-boiled eggs and beetroot, and is commonly served as a side dish or light meal in Dutch cuisine.

Eggplant salad is prepared by roasting or grilling eggplant until tender, then mashing it into a paste-like consistency known as eggplant caviar, which is typically mixed with olive oil, lemon juice, and garlic. The dish is commonly served as a cold appetizer or meze course in Mediterranean and Middle Eastern cuisine, often accompanied by bread or crackers.

A warm chocolate cake is served with vanilla ice cream and topped with chocolate syrup, creating a contrast between the heated cake and cold ice cream. This dessert is typically presented as an individual portion and served at the end of a meal.

Bhetki macher malaikari is a Bengali fish curry made with bhetki (Asian sea bass) cooked in a creamy sauce prepared with coconut milk, yogurt, and aromatic spices including ginger, garlic, and green chilies. The dish is typically served with steamed rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Bhetki machher chop is a Bengali fish cake made from minced bhetki (barramundi) fish mixed with potatoes, onions, and spices, then shaped into patties, coated with breadcrumbs, and deep-fried until golden. It is typically served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by tamarind chutney or lemon wedges.

Chitol machher muithya is a Bengali fish dish made from chitol (Indian rohu) fish that is minced, mixed with spices and seasonings, then shaped into balls or patties and steamed or fried. The dish is typically served as a main course or appetizer in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by rice or bread.

Koi machh o sorse bata is a Bengali fish curry where fish is cooked in a mustard-based sauce made from ground mustard seeds and other spices. The dish is traditionally served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Machher dim er dhoka is a Bengali fish cake made by combining fish, eggs, and spices into a mixture that is shaped and fried until golden. The dish is typically served as a savory appetizer or side dish in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by chutney or as part of a meal.

Machher matha diye muger dal is a Bengali dish made by cooking mung beans (moong dal) with fish head as the primary protein and flavoring agent. The dish is traditionally prepared by simmering the dal with fish head, onions, and spices until both components are tender, and is typically served as a comforting curry alongside steamed rice.

Pabda machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry made with pabda (a small freshwater fish), prepared with onions, tomatoes, and spices including turmeric, cumin, and chili. The dish is traditionally served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Rui machher tok is a Bengali fish curry made with rui fish (a freshwater carp) cooked in a tamarind-based sour broth, typically flavored with mustard oil, nigella seeds, and green chilies. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as part of Bengali cuisine meals.

Machher dim bhapa is a Bengali steamed dish made with fish eggs (roe) mixed with spices and coconut, wrapped in banana leaves and steamed until cooked through. The dish is traditionally served as a main course with rice and is common in Bengali cuisine, particularly during festive occasions.

Rold Gold Heartzels are heart-shaped pretzel snacks made from baked wheat dough seasoned with salt, produced by the Rold Gold brand. They are a packaged snack food typically served as a standalone snack or alongside dips and are available in retail settings.

Cipera is a traditional Indonesian gulai-style dish originating from the Pakpak people of North Sumatra, prepared by simmering meat—typically chicken or buffalo—in a spiced coconut-based broth thickened with roasted and ground rice. It is commonly served with steamed rice as part of ceremonial meals and traditional Pakpak gatherings.

Yellow Soup is a traditional Leipzig dish historically served as the opening course at the annual banquet of the city council and councillors, a custom documented from around 1853. The soup derives its characteristic color from saffron and is typically prepared with a meat or poultry broth enriched with egg yolks, served in a formal, ceremonial civic dining context.

Fish cutlet is a breaded and fried patty made from ground or minced fish mixed with binders such as breadcrumbs and eggs, then shaped into a flat cutlet form. It is typically served as a main course with accompaniments such as potatoes, rice, or vegetables, and is common in Eastern European and international cuisine.

Doi katla is a Bengali dessert made by cooking fish (typically catfish) in sweetened yogurt with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and ginger. The dish is traditionally served warm as a festive preparation, particularly during special occasions and celebrations in Bengali cuisine.

Bhetki Paturi is a Bengali fish dish where bhetki (Asian sea bass) is marinated in a spice paste made from mustard, coconut, and green chilies, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed or grilled. The dish is traditionally served as a main course in Bengali cuisine, with the fish remaining moist and infused with the aromatic spices from the cooking process.

Pomfret machh bhaja is a Bengali-style fried fish dish made with pomfret (a flatfish) that is typically coated in a light batter or spice mixture and deep-fried until crispy. The dish is commonly served as an appetizer or main course accompaniment, often with lemon wedges and served hot.

Pabda machchher jhal posto is a Bengali fish curry featuring pabda (a freshwater fish) cooked with a spice paste made from mustard seeds (posto), turmeric, and chilies, creating a pungent and savory sauce. The dish is typically served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

This Bengali dish features fish roe (dim) from rui fish (a freshwater carp) cooked with bitter melon (uchchhe), which is sliced and typically stir-fried or simmered with the roe and spices. The dish is traditionally served as a main course accompaniment to rice in Bengali cuisine, combining the delicate texture of fish roe with the characteristically bitter flavor of the melon.

Asado is a method of grilling meat over an open flame or hot coals, traditionally using beef cuts such as ribs, flank steak, and offal, seasoned simply with salt. The dish is typically served as a social gathering meal, often accompanied by bread, chimichurri sauce, and grilled vegetables.

Blaue Zipfel are poached bratwurst sausages prepared by simmering in a vinegar-based broth with onions, typically served in their cooking liquid. The dish originates from Franconia in Germany and is commonly eaten as a main course, often accompanied by bread or potato salad.

Taco al pastor features marinated pork cooked on a vertical rotisserie (trompo), then sliced and served in soft corn or flour tortillas with pineapple, onions, and cilantro. The meat is typically seasoned with a blend of dried chiles, spices, and vinegar, and the dish is commonly garnished with lime and served as a street food or casual meal.

Russisch ei is a hard-boiled egg halved and filled with a mixture of ground meat, typically beef or veal, combined with onions, spices, and sometimes breadcrumbs, then breaded and deep-fried until golden. It is traditionally served hot as an appetizer or main course, often accompanied by a sauce such as mushroom or tomato-based sauce.

Sườn nướng is a Vietnamese grilled spare ribs dish where marinated ribs are cooked over charcoal or an open flame until caramelized and tender. The ribs are typically marinated in a mixture of fish sauce, garlic, sugar, and spices, then served as a main course often accompanied by rice and fresh vegetables.

Sautéed pork consists of pork meat cooked quickly in a hot pan with minimal fat, typically cut into thin slices or small pieces to ensure even cooking and browning. The dish is commonly served as a main course with vegetables, grains, or sauce, and is prepared across numerous cuisines with variations in seasoning and accompanying ingredients.

aliases: tripas a la moda do porto
dobrada a la moda do porto
description: Tripas à moda do Porto is a traditional Portuguese stew made with tripe, beans, rice, and carrots, slow-cooked together with seasonings until tender. The dish is typically served as a hearty main course in Porto and throughout northern Portugal.

Karbonade is a Dutch and Belgian meat dish consisting of beef or pork that is browned and then braised in a rich gravy made with beer or beef stock and onions until tender. It is typically served with potatoes, fries, or bread to accompany the flavorful sauce.

Macho Camacho is a Bolivian spicy dish typically prepared with dried llama or beef jerky (charque) that is shredded and combined with eggs, onions, tomatoes, and locoto or other hot peppers. It is commonly served with boiled potatoes and accompanied by llajua sauce, often eaten as a hearty main course.

Jeotgukgalbi is a Korean grilled short rib dish where beef galbi (short ribs) are marinated in a sauce made with salted fish or fish sauce (jeotgal), soy sauce, sugar, and aromatics before being grilled over high heat. The dish is typically served with steamed rice and banchan (side dishes) as part of a main course meal.

Tomato halves are hollowed and filled with a mixture of shrimp or prawns combined with mayonnaise, lemon juice, and fresh parsley. This chilled appetizer or light lunch dish is typically served cold on a plate, often accompanied by bread or salad.

Tava kebab is prepared by marinating meat (typically lamb or chicken) in yogurt and spices, then cooking it on a flat griddle or tava until charred and cooked through. The dish is traditionally served with flatbread, onions, and lemon, and is common in South Asian and Middle Eastern cuisines.

Bogobe jwa lerotse is a Setswana porridge made by cooking ground sorghum flour with water or milk to create a thick, smooth consistency, often sweetened with sugar or honey. The dish is traditionally served as a breakfast or light meal, either warm as a porridge or allowed to cool and set into a firmer texture.

Ants Climbing a Tree is a Chinese stir-fried dish made by cooking cellophane noodles with minced meat (typically pork or beef), aromatics, and soy-based seasonings until the meat clings to the noodles, creating the visual effect that gives the dish its name. It is typically served as a main course or side dish in casual dining contexts and Chinese restaurants.

Cazabe is a flatbread made from cassava root (yuca) that is grated, pressed to remove moisture, and then cooked on a griddle or hot surface until firm. It is a traditional staple food in Caribbean and South American cuisines, particularly among indigenous populations, and is typically served as an accompaniment to meals or eaten on its own.

Galbi-jjim is a Korean braised short rib dish where beef short ribs are slow-cooked with soy sauce, sugar, garlic, and sesame oil until tender, typically finished with vegetables such as carrots, potatoes, and chestnuts. The dish is served as a main course, commonly presented over steamed rice with the braising liquid spooned over the meat.

A meat pie consists of seasoned ground or diced meat baked inside a pastry crust, with fillings typically including beef, pork, or game combined with onions, herbs, and sometimes gravy or stock. It is traditionally served warm as a main course or light meal, often accompanied by vegetables or eaten as a portable hand-held portion.

Beef Rydberg is a Swedish dish consisting of diced beef and fried potatoes combined with fried onions, typically served with a raw egg yolk on top or mixed into the dish. The components are cooked separately and then combined, with the raw egg serving as a binder and enrichment when mixed together at the table.

Honetsukidori is Japanese fried chicken prepared with bone-in cuts, typically marinated and deep-fried until the skin is crispy while the meat remains tender. It is commonly served as a casual appetizer or main course, often accompanied by salt, lemon, or a dipping sauce.

Bhetki machher patapora is a Bengali fried fish dish made by coating bhetki (Asian sea bass) fillets in a spiced gram flour batter and deep-frying them until crispy. The dish is typically served as an appetizer or main course accompaniment, often with lemon wedges and accompanied by rice or bread.

Churrasco is grilled meat, typically beef, that is seasoned and cooked over an open flame or hot grill until charred on the outside. It is traditionally served sliced, often accompanied by chimichurri sauce, grilled vegetables, and bread as part of a main course.

Philippine asado is a braised pork dish made by simmering cubed pork shoulder with soy sauce, vinegar, brown sugar, garlic, and bay leaves until the meat becomes tender and the sauce reduces to a glaze. The dish is traditionally served with white rice and is commonly prepared for celebrations and family meals.

Mostbröckli is a Swiss dried fruit snack made by thinly slicing fresh fruit, typically apples or pears, and drying them until they become chewy and concentrated in flavor. The dried fruit pieces are traditionally produced in autumn using the season's harvest and served as a portable snack or dessert confection.

Pichón de Cliza is a Bolivian dish consisting of young pigeon (squab) sourced from the Cliza region of Cochabamba, typically roasted or stewed and seasoned with local spices. It is traditionally served with sides such as rice, potatoes, or chuño, and is often featured at regional festivities and family gatherings.

A crêpe is a thin pancake made from a simple batter of flour, eggs, milk, and butter, cooked on a griddle until lightly browned. It is typically served either sweet with fillings such as jam, Nutella, or fresh fruit, or savory with ingredients like ham, cheese, or eggs.

Lasagna is prepared by layering sheets of pasta with ragù (meat sauce), béchamel sauce, and cheese, then baking until golden. It is a traditional Italian dish typically served as a main course, often accompanied by bread and wine.

Sauerkraut soup is prepared by simmering fermented cabbage with broth, typically including pork products such as ribs or sausage, along with potatoes and onions. The dish is commonly served hot as a main course in Central and Eastern European cuisines, particularly in German, Polish, and Russian traditions.

Labskaus is a traditional German-Scandinavian dish made by mixing corned beef or salt pork with potatoes, onions, and pickled cucumbers or herring, typically served with a fried egg on top and accompanied by pickled beets and gherkins. The ingredients are chopped and combined into a hash-like consistency, then fried until heated through, resulting in a hearty working-class dish commonly served for lunch or dinner.

Samlor prahal is a Cambodian fish soup made by simmering freshwater fish in a fragrant broth built from fish or chicken stock combined with kroeung, a traditional paste of garlic, shallots, and chilies, along with fresh herbs and lemongrass. The dish is typically served as a communal soup course, garnished with fresh vegetables and aromatic herbs, and eaten with rice as part of a traditional Cambodian meal.

Ciccioli are small pieces of rendered pork fat and meat obtained from the lard-making process, typically seasoned and cooked until crispy. They are traditionally served as a snack or appetizer in Italian cuisine, often eaten on bread or added to other dishes.

Caldo verde is a Portuguese soup made by combining potato broth with thinly sliced kale and typically finished with olive oil and sliced cured sausage. The dish is traditionally served hot as a starter or light meal, particularly in Portuguese cuisine.

Achu soup is a West African dish, particularly from Cameroon, made by cooking fish with palm oil, spices, and cocoyam in water to create a thick, flavorful broth. The cocoyam softens during cooking and thickens the soup, which is traditionally served hot as a main course, often accompanied by fufu or other starchy sides.

Abula is a Nigerian soup prepared by cooking beans with palm oil, peppers, onions, and seasonings including Maggi, iru (locust beans), and potash, which acts as a thickening agent. The dish is traditionally served hot as a main course, typically accompanied by a starch such as fufu or pounded yam.

Tom yum kung is a hot and sour soup prepared by simmering shrimp in broth with lemongrass, galangal, lime juice, and Thai chilies, typically finished with fish sauce and garnished with cilantro. The dish is traditionally served as a starter or light meal in Thai cuisine and is characterized by its bold balance of spicy, sour, and aromatic flavors.

Chicken pieces are coated in a buttermilk-based batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy. The dish is typically served with dipping sauces and sides such as fries or coleslaw, common in casual dining and fast-casual restaurants.

A snack wrap consists of a tortilla or flatbread wrapper filled with various ingredients such as vegetables, proteins, and sauce, then rolled tightly into a cylindrical form. It is typically served cold or at room temperature as a quick, handheld meal or appetizer.

Mille-feuille consists of layers of crisp puff pastry alternated with pastry cream, traditionally assembled in a rectangular shape and topped with a thin layer of icing. The dessert is typically sliced into individual portions and served as a pastry course or dessert in French cuisine.

Toheroa soup is a creamy soup made from toheroa clams, which are harvested from New Zealand beaches, typically combined with stock, cream, and aromatics. The soup is traditionally served as a starter or light meal and has been a regional delicacy in New Zealand for generations.

Ulin-Ulin is a traditional kue from Banjarmasin in South Kalimantan, Indonesia, prepared from a batter of glutinous rice flour and coconut milk that is cooked into a soft, custard-like cake and typically sweetened with palm sugar. It is commonly sold in traditional markets (jajan pasar) and served as a snack or accompaniment to tea.

Pizza carrée is a square-shaped pizza from pied-noir cuisine of Algeria, typically prepared on a rectangular tray with a bread-like dough topped with tomato sauce, anchovies, olives, and sometimes cheese before being baked. It is commonly cut into square portions and served as a street food or casual snack.

Telebhaja is a Bengali fritter made by coating potato slices in a spiced batter and deep-frying them in vegetable oil until crispy, with onions and salt incorporated into the mixture. The dish is typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by chutney or eaten on its own.

Ärter med fläsk is a traditional Swedish dish consisting of yellow pea soup served with salt pork or bacon. The pork is typically simmered with the peas and served as an accompaniment, making this a hearty main course commonly eaten on Thursdays in Swedish cuisine.

Beef stew is prepared by simmering cubed beef with vegetables such as carrots, potatoes, and onions in a seasoned broth until the meat becomes tender, typically requiring several hours of cooking. The dish is traditionally served hot in a bowl as a complete meal, common across many cuisines as a hearty comfort food.

The McPlant is a plant-based burger patty made from soy, potato starch, and other vegetable ingredients, served on a standard hamburger bun with lettuce, tomato, onions, pickles, and ketchup. It is prepared using the same cooking methods as McDonald's traditional beef burgers and served as a main menu item.

Semolina soup is prepared by cooking semolina flour in broth or water, often with added vegetables, herbs, and sometimes meat or legumes depending on regional variations. It is typically served as a warm starter or light main course, common across Mediterranean and Middle Eastern cuisines.

Torrone di Caltanissetta is a Sicilian nougat confection made from honey, egg whites, and roasted almonds or other nuts, layered with wafer sheets and traditionally wrapped in edible paper. It is typically served as a dessert or sweet treat, particularly during festive occasions and holidays in Sicily.

A crêpe filled with sliced bananas and topped with caramel sauce, flambéed with rum, and finished with whipped cream. Served as a dessert, typically presented warm to showcase the caramelized banana filling and melted caramel.

Bananas Foster consists of vanilla ice cream topped with a warm caramelized sauce made from butter, brown sugar, cinnamon, and sliced bananas, typically finished with rum or banana liqueur that may be flambéed tableside. The dish is traditionally served as a dessert with the sauce spooned over the ice cream while still hot, creating a contrast between the warm fruit and cold ice cream.

Bananas Foster cheesecake combines a creamy cheesecake base with caramelized bananas and rum sauce, typically featuring a graham cracker crust and topped with whipped cream. The dessert is served chilled or at room temperature and draws from the classic Bananas Foster preparation of flambéed bananas with butter, brown sugar, and rum.

A marquesita is a crispy, cone-shaped pastry made by wrapping thin batter around a heated metal mold, then filled with a combination of melted Edam cheese and dulce de leche or other sweet fillings such as chocolate, condensed milk, or marmalade. The pastry is typically served warm as a street food or dessert snack.

Boondi laddu is a spherical Indian sweet made from gram flour batter that is dripped through a special perforated ladle into hot oil to form small round droplets, which are then collected and bound together with sugar syrup or jaggery. These sweets are typically served as a festive dessert or offered as a religious offering during celebrations and festivals.

Wine cream is a dessert prepared by combining egg yolks with wine and sugar, typically heated over gentle heat while whisking until thick and mousse-like, then folded together with whipped cream. It is traditionally served chilled as a light dessert or accompaniment to fruit, cake, or other sweet courses.

Ube is a Filipino dessert made from ube (purple yam), typically mashed or pureed and combined with condensed milk, evaporated milk, and butter, then cooked until smooth and creamy. It is commonly served as a standalone dessert, layered in cakes, or used as a filling for pastries and ice cream.

Palm nut soup is a creamy Cameroonian soup made by extracting and cooking the rich oil from palm nuts, then combining it with meat or fish, vegetables, and spices to create a thick, savory base. The dish is typically served hot with starchy accompaniments such as fufu, cassava, or plantains, and is a staple in Cameroonian cuisine.

Lema is an Indonesian dish made from bamboo shoots that are finely chopped and fermented, then typically cooked with fish and spices. It is commonly served as a side dish accompanying rice in traditional meals.
