Khanom thuai is a Thai dessert made from a batter of rice flour, tapioca flour, and coconut milk that is steamed in small cup-shaped molds, creating a delicate custard-like texture. It is typically served as a sweet snack or dessert, often accompanied by a light syrup or served plain at room temperature.

Kashk e badamjan is an Iranian appetizer made from fried eggplant slices layered with yogurt or whey (kashk) and topped with a garlicky mint-infused oil and caramelized onions. The dish is typically served at room temperature as a meze or starter course.

Pârjoale are Romanian meatballs made from ground meat (typically pork, beef, or a combination) mixed with breadcrumbs, eggs, and seasonings, then pan-fried until browned. They are commonly served as a main course or appetizer, often accompanied by sour cream or tomato sauce.

Ilish shukto is a Bengali vegetable curry made by stir-frying mixed vegetables such as potatoes, eggplant, beans, and bitter gourd with a tempering of mustard seeds, cumin, and dried chilies, then simmering in a light gravy. This dish is traditionally served as a side accompaniment to rice and fish curries, particularly during meals featuring ilish (hilsa fish).

Mett is a German raw minced pork preparation made from finely ground pork meat, typically seasoned with salt, pepper, and spices, then spread on bread or rolls. It is traditionally served as a cold appetizer or light meal, often accompanied by onions, pickles, and mustard.

Orzotto is an Italian risotto-style dish made by cooking pearl barley with broth, butter, and cheese using the same gradual liquid absorption technique as traditional risotto. It is typically served as a first course or main dish, often with vegetables, mushrooms, or meat additions.

Olluquito is a traditional Peruvian dish made from ulluco tubers that are diced and cooked with charqui (dried cured meat), typically prepared with onions and served as a hearty main course. The dish is commonly found in Andean regions of Peru, where ulluco has been cultivated for centuries as a staple root vegetable.

Fırın sütlaç is a baked rice pudding made from rice, milk, sugar, and butter, traditionally cooked in an oven until it develops a golden-brown surface. The dish is served warm or at room temperature as a dessert, often accompanied by ground cinnamon or crushed pistachios on top.

A palate cleanser is a small, typically light food or beverage served between courses to neutralize flavors and refresh the palate. Common preparations include sorbet, granita, or a small glass of citrus juice or ginger ale, served chilled or frozen to reset taste perception before the next course.

Yellow rice is prepared by cooking rice with turmeric, saffron, or annatto, which gives the grain its characteristic golden color, often with the addition of broth, onions, and spices. This dish is commonly served as a side accompaniment to main courses in Latin American, Spanish, and Asian cuisines.

Čvarci are rendered pork fat cubes made by slowly cooking fatty pork meat until the fat melts and the meat becomes crispy and golden. These savory, crunchy pieces are typically served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by bread, or used as a topping for dishes like cornbread and potato preparations in Central and Eastern European cuisines.

Coburger Bratwurst is a German sausage made from finely minced pork, beef, or veal mixed with spices and traditionally encased in natural casings. The sausage is typically grilled or pan-fried and served on a roll with mustard and sauerkraut as a traditional street food in Coburg, Bavaria.

Ayam goreng is Indonesian fried chicken prepared by marinating chicken pieces in spices such as turmeric, garlic, and shallots before deep-frying until golden and crispy. The dish is typically served as a main course with steamed rice and sambal (chili paste), commonly found in Indonesian restaurants and street food establishments.

Eight treasure rice is a glutinous rice dish cooked with eight distinct ingredients such as dried fruits, nuts, preserved vegetables, and sometimes meat, traditionally prepared by lining a bowl with the cooked rice mixture and steaming until set. The dish is typically unmolded onto a plate and served as a sweet or savory preparation, common in Chinese cuisine for celebratory occasions and festive meals.

aliases: escalope de saumon a l oseille
description: Saumon à l'oseille is a French dish consisting of salmon fillet served with a sauce made from sorrel (Rumex), typically combined with butter, cream, or white wine. The dish is traditionally served as a main course, with the sorrel providing a tart, herbaceous flavor that complements the richness of the salmon.

Apam Barenda is a traditional steamed cake from South Kalimantan, Indonesia, made from a batter of rice flour, coconut milk, sugar, and yeast, with portions tinted in contrasting colors—typically white, red, and brown from palm sugar—then layered or swirled before steaming. It is commonly served as a snack or kue in jajan pasar (traditional market) offerings and is often presented during customary Banjar ceremonies and communal gatherings.

Spicy porridge is an Indonesian rice porridge made by simmering rice with water or broth until it reaches a thick, congee-like consistency, then seasoned with chili peppers, garlic, and spices for heat and flavor. It is typically served as a breakfast or comfort food dish, often accompanied by side condiments such as fried shallots, fresh herbs, and protein additions like eggs or meat.

Kue rangi is an Indonesian steamed cake made from a mixture of rice flour, coconut milk, and sugar, traditionally colored with natural food dyes to create its distinctive striped or layered appearance. The cake is steamed until cooked through and is commonly served as a snack or dessert at celebrations and gatherings throughout Indonesia.

Ayam goreng Kalasan is a deep-fried chicken dish from the Kalasan region near Yogyakarta, Indonesia, prepared by marinating chicken in a spice paste containing turmeric, garlic, and other aromatics before frying until golden and crispy. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and sambal, often accompanied by fresh vegetables or pickled condiments.

Beberuk terong is an Indonesian dish made from eggplant that is typically hollowed out and filled with a mixture of grated coconut, spices, and sometimes minced meat or fish, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed or grilled. The dish originates from Indonesian cuisine and is commonly served as a side dish or main course component in traditional meals.

Bubur ketan hitam is a traditional Indonesian dessert made from black glutinous rice cooked with coconut milk, palm sugar, and salt until it reaches a thick, porridge-like consistency. It is typically served warm or at room temperature, often topped with a drizzle of coconut milk or served alongside a separate coconut milk sauce for dipping.

Gushtab is a Kashmiri meatball dish prepared by mixing ground meat with spices and herbs, then shaping into balls that are poached or cooked in a yogurt-based gravy. It is traditionally served as part of Kashmiri cuisine, often accompanying rice or bread, and is commonly prepared during special occasions and festive meals.

Pilav is prepared by toasting rice in fat, then cooking it in broth until the liquid is absorbed, creating separate, fluffy grains with a delicate flavor from the cooking medium. This rice dish is commonly served as an accompaniment to meat, vegetable, or legume dishes in Turkish and broader Middle Eastern cuisine.

Chimole is a traditional Guatemalan soup prepared by simmering chicken or other meat with a dark paste made from charred dried chiles, spices, and ground seeds, creating a rich, complex broth. The dish is typically served in bowls with tortillas and is common in Guatemalan cuisine, particularly in the highlands.

Mala hot pot is a Chinese communal dining dish featuring a simmering broth infused with Sichuan peppercorns and chili peppers, creating a distinctive numbing and spicy flavor profile. Diners cook raw ingredients—typically thinly sliced meat, seafood, vegetables, and noodles—in the shared broth at the table, then dip them in personal sauce mixtures before eating.

Sate lilit is an Indonesian grilled dish made from minced fish or chicken mixed with grated coconut, spices, and herbs, then wrapped around bamboo or wooden skewers and grilled over charcoal. It is typically served as an appetizer or main course with peanut sauce and accompaniments such as rice or satay dipping sauces.

Bongkrèk is an Indonesian fermented tempeh dish made by fermenting soybeans with coconut and spices, resulting in a pungent, umami-rich product with a distinctive flavor profile. The fermented tempeh is typically served as a condiment or side dish, often fried and eaten with rice and other Indonesian meals.

Buttered rice is prepared by cooking rice in water or broth and then finishing it with butter, which is stirred through the grains to coat them evenly. This simple preparation is served as a side dish in international cuisine, commonly accompanying meat, fish, or vegetable main courses.

Omo tuo is a Ghanaian dish made by rolling cooked rice into balls and serving them in a rich peanut or tomato-based soup, typically containing meat or fish. The dish is commonly eaten for breakfast or lunch and is prepared by first cooking rice until soft, then shaping portions into balls that are placed in the accompanying broth.

A savory cake made from grated radish mixed with rice flour and other ingredients, formed into a block, sliced, and deep-fried until golden and crispy on the outside. Typically served as a dim sum dish or appetizer, often accompanied by dipping sauces such as soy sauce or sweet chili sauce.

aliases: wikipedia day nyc 2024 spherical wikipedia logo cake
description: This spherical cake is sculpted and frosted to replicate the Wikipedia globe logo, typically constructed from layered sponge or butter cake with buttercream or fondant covering decorated to resemble the puzzle-piece segments and multilingual glyphs of the emblem. It was created as a commemorative centerpiece for Wikipedia Day NYC 2024 and served at the event to attendees celebrating the encyclopedia's anniversary.

Spanish fricco is a Westphalian stew made by slowly simmering beef with potatoes and onions until the ingredients become tender and the flavors meld. It is typically served hot as a hearty main course, often accompanied by bread or pickled vegetables.

Dadar gulung is an Indonesian crepe made from a thin, green-colored batter (colored with pandan leaves or spinach) that is filled with grated coconut mixed with palm sugar and sometimes corn. The filled crepe is rolled into a cylinder and typically served as a sweet snack or dessert.

Getuk pisang is an Indonesian sweet snack made by boiling and mashing ripe bananas with sugar and sometimes grated coconut, then shaping the mixture into cylindrical or oval pieces that are typically coated with grated coconut. It is commonly served as a dessert or between-meal treat in Indonesian cuisine.

Nasi Brongkos is an Indonesian rice dish prepared by cooking rice with beef or chicken, potatoes, and eggs in a seasoned broth until the liquid is absorbed, creating a one-pot meal with tender meat and vegetables. The dish is traditionally served as a complete main course, often accompanied by fresh vegetables and sambal on the side.

Ongol-ongol is an Indonesian sweet snack made from cassava flour and grated coconut, formed into small balls and boiled until they float to the surface. The cooked balls are typically served warm in a sweet ginger syrup or brown sugar sauce and consumed as a dessert or afternoon treat.

Kikil satay is an Indonesian skewered meat dish made from beef tendon (kikil) that is marinated, threaded onto skewers, and grilled over charcoal until charred on the outside. It is typically served with peanut sauce and accompanied by rice cakes or bread as a street food or appetizer.

Matang satay consists of grilled meat skewers (typically beef, chicken, or goat) that are fully cooked until well-done, seasoned with spices and served with peanut sauce for dipping. The dish is commonly served as a street food or appetizer in Indonesian cuisine, often accompanied by rice cakes or bread.

Sarımsaklı yoğurt is a Turkish yogurt-based condiment made by blending or stirring minced garlic into thick yogurt, creating a smooth, creamy sauce. It is typically served as an accompaniment to meat dishes, kebabs, and grilled vegetables, or used as a dipping sauce for bread.

Trilece is a layered dessert made with three types of milk—evaporated milk, condensed milk, and heavy cream—poured over a sponge cake base and left to soak. The dessert is typically topped with whipped cream and maraschino cherries, then chilled and served as a sweet treat in Middle Eastern and Turkish cuisine.

Horok-horok is an Indonesian dish consisting of grated cassava mixed with grated coconut, brown sugar, and spices, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed until cooked through. The resulting cake has a dense, slightly sweet texture and is typically served as a dessert or snack in Indonesian cuisine.

Javanese fried rice is prepared by stir-frying cooked rice with a blend of spices including turmeric, garlic, and shallots, combined with proteins such as chicken, shrimp, or beef, along with vegetables like cabbage and green beans. The dish is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by a fried egg on top and sambals or chili paste on the side.

Seafood pizza is an Italian-style pizza topped with various seafood such as shrimp, mussels, clams, or squid, typically combined with tomato sauce, cheese, and olive oil, then baked in an oven until the crust is crispy. It is commonly served as a main course in Italian restaurants and coastal establishments, often accompanied by lemon wedges and fresh herbs.

Tapai singkong is an Indonesian fermented dessert made from cassava (singkong) that is cooked, mixed with yeast and sugar, then left to ferment for several days until it develops a sweet, slightly alcoholic flavor with a soft, custard-like texture. It is typically served as a sweet snack or dessert, either chilled or at room temperature, and is popular throughout Indonesia particularly in Java and Sumatra.

Düğün çorbası is a creamy soup traditionally made with lamb or chicken broth, chickpeas, lentils, and rice, thickened with a roux and finished with egg yolk and lemon juice. The soup is typically served at Turkish weddings and special occasions as a starter course, often garnished with fresh mint and red pepper.

Corny is a trademarked brand of granola bar made from pressed rolled oats bound with syrup or honey and combined with ingredients such as nuts, dried fruit, or chocolate. It is typically sold pre-packaged as a portable snack eaten between meals or as a quick breakfast item.

Sisu is a Finnish pastille candy manufactured by Cloetta Suomi, produced by compressing powdered sugar with flavorings such as menthol, eucalyptus, or salty ammonium chloride (salmiak) into small, hard lozenges. The pastilles are sold in small tins or packets and typically consumed individually as a breath freshener or throat soothing sweet.

Boal machh bhapa is a Bengali steamed fish dish made with boal (a freshwater fish) wrapped in mustard paste and cooked en papillote or in banana leaves. The fish is typically seasoned with mustard seeds, green chilies, and spices, then steamed until tender, and is commonly served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Ilish machher tok is a Bengali fish curry prepared with hilsa fish (ilish) cooked in a tamarind-based sour gravy, typically featuring onions, green chilies, and traditional spices. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is a popular preparation during the hilsa fishing season in Bengal.

This Bengali fish curry features magur (catfish) cooked in a spiced broth with cabbage (fulkopi) as the primary vegetable, creating a savory and mildly tangy dish. The fish and vegetables are simmered together with spices and typically served with rice as an everyday home-cooked meal in Bengali cuisine.

Koi machh bhapa is a Bengali steamed fish dish where fish is wrapped in mustard paste, turmeric, and spices before being steamed in banana leaves or parchment paper. The dish is traditionally served with rice and is a staple preparation in Bengali cuisine that preserves the delicate flavor of the fish while infusing it with aromatic seasonings.

Ritha machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with freshwater fish, typically carp, cooked in a light broth flavored with turmeric, mustard oil, and aromatic spices. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: shol machher matha diye enchor er torkari
displayName: Shol machher matha diye enchor er torkari
aliases: shol machher matha diye enchor er torkari
description: This Bengali dish combines fish head, typically from shol (snakehead fish), cooked with sour tamarind paste and spices to create a tangy curry. The preparation involves simmering the fish head with onions, garlic, and other aromatics in a tamarind-based broth, and it is traditionally served with rice as a main course.

Bele machher sorshe jhal is a Bengali fish curry made with hilsa fish cooked in a sauce of mustard seeds, mustard paste, and green chilies, creating a pungent and spicy preparation. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is a staple in Bengali cuisine, particularly popular during the hilsa fishing season.

canonicalName: ilish machher matha diye kochu shaag
displayName: Ilish machher matha diye kochu shaag
aliases: ilish machher matha diye kochu shaag
description: This Bengali dish combines taro leaves (kochu shaag) cooked with hilsa fish head (ilish machher matha), creating a flavorful preparation where the fish head imparts richness to the greens through simmering together. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice and represents traditional Bengali cuisine from West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Turli Tava is a mixed vegetable dish from Central Asian cuisine, typically prepared by stir-frying or sautéing seasonal vegetables such as carrots, potatoes, onions, and peppers in oil or ghee until tender. The dish is commonly served as a side course or main dish accompaniment in Uzbek and Tajik cuisines, often alongside rice or bread.

Alu begun diye telchorchori is a Bengali vegetable stir-fry that combines potatoes and eggplant with onions, tomatoes, and spices, cooked together in oil or ghee until the vegetables are soft and well-integrated. The dish is typically served as a side dish with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Gule machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry where fish is cooked in a spiced gravy made with onions, tomatoes, and aromatic spices including turmeric, cumin, and chilies. The dish is typically served hot with rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: ilish machher matha diye chal kumror ghonto
displayName: Ilish machher matha diye chal kumror ghonto
aliases: ilish machher matha diye chal kumror ghonto
description: This Bengali dish combines rice cooked with pumpkin and seasoned with ilish fish head, creating a one-pot preparation where the fish imparts flavor to the vegetables and grains. The dish is typically served as a main course in Bengali cuisine, with the fish head providing both taste and texture to the mild, vegetable-based rice.

Cold borscht is a chilled soup made from beetroot, potatoes, cucumbers, and other vegetables, typically prepared by boiling these ingredients and then cooling the mixture before serving. It is traditionally served cold during warm months in Belarusian cuisine, often garnished with sour cream and fresh herbs.

Vepřo knedlo zelo is a traditional Czech dish consisting of roasted pork served with bread dumplings and sauerkraut. The pork is typically seasoned and roasted until tender, then plated alongside steamed or boiled bread dumplings and tangy sauerkraut as standard accompaniments.

Nawat is a type of candy prepared by boiling sugar into a concentrated syrup that is then crystallized or set into firm sweetmeat pieces. It is typically served as a confection alongside tea or offered during festive occasions.

Red beans and rice is a New Orleans classic made by simmering dried red beans with aromatics, spices, and often smoked sausage or ham until the beans break down into a creamy consistency, then served over white rice. The dish is traditionally prepared on Mondays using leftover meat from Sunday dinner and is seasoned with cayenne pepper, bay leaves, and other Creole spices.

Polish potato pancakes are made from grated raw potatoes mixed with onions, eggs, and flour, then pan-fried until golden and crispy on both sides. They are traditionally served hot as a side dish or appetizer, often topped with sour cream or applesauce.

An amuse-bouche is a small, single-bite appetizer prepared by the chef and served complimentary before the meal begins, typically featuring refined technique and carefully selected ingredients. It is designed to stimulate the palate and set the tone for the dining experience, often showcasing the restaurant's culinary style through unexpected flavor combinations or elegant plating.

Noon chai is a traditional tea beverage prepared by simmering black tea with milk, baking soda, and salt, creating a frothy pink mixture. It is commonly served in Kashmir as a breakfast drink or social beverage, typically accompanied by bread or savory snacks.

Chingrir khichudi is a Bengali dish made with rice and lentils cooked together with shrimp, typically seasoned with turmeric, cumin, and other spices. The dish is traditionally served as a comforting one-pot meal, often accompanied by fried onions and a side of yogurt or pickles.

Olivier salad is a traditional Russian dish made by combining diced boiled potatoes, carrots, peas, eggs, and pickled cucumbers with mayonnaise and sometimes ham or chicken. It is typically served chilled as a side dish or appetizer, particularly during festive occasions and New Year celebrations.

Zwiebelrostbraten is a German dish consisting of beef steak topped with a thick layer of caramelized onions, prepared by searing the meat and then cooking it with slowly caramelized onions until tender. The dish is typically served with boiled potatoes or egg noodles and is accompanied by a pan sauce made from the cooking juices.

Gimbap is a Korean dish made by rolling cooked rice, vegetables, seafood, and other fillings in a sheet of seaweed (nori), then slicing the roll into bite-sized pieces. It is typically served as a light meal or side dish, often accompanied by soy sauce or gochujang for dipping.

Backhendl is a breaded and deep-fried chicken dish originating from Austrian and Bavarian cuisine, where chicken pieces are coated in breadcrumbs and seasonings before being fried until golden. It is typically served with lemon wedges and accompanied by potato salad, vegetables, or rice as a casual main course.

Zwetschkenknödel are boiled dumplings made from potato or bread dough wrapped around whole fresh plums, typically served as a dessert or sweet course. The dumplings are traditionally dusted with powdered sugar and breadcrumbs fried in butter, and accompanied by sour cream or custard sauce.

Biangbiang noodles are a traditional Chinese hand-pulled wheat noodle dish from the Shaanxi province, characterized by thick, irregular, flat noodles that are stretched and slapped against a work surface during preparation. The noodles are typically served in a savory broth or with a spiced oil-based sauce, often topped with vegetables, garlic, and chili oil.

Bánh bao is a Vietnamese steamed bun made from a soft, pillowy dough that is filled with minced meat, typically pork, along with onions and seasonings. The buns are steamed until cooked through and served as a popular breakfast item, snack, or light meal, often accompanied by pickled vegetables or a dipping sauce.

Sport slice (Sport szelet) is a Hungarian chocolate bar made from a rum-flavored filling of cocoa, ground biscuits, and sugar, coated in a thin layer of dark chocolate. It is commonly sold as a small, individually wrapped snack and eaten on its own as a sweet treat or with coffee.

Rose's is a brand of marmalade produced by boiling citrus fruits, typically limes or oranges, with sugar and water until the mixture sets into a preserve containing fruit peel. It is commonly served spread on toast, scones, or bread at breakfast, and is also used as an ingredient in baking and glazes.

Utopenec is a Czech and Slovak dish consisting of sausage, typically a smoked variety, that is pickled in vinegar brine with onions, spices, and sometimes other vegetables. It is served cold as an appetizer or snack, commonly accompanied by bread or as part of a charcuterie selection.

Bauernfrühstück is a German breakfast dish made by frying diced potatoes, onions, and cured meat (typically ham or bacon) together in a skillet until golden and crispy. The mixture is commonly topped with a fried egg and served as a hearty breakfast or brunch meal, often accompanied by bread or rolls.

Tarta de Santiago is a traditional Galician almond cake made with ground almonds, sugar, eggs, and butter, baked until golden and typically topped with a cross pattern dusted with powdered sugar. The cake is served at room temperature as a dessert and is particularly associated with the pilgrimage route to Santiago de Compostela.

Mandu are Korean dumplings made with a thin wrapper filled with a mixture of ingredients such as ground meat, tofu, glass noodles, and vegetables, then either steamed, boiled, or pan-fried. They are typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by a dipping sauce made from soy sauce and vinegar.

Pea soup is prepared by simmering dried or fresh peas with water or broth, often combined with onions, carrots, and seasonings until the peas break down into a thick consistency. It is traditionally served hot as a starter or light meal, sometimes with bread or croutons on the side.

Tarte flambée is a thin-crust pizza-like dish from the Alsace region topped with crème fraîche, onions, and bacon, then baked in a wood-fired oven until the crust is crispy and the edges are slightly charred. It is typically served as an appetizer or light main course, often accompanied by white wine or beer.

Stoemp is a traditional Belgian and Dutch dish made by mashing potatoes with vegetables such as carrots, onions, or leeks, then mixing in butter and milk to create a creamy consistency. It is typically served as a side dish or light main course, often accompanied by sausages, bacon, or a fried egg.

Dondurma is a Turkish ice cream made from milk, sugar, and mastic resin, which gives it a distinctive chewy texture and slightly piney flavor. It is traditionally churned slowly by hand or machine while freezing and served as a frozen dessert, often eaten plain or with toppings.

Bun cha consists of grilled pork patties and pork belly served over rice vermicelli with fresh vegetables such as herbs and lettuce. The dish is typically accompanied by nước chấm, a fish sauce-based dipping sauce, and is eaten by dipping components into the sauce or mixing them together.

Hotteok is a Korean-Chinese fried pancake made by filling a simple dough with a sweet mixture of brown sugar, cinnamon, and chopped nuts or seeds, then pan-frying until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a street food snack or dessert, often eaten warm and sometimes accompanied by a sweet dipping sauce.

Turkish delight is a confection made by cooking a mixture of cornstarch, sugar, and water into a gel, then cutting it into cubes and coating them with powdered sugar or coconut. It is commonly flavored with rose water, lemon, or pistachio and served as a sweet treat or dessert accompaniment to coffee and tea.

Bánh chưng is a traditional Vietnamese square cake made by wrapping glutinous rice, pork, and mung beans in dong leaves (Phrynium placentarium), then boiling for many hours until the ingredients bind together. It is typically served during Lunar New Year celebrations and eaten in slices, either at room temperature or reheated.

Gbegiri is a Nigerian soup made from pureed beans (typically black-eyed peas or brown beans) cooked with spices, locust beans, and sometimes meat or fish stock until reaching a smooth, creamy consistency. It is traditionally served hot as a standalone soup or paired with other soups like egusi soup, and is commonly eaten with fufu, rice, or pounded yam.

Onsen tamago is a Japanese soft-cooked egg prepared by immersing it in hot spring water (typically 60-70°C) for 30 minutes, resulting in a creamy yolk and barely set whites. It is typically served as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by soy sauce, dashi, or salt for seasoning.

Salisbury steak is a ground beef patty mixed with breadcrumbs, eggs, and seasonings, then pan-fried until browned. It is typically served with gravy and accompanied by vegetables such as onions, mushrooms, or potatoes.

Foli machher bora consists of fish wrapped in leafy greens, coated in a spiced gram flour batter, and deep-fried until crispy. This Bengali preparation is typically served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by chutney or served with rice and curry dishes.

canonicalName: kancha kola diye shingi machher jhol
displayName: Kancha kola diye shingi machher jhol
aliases: kancha kola diye shingi machher jhol
description: Shingi machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with shingi fish (a freshwater catfish) cooked in a broth-based gravy typically seasoned with spices, onions, and tomatoes. The dish is traditionally prepared with unripe banana (kancha kola) which adds a slightly tangy flavor and is served as a main course with rice or bread.

Puya machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made by simmering fish in a spiced broth flavored with turmeric, ginger, garlic, and green chilies, typically finished with mustard oil. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as part of everyday Bengali meals.

Atlantic herring is cured and preserved in a vinegar-based brine, often with added spices such as bay leaves, peppercorns, and mustard seeds. This preserved fish is typically served as an appetizer or snack, commonly accompanied by onions and potatoes or as part of a cold seafood selection.

Aguachile is a Mexican ceviche-style dish made with raw shrimp or fish cured in a mixture of lime juice, fresh chiles, and salt, creating an acidic marinade that firms the seafood without heat. The dish is typically served chilled as an appetizer or light course, often garnished with diced cucumber, onion, cilantro, and avocado.

White boiled shrimp are shrimp cooked by boiling in salted water, typically until the flesh turns opaque and firm. They are commonly served chilled or at room temperature as an appetizer, in seafood platters, or as a component in salads and cold dishes across various international cuisines.

Angels on horseback is a classic English appetizer consisting of oysters wrapped in bacon and typically broiled or fried until the bacon is crispy. The dish is traditionally served hot as a starter course, often on toast points or with cocktails.

Chingri fry is a Bengali shrimp dish prepared by marinating shrimp in spices and then deep-frying until crispy, often finished with a coating of fried onions and garlic. It is typically served as an appetizer or side dish in Bengali and Indian cuisine.

Chingri posto is a Bengali shrimp curry prepared by cooking prawns with a paste made from poppy seeds (posto), onions, and spices, creating a rich and creamy sauce. The dish is typically served with rice or bread as part of Bengali cuisine and represents a traditional preparation method common in Eastern India.

Chingri machher jhuro is a Bengali preparation of shrimp cooked with grated coconut, spices, and typically onions and garlic, resulting in a dry curry with a textured consistency. The dish is commonly served with rice or bread as part of Bengali cuisine and features prominently in regional fish and seafood preparations.

Chingrir porota is a Bengali dish consisting of shrimp curry served with layered flatbread (porota), where the shrimp is cooked in a spiced gravy typically made with onions, tomatoes, and aromatic spices. The dish is commonly served as a main course in Bengali cuisine and Indian restaurants, with the crispy, flaky porota used to scoop and eat the curry.

A sushi burrito is a cylindrical roll made by wrapping sushi rice, raw or cooked fish, vegetables, and other fillings in a large sheet of nori (seaweed), typically prepared using similar rolling techniques as traditional sushi but with a larger format. It is served as a handheld item, often sliced in half, and may include ingredients such as avocado, cucumber, and sauces common in contemporary sushi preparations.

Bambara nut pudding is a dessert made from ground bambara nuts (also called earth peas) mixed with sugar and often combined with coconut milk or other binding ingredients, then cooked until it reaches a thick, pudding-like consistency. The dish is traditionally served as a sweet treat or dessert in West African cuisine, sometimes garnished with nuts or served chilled.
Portion of the body starting from the ankle and ending at the knee.

Crab legs are typically boiled or steamed until cooked through, then served hot, often with melted butter and lemon for dipping. The meat is extracted from the shell by cracking or using provided tools and is commonly served as an appetizer or main course in seafood restaurants.

Agidi is a West African cornmeal pudding made by cooking cornmeal with water until it reaches a thick, smooth consistency, then pouring it into banana leaves for molding and cooling. It is typically served as a breakfast dish or snack, often accompanied by a savory soup or sauce for dipping.

Thalassery biryani is a rice dish from Kerala, India, prepared by layering partially cooked basmati rice with marinated chicken that has been cooked with spices including cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves, then slow-cooked together until the rice absorbs the flavors. The dish is traditionally served as a complete meal, often accompanied by raita, pickle, and shorba (broth).

canonicalName: aliments marines a l huile d olive
aliases: aliments marines a l huile d olive
description: Marinated foods preserved and flavored in olive oil, a preparation method that involves submerging vegetables, seafood, or other ingredients in quality olive oil often with herbs and spices for extended periods. This dish is typically served as an appetizer or part of a charcuterie board, common throughout Mediterranean cuisine.

Bika is an Indonesian cake made from a batter of rice flour, coconut milk, and eggs, traditionally baked in a mold lined with grated coconut until golden brown. The cake is typically served as a sweet snack or dessert, with a moist, dense texture and coconut flavor throughout.

Okro soup is prepared by cooking okra with meat or fish, onions, and spices in a broth base, often thickened by the natural mucilaginous properties of the okra pods. The soup is traditionally served hot with fufu, cassava, or other starchy accompaniments as a main course in West African cuisine.

A Japadog is a hot dog topped with Japanese-inspired ingredients such as okonomiyaki sauce, bonito flakes, aonori seaweed powder, and mayonnaise, typically served in a standard hot dog bun. The dish originated in Vancouver, Canada, and combines North American street food with Japanese flavor profiles and garnishes.

Spaghetti with meatballs consists of long, thin pasta strands topped with seasoned ground meat balls that are typically browned and simmered in tomato-based sauce. This Italian-American dish is traditionally served as a main course, often garnished with grated cheese and fresh herbs.

Conventual sweets are traditional confections historically prepared in convents and monasteries, typically made with simple ingredients such as eggs, sugar, nuts, and dried fruits, often incorporating local herbs and liqueurs. These hand-crafted pastries and candies were traditionally served during religious celebrations and special occasions, and remain popular as artisanal gifts and desserts in various European regions.

Tinutuan is an Indonesian rice porridge made by boiling rice with water or broth until it reaches a soft, congee-like consistency, typically seasoned with garlic, ginger, and other aromatics. It is commonly served as a breakfast dish or light meal, often accompanied by side proteins such as chicken, eggs, or fish, along with fried shallots and fresh herbs.

Çoban salad is a traditional Turkish salad made by combining finely chopped tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and parsley with a simple dressing of olive oil, lemon juice, and salt. It is typically served as a side dish or appetizer, often accompanying grilled meats and kebabs.

Frit mallorquí is a traditional Majorcan dish of diced pork offal, typically including liver, heart, and kidneys, fried with potatoes and onions until golden and crispy. It is commonly served as a breakfast or lunch dish, often accompanied by bread and eaten as a hearty, rustic meal.

Grilled lapas are limpets (Patella vulgata) cooked directly over heat or on a hot surface until the meat loosens from the shell. This Portuguese preparation is typically served as an appetizer or snack, often with olive oil, garlic, and lemon for dipping.

Elbasan tava is a baked meat and vegetable dish from Albania, made by layering meat (typically lamb or beef), onions, and tomatoes in a clay pot, then cooking it covered in an oven until the ingredients are tender and flavors meld together. The dish is traditionally served hot in its cooking vessel as a main course, often accompanied by bread or rice.

Cheer is a traditional Ukrainian dish typically prepared by simmering maize flour with milk to create a smooth, porridge-like consistency. It is commonly served warm as a comforting breakfast or light meal, sometimes accompanied by butter, cheese, or sweet toppings depending on regional preferences.

Virado is a Brazilian dish made by cooking beans with pork or other meats until they break down into a thick, paste-like consistency, then mixing with cassava flour and seasonings. It is typically served as a hearty main course, often accompanied by rice, greens, and orange slices.

Migas alentejanas is a Portuguese dish from the Alentejo region made by frying cubed bread with diced pork, pork ribs, and bacon, then seasoning with garlic. It is traditionally served as a hearty main course, often accompanied by a fried egg or served as part of a meat-based meal.

Tetrazzini is a baked pasta dish combining cooked poultry meat with pasta in a creamy Parmesan sauce, typically topped with additional Parmesan cheese before baking until golden. This Italian-American dish is commonly served as a main course and is often made with chicken or turkey.

Soup joumou is a Haitian pumpkin soup made by boiling pumpkin with beef or other meat, along with vegetables such as celery, cabbage, and potatoes, seasoned with garlic, onions, and spices. Traditionally served on January 1st to celebrate Haitian Independence Day, it is a national dish with historical significance in Haitian culture.

Torikatsu is a breaded and deep-fried chicken cutlet made by coating thin slices of chicken breast with flour, egg, and panko breadcrumbs before frying until golden. The dish is typically served with shredded cabbage, tonkatsu sauce, and steamed rice as part of Japanese yōshoku cuisine.

Rujak cingur is an Indonesian salad from East Java that combines fresh vegetables such as cabbage, bean sprouts, and cucumber with a spicy peanut sauce made from ground peanuts, chili peppers, and shrimp paste. The dish is typically served at room temperature as a street food or appetizer, often topped with crispy fried shallots and accompanied by rice cakes or crackers for scooping.

Lacón con grelos is a traditional Galician dish consisting of cured pork shoulder (lacón) simmered with turnip greens (grelos), potatoes, and chorizo or other cured meats. The dish is typically served as a hearty main course, with the tender meat and vegetables combined in a flavorful broth.

Swikee is an Indonesian dish made from frog legs that are typically marinated in spices and then deep-fried until crispy. It is commonly served as a main course or appetizer in Indonesian cuisine, often accompanied by sambal or other dipping sauces.

Pastitsada is a Corfiote dish consisting of rooster or chicken braised in a tomato-based sauce seasoned with garlic, bay leaves, and red wine, then served over pasta or rice. The meat is slow-cooked until tender, absorbing the rich flavors of the sauce, and is traditionally presented as a main course in Greek cuisine.

Pesarattu is an Indian crepe made from green moong dal batter that is fermented and then spread thin on a griddle until crispy. It is typically served as a breakfast or snack item, often accompanied by ginger chutney and served hot off the griddle.

Watermelon steak is a thick slice of watermelon cut perpendicular to the fruit's axis, typically 1-2 inches in thickness. It is commonly served chilled as a refreshing dessert or light dish, sometimes with accompaniments such as feta cheese, mint, or a light dressing.

Pasta al pomodoro is prepared by tossing freshly cooked pasta with a simple sauce made from tomatoes, garlic, olive oil, and salt, sometimes finished with basil. The dish is typically served as a first course in Italian cuisine and represents a foundational preparation that highlights the quality of its few core ingredients.

Shrimp curry is prepared by cooking shelled shrimp in a spiced sauce made from curry paste or powder combined with coconut milk or broth, along with onions, garlic, and ginger. The dish is typically served over steamed rice or with bread, and may include vegetables such as bell peppers, tomatoes, or green beans.

Sate Padang is an Indonesian skewered meat dish from West Sumatra where marinated meat is grilled over charcoal, then served with a rich peanut sauce made from ground peanuts, garlic, chilies, and spices. The dish is typically accompanied by rice cakes or compressed rice, and the meat is often cut into small pieces before being threaded onto bamboo skewers.

Kesmaşî is a traditional Kurdish dish whose name suggests a preparation involving hand-cut noodles or dough pieces (from the root 'kesme,' meaning 'cut'), typically boiled and combined with yogurt, garlic, or a meat-based broth. It is commonly served as a warming main course, often accompanied by flatbread and seasonal greens in line with regional Kurdish culinary traditions.

Kuler ombol is a traditional Bengali chutney-style preparation typically made from Indian jujube (kul) berries, commonly cooked down with sugar or jaggery and tempered with mustard seeds, dried red chilies, and sometimes panch phoron for a sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy flavor. It is usually served as a side accompaniment at the end of a Bengali meal, often alongside rice and papad, and is especially popular during the spring season when jujubes are in harvest.

Rajas con crema consists of roasted poblano peppers sliced into strips and cooked with onions in a cream-based sauce, typically including Mexican crema and cheese. This dish is traditionally served as a side or main course component in Mexican cuisine, often accompanied by rice and tortillas.

Açorda is a Portuguese soup or stew made by combining bread, typically torn or sliced, with broth (usually vegetable or fish-based) and garlic, often finished with a poached egg and fresh herbs like cilantro. It is traditionally served as a rustic, hearty dish, particularly common in Portuguese coastal regions and rural areas.

Spanakorizo is a Greek dish made by cooking spinach with rice, typically prepared by sautéing onions and garlic before adding rice and spinach, then simmering with broth until the rice is tender. The dish is traditionally finished with lemon juice and olive oil, and is commonly served as a main course or side dish in Greek cuisine.

Pares is a Filipino beef stew made by braising beef chuck or brisket with onions, garlic, and soy sauce until tender, typically simmered for several hours to develop deep flavors. It is traditionally served over white rice as a breakfast or lunch dish, often accompanied by a clear broth on the side for dipping.

Tahu Sumedang is a fried tofu dish originating from Sumedang, West Java, made by deep-frying pressed tofu that has been stuffed with a mixture of spiced ground meat, typically beef, along with garlic, shallots, and spices. It is commonly served as a snack or appetizer, often accompanied by a peanut sauce or sweet soy sauce for dipping.

Zarda is a sweet rice dish prepared by cooking basmati rice with milk, sugar, and saffron, often with the addition of nuts and dried fruits like raisins and almonds. It is traditionally served as a festive dessert or special occasion dish in Pakistani and Indian cuisine, commonly presented at celebrations and religious events.

Sindhi biryani is a one-pot rice dish made by layering partially cooked basmati rice with marinated meat (typically beef or mutton), caramelized onions, yogurt, and spices including cardamom, cinnamon, and bay leaves, then slow-cooked together until the rice absorbs the meat's flavors. The dish is traditionally served with raita, pickled vegetables, and fresh herbs, and is characteristic of Sindhi cuisine from Pakistan.

Stuffed onions are prepared by hollowing out large onion bulbs and filling them with a mixture of ground meat, rice, herbs, and spices, then baking or simmering until tender. The dish is commonly served as a main course in Turkish and broader Middle Eastern cuisine, often accompanied by yogurt or tomato-based sauce.

Siomay is an Indonesian steamed dumpling dish made from a mixture of fish, shrimp, and tapioca flour wrapped in wonton or wheat flour skin. It is typically served with peanut sauce, sweet soy sauce, and cucumber slices as a snack or light meal.

Soto Madura is a traditional Indonesian soup from Madura that features beef or chicken simmered in a turmeric and spice-based broth, typically enriched with coconut milk and aromatic ingredients such as garlic, shallots, and galangal. The dish is commonly served with rice or flatbread and often garnished with fried shallots, fresh herbs, and hard-boiled eggs.

Sushi pizza is prepared by topping a crispy or chewy pizza base with ingredients commonly used in sushi, such as raw or cooked fish, avocado, cucumber, and rice, typically finished with soy sauce or wasabi drizzle. It is served as a fusion dish combining Italian pizza format with Japanese flavors and ingredients, typically cut into slices and eaten as an appetizer or light meal.

Bacha machher sorse jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared with young fish cooked in a tangy mustard and chili-based sauce, typically made with mustard paste, green chilies, and spices. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Bacha machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry made with young fish cooked in a spiced gravy based on onions, tomatoes, and traditional Bengali spices including turmeric, cumin, and chili. The dish is typically served with steamed rice or bread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: begun diye bok machher tel jhal
displayName: Begun diye bok machher tel jhal
aliases: begun diye bok machher tel jhal
description: This Bengali fish curry features fish cooked in a spiced oil-based gravy with eggplant (begun), tomatoes, and green chilies, resulting in a tangy and moderately spiced dish. The fish is typically simmered until tender and served with rice or bread as part of traditional Bengali meals.

Bokful ghonto is a traditional Bengali dish, typically prepared as a ghonto — a style of mashed or mixed vegetable preparation slow-cooked with spices such as cumin, turmeric, ginger, and bay leaves, often finished with a tempering of whole spices in mustard oil or ghee. It commonly features the bokful (agati flower) as its main ingredient, sometimes combined with potatoes or lentils, and is usually served warm alongside steamed rice as part of a home-style Bengali meal.

Foli machher sorse jhal is a Bengali fish curry where fish is cooked in a spiced mustard-based gravy with turmeric, chili, and other aromatics. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as part of traditional Bengali meals.

Mola machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared by cooking small freshwater mola fish with onions, tomatoes, and spices including turmeric, chili powder, and mustard oil, creating a light to medium-bodied gravy. The dish is typically served hot with steamed rice as part of everyday Bengali meals.

Kajoli machher sorse jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared with hilsa fish cooked in a mustard-based sauce that combines ground mustard seeds, turmeric, and green chilies for a sharp, pungent flavor. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is characteristic of Bengali cuisine, particularly popular in West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Kachki machher bhuna is a Bengali fish preparation where raw fish is marinated and cooked with a spiced paste of onions, ginger, garlic, and dried chilies, then fried until the exterior becomes caramelized. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or flatbread and is notable for its dry, intensely flavored preparation method that develops a crispy outer layer.

Kachki machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry made with raw or minimally cooked fish, typically hilsa or similar freshwater fish, cooked in a spiced yogurt-based or tamarind-based gravy with mustard oil, green chilies, and aromatic spices. The dish is traditionally served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine, particularly popular during the monsoon season when hilsa fish is in season.

Shol machher bhuna is a Bengali fish curry made by pan-frying shol fish (freshwater Indian catfish) with onions, ginger, garlic, and spices until the fish is cooked through and the sauce reduces to a thick consistency. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Pangas machher vuna is a Bengali stir-fried preparation of pangas fish cooked with onions, tomatoes, and spices including turmeric, cumin, and coriander. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or flatbread as part of Bengali cuisine.

Pangas machher tok jhal is a Bengali fish curry made with pangas (swai fish) cooked in a tangy and spicy gravy of tomatoes, green chilies, and mustard oil, often finished with a tempering of whole spices. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: narkel bata diye ban machher bhuna
displayName: Narkel bata diye ban machher bhuna
aliases: narkel bata diye ban machher bhuna
description: Ban machher bhuna is a Bengali preparation of freshwater fish stir-fried with a paste made from grated coconut and spices, creating a dry curry with concentrated flavors. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or flatbread in Bengali cuisine.

Tengra machh bhaja is a Bengali preparation of tengra fish that is typically marinated and deep-fried until crispy. The dish is commonly served as an appetizer or side dish in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by rice and curry dishes.

Tilapia machher tel jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared by cooking tilapia in a spiced oil-based gravy with turmeric, chili, and mustard, creating a tangy and moderately spiced sauce. The dish is typically served with steamed rice or bread as part of Bengali cuisine.

Tilapia fish cooked in a mustard-based sauce with green chilies, creating a tangy and spiced preparation characteristic of Bengali cuisine. The dish is typically served with steamed rice and features the fish simmered in the aromatic mustard gravy until tender.

Kotlet schabowy is a breaded and fried pork cutlet made by pounding thin slices of meat, coating them in breadcrumbs after dipping in egg, and frying until golden brown. It is typically served with boiled potatoes and cucumber salad or sour cream, representing a traditional Polish main course.

Kemplang is an Indonesian cracker made from fish (typically mackerel or other preserved fish) mixed with tapioca starch and seasonings, then formed into thin sheets and sun-dried until crisp. The dried sheets are deep-fried before serving as a crunchy snack or side dish, often eaten with sambal or as an accompaniment to rice meals.

Matar paneer is a North Indian curry made by cooking cubed paneer cheese and green peas in a tomato-based sauce seasoned with spices such as cumin, coriander, and garam masala. The dish is typically served hot with bread or rice as part of a main course.

Omo Tuwe is typically a hearty dish likely rooted in West African culinary traditions, commonly featuring a soft, dough-like staple made from boiled and pounded starches such as yam, cassava, or maize flour. It is often served alongside a richly seasoned soup or stew containing meat, fish, vegetables, and aromatic spices, intended to be eaten by hand in communal settings.

Panifoler payesh is a traditional Bengali sweet pudding, typically prepared by slowly simmering milk with rice or vermicelli until thickened, then sweetened with sugar or jaggery and often flavored with cardamom, bay leaf, and garnished with nuts and raisins. It is commonly served chilled or at room temperature as a dessert during festivals, religious ceremonies, and special family occasions.

Revuelto Gramajo is an Argentine scrambled egg dish prepared by mixing beaten eggs with diced potatoes, ham, and onions cooked together in a skillet until set. It is traditionally served as a breakfast or light lunch dish, often accompanied by bread or toast.

Tomatoes are halved or hollowed out and filled with a mixture typically containing rice, ground meat, herbs, and sometimes breadcrumbs, then baked until tender. This Italian dish is commonly served as a main course or side dish, often at room temperature or slightly warm.

Patatas aliñás is a Spanish potato salad made by boiling potatoes until tender, then dressing them while still warm with extra virgin olive oil, sherry vinegar, diced red onion, and fresh parsley. The dish is typically served at room temperature as a side dish or tapa, allowing the potatoes to absorb the vinaigrette.

Nettle soup is prepared by sautéing fresh nettle leaves with onions and potatoes, then simmering in vegetable or chicken broth until tender and blending until smooth. The soup is typically served hot, often finished with cream or sour cream, and commonly offered as a spring seasonal dish in Turkish cuisine.

Kerak telor is an Indonesian street food made by frying a mixture of duck or chicken eggs, shredded coconut, and spices in a wok until a crispy, caramelized crust forms on the bottom. It is typically served on a banana leaf and eaten as a breakfast dish or snack, often found in Jakarta and other Indonesian cities.

Kasha varnishkes is a traditional Eastern European dish made by combining cooked buckwheat porridge with farfalle pasta, caramelized onions, and schmaltz. It is typically served as a side dish or main course, particularly in Jewish cuisine.

Ketoprak is an Indonesian salad made from blanched bean sprouts, cabbage, and fried tofu cubes, typically bound together with a peanut sauce made from ground peanuts, garlic, and chilies. The dish is served at room temperature or chilled and is commonly eaten as a light meal or appetizer, often topped with crispy fried shallots and a squeeze of lime.

Jiggs dinner is a traditional Newfoundland boiled dinner consisting of salt beef or ham boiled with vegetables including cabbage, carrots, potatoes, and turnip in a single pot. The dish is typically served on Sundays or holidays with the cooking liquid reserved for gravy, and the meat and vegetables are portioned together on individual plates.

Zarb is a Middle Eastern dish in which lamb meat is marinated and cooked on a vertical rotating spit, similar to shawarma or döner kebab, then sliced and served in pita bread or flatbread. It is typically accompanied by vegetables, tahini sauce, and garlic sauce, and is commonly eaten as a casual meal or street food.

Yayla çorbası is a Turkish soup made with yogurt, rice or bulgur, and meat or vegetables, typically thickened with flour and egg to create a creamy consistency. The soup is traditionally served hot as a starter course and is common in Turkish home cooking and regional variations throughout Anatolia.

Bükme is a traditional Turkish pastry made by folding thin dough sheets with a filling of cheese, potatoes, or meat, then baking until golden and crispy. It is typically served as a savory snack or appetizer, often accompanied by yogurt or eaten fresh from the oven.

cuisine: cuisine of the province of almería
tags: cuisine of the province of almería
description: Patatas a lo pobre is a traditional Almería dish of thinly sliced potatoes and onions fried together in olive oil until golden and tender. The dish is typically served as a main course or side dish, often accompanied by fried eggs and sometimes cured ham or other proteins.

Hünkârbeğendi is a Turkish dish consisting of tender pieces of lamb or beef stewed in a tomato-based sauce and served over a creamy eggplant purée made from roasted eggplants, butter, and béchamel sauce. The name translates to "the Sultan was pleased," and it is traditionally served as a main course in Turkish cuisine, often accompanied by rice pilaf.

Lonkar chop is a Bengali-style fried cutlet, typically prepared by coating a spiced filling in breadcrumbs and deep-frying until golden and crisp. It commonly includes ingredients such as minced meat, potatoes, or vegetables seasoned with chilies and aromatic spices, and is often served as a street-food snack or evening appetizer alongside mustard sauce, kasundi, or salad.

Nasi liwet is an Indonesian rice dish prepared by cooking rice together with coconut milk, bay leaves, and other aromatics in a single pot, creating fragrant, slightly creamy grains. The dish is traditionally served with side accompaniments such as fried chicken, sambal, and fresh vegetables, commonly eaten for breakfast or casual meals throughout Indonesia.

Kuli-kuli is a West African snack made from peanuts that are roasted, ground, and mixed with seasonings including chili pepper, salt, and sugar, then formed into small balls or sticks. The snack is typically eaten as a savory-sweet treat or appetizer and is commonly sold as street food in Nigeria and neighboring countries.

Paçanga is a Turkish sausage made from minced meat, typically beef or lamb, mixed with spices and fat, then stuffed into casings and either grilled or fried. It is commonly served as a main course or in sandwiches, often accompanied by bread, onions, and traditional condiments.

Crab bisque is a creamy soup made by simmering crab meat, shells, and aromatics in stock, then pureeing the mixture and finishing with cream and seasonings. It is traditionally served hot as an appetizer course, often garnished with crab roe, croutons, or fresh herbs.

New York City pizza features a thin to medium crust topped with tomato sauce, mozzarella cheese, and optional toppings, baked in a gas or coal-fired oven at high temperature. The pizza is typically cut into 8 triangular slices and served hot as a casual meal or street food, often eaten by hand.

Ofe Owerri is a Nigerian soup prepared by cooking meat, fish, and snails with palm oil, peppers, and seasonings until the ingredients are tender and infused with flavor. The dish is traditionally served in bowls as a main course, typically accompanied by starchy side dishes such as fufu or cassava products.

Unripe plantain porridge is prepared by boiling peeled unripe plantains until soft, then blending or mashing them into a smooth, thick consistency, typically with added vegetables, protein, and seasonings such as onions, peppers, and stock. This dish is commonly served as a main course or breakfast item in West African cuisine, particularly in Nigeria and other regions across the continent.

Beans and corn porridge is a thick, savory dish made by cooking maize and beans together with water, palm oil, and seasonings including salt, onion, and pepper until they reach a porridge consistency. The dish is typically served as a main course, often with added fish for protein, and may include cooking banana for additional texture and flavor.

canonicalName: fura da nono millet and milk
aliases: fura da nono millet and milk
description: Fura da nono is a West African beverage made from millet flour that is mixed with water to create a paste, then combined with fermented milk (nono). The drink is typically served cold as a refreshing breakfast or midday beverage, particularly in Sahel regions including Niger, Mali, and northern Nigeria.

A burrito bowl combines the fillings of a burrito served over rice in a bowl instead of wrapped in a tortilla. Typically layered with seasoned meat, beans, rice, cheese, and toppings such as sour cream, salsa, and vegetables, it is eaten with a fork and commonly served as a casual lunch or dinner dish.

Tacos de cabeza are prepared using meat from the head of beef, typically including cheeks, tongue, and other facial muscles, which is slow-cooked and then shredded or diced for serving. The cooked meat is traditionally served in corn tortillas with onions, cilantro, and salsa, commonly found as a street food and breakfast item throughout Mexico.

Beef Wellington consists of a beef tenderloin coated with mushroom duxelles and pâté, wrapped in puff pastry, and roasted until golden. The dish is traditionally served sliced, accompanied by red wine sauce or gravy, as a main course in fine dining contexts.

Oeuf mayonnaise consists of hard-boiled eggs halved lengthwise and topped with a generous coating of mayonnaise, typically garnished with anchovies, capers, or fresh herbs. This classic dish is commonly served as a cold appetizer or light main course in French and European cuisine.

Lobster macaroni and cheese combines cooked macaroni pasta with a creamy cheese sauce and chunks or pieces of lobster meat. The dish is typically served as a main course, either as a standalone entrée or as a side dish at upscale restaurants and seafood establishments.

Pyzy are Polish potato dumplings made from grated raw potatoes mixed with potato starch and flour, then boiled until they float to the surface. They are typically served as a side dish alongside meat stews, particularly bigos or other traditional Polish meat dishes.

Lonkar Dolma is a Bengali-style stuffed dish, typically prepared by filling chilies or a similar vegetable with a spiced mixture that commonly includes mashed potatoes, grated coconut, or seasoned lentils, then steamed or lightly fried. It is often served as a side dish or snack alongside rice, dal, or flatbreads, accompanied by chutneys or mustard-based condiments characteristic of Bengali cuisine.

Bhapa pabda is a Bengali steamed fish dish made with catfish (pabda) that is marinated in a spice paste of mustard, turmeric, and green chilies, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed until cooked through. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread and represents a traditional preparation method from Bengali cuisine.

Parse machher tel jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared by cooking fish pieces in a spiced oil-based sauce with mustard oil, nigella seeds, and dried red chilies, creating a pungent and fiery flavor profile. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Parse machher tok is a Bengali fish curry made by simmering fish in a tamarind-based sour broth flavored with mustard oil, nigella seeds, and green chilies. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as part of Bengali cuisine.

Pabda machher sorse jhal is a Bengali fish curry made with pabda (a freshwater fish), mustard paste, and spices, typically prepared by cooking the fish in a mustard-based sauce with turmeric and green chilies. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Pabda machher rosa is a Bengali fish curry made with pabda (a small freshwater fish), traditionally prepared with a spiced tomato-based gravy enriched with onions, ginger, garlic, and mustard oil. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Pabda machher bhuna is a Bengali fish curry made by frying pabda fish (a freshwater catfish) with onions, ginger, garlic, and spices until the sauce reduces and coats the fish. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Pabda posto is a Bengali fish curry made with pabda (a small freshwater fish) cooked in a paste of ground poppy seeds, mustard oil, and spices including turmeric and green chilies. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as part of Bengali cuisine.

Topse machher paturi is a Bengali fish dish where fish is marinated in a paste of mustard, turmeric, and green chilies, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed or roasted until cooked through. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or bread, with the banana leaf imparting a subtle flavor to the tender, spiced fish.

Gondhoraj Ilish Paturi is a Bengali preparation of hilsa fish (ilish) wrapped in banana leaves with aromatic gondhoraj limes, mustard paste, and spices, then steamed or baked until cooked through. The dish is traditionally served as a main course in Bengali cuisine, with the banana leaf imparting subtle flavor while keeping the fish moist during cooking.

canonicalName: shim alu bori diye tengra machher jhol
displayName: Shim alu bori diye tengra machher jhol
aliases: shim alu bori diye tengra machher jhol
description: This Bengali fish curry features tengra fish cooked in a light broth with potato and bori (dried lentil balls), creating a savory and mildly spiced dish. It is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Sorshe diye khoyra machher jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared with freshwater fish (khoyra/koi fish), mustard seeds, and mustard paste as primary flavoring agents, creating a pungent and tangy sauce. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as part of everyday Bengali meals, particularly in West Bengal and Bangladesh.

An empanada is a baked or fried pastry pocket made from wheat or corn dough, typically filled with seasoned meat, cheese, vegetables, or other savory ingredients. It is commonly served as an appetizer, snack, or main course across Latin American, Spanish, and Caribbean cuisines.

Japchae is a Korean dish made with stir-fried sweet potato glass noodles combined with vegetables such as spinach, carrots, and mushrooms, seasoned with soy sauce, sesame oil, and garlic. It is typically served as a side dish or main course, either warm or at room temperature, and is commonly prepared for special occasions and family meals.

Mont d'Or chaud is a warm cheese dish featuring Vacherin Mont-d'Or, a creamy Alpine cheese from the Swiss-French border region, typically heated until soft and served in its characteristic wooden box. The cheese is traditionally served warm with bread, potatoes, or pickled vegetables for dipping, and is a popular winter specialty in Swiss and French Alpine cuisine.

Hochzeitssuppe is a German wedding soup traditionally prepared by combining beef or chicken broth with meatballs, pancake strips (Frittaten), and sometimes liver dumplings or noodles. The soup is typically served hot as a starter course at German celebrations and festive occasions.

Nobabi motorshunti is a Bengali-style dish typically prepared with motorshunti (green peas), which are commonly simmered in a spiced gravy featuring onions, ginger, garlic, and a blend of traditional spices such as cumin, turmeric, and garam masala. The 'nobabi' (nawabi) designation suggests a richer, aristocratic preparation, often incorporating ghee, cream, or cashew paste, and it is usually served as a side dish alongside rice, pulao, or Indian breads like luchi or naan.

aliases: libro di cucina ricette spaghetti alla carbonara
description: Carbonara is prepared by tossing hot pasta with a sauce made from eggs, cheese (typically Pecorino Romano or Parmigiano-Reggiano), guanciale (cured pork jowl), and black pepper, where the heat of the pasta cooks the egg mixture into a creamy consistency. The dish is traditionally served immediately as a main course in Italian cuisine, with the pasta coated in the rich sauce and topped with additional cheese and pepper.

Gâteau Basque is a traditional Basque pastry consisting of two layers of crisp, buttery shortbread cake with a filling of pastry cream or black cherry jam in the center. The cake is typically finished with a crosshatch pattern scored on top and served sliced as a dessert or afternoon treat.

Krautfleckerl is a Czech dish made from square egg noodles (fleckerl) mixed with sautéed cabbage, typically seasoned with caraway seeds and sometimes bacon or other pork products. It is traditionally served as a hearty side dish or main course, often accompanying roasted meats or served on its own.

Fabada asturiana is a traditional Asturian stew made by slowly simmering large faba beans with chorizo, morcilla (blood sausage), and pancetta until tender, creating a rich and hearty broth. The dish is typically served as a main course in a bowl, often accompanied by crusty bread, and is considered a staple of Asturian cuisine.

Osechi is a traditional Japanese New Year celebration dish consisting of multiple carefully prepared foods arranged in a tiered lacquered box, with each item selected for symbolic meaning and auspicious properties. The preparation involves cooking and seasoning various ingredients such as vegetables, fish, eggs, and legumes days in advance, allowing flavors to develop before serving at the beginning of the year.

Grass jelly is a dark green or black dessert made by boiling and cooling an infusion from Platostoma palustre (a mint-family plant), resulting in a gelatinous texture similar to jelly. It is typically served chilled as a refreshing dessert or beverage ingredient, often sweetened with sugar syrup and consumed in warm climates across East and Southeast Asia.

Amatriciana sauce is prepared by combining guanciale (cured pork jowl), tomatoes, and pecorino Romano cheese, with the guanciale rendered until crispy before the tomatoes are added. The sauce is traditionally served with tonnarelli or bucatini pasta and finished with grated pecorino Romano and black pepper.

Chili is a stew made by simmering meat (typically beef) with chili peppers, tomatoes, and spices such as cumin and garlic until the flavors meld into a thick consistency. It is traditionally served in bowls, often topped with cheese, sour cream, or onions, and commonly accompanied by cornbread or served over rice in Tex-Mex cuisine.

Gachas is an Andalusian dish made by cooking wheat flour in salted water or broth until it reaches a thick, porridge-like consistency, often enriched with olive oil and garlic. It is traditionally served as a rustic breakfast or light meal, sometimes garnished with cured meat or eaten plain as a simple comfort food.

Takoyaki are spherical battered fritters made from a wheat flour batter filled with diced octopus, tempura scraps, pickled ginger, and green onion, cooked in specialized takoyaki molds that create their characteristic round shape. They are typically served hot as a street food or appetizer, topped with takoyaki sauce, mayonnaise, bonito flakes, and aonori seaweed powder.

Pişmaniye is a traditional Turkish confection made by pulling and folding strands of flour that have been cooked in butter and sugar, creating a light, flaky texture with a delicate golden color. It is typically served as a dessert or sweet treat, often accompanied by tea or coffee, and is known for its distinctive thread-like appearance and melt-in-mouth consistency.

Grünkohlessen is a traditional German dish featuring kale that is typically boiled or braised until tender, often seasoned with salt, pepper, and sometimes mustard or vinegar. It is traditionally served in winter months, commonly accompanied by smoked meats such as sausage or bacon, potatoes, and bread.

Purvora lau is a Bengali dish that typically features lau (bottle gourd) as its main ingredient, commonly prepared by simmering the gourd with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and green chilies, often alongside lentils, shrimp, or small fish for added flavor. It is usually served warm as a light curry or stew accompanying steamed rice, forming part of a traditional multi-course Bengali meal.

Mixed pickle is a preserved condiment made by pickling a combination of vegetables such as cucumbers, onions, peppers, and other seasonal produce in a brine of vinegar, salt, and spices. It is typically served as a side dish or condiment alongside main courses, particularly in South Asian, Middle Eastern, and European cuisines.

Berner Platte is a traditional Swiss meat and vegetable platter originating from Bern, featuring boiled meats such as beef, pork, and sausages alongside sauerkraut, potatoes, and dried legumes. The dish is typically served as a hearty one-plate meal, often accompanied by mustard and bread, and represents classic Swiss-German cuisine.

Bibeleskaes is a traditional Alsatian dish made from fresh curd mixed with onions, caraway seeds, and seasonings, then typically served warm or at room temperature. It is commonly eaten as a main course or appetizer, often accompanied by bread or potatoes, and represents a staple of Alsatian cuisine.

Kongbap is a Korean rice dish made by cooking rice together with soybeans, creating a nutritious mixture with distinct textures. The dish is typically served as a staple side or main course, often accompanied by other banchan (side dishes) in Korean meals.

Hwajeon is a Korean pan-fried pancake made by coating flower petals and sometimes other ingredients in a thin wheat flour batter and frying until golden. It is traditionally served as a seasonal springtime dish, often enjoyed during spring festivals and holidays with tea or as a light dessert.

Schlachteplatte is a German charcuterie platter traditionally prepared from freshly slaughtered pork, featuring various cured and cooked meat products such as blood sausage, liver sausage, head cheese, and fresh pork cuts. The platter is typically served cold or at room temperature with bread, mustard, and pickled vegetables as an accompaniment.

Pig jaw is a cut of pork from the head region that is typically braised, stewed, or slow-cooked until the meat becomes tender and gelatinous. It is served as a main course in various international cuisines, particularly in Asian, European, and Latin American cooking traditions, often with vegetables and aromatic seasonings.

A muffuletta is a large sandwich made on round Sicilian-style bread filled with Italian cold cuts, cheese, and a distinctive olive salad relish made from chopped green and black olives, peppers, and spices. The sandwich is typically pressed or weighted down, allowing the flavors to meld, and is commonly served cut into quarters as a substantial meal or appetizer.

A quesadilla is prepared by filling a wheat tortilla with cheese and other ingredients, then cooking it on a griddle or skillet until the tortilla is golden and crispy and the cheese melts. It is typically cut into triangles and served as a main course or appetizer, often accompanied by sour cream, salsa, or guacamole.

Bouillabaisse is a traditional Provençal fish stew prepared by simmering various Mediterranean fish and seafood with saffron, fennel, orange zest, and garlic in a tomato and fish stock base. The dish is traditionally served in bowls with toasted bread, rouille (a garlic-saffron mayonnaise), and grated Gruyère cheese on the side.

Shuizhu is a Chinese cooking technique in which raw ingredients such as beef and vegetables are brought to the table in a pot of simmering broth infused with chili peppers and oil, then cooked and eaten immediately by diners. The dish is typically served communally as a hot pot experience where each person cooks and seasons their own portions to taste.

Rojak is an Indonesian salad made with a mix of blanched and raw vegetables such as cabbage, bean sprouts, cucumber, and long beans, combined with fried tofu and sometimes peanuts, all tossed together with a peanut sauce made from ground peanuts, chilies, garlic, and palm sugar. The dish is typically served at room temperature as a side dish or light meal, and is particularly common in Javanese cuisine.

Migas is a traditional Andalusian dish made by frying diced bread with garlic, paprika, and olive oil until crispy, often finished with eggs scrambled into the mixture. It is typically served as a breakfast or lunch dish, sometimes accompanied by grapes, melon, or cured meats on the side.

Coto Makassar is a traditional Indonesian beef soup from South Sulawesi, prepared by simmering beef with turmeric, garlic, shallots, galangal, and other spices until tender, then finished with ground peanuts or candlenuts for richness. The dish is typically served hot in bowls with the broth, accompanied by rice or soto bread, and garnished with hard-boiled eggs and fried shallots.

Hing koraisuntir tikki is a Bengali-style savory patty typically prepared with green peas (koraishuti) and asafoetida (hing), often combined with mashed potato, ginger, and warming spices before being shaped into small discs and shallow-fried until golden. It is commonly served as a tea-time snack or appetizer, usually accompanied by chutneys such as tamarind or coriander.

Leberknödel are boiled dumplings made from a mixture of liver (typically pork or beef), breadcrumbs, eggs, and seasonings, formed into balls and cooked in broth or water. They are traditionally served in clear broth as a soup course in German and Austrian cuisine, or as an accompaniment to main dishes.

Frankfurter Würstchen are small, finely ground pork sausages that are seasoned, encased, and typically poached or simmered in water. They are traditionally served hot, often accompanied by mustard and bread rolls, and are commonly eaten as a quick meal or snack in German cuisine.

Paprikash is a Hungarian stew made by braising meat (typically chicken, beef, or pork) with onions and paprika, creating a rich, red sauce that is often finished with sour cream. The dish is traditionally served with egg noodles, dumplings, or boiled potatoes to accompany the flavorful sauce.

A Bolivian baked pastry with a thin, crispy outer crust filled with seasoned meat, potatoes, peas, and hard-boiled eggs in a savory gravy. Typically served as a breakfast or snack item, often eaten by hand as the filling can be quite moist and flavorful.

Champon is a Japanese noodle soup made by cooking thick egg noodles in a rich broth typically prepared from pork bones, seafood, and vegetables. The dish is traditionally served in a single bowl with the noodles, broth, and various toppings such as pork, seafood, and vegetables, and is commonly found in Japanese ramen restaurants.
