Sour rye soup is a traditional Eastern European soup made by fermenting rye flour or rye bread to create a sour base, then combining it with broth, vegetables such as beets or cabbage, and sometimes meat or mushrooms. The dish is typically served hot as a main course or starter, often garnished with sour cream or fresh dill.

Mititei are grilled Romanian sausages made from a mixture of ground beef and lamb meat seasoned with garlic and black pepper, formed into small cylindrical shapes without casing. They are traditionally served hot off the grill as a main course or appetizer, often accompanied by mustard and bread.

Beef cheeks are a tough cut of meat that requires slow cooking methods such as braising or stewing for several hours to achieve tenderness, typically prepared with aromatic vegetables, stock, and wine or other liquids. The dish is commonly served as a main course in European and international cuisine, often accompanied by root vegetables, pasta, or polenta, with the cooking liquid reduced to create a rich sauce.

Charoset is a paste made from chopped nuts, apples, spices, and wine, traditionally prepared by hand mixing the ingredients together. It is served as part of the Passover Seder meal, where it symbolizes the mortar used by enslaved Hebrews in ancient Egypt.

A Galician empanada is a baked pastry pie with a thin crust filled with meat, seafood, or vegetables, typically seasoned with onions, peppers, and local spices. The filled dough is baked until golden and served in slices as a main course or appetizer, commonly found in Galicia, Spain.

Abacha is a Nigerian dish made from cassava that has been grated, fermented, and formed into a solid block, then sliced and mixed with a spiced soup or sauce typically containing leafy vegetables, fish, and locust beans. It is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by a side of cassava flour or eaten cold as a snack.

Taiwanese hot pot is a communal cooking method where diners cook raw ingredients in a simmering broth at the table, typically featuring a divided pot with different broth flavors such as spicy and mild varieties. Common ingredients include thinly sliced meats, seafood, vegetables, tofu, and noodles, which are cooked individually and dipped in sauce before consumption.

Nasi tumpang is an Indonesian rice dish where seasoned rice is cooked together with coconut milk and various ingredients such as beans, corn, and sometimes meat or seafood, creating a one-pot meal. The dish is traditionally served in a cone or mound shape and commonly eaten as a casual lunch or everyday family meal in Indonesia.

Petis is an Indonesian condiment made from fermented shrimp or fish paste that is cooked down into a thick, dark brown concentrate, often with added spices and sometimes palm sugar. It is typically served as a condiment or flavor enhancer with rice dishes, grilled meats, and vegetables, or used as a base ingredient in various Indonesian sauces and preparations.

Moskovski kartofel, meaning 'Moscow-style potatoes,' is typically a baked potato dish commonly prepared with sliced or cubed potatoes layered with ingredients such as cheese, mushrooms, onions, and a creamy sauce or mayonnaise-based topping. It is often served hot as a main course or hearty side dish, sometimes accompanied by fresh herbs, pickles, or a simple salad.

Moong Monohar is a Bengali dish typically prepared with moong dal (split yellow mung beans) that is roasted and then slow-cooked with a blend of spices such as cumin, ginger, and bay leaves, often enriched with ghee and garnished with raisins or coconut. It is commonly served warm as part of a traditional Bengali meal alongside steamed rice, luchi, or as a comforting accompaniment during festive occasions.

Fideos con caballa is a Spanish noodle dish featuring short pasta cooked with Andalusian mackerel, tomatoes, garlic, pimiento, and olive oil in a single-pan preparation. The dish is typically served as a main course and represents traditional Spanish coastal cuisine combining preserved or fresh fish with simple vegetable aromatics.

Maryland crab soup is a tomato-based broth typically prepared with blue crab meat, vegetables such as celery and onions, and seasonings including Old Bay spice. The soup is traditionally served hot as a starter course in Chesapeake Bay regional cuisine.

Cacciatore is an Italian braised dish where meat, traditionally chicken or rabbit, is slowly cooked in a tomato-based sauce with onions, garlic, and typically mushrooms and olives. The dish is commonly served with pasta, polenta, or crusty bread to accompany the flavorful sauce.

Kolbulle are Danish meatballs made from ground pork, beef, or a mixture, combined with onions, breadcrumbs, eggs, and seasonings, then pan-fried until browned. They are typically served as a warm appetizer or main course with gravy, pickled cucumber, and potatoes.

Romeritos is a Mexican vegetable dish made with romero (a leafy green plant similar to spinach) that is typically boiled and served with potatoes and shrimp in a mole sauce made from dried chiles, nuts, and spices. It is traditionally served as a side dish or main course during Mexican holidays, particularly Semana Santa (Holy Week).

Stir-fried milk is a Chinese dessert dish made by heating sweetened milk with cornstarch or egg white in a wok or pan over high heat, creating a custard-like mixture that is continuously stirred until it forms a creamy, smooth paste. The dish is typically served warm as a sweet course, often flavored with vanilla, almond, or fruit essences, and may be garnished with nuts or sesame seeds.

Balila is a Lebanese chickpea-based dish made by cooking dried chickpeas until tender and seasoning them with garlic, lemon juice, and spices. It is traditionally served warm as a breakfast or light meal, often eaten with bread.

Callos con garbanzos is a stew made from diced tripe that is cleaned, boiled until tender, and combined with cooked chickpeas in a savory broth typically seasoned with garlic, paprika, and spices. This hearty dish is traditionally served hot as a main course, particularly in Spanish and Turkish cuisines, and is often enjoyed at midday meals.

Cucumber salad is prepared by slicing or chopping fresh cucumbers and combining them with a dressing, typically vinegar-based or creamy, along with seasonings such as salt, pepper, and herbs. It is commonly served as a cold side dish or appetizer at meals, particularly during warm weather or alongside main courses.

Bibimbap is a Korean rice bowl topped with seasoned vegetables, meat, and a fried egg, mixed together with gochujang (red chili paste) before eating. The dish is typically served in a heated stone or ceramic bowl, which keeps the rice warm and creates a crispy layer on the bottom.

Rempeyek is an Indonesian snack made from rice flour mixed with spices and ingredients such as peanuts, anchovies, or shallots, then formed into thin, crispy wafers and deep-fried. It is commonly served as an appetizer, side dish, or snack with meals, often enjoyed alongside rice dishes or as an accompaniment to main courses.

Lampredotto is a Florentine street food made from the fourth stomach of beef, cleaned, boiled, and typically served sliced in a sandwich with green sauce (salsa verde) or other condiments. This traditional Tuscan dish is commonly eaten as a quick meal from food stalls and informal restaurants in Florence.

Pukis is an Indonesian steamed cake made from a simple batter of flour, eggs, sugar, and milk, cooked in a specialized mold that creates its characteristic round shape with a flat top and textured surface. The cake is typically served as a snack or dessert, often enjoyed warm with sweet toppings such as chocolate sprinkles, cheese, or condensed milk.

Bigos is a Polish stew made by layering sauerkraut and fresh cabbage with various meats such as pork, beef, or game, along with mushrooms, tomatoes, and spices, then slow-cooked until the flavors meld together. It is traditionally served hot as a main course, often with rye bread or potatoes, and tastes better on subsequent days as the ingredients continue to develop.

Kaeng ranchuan is a traditional Thai curry typically prepared by simmering a spiced paste of chilies, shallots, garlic, and aromatic herbs with meat or fish and vegetables in a seasoned broth. It is commonly served alongside steamed rice as part of a shared meal, offering a balance of savory, spicy, and subtly sweet flavors characteristic of Thai cuisine.

Sheem pur is a Bengali dish typically prepared by stuffing broad beans (sheem) with a spiced filling, often made from ground fish, shrimp, or seasoned lentils combined with aromatics like mustard, green chilies, and turmeric. It is commonly pan-fried or lightly steamed and served as a side dish alongside steamed rice and dal in a traditional Bengali meal.

Lángos is a deep-fried bread made from yeast dough, typically stretched thin before frying until golden and puffy. It is traditionally served hot with garlic and sour cream, or sometimes with savory toppings such as grated cheese and herbs, and is commonly eaten as a street food or appetizer in Hungarian cuisine.

Bean pie is a sweet dessert made with a filling of mashed beans (typically navy or lima beans), sugar, eggs, and spices like nutmeg and vanilla, baked in a pie crust until set. The dish is traditionally served as a dessert in African-American Muslim cuisine and communities, particularly associated with Nation of Islam food practices.

Loaded fries consist of french fries topped with melted cheese, cooked meat (typically bacon or ground beef), and various vegetables, finished with sauce such as gravy or sour cream-based dressing. This American dish is commonly served as a side dish, appetizer, or casual meal, often found in diners, fast-food restaurants, and casual dining establishments.

Tourte de blettes is a traditional sweet and savory pie from Nice filled with Swiss chard, pine nuts, raisins, and apples, bound with eggs and sometimes anchovies, baked in pastry until golden. It is typically served as a dessert or snack in Niçois cuisine.

Refried beans are made by cooking dried beans until tender, then mashing them and frying the paste in lard or oil until a smooth, creamy consistency is achieved. This Mexican staple is typically served as a side dish alongside main courses, in burritos and enchiladas, or as a dip with tortilla chips.

Lula kebab is a minced meat kebab made by mixing ground lamb or beef with onions, herbs, and spices, then molding the mixture onto skewers and grilling over charcoal or an open flame. It is typically served hot with bread, fresh vegetables, and yogurt-based sauces as part of Middle Eastern and Central Asian cuisine.

Ugali is a cornmeal porridge made by cooking maize flour with water or broth until it reaches a thick, dough-like consistency, often stirred continuously to prevent lumps. It is a staple carbohydrate in East and Southern African cuisines, typically served alongside vegetables, beans, or meat dishes for meals.

Hash is a dish made by chopping and finely mixing cooked meat, potatoes, and onions, then pan-frying the mixture until browned and crispy. It is commonly served for breakfast or brunch, often topped with a fried egg and accompanied by bread or toast.

Birthday cake is a layered or single-layer cake made from basic ingredients such as flour, sugar, eggs, and butter, typically baked and frosted with buttercream, fondant, or other icing. It is traditionally served at birthday celebrations, often decorated with candles and personalized elements, and shared among guests as a dessert.

Virgin boy eggs are chicken eggs that have been boiled in the urine of prepubescent boys, a traditional preparation from Zhejiang Province in China. The eggs develop a dark, translucent appearance with a distinctive sulfurous aroma, and are typically served as a street food snack or breakfast item.

aliases: cookbook pan fried white brined cheese saganaki
description: Saganaki is a Greek fried cheese dish made by coating slices of cheese, typically feta or halloumi, in flour and sometimes egg, then pan-frying until golden and crispy on the outside while remaining soft inside. It is traditionally served hot as an appetizer, often accompanied by lemon wedges, and may be flambéed tableside in some presentations.

Cuy chactado is a Peruvian dish of guinea pig that is flattened and grilled or fried until the skin is crispy while the meat remains tender. It is typically seasoned with cumin and served with potatoes, corn, and aji verde sauce as a traditional main course in Andean cuisine.

Daitokuji nattō is a Kyoto-style preparation of fermented soybeans mixed with miso, kombu seaweed, and sometimes candied vegetables, creating a thick paste rather than the typical stringy nattō form. It is traditionally served as a condiment with rice or used in Buddhist vegetarian cuisine, with origins in the Daitokuji temple district.

Peper plastic chutney is a Bengali-style condiment typically prepared by simmering thin, translucent strips of raw papaya with sugar, lime juice, and mild spices until they take on a glossy, 'plastic-like' appearance. It is commonly served as a sweet-tangy accompaniment toward the end of a traditional Bengali meal, often paired with papads and rice.

Pepe dolma is a stuffed dish from Bengali cuisine, typically made by hollowing out pepe (raw green papaya) and filling it with a seasoned mixture that often includes minced meat or spiced lentils, along with aromatics such as onion, ginger, and garlic. It is commonly simmered or steamed in a light gravy and served warm as a main course alongside steamed rice.

Krautkrapfen is a German fried pastry made by wrapping cabbage filling in dough, then deep-frying until golden brown. It is typically served as a savory snack or appetizer, often accompanied by sour cream or mustard for dipping.

A corn dog consists of a hot dog or sausage coated in a thick cornmeal batter and deep-fried until golden brown. It is typically served on a wooden stick as a casual street food or appetizer, often accompanied by condiments such as mustard or ketchup.

Krautshäuptchen are German cabbage rolls made by wrapping seasoned ground meat and rice in blanched cabbage leaves, then braising them in a tomato-based or sour cream sauce. The rolls are typically served hot as a main course, often accompanied by boiled potatoes or bread.

Topfenstrudel is a traditional Central European pastry made by rolling a thin sheet of dough with a filling of topfen (quark cheese), eggs, sugar, and often raisins, then baking until golden brown. It is typically served warm as a dessert, often dusted with powdered sugar and accompanied by vanilla sauce or sour cream.

Fërgesë Tirane is an Albanian dish made with roasted red peppers and tomatoes that are stewed together with cheese, typically white cheese similar to feta, and sometimes includes meat. The mixture is traditionally served warm as a main course, often accompanied by bread for soaking up the sauce.

Gefüllte Kalbsbrust is a veal breast that is butterflied or opened and filled with a stuffing mixture, typically containing breadcrumbs, herbs, and sometimes offal or forcemeat, then roasted or braised. This Central European dish is traditionally served sliced with pan gravy or sauce and accompaniments such as potatoes or vegetables.

Güllaç is a traditional Turkish dessert made from thin sheets of pastry layered with pomegranate seeds, chopped pistachios, and walnuts, then soaked in a light sugar syrup infused with rose water and topped with a cream sauce. It is typically chilled and served during Ramadan and special occasions as a cold, refreshing dessert.

Dirty rice is a Louisiana Creole dish made by cooking rice with seasoned pork, liver, and vegetables including celery and onions until the rice absorbs the meat's flavors and takes on a darkened appearance. It is traditionally served as a main dish or side in Southern United States cuisine, particularly in Cajun and Creole cooking.

Tavukgöğsü is a Turkish dessert made from shredded chicken breast that is cooked with milk, sugar, and cornstarch to create a creamy pudding-like consistency, then typically topped with ground pistachios or cinnamon. It is traditionally served chilled as a sweet course or dessert at the end of meals.

Intxaursaltsa is a Basque sauce made from ground walnuts, garlic, and red peppers, typically prepared with a base of fish or meat stock. The sauce is traditionally served with salt cod or other fish dishes as an accompaniment in Basque cuisine.

California roll is a type of sushi roll made with imitation crab, avocado, and cucumber, typically wrapped in rice on the outside with nori seaweed on the inside. It is served cold, sliced into rounds, and is commonly served as an appetizer or part of a sushi assortment.

canonicalName: crottin de berry a l huile d olive
displayName: Crottin de Berry à l’Huile d’Olive
aliases: crottin de berry a l huile d olive
description: Crottin de Berry is a small, round French goat cheese from the Berry region that is typically aged for several weeks until it develops a firm texture and nutty flavor. It is traditionally served drizzled with olive oil, often accompanied by bread or as part of a cheese course in French cuisine.

Tonkatsu is a breaded and deep-fried pork cutlet made by coating a thin slice of pork loin with panko breadcrumbs after dipping it in egg and flour. It is typically served with shredded cabbage, rice, miso soup, and tonkatsu sauce, a sweet and savory brown sauce similar to Worcestershire sauce.

Goxua is a Basque dessert consisting of layers of sponge cake, pastry cream, and whipped cream, typically topped with a crispy caramel or meringue coating. It is traditionally served chilled as a special occasion dessert in Basque Country, particularly in Guipuzcoa.

Ol Saag Bata is a traditional Bengali preparation typically made by mashing boiled elephant foot yam (ol) together with leafy greens (saag), seasoned with mustard oil, green chilies, garlic, and salt. It is commonly served at room temperature as a side dish alongside steamed rice and dal as part of a homestyle Bengali meal.

Kilishi is a West African dried meat snack made by slicing beef into thin strips, seasoning with cayenne pepper, salt, and onion, then drying the meat until it becomes hard and shelf-stable. It is typically eaten as a portable protein-rich snack or appetizer, often served at room temperature.

A pincho is a small appetizer consisting of a slice of bread topped with various ingredients such as cured meats, cheese, seafood, or vegetables, typically secured with a toothpick. These bite-sized portions are served at bars and social gatherings in Basque Country, often accompanied by drinks as part of traditional tapas culture.

Bakmi is an Indonesian stir-fried noodle dish made with wheat noodles, vegetables, and protein such as chicken, shrimp, or tofu, seasoned with soy sauce and spices. It is typically served as a main course and can be prepared with either crispy or soft noodles depending on regional variation and personal preference.

Gefilte fish is a traditional Jewish dish made by grinding fish with bread, eggs, and spices, then poaching the mixture in fish or vegetable broth, often with added carrots and onions. It is typically served cold as an appetizer, particularly during Jewish holidays and Shabbat meals.

Tteokbokki is a Korean dish made from cylindrical rice cakes (tteok) that are boiled and then stir-fried in a spicy gochujang-based sauce, typically combined with fish cakes, vegetables, and boiled eggs. It is commonly served as a street food snack or casual meal, often accompanied by a slightly sweet and spicy sauce and sometimes offered with ramen noodles added to the mixture.

Koshari is an Egyptian street food dish made by layering cooked rice, macaroni, and vermicelli pasta, then topping with cooked lentils and chickpeas. The assembled dish is finished with fried onions and spiced tomato sauce, then served as a complete meal in a bowl or plate.

Lacón Gallego is a cured and boiled pork shoulder from Galicia, typically prepared by salting the meat for several days followed by boiling with vegetables such as potatoes, cabbage, and turnips. The dish is traditionally served sliced with the accompanying vegetables and broth, representing a classic element of Galician peasant cuisine and festive meals.

Jambalaya is a one-pot rice dish prepared by sautéing meat and vegetables, then simmering them with long-grain rice and broth until the rice absorbs the liquid and flavors. The dish typically contains a combination of proteins such as chicken, sausage, or seafood, along with the holy trinity of vegetables (onions, celery, and bell peppers), and is seasoned with spices like cayenne pepper and bay leaf.

Lohikeitto is a Finnish salmon soup made by simmering salmon, potatoes, onions, and dill in a broth base, typically finished with cream or milk to create a rich, creamy consistency. The dish is traditionally served hot as a main course, often accompanied by bread or rye bread, and represents a classic preparation in Finnish cuisine.

Moules-frites consists of mussels cooked in a broth of white wine, garlic, onions, and herbs, typically served in their shells. The dish is accompanied by thick-cut Belgian fries and traditionally eaten as a main course, often with a side of mayonnaise for dipping the fries.

Ful medames is a traditional Egyptian dish made by slow-cooking dried broad beans until tender, then mashing them and seasoning with olive oil, garlic, lemon juice, and spices. It is typically served warm for breakfast or as a mezze appetizer, often topped with a drizzle of olive oil and accompanied by bread.

Sujuk is a spiced sausage made from minced meat and offal, seasoned with garlic, cumin, and other spices, then stuffed into casings and dried or cured. It is typically served sliced as a cold appetizer, in sandwiches, or fried as a breakfast item.

Bánh khảo is a Vietnamese crispy rice cake made by deep-frying glutinous rice flour batter until golden and crispy, typically shaped into a round or dome form. It is commonly served as a snack or dessert, often accompanied by sweet toppings such as sesame seeds, sugar coating, or served with a sweet dipping sauce.

Gadal pata bata is a traditional Bengali preparation typically made by grinding or mashing gadal leaves (a regional green) into a coarse paste, often combined with green chilies, mustard oil, salt, and sometimes garlic or onion. It is commonly served as a side accompaniment with steamed rice at the beginning of a meal, valued for its pungent, earthy flavor characteristic of Bengali 'bata' dishes.

Monyo is a traditional Beninese sauce, typically prepared by simmering tomatoes, onions, chili peppers, and palm oil with aromatic spices to create a rich, flavorful base. It is commonly served alongside staple starches such as rice, boiled yams, or fufu, and may be enhanced with fish, meat, or vegetables depending on regional preferences.

Clamato is a beverage made by combining tomato juice with clam juice, typically served chilled as a drink or cocktail mixer. It originated in Mexico and is commonly consumed on its own, in beer cocktails like michelada, or as a base for seafood-flavored mixed drinks.

Fried oysters are oysters that have been breaded and deep-fried until golden and crispy, typically served with lemon wedges and a dipping sauce such as tartar sauce or cocktail sauce. This preparation is common in seafood restaurants and casual dining establishments, particularly in coastal regions and areas with strong oyster-farming traditions.

This Portuguese dish features clams sautéed in olive oil with garlic, lemon juice, and peppercorn, finished with fresh cilantro. It is traditionally served as an appetizer or main course, often accompanied by bread to soak up the flavorful cooking liquid.

Spekdik is a Dutch-Indonesian layered pastry dessert made with thin sheets of phyllo or pastry dough alternated with a filling of spiced meat (typically beef or pork), onions, and sometimes eggs, then baked until crispy. The dish is traditionally served as a savory-sweet treat or appetizer, often cut into rectangular pieces and eaten warm or at room temperature.

Pan de jamón is a spiral bread loaf made by rolling sweet dough with fillings of ham, raisins, and olives before baking. It is traditionally served sliced as a bread course or light meal, particularly in Latin American cuisine.

Dāoxiāo miàn is a Chinese noodle dish where noodles are cut directly into boiling broth or water using a knife, creating irregularly shaped pieces with a chewy texture. The noodles are typically served in a savory broth with vegetables and meat toppings, and the dish is common in northern Chinese cuisine, particularly in Shaanxi province.

Ojingeo-bokkeum is a Korean stir-fried squid dish in which squid is sliced and cooked quickly over high heat with vegetables, gochugaru (red chili pepper), and seasonings until tender. The dish is typically served as a banchan (side dish) or main course with steamed rice.

Pad Thai is prepared by stir-frying rice noodles with eggs, tofu, dried shrimp, and mung bean sprouts, then seasoning with tamarind paste, lime juice, and topped with crushed peanuts. This Thai noodle dish is typically served as a main course, either as a street food or restaurant dish, and is commonly eaten for lunch or dinner.

Datong knife-shaved noodles are prepared by holding a block of dough and shaving thin noodle strands directly into boiling water or broth using a sharp knife. The noodles are typically served in a savory broth with vegetables and meat toppings, and are a traditional dish from the Datong region of Shanxi Province in China.

A poblano or other large chili pepper is roasted, peeled, and stuffed with cheese (typically Oaxaca or mozzarella), then dipped in egg batter and fried until golden. The dish is traditionally served with tomato sauce and accompanied by rice and beans as a main course.

Nagoya curry udon is a thick udon noodle soup served in a rich curry broth, typically made with chicken or beef stock combined with curry roux and miso. The dish is garnished with chicken, onions, and other vegetables, and is commonly served as a hearty lunch or dinner entrée in Nagoya and throughout Japan.

Hoe-deopbap is a Korean mixed rice bowl where seasoned vegetables, protein (typically beef or seafood), and a raw or lightly cooked egg are arranged on top of warm rice and mixed together before eating. The dish is traditionally served with gochujang (red chili paste) and sesame oil, which diners blend throughout the rice and ingredients.

Lo mein is a Chinese noodle dish made with wheat noodles that are boiled and then stir-fried with vegetables, protein (commonly chicken, shrimp, or pork), and sauce. It is typically served as a main course in Chinese restaurants and casual dining settings.

Rava idli is a South Indian steamed cake made from semolina, yogurt, and spices, with the batter typically enriched with ghee and cashews before being steamed in molds until soft and fluffy. It is commonly served as a breakfast or snack item, often accompanied by sambar and coconut chutney.

Lau pata bata is a traditional Bengali preparation typically made from tender bottle gourd leaves (lau pata) that are steamed or lightly cooked and then mashed (bata) with ingredients such as mustard oil, green chilies, garlic, and salt. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice at the start of a meal, often accompanied by dal and other vegetable preparations.

Korolar dolma is a Bengali-style stuffed dish, typically featuring bitter gourd (korola) hollowed out and filled with a spiced mixture that commonly includes minced fish or meat, onions, garlic, ginger, and mustard or panch phoron seasoning. It is usually shallow-fried or simmered in a light gravy and often served with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Morioka jajamen is a noodle dish from Morioka, Japan, featuring wheat noodles served in a bowl with a thick starch-based sauce made from potato or sweet potato starch, vegetables, and meat or seafood. The dish is typically eaten by mixing the noodles with the sauce and vegetables before consuming, and is traditionally served as a local specialty in Iwate Prefecture.

Ojingeo-sundae is a Korean dish consisting of squid tubes stuffed with a mixture of glass noodles, vegetables, and seasonings, then sliced and typically pan-fried or steamed. It is commonly served as an appetizer or side dish in Korean cuisine, often accompanied by a dipping sauce.

Gomoku Gohan is a Japanese rice dish prepared by cooking rice with various ingredients including vegetables, mushrooms, and sometimes meat or seafood mixed throughout. The dish is typically served as a main course in bowls and features a savory flavor profile from the combined ingredients and seasonings incorporated during the cooking process.

Toyohashi curry noodles are made by serving noodles topped with a thick, mildly spiced curry sauce containing meat and vegetables, originating from Toyohashi in Aichi Prefecture, Japan. The dish is typically served as a casual noodle meal, with the curry sauce coating the noodles rather than being served as a broth.

Akhni is a fragrant rice dish prepared by cooking rice in a broth made from meat stock infused with whole spices such as cardamom, cinnamon, and bay leaves. The dish is typically served as a main course in South Asian cuisine, often accompanied by meat or vegetables cooked separately and mixed in.

Misti kumror polau is a Bengali rice dish prepared by cooking rice with sweet pumpkin (misti kumro), ghee, and spices including cinnamon, cardamom, and bay leaves until the pumpkin softens and flavors meld together. This dish is typically served as part of Bengali meal celebrations and special occasions, often accompanying meat curries or dal preparations.

Sobji polau is a rice pilaf dish prepared by cooking rice with vegetables such as carrots, peas, and potatoes, often with the addition of spices and ghee or oil. It is typically served as a main course or side dish in Central Asian and South Asian cuisines.

Ilish machh bhaja tel is a Bengali preparation of hilsa fish (ilish) that is shallow-fried in oil until the flesh becomes tender and the edges crisp. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice and is a traditional preparation that highlights the delicate flavor of the fish with minimal seasoning.

Sheem bhorta is a Bengali vegetable dish made by mashing boiled string beans with spices, onions, and mustard oil until achieving a coarse paste consistency. It is typically served as a side dish or accompaniment to rice and fish or meat curries in Bengali cuisine.

Dudh shukto is a Bengali vegetable curry prepared by sautéing mixed vegetables such as potatoes, carrots, beans, and pumpkin with spices, then simmering them in a creamy yogurt or milk-based sauce. The dish is typically served as a side course with rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

An omelette made with smoked haddock, cream, and cheese, typically finished under the grill until golden. Named after the English playwright Arnold Bennett, it is traditionally served as a breakfast or brunch dish.

Sweetened potato casserole is prepared by layering cooked sweet potatoes with butter, sugar, and spices such as cinnamon and nutmeg, then baking until golden. The dish is typically served as a side dish during holiday meals, particularly Thanksgiving and Christmas dinners in North American cuisine.

Kaki no ha zushi is sushi wrapped in persimmon leaves (kaki no ha), a traditional Japanese preparation from the Nara region that uses the leaves as a natural wrapper and flavor element. The dish typically contains sushi rice and fillings such as mackerel, cured fish, or vegetables, pressed together and served chilled, with the persimmon leaf imparting a subtle aroma and preservative qualities.

Dodol betawi is a traditional Indonesian sweet made by slowly cooking glutinous rice flour, coconut milk, brown sugar, and butter until thick and caramelized, then often mixed with peanuts or other ingredients before being set and cut into pieces. This chewy confection originates from Jakarta (Betawi culture) and is typically served as a snack or gift, particularly during festive occasions and holidays.

Bard is typically a savory dish featuring meat or poultry wrapped in thin slices of fat or bacon, a technique known as barding, which keeps the protein moist during roasting. It is commonly served as a main course, often accompanied by roasted vegetables, potatoes, or a light pan sauce.

Empanadillas are small fried pastries with a flaky or crispy exterior, typically filled with savory ingredients such as meat, cheese, or seafood mixed with vegetables and seasonings. They are commonly served as appetizers or snacks in Filipino cuisine and international contexts.

Octopus-shaped sausages are molded pork or chicken sausages formed into an octopus silhouette with tentacle-like protrusions, typically breaded and deep-fried or pan-fried until golden. Commonly served as a children's dish or side item in Japanese cuisine and international restaurants, often accompanied by ketchup or other dipping sauces.

Cilok is an Indonesian snack made from tapioca flour mixed with water and seasonings, formed into small balls or cylinders, and boiled until they float to the surface. It is typically served warm with a spicy peanut sauce or sweet soy-based sauce and often accompanied by vegetables, making it a popular street food and casual snack throughout Indonesia.

Shirasudon is a rice bowl topped with shirasu, which are tiny whitebait or juvenile fish that are typically salted and dried, then served raw or lightly cooked. The dish is commonly garnished with soy sauce, nori seaweed, and sometimes a raw egg or pickled vegetables, and is eaten as a light meal or lunch.

Tarako spaghetti is prepared by tossing cooked spaghetti with tarako (salted pollock roe), butter, and soy sauce, creating a creamy and briny sauce. The dish is typically served hot as a pasta course, often garnished with nori and sometimes topped with a raw egg yolk that cooks from the heat of the pasta.

Pekasam is an Indonesian dish of fish preserved and cooked in a spiced vinegar-based sauce, typically made with freshwater fish such as carp or catfish combined with turmeric, ginger, and other aromatics. The dish is traditionally served as a main course or side dish, often at room temperature or slightly warm, and is valued for its tangy, complex flavor profile and extended shelf life due to the preservation method.

Tanmen is a Japanese ramen dish featuring a rich broth typically made from chicken, pork, or seafood, combined with a miso or soy-based seasoning base. The noodles are served in the hot broth and topped with vegetables such as bean sprouts, carrots, and corn, along with sliced meat or egg, making it a complete noodle soup commonly enjoyed as a main course.

Pizza toast is prepared by toasting bread slices and topping them with tomato sauce, cheese, and various pizza ingredients such as vegetables or cured meats before baking or broiling until the cheese melts. This dish is commonly served as a casual appetizer, snack, or light meal in Japanese casual dining and cafes.

Sarmale are cabbage rolls made by wrapping seasoned ground meat and rice in fermented or fresh cabbage leaves, then simmered in a tomato-based or sour cream sauce. This traditional Moldovan dish is typically served hot as a main course, often accompanied by sour cream or polenta.

Mehallabiyya is a creamy Middle Eastern pudding made from milk, cornstarch or rice flour, and sugar, flavored with rose water or orange blossom water and often topped with nuts or coconut. It is typically chilled and served as a dessert in small bowls or glasses, common in Iranian and broader Levantine cuisine.

A burger sandwich featuring two ramen cake patties made from noodles that are pan-fried until crispy, sandwiching typical burger fillings such as meat, cheese, and vegetables between them. Originating from Kitakata in Fukushima Prefecture, Japan, it combines elements of traditional ramen with the hamburger format and is typically served as a casual meal or street food.

Abekawamochi is a Japanese mochi dessert made from glutinous rice flour dough that is dusted with kinako (roasted soybean powder) and sometimes topped with a sweet syrup or sauce. The dish is typically served chilled or at room temperature as a confection or light dessert.

Kumamoto ramen is a tonkotsu-style ramen featuring a rich pork bone broth simmered for extended periods to achieve its signature milky white color and deep flavor. The dish is typically served with thin noodles, chashu pork, kikurage mushrooms, and garlic oil, with a raw egg and pickled ginger as common accompaniments.

Doria is a Japanese yōshoku dish consisting of a creamy curry sauce served over rice, typically topped with fried shrimp or chicken. The dish is finished with a layer of melted cheese and breadcrumbs, then briefly gratinéed until golden brown before serving.

Äggakaka is a Swedish baked dish made from eggs, milk, wheat flour, and diced apples, combined and baked until set. It is traditionally served warm with lingonberries, apple sauce, and pork, often as a dessert or light main course.

Gima shaker chorchori is a Bengali mixed vegetable dish typically prepared by stir-frying or slow-cooking gima shak (a local leafy green) with assorted vegetables, mustard oil, and a tempering of panch phoron or mustard seeds. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice and dal in a traditional Bengali meal.

Bánh giày is a Vietnamese cake made from glutinous rice flour mixed with water and salt, then steamed in a banana leaf-lined mold until it forms a dense, slightly chewy cake. It is typically served as a breakfast dish or snack, often accompanied by savory toppings such as pâté, Vietnamese cold cuts, or pickled vegetables.

A halal snack pack consists of doner kebab meat served in a container with accompaniments such as cheese, sauce, and sometimes chips or rice. It is typically consumed as a late-night casual meal or takeaway food, commonly found in urban areas with established halal food vendors.

Sop saudara is an Indonesian soup made with offal (liver, heart, and lungs) that is boiled with aromatic spices including turmeric, garlic, shallots, and ginger, then served in a rich broth. The dish is traditionally eaten as a communal meal or special occasion food, typically accompanied by rice or consumed as a warming soup course.

Peruvian pollada is a grilled or roasted chicken dish where the bird is marinated in a mixture of vinegar, cumin, garlic, and other spices before cooking until the meat is tender and the skin is caramelized. It is traditionally served with potatoes, corn, and ají verde (green chili sauce), commonly appearing at family gatherings and celebrations.

canonicalName: fruit sweets in the turkish cuisine
displayName: Fruit sweets in the Turkish cuisine
aliases: fruit sweets in the turkish cuisine
description: Turkish fruit sweets are confections made by cooking fresh or dried fruits with sugar to create a thick, paste-like preserve that is often cut into pieces and dusted with powdered sugar or coconut. These sweets are traditionally served as a dessert or offered as an accompaniment to Turkish coffee and tea.

Bhendi fry is a vegetable dish made from okra (lady's fingers) that is cut into pieces, coated in a spiced flour mixture, and deep-fried until crispy. It is typically seasoned with onions, green chilies, and spices such as turmeric and chili powder, and served as a side dish or appetizer in Indian cuisine.

Celery root salad is prepared by peeling and julienning raw celery root, then dressing it with a vinaigrette or creamy sauce, often incorporating mustard, olive oil, and lemon juice or vinegar. The dish is typically served chilled as a side salad or appetizer in European cuisine, particularly in French bistro traditions.

Svið is a traditional Icelandic dish made from a sheep's head that is boiled or steamed until tender, typically served with potatoes and turnips. The meat is eaten directly from the bone and is often accompanied by a rich broth, making it a substantial dish historically prepared during autumn slaughter season.

Chocolate pizza is a dessert pizza made with a thin or thick dough base topped with melted chocolate, chocolate spread, or chocolate sauce. It is typically baked until the crust is crispy and the chocolate is warm, then often garnished with nuts, fruit, or powdered sugar and served as a sweet course or dessert.

Filet américain is a Belgian and Dutch dish consisting of finely minced raw beef mixed with seasonings such as salt, pepper, and sometimes Worcestershire sauce or capers. It is typically served as an appetizer on bread, crackers, or toast, often garnished with onions, pickles, or anchovies.

Tuwo masara is a porridge made by cooking cornmeal with water or broth until it reaches a thick, smooth consistency, often stirred continuously to prevent lumping. It is typically served as a staple breakfast or lunch dish in West African cuisine, frequently accompanied by soups, stews, or vegetables.

Nsala soup is a Nigerian dish prepared by simmering fish, chicken, crayfish, and yam in a broth seasoned with dawadawa (locust bean paste), creating a savory and aromatic soup. It is typically served hot as a main course, often accompanied by fufu or other starch-based sides.

Indori Poha is a breakfast dish made from flattened rice (poha) that is rinsed, drained, and mixed with sautéed onions, potatoes, peanuts, and spices including turmeric and chili powder. It is typically served hot with a squeeze of lemon juice and is a popular street food and home breakfast in Indore, Madhya Pradesh.

Mabo harusame is a Chinese-style dish consisting of a spicy meat sauce typically made with ground pork, tofu, and vegetables served over thin glass noodles (harusame). The dish is characterized by its savory and mildly spicy sauce made with soy sauce, doubanjiang (spicy bean paste), and aromatics, and is commonly served as a main course in Japanese and Asian restaurants.

Sindhi pulao is a rice dish prepared by cooking basmati rice with meat (typically beef or mutton), potatoes, and aromatic spices including cumin, cinnamon, and bay leaves in a single pot using the layering method. The dish is traditionally served as a complete meal, popular in Sindhi cuisine, and commonly accompanied by raita or pickled vegetables.

Piña is a traditional Spanish bread, typically prepared with a simple dough of wheat flour, water, yeast, and salt, and shaped into a rounded form that resembles a pine cone, which gives the bread its name. It is commonly baked until golden and crusty, and is often served as an accompaniment to meals, alongside soups and stews, or used for sandwiches and tapas.

Mallung is a Sri Lankan dish made by stir-frying grated coconut with finely chopped vegetables such as carrots, green beans, and onions, typically seasoned with curry leaves, chilies, and spices. It is commonly served as a side dish or accompaniment to rice and curries in Sri Lankan meals.

Xidoufen is a Chinese savory soup made by cooking peas until soft and then grinding them into a smooth paste, which is mixed with broth to create a creamy consistency. The dish is typically served hot as a breakfast item or light meal, often garnished with crispy fried shallots, cilantro, or chili oil.

Sholezard is a Persian saffron rice pudding made by cooking rice with water or broth, then sweetening it with sugar and infusing it with saffron for distinctive color and flavor. The dish is traditionally garnished with slivered almonds and pistachios, then drizzled with melted butter and sprinkled with cinnamon, typically served as a festive dessert or sweet rice course during celebrations and special occasions.

Goat meat pepper soup is prepared by simmering tender goat meat with hot peppers, onions, and aromatic spices in a rich broth base until the meat becomes soft and flavors meld together. The soup is typically served hot as a starter or light meal, with the peppery broth and meat pieces consumed directly from the bowl, often accompanied by bread or eaten with a spoon.

Kue gapit is an Indonesian sweet snack made from a simple batter of flour, eggs, sugar, and coconut milk that is cooked between two heated metal plates or molds, creating thin, crispy wafers. The cookies are typically folded or shaped while still warm and served as a popular homemade treat or gift, particularly in Central Java.

Rakott krumpli is a layered potato and meat casserole made by alternating sliced potatoes with ground pork or bacon, onions, and sometimes sauerkraut, then baked until golden. The dish is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by sour cream, and is common in Hungarian and Ukrainian home cooking.

Kue satu is an Indonesian steamed cake made from mung beans (Vigna radiata), typically ground into flour and mixed with coconut milk, sugar, and eggs to create a soft, spongy texture. The batter is steamed in a round mold until set, resulting in a pale, delicate cake commonly served as a traditional snack or dessert.

Budyń is a Polish pudding made from a starch base (typically potato or cornstarch) mixed with milk, sugar, and flavorings such as vanilla, chocolate, or fruit, then chilled until set to a smooth, custard-like consistency. It is commonly served as a dessert or sweet course, often topped with fruit, jam, or chocolate sauce.

Panrkhash is an Armenian dish made from potatoes, meat (typically pork or lamb), and onions that are fried together in a skillet until golden and crispy. The ingredients are seasoned with salt and pepper, and the dish is traditionally served hot as a main course or side dish, often accompanied by bread and pickled vegetables.

Balık böreği is a Turkish pastry made by wrapping seasoned fish fillets in thin sheets of phyllo dough and deep-frying until golden and crispy. It is typically served as an appetizer or main course, often accompanied by lemon wedges and a yogurt-based sauce for dipping.

Bean soup is prepared by simmering dried or fresh beans with water or broth, typically combined with onions, garlic, and seasonings until the beans are tender. The soup is commonly served as a starter or main course in various cuisines, often accompanied by bread or garnished with herbs and vegetables.

A Brussels waffle is a Belgian waffle made with a yeast-based batter and cooked in a rectangular waffle iron, resulting in a crispy exterior and light, airy interior with large pockets. It is typically served as a sweet treat topped with powdered sugar, chocolate, whipped cream, or fruit, and is commonly consumed as a snack or dessert in Belgium and internationally.

Knieper are large claws from the blue crab (Callinectes sapidus), typically boiled or steamed until tender and served as an appetizer or main course. The meat is extracted from the claw and eaten plain or accompanied by melted butter and lemon, commonly found in coastal German and Northern European cuisine.

Leipziger Lerche is a traditional German pastry from Leipzig consisting of a crispy pastry shell filled with a mixture of ground meat, hard-boiled eggs, and spices, topped with aspic. The dish is typically served cold as an appetizer or light meal and is considered a regional specialty of Saxony.

Hefekloß is a sweet yeast dumpling made from a dough of flour, yeast, milk, eggs, and butter, typically studded with raisins or plums. The dumplings are poached in boiling water or broth until they float to the surface, then served warm as a dessert or side dish, often accompanied by vanilla sauce, fruit compote, or melted butter.

Bazo bread is a traditional bread found in both Mexican and Spanish culinary traditions, typically prepared from a simple dough of wheat flour, water, salt, and yeast, then baked until it develops a firm crust and soft interior. It is commonly served as an accompaniment to meals, used for sandwiches, or paired with soups, stews, and spreads.

Cantos bread is a traditional Spanish bread typically prepared from a simple dough of wheat flour, water, salt, and yeast, baked until it develops a firm, rustic crust and a soft interior. It is commonly served as an accompaniment to meals, used for sopping up sauces, or sliced for tapas and sandwiches.

A cold platter is an assortment of pre-prepared cold foods arranged on a single serving plate, typically including cured meats, cheeses, vegetables, and sometimes seafood or fruits. It is served chilled without further cooking and is commonly presented as an appetizer, light meal, or part of a buffet spread.

Liver and onions is prepared by sautéing sliced liver, typically beef or calf liver, with caramelized or sautéed onions in a pan with butter or oil. The dish is commonly served as a main course with side vegetables or potatoes and is traditional in European and American cuisines.

Sea cucumber is prepared by cleaning and rehydrating the dried product, then braising or stir-frying with aromatics such as garlic, ginger, and soy sauce, often combined with other seafood or vegetables. It is typically served as part of multi-course meals in East Asian cuisine, valued for its gelatinous texture and subtle flavor.

Hákarl is a traditional Icelandic dish made from Greenland shark meat that has been fermented and dried through a multi-month curing process, resulting in a pungent ammonia-like odor and strong flavor. The dish is typically served in small cubes, often accompanied by brennivín (caraway-flavored schnapps), and is considered a traditional delicacy in Icelandic cuisine.

Mettigel is a German charcuterie preparation made from finely minced raw pork mixed with spices, onions, and seasonings, typically shaped into a ball or hedgehog form and served with a decorative coating of chopped onions or capers. It is traditionally served as an appetizer or snack with bread or crackers, commonly found in German delicatessens and on restaurant menus.

Crab soup is prepared by simmering crab meat or shells with aromatics, stock, and seasonings to create a rich broth, often finished with cream or liaison. Typically served hot as a starter course, this French-influenced soup showcases crab as the primary protein and flavor component.

Kibinai are baked pastries made from dough filled with seasoned mutton, traditionally associated with Karaite Jewish cuisine. They are typically served as a main dish or appetizer and are characterized by their enclosed pastry form that seals in the meat filling during baking.

Leskovački roštilj is a Serbian mixed grill platter featuring various grilled meats including pork, beef, and chicken, typically seasoned with paprika and salt, then cooked over charcoal or open flame. The dish is traditionally served as a communal meal with fresh bread, onions, and ajvar (roasted red pepper spread), commonly enjoyed at outdoor gatherings and restaurants throughout Serbia.

Lamian is a hand-pulled noodle dish where dough is repeatedly stretched and folded to create thin, chewy noodles that are boiled and served in a savory broth. The dish is typically topped with vegetables, meat (commonly beef or lamb), and aromatics such as garlic and scallions, and is commonly served as a main course in Chinese cuisine.

Pasta con le sarde is a traditional Sicilian pasta dish made with fresh pasta mixed with sardines and wild fennel, typically finished with saffron and topped with grated cheese. The dish is commonly served as a main course in Sicilian cuisine and represents a characteristic combination of seafood and local herbs from the Mediterranean region.

Tramezzino is an Italian sandwich made with soft, crustless white bread that is cut into triangles and filled with various ingredients such as vegetables, cured meats, cheese, or seafood. It is typically served cold as a quick lunch or light meal, often found in Italian bars and cafés.

Lontong is an Indonesian dish made from rice that is cooked in a banana leaf cylinder, then sliced and served with various accompaniments such as peanut sauce, hard-boiled eggs, and vegetables. It is commonly eaten as a breakfast or lunch dish and is often served with sambal, fried shallots, and either meat curry or vegetable curry.

Ousá bread is a traditional rustic loaf from Galicia in northwestern Spain, typically prepared with wheat flour, water, salt, and a natural sourdough starter, then baked in a wood-fired oven to develop a thick, crusty exterior and dense, chewy crumb. It is commonly served as an accompaniment to hearty Galician dishes such as stews, cured meats, and cheeses, or simply enjoyed with olive oil.

Ostkaka is a Swedish baked custard dessert made from milk curds, eggs, sugar, and cream, traditionally set in a pastry crust and sometimes flavored with almonds or cardamom. It is typically served warm or at room temperature, often accompanied by jam and whipped cream, and is particularly common during spring and early summer celebrations.

Kaspressknödel are Austrian potato and cheese dumplings formed into balls or oval shapes, typically filled with cheese (usually Emmental or similar hard cheese) and breadcrumbs, then boiled or steamed until they float to the surface. They are traditionally served in beef broth as a soup course or as a side dish to meat courses.

Skordalia is a Greek sauce made by blending garlic with mashed potatoes or bread, then emulsifying with olive oil and vinegar or lemon juice to create a creamy, pungent condiment. It is traditionally served as a meze (appetizer) or accompaniment to fish, seafood, and fried vegetables.

Quatre mendiants is a French confection consisting of four types of dried fruits and nuts—typically raisins, dried figs, almonds, and hazelnuts—arranged on a chocolate base or mixed into melted chocolate. The dish is traditionally served as a petit four or after-dinner sweet, often during the Christmas season.

Käsefladen is a Swiss savory tart consisting of a thin yeast dough base topped with a creamy custard-like filling made from cheese (typically Appenzell), eggs, and cream, then baked until golden. It is traditionally served as a main course or hearty appetizer in Swiss cuisine, often accompanied by a simple salad.

Cheela is a savory Indian pancake made by spreading a fermented batter of rice and lentils on a griddle and cooking until golden on both sides. It is typically served with chutney or curry as a breakfast or light meal item.

Niño envuelto is an Argentine dish consisting of a rolled cut of beef, typically flank or round steak, that is pounded thin, filled with a mixture of hard-boiled eggs, vegetables, and seasonings, then rolled and secured before being braised in broth or tomato sauce. The dish is traditionally served sliced, revealing the spiral of fillings inside, and is commonly accompanied by the cooking broth as a light sauce.

Five Guys hamburgers are made to order by grilling seasoned beef patties on a flat-top griddle and serving them on a toasted bun, typically with customizable toppings selected from a range of fresh ingredients. The burgers are served as a main dish, commonly accompanied by french fries and a beverage as part of a casual dining meal.

A falafel burger consists of fried or baked chickpea fritters (falafel) served inside a bread roll, typically with vegetables and sauce. This vegetarian sandwich is commonly served as a casual meal or street food in Middle Eastern and international cuisine.

MOS Burger is a Japanese burger featuring a beef patty typically served between two layers of fluffy buns, often with lettuce, tomato, and a savory sauce. The burger is commonly served as a standalone item or meal set and is characteristic of Japanese fast-casual burger chains, combining Western burger format with Japanese flavor profiles and preparation standards.

Lemon ricotta pancakes are made by combining ricotta cheese, eggs, flour, baking powder, and salt, with lemon zest and lemon juice folded into the batter, then cooked on a griddle with butter until golden. They are typically served warm, often topped with butter, syrup, or fresh fruit as a breakfast or brunch dish.

Bánh trôi is a Vietnamese dessert made from glutinous rice flour dough formed into small balls, typically filled with sweetened panela (unrefined cane sugar) or sesame, then boiled until they float to the surface. The cooked dumplings are traditionally served in a light ginger or brown sugar syrup, either hot or at room temperature, as a sweet snack or dessert.

Mollete is an open-faced sandwich made from a bolillo roll or similar bread that is split lengthwise, topped with refried beans, melted cheese, and often jalapeños or other toppings, then toasted or broiled until the cheese melts. It is typically served as a breakfast or light lunch item in Mexico and other Latin American countries.

Obwarzanek krakowski is a ring-shaped bread from Kraków made by boiling dough in water before baking, resulting in a dense, chewy texture with a crispy crust. It is traditionally topped with sesame seeds or poppy seeds and served as a street food or breakfast item, often eaten plain or with savory spreads.

Spaccata is typically a split or cleaved preparation, as the name derives from the Italian word meaning 'split,' and it is commonly made by halving a primary ingredient such as bread, meat, or vegetables before cooking or filling it. It is often served warm as a shareable starter or main course, sometimes stuffed with cheeses, cured meats, or herbs depending on regional interpretation.

Mishṭāʿ al-ǧrīsh is a Lebanese flatbread typically prepared with coarsely ground wheat (jreesh), mixed into a simple dough with water and salt and baked or griddled until firm on the outside while remaining tender within. It is commonly served warm alongside dips such as labneh, olive oil, or za'atar, and often accompanies mezze spreads or hearty stews in traditional Lebanese meals.

Onions are cooked quickly in a hot pan with fat over high heat until softened and lightly browned, developing a caramelized exterior while maintaining some texture. This preparation is commonly served as a side dish, component of composed plates, or ingredient in other dishes across many cuisines.

Bean burgers are patties made primarily from cooked and mashed black turtle beans, combined with binders and seasonings, then pan-fried or grilled until firm. They are typically served on a bun with vegetables and condiments as a vegetarian sandwich option.

A lamb burger consists of ground lamb meat formed into a patty and cooked by grilling, pan-frying, or broiling, typically served on a bun with various toppings such as lettuce, tomato, onion, and sauce. The dish is commonly found in Mediterranean, Middle Eastern, and contemporary international cuisines, often featuring lamb-specific condiments like tzatziki, harissa, or mint-based accompaniments.

Honey walnut shrimp consists of shrimp that are battered, deep-fried until golden, and then tossed with a sweet honey sauce, typically served over a bed of candied or caramelized walnuts. The dish is commonly served as an appetizer or main course in Asian-fusion and Chinese restaurants, often accompanied by steamed rice or noodles.

Bánh tráng trộn is a Vietnamese snack made from crispy rice paper sheets that are broken into pieces and mixed with seasoning ingredients such as salt, spices, and often dried shrimp or sesame seeds. The mixture is typically served as a crunchy appetizer or side dish at room temperature.

Lasagne alla bolognese is prepared by layering sheets of pasta with a meat-based ragù sauce (typically made from ground beef, tomatoes, and aromatics) and a creamy béchamel sauce, then baked until golden. This traditional Italian dish from the Emilia-Romagna region is typically served as a main course, cut into rectangular portions.

Curry antillais aux bananes is a Caribbean curry dish prepared by cooking meat or seafood with onions, garlic, and curry spices, then combined with sliced bananas that are added near the end of cooking to maintain their texture. The dish is typically served with rice or root vegetables as a complete meal, reflecting the culinary traditions of the French Caribbean islands.

Kurume yakitori is a Japanese grilled chicken skewer dish originating from Kurume city in Fukuoka Prefecture, characterized by small pieces of chicken and offal threaded on bamboo skewers and grilled over charcoal while basted with a sweet and savory tare sauce. The skewers are typically served hot as an appetizer or casual meal, often accompanied by condiments such as salt, citrus, or spicy pepper.

A croquette is a fried breaded cylinder or ball made by coating a thick béchamel or mashed potato base mixed with meat, fish, cheese, or vegetables, then rolling it in breadcrumbs and deep-frying until golden. It is typically served as an appetizer, side dish, or snack, often accompanied by a dipping sauce such as aioli or tomato-based condiment.

Shahi jilapi is a deep-fried spiral pastry dessert made from a batter of flour, yogurt, and spices that is piped into hot oil and then soaked in a sugar syrup flavored with cardamom and rose water. It is typically served warm as a sweet dish in Indian cuisine, often during festivals and special occasions.

Sotong sumbat is an Indonesian and Malaysian dish consisting of squid stuffed with a mixture of ground meat, spices, and aromatics, then either grilled, fried, or stewed. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or as part of a seafood spread, often accompanied by sambal or other condiments.

Kuih limas is a Malaysian steamed cake made from a batter of rice flour, coconut milk, and sugar, cooked in a distinctive pyramidal mold that gives the cake its characteristic shape and name. The cake is typically served as a traditional sweet snack or dessert, often found at Malaysian markets and hawker stalls.

Lepat pisang is a steamed banana cake made from mashed bananas mixed with rice flour, coconut milk, and palm sugar, then wrapped in banana leaves before cooking. It is a traditional Southeast Asian dessert typically served as a sweet snack or breakfast item, with a soft, slightly sticky texture.

Pardulas are Sardinian pastries consisting of a thin pastry shell filled with fresh cheese (typically ricotta), saffron, and honey. They are traditionally baked until golden and served as a dessert or sweet treat, particularly during Easter celebrations.

Baga tami is typically a savory preparation that reflects international fusion influences, commonly featuring a base of grains or flatbread accompanied by seasoned proteins or vegetables. It is often served warm as a main course, sometimes paired with a side sauce or fresh garnish to complement its flavors.

Kuih kasturi is a traditional Malaysian bite-sized sweet made from glutinous rice flour dough that is filled with a mixture of grated coconut and palm sugar, then steamed in small molds until set. The resulting translucent cake has a soft, chewy texture and is typically served as a dessert or snack at room temperature, often during festivals or special occasions.

Dobos torte is a multilayered sponge cake assembled with chocolate buttercream between each layer and finished with a thin caramel coating on top. The cake is traditionally served as a dessert in slices and originated in Hungary in the 19th century.

The Impossible burger is a plant-based patty made from soy protein, coconut oil, potato starch, and heme (a plant-derived iron compound), designed to simulate the taste and texture of ground beef. It is typically grilled or pan-seared and served on a bun with standard burger accompaniments such as lettuce, tomato, onion, and condiments.

Miso katsudon is a Japanese dish consisting of a breaded and deep-fried pork cutlet (katsu) served over steamed rice in a donburi bowl, typically topped with a miso-based sauce and garnished with green onions and sesame seeds. The dish combines the crispy texture of the fried cutlet with the umami-rich miso sauce, and is commonly served as a single-portion meal in Japanese restaurants.

Joulutorttu is a Finnish Christmas pastry made from puff pastry folded into a square with a filling of apple compote and prune jam in the center. The pastry is typically baked until golden and often brushed with egg wash, traditionally served during the Christmas season as a festive treat.

A cinnamon roll is made by rolling sweet yeast dough layered with cinnamon and sugar, then slicing and baking until golden. It is typically served warm, often with a glaze or cream cheese frosting, as a breakfast pastry or dessert.

Babka is a sweet braided or swirled bread made from rich yeast dough containing eggs, butter, and sugar, typically filled with chocolate, cinnamon, or other sweet ingredients, then baked until golden brown. It is traditionally served as a breakfast pastry or dessert, often sliced and eaten plain or with coffee or tea.

Butifarra is a fresh pork sausage made from minced pork meat, fat, and seasonings, typically stuffed into natural casings and cooked by grilling or frying until browned. It is commonly served as a main course or in sandwiches, often accompanied by beans, rice, or traditional side dishes in Peruvian cuisine.

Blood sausage is a cured sausage made by mixing animal blood with meat, fat, and grains or breadcrumbs, then stuffing the mixture into casings and cooking by boiling or frying. It is served sliced, either hot or cold, as a breakfast item, appetizer, or main course component in various European and international cuisines.

Bak kut teh is a Malaysian pork rib soup made by simmering pork ribs and spare ribs in a broth seasoned with garlic, Chinese medicinal herbs, and spices such as star anise and cinnamon for several hours. The dish is traditionally served with rice or youtiao (fried dough sticks) and consumed as a breakfast or brunch item.

A plant-based burger patty made primarily from pea protein, coconut oil, and potato starch, designed to replicate the taste and texture of beef, typically cooked on a grill or griddle. The patty is served on a bun with standard burger accompaniments such as lettuce, tomato, onion, and condiments.

A jello shot is prepared by dissolving gelatin in hot liquid, mixing it with alcohol (typically vodka, rum, or other spirits), and allowing it to set in a refrigerator until firm. It is served as a small, chilled portion in a shot glass or small cup and consumed as a single bite or gulp, commonly at bars, parties, and social gatherings.

Schwenker is a Saarland specialty consisting of marinated meat, typically pork or beef, that is grilled over an open flame or charcoal. The dish is traditionally served with bread, mustard, and onions as accompaniments.

Trout is pan-fried in butter until golden, then finished with a brown butter sauce (beurre noisette) and garnished with fresh lemon and parsley. This French preparation is typically served whole or filleted as a main course, often accompanied by boiled potatoes or vegetables.

Pintera bread is a traditional Spanish bread, typically prepared from a simple dough of wheat flour, water, yeast, and salt, and baked until it develops a firm crust and soft interior. It is commonly served as an accompaniment to meals, used for sopping up sauces, or sliced for tapas and sandwiches.

Pinnekjøtt is a traditional Norwegian dish made from salted and dried lamb ribs that are rehydrated and then boiled or steamed, typically served with mashed rutabaga, potatoes, and mustard. The meat is often aged for several months to develop its characteristic flavor and is commonly prepared during Christmas and winter celebrations.

A New Jersey sloppy joe is prepared by toasting rye bread and topping it with seasoned ground beef cooked in a tomato-based sauce, typically finished with a slice of melted cheese. The sandwich is served hot as a casual lunch or dinner item, often accompanied by pickles or other condiments.

Montreal-style smoked meat is prepared by curing beef brisket with a spice blend, smoking it over hardwood, and then steaming before slicing. It is traditionally served on rye bread with mustard as a sandwich, typically enjoyed as a deli staple.

Cordero asado is lamb that is roasted or grilled over an open flame or in an oven, typically seasoned with salt, pepper, and regional spices. The dish is commonly served in Latin American cuisine, particularly in Argentina and other South American countries, often accompanied by chimichurri sauce and grilled vegetables.

McArabia is a grilled chicken sandwich served on Arabic pita bread, typically filled with seasoned grilled chicken breast, lettuce, tomatoes, onions, and garlic sauce. It is a menu item from McDonald's restaurants in Middle Eastern and North African markets, served as a quick-service meal alternative to standard burger offerings.

Bossam is a Korean dish consisting of boiled pork belly, typically Boston butt or similar cuts, served with accompaniments such as kimchi, salted shrimp, and ssamjang sauce. The meat is traditionally wrapped in lettuce leaves along with the side dishes and eaten as a communal dish, often served at the table with broth from the cooking liquid.

aliases: sandwich francais au bœuf avec sauce
sandwich francais au boeuf avec sauce
description: A French dip sandwich consists of thinly sliced roast beef piled on a split baguette, typically served hot with melted cheese and sometimes peppers or onions. The sandwich is customarily accompanied by a small bowl of warm beef au jus for dipping.

Banglar Rosogolla consists of soft, spongy balls made from chena (fresh cheese curds) and semolina that are boiled in a light sugar syrup flavored with cardamom and rose water. The balls are served chilled or at room temperature, typically as a dessert or sweet course in Bengali cuisine.

Paomo is a traditional Chinese soup dish from the Shaanxi province consisting of torn pieces of flatbread mixed into a rich broth made from lamb or beef stock, often flavored with cumin and other spices. The dish is typically served hot as a breakfast or lunch meal, sometimes accompanied by meat pieces and pickled vegetables.

Pheasant under glass is a classical French dish consisting of roasted pheasant served beneath a glass dome, traditionally accompanied by vegetables, mushrooms, and a rich sauce made from the bird's drippings. The dish is typically presented as a formal entrée in fine dining establishments, with the glass cloche serving both as a presentation element and to retain heat and aromatics at the table.

A cucumber sandwich consists of thinly sliced cucumber placed between two slices of white bread, typically with butter spread on the bread. It is commonly served as a light lunch or tea sandwich, often cut into triangles or rectangles.

White cake is prepared by combining egg whites, white sugar, pastry flour, baking powder, and sodium bicarbonate to create a light, tender crumb structure. The batter is baked until golden and is traditionally served as a dessert, often with frosting or as part of layered cake compositions.

Leche rizada is a Spanish custard dessert made by heating milk with sugar and cinnamon, then curdling it with a mixture of cornstarch and egg yolks to create a thick, creamy texture. The dish is typically served chilled or at room temperature, often dusted with additional cinnamon on top.

Batik cake is an Indonesian-Malaysian layered sponge cake made by pouring alternating batches of chocolate and vanilla batter into a baking tin, creating a distinctive marbled pattern reminiscent of batik fabric when sliced. The cake is typically served at room temperature as a dessert or snack, often accompanied by tea or coffee.

Szczecin bread is a regional Polish-style loaf typically prepared with wheat and rye flour, often featuring a firm crust and a dense, slightly tangy crumb characteristic of northwestern Polish baking traditions. It is commonly served sliced as an accompaniment to soups, spreads, or cold cuts, and may appear alongside main courses as a staple table bread.

Ajopuchero is a Spanish stew made by simmering meat (typically pork or beef), chickpeas, and vegetables in a broth flavored with garlic, paprika, and spices. The dish is traditionally served hot as a main course, often with bread to accompany the flavorful broth.

Nuégados are a Spanish dessert made from choux pastry that is piped into strips, fried until golden, and typically coated with honey or a honey-based glaze. They are traditionally served as a sweet treat, often enjoyed during special occasions or festive celebrations in Spanish cuisine.

Eru is a West African leafy green soup made by boiling finely chopped or pounded leafy vegetables, typically including eru leaves (Gnetum africanum) or similar greens, with stockfish, dried meat, and spices until tender. The dish is traditionally served hot as a main course, often accompanying fufu, cassava, or other starchy sides in Cameroonian and Nigerian cuisine.

Hunter's chicken is prepared by cooking chicken pieces and finishing with sauce chasseur, a brown sauce made with mushrooms, tomatoes, and shallots. The dish is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by potatoes or rice to complement the rich sauce.

Pancong is an Indonesian sweet pancake made from a batter of flour, eggs, and coconut milk, cooked on a griddle until golden brown. It is typically served as a breakfast item or snack, often accompanied by sweet toppings such as palm sugar syrup or condensed milk.

Kue precet is an Indonesian snack made from a simple batter of flour, eggs, sugar, and coconut milk that is cooked in a special hinged mold over an open flame or charcoal. The result is a thin, crispy, golden cookie with characteristic ridged patterns from the mold, typically served as a traditional street snack or accompaniment to tea and coffee.

Bakso Malang is a soup dish featuring meatballs simmered in clear broth, typically served with rice vermicelli, tofu, and accompanied by krupuk crackers. The dish originates from Malang, East Java, and is commonly eaten as a main course for breakfast or lunch.

Puzhukku is a Kerala vegetable stew made by cooking mixed vegetables such as carrots, beans, potatoes, and peas with grated coconut, turmeric, and spices, traditionally prepared in layers or combined in a single pot. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice or flatbreads and is common in Kerala households as a vegetarian preparation.

Edikang Ikong is a Nigerian vegetable soup prepared by cooking leafy greens, typically waterleaf and pumpkin leaves, with fish, palm oil, and seasonings until tender. The dish is traditionally served in bowls alongside fufu, cassava, or other starch-based sides as a main course in Nigerian cuisine.

Lamprais is a Sri Lankan dish made by wrapping spiced meat (typically beef or mutton) and rice in banana leaves and then baking or steaming the parcels until cooked. The dish combines Portuguese and Sri Lankan culinary influences, featuring layers of rice, meat curry, and sometimes hard-boiled eggs, traditionally served as a main course.

Hủ tiếu is a Vietnamese noodle soup made with thin tapioca or wheat noodles simmered in a clear broth typically flavored with pork, shrimp, or crab. The dish is traditionally served in a bowl with the broth, noodles, and protein, often garnished with herbs, vegetables, and crispy fried shallots or garlic.

Spinach lasagne is prepared by layering sheets of pasta with a filling of spinach, ricotta cheese, and béchamel or tomato-based sauce, then baking until the cheese melts and the top browns. The dish is typically served as a main course in portions cut into squares, often accompanied by a simple salad or bread.

Java Rice is an Indonesian-influenced rice dish popular in the Philippines, prepared by stir-frying cooked rice with vegetables, protein (commonly shrimp or chicken), and seasonings including soy sauce and garlic. It is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by a fried egg on top and eaten with condiments such as vinegar or banana ketchup.

French tacos are a street food consisting of a grilled or fried crepe or flatbread filled with ingredients such as meat (typically seasoned ground beef or chicken), cheese, and sauce, folded into a compact shape. They are served as a casual, handheld meal popular in France and typically eaten as a quick lunch or dinner option.

Kkwabaegi is a Korean twisted doughnut made from a sweet dough that is rolled, twisted, and deep-fried until golden brown, typically dusted with cinnamon sugar or coated with a glaze. It is commonly served as a street food snack or dessert, often enjoyed warm with coffee or tea.

Tarte à l'badrée is a regional French tart, likely from northern France or Picardy, typically prepared with a shortcrust pastry base filled with a creamy mixture of eggs, cream, and sugar, sometimes flavored with vanilla or local fruit. It is commonly baked until golden and served in slices as a dessert or afternoon treat, often accompanied by coffee or tea.

Stuffed mushrooms are prepared by removing the stems from large mushroom caps and filling the cavities with a mixture typically containing breadcrumbs, garlic, herbs, and cheese or meat. They are baked until the mushrooms are tender and the filling is golden, and are commonly served as an appetizer or side dish in French cuisine.

Languette is a French beef dish featuring thinly sliced beef tongue, typically braised or stewed with vegetables and a rich sauce until tender. It is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by potatoes or root vegetables, and is considered a classic preparation in French cuisine.

Ayam masak kicap is an Indonesian chicken dish where chicken pieces are braised in kicap manis (sweet soy sauce), typically with garlic, shallots, and spices until tender and deeply colored. The dish is commonly served with rice and is a staple of Indonesian home cooking and casual dining.

Cherry soup of Sint-Truiden is a chilled dessert soup made with sour cherries from the Sint-Truiden region of Belgium, typically prepared by stewing the fruit with sugar and thickening the liquid to create a smooth, tart base. The soup is traditionally served cold as a dessert course, often garnished with whipped cream or sour cream, and sometimes accompanied by small cake pieces or biscuits.

Stew peas is a Jamaican dish made by simmering dried pigeon peas with meat (typically beef or salt pork), coconut milk, and seasonings until tender. The dish is traditionally served as a main course, often accompanied by rice or ground provisions like yams and dumplings.

Malarrabia is a Peruvian seafood dish featuring grouper fish cooked with plantains, annatto, and ají amarillo in a seasoned broth with onions and garlic, served over white rice. The dish combines the savory Peruvian seasoning blend (aderezo peruano) with the mild sweetness of plantains and the distinctive flavor of yellow chili peppers.

Halva is prepared by cooking semolina with butter or oil, then combining it with a hot sugar syrup flavored with vanilla or rose water, and sometimes incorporating nuts like pine nuts or pistachios. The mixture is poured into molds and cooled until set, then typically served at room temperature as a sweet treat or dessert course in Turkish cuisine.
