Chang-Wang noodles are a Guizhou specialty from Guiyang, China, made by simmering pork intestines (chang) and pork blood curd (wang) in a spicy broth seasoned with chili oil, pickled vegetables, and Sichuan pepper, then served over rice noodles. The dish is commonly eaten as a breakfast or street-food meal, often accompanied by soybeans, chopped scallions, and crispy pork crackling.

Pelara is a Malaysian fish-based dish that is typically prepared by cooking fish with a blend of local spices and aromatics, often incorporating ingredients such as chilies, turmeric, and tamarind for a characteristic tangy and savory flavor. It is commonly served with steamed rice as part of a traditional meal, accompanied by vegetables or sambal on the side.

Chingri machher bol is a Bengali dish consisting of shrimp and fish paste formed into balls or cylindrical shapes, typically bound with spices and sometimes breadcrumbs or rice, then deep-fried until golden. The dish is traditionally served as an appetizer or side course in Bengali cuisine, often accompanied by rice and curry dishes.

Narkel chingri is a Bengali shrimp curry in which shrimp are cooked in a coconut-based gravy flavored with mustard oil, onions, garlic, ginger, and spices. The dish is typically served with steamed rice and is a traditional preparation from coastal regions of Bengal.

# Niramish Sombol

Niramish sombol is a vegetarian Bengali curry prepared by slow-cooking mixed vegetables such as potatoes, cauliflower, peas, and carrots with spices including cumin, turmeric, and ginger-garlic paste, typically finished with coconut milk or yogurt. The dish is commonly served as a main course or side dish at Bengali meals, particularly during religious observances or family gatherings.

Mochar Chhenchki is a Bengali dry stir-fry made from banana blossom (mocha), typically cut into small pieces and cooked with mustard oil, turmeric, green chilies, grated coconut, and panch phoron (a five-spice blend). It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Sojne patar bora are fritters made by coating drumstick (moringa) leaves in a spiced chickpea flour batter and deep-frying them until crisp. They are a traditional Bengali preparation typically served as a snack or side dish, often accompanied by rice or eaten during the monsoon season.

Pork ribs rice is a Chinese dish consisting of tender pork ribs served over or mixed with cooked rice, typically prepared by braising or stewing the ribs until they are fall-off-the-bone tender. The dish is commonly served as a main course in casual dining settings and home meals throughout Chinese cuisine regions.

Chingri Kobiraji is a Bengali appetizer consisting of shrimp coated in a spiced batter and deep-fried until golden, typically made with onions, green chilies, and ginger incorporated into the coating. The dish is served as a starter, often with lemon wedges and accompanied by chutneys or sliced onions.

Panifol er dalna is a Bengali vegetarian curry made from water chestnuts (panifol) simmered in a spiced gravy of onions, ginger, tomatoes, and ground spices such as turmeric, cumin, and garam masala. It is typically served hot with steamed rice or luchi as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Lebu polao is a Bengali lemon-flavored rice dish prepared by cooking rice with lemon juice, lemon zest, and aromatic spices such as cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon, often with ghee providing richness. It is typically served as part of a larger meal alongside curries, grilled meats, or vegetable dishes, and is common at festive or celebratory occasions.

Cilung is an Indonesian street food made from tapioca starch batter that is cooked by winding it around a skewer or stick over a hot griddle or open flame, resulting in a chewy, cylindrical snack. It is commonly served with a variety of savory or spicy sauces and is a popular vendor food found in West Java, particularly among school children and in casual street settings.

Sorpunti machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering sorpunti (spotted barb) fish in a light, spiced gravy made with mustard oil, turmeric, green chilies, and often potatoes or eggplant, seasoned with panch phoron or nigella seeds. It is typically served with steamed rice as part of an everyday home-style meal in West Bengal and Bangladesh.

canonicalName: alu tomato diye parse machher patla jhol
displayName: Alu tomato diye parse machher patla jhol
aliases: alu tomato diye parse machher patla jhol
description: Alu tomato diye parse machher patla jhol is a light Bengali fish curry made with parse (parshe) fish, potatoes, and tomatoes simmered in a thin, turmeric- and cumin-spiced broth. It is typically prepared by lightly frying the fish before cooking it with the vegetables in water or a minimal gravy, and is commonly served with steamed rice as an everyday meal in Bengali households.

Pat sak diye motor dal is a Bengali lentil dish prepared by cooking yellow split peas (motor dal) with jute leaves (pat sak), tempered with mustard seeds, dried red chilies, and sometimes garlic or onion. It is typically served as part of a multi-course meal alongside steamed rice in Bengali households across Bangladesh and West Bengal.

Soor plooms are a traditional Scottish hard boiled sweet made from sugar, water, and tartaric or citric acid, which gives them a distinctively sharp, sour flavour. They are small, round, typically yellow-green in colour, and are eaten as a confectionery snack, commonly associated with the town of Galashiels in the Scottish Borders.

Model is a Palembang-style soup from South Sumatra consisting of stuffed tofu or fishcake dumplings made from a mixture of ground fish and tapioca flour, served in a clear broth flavored with shrimp, garlic, and mushrooms. It is typically accompanied by yellow egg noodles, rice vermicelli, fried shallots, and sambal, and eaten as a main dish or snack.

Jaynagarer Moa is a traditional Bengali sweet originating from Jaynagar in West Bengal, India, made from kanakchur khoi (puffed rice from a specific aromatic rice variety), nolen gur (date palm jaggery), and ghee, bound together into round balls. It is a seasonal confection typically produced and consumed during the winter months when fresh date palm jaggery is available, and is often given as a gift or served as a festive snack.

Kurul shaag bhaja is a Bengali preparation in which kolmi shaag (water spinach, Ipomoea aquatica) or a similar leafy green is stir-fried in oil with garlic, dried red chilies, and mustard seeds until wilted and lightly charred. It is typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice and lentils as part of a larger meal.

Pretz are thin, straight baked pretzel-style snack sticks produced by Ezaki Glico in Japan, made from wheat flour seasoned with various flavorings such as salad, roast, and regional varieties. They are sold in small boxes as a ready-to-eat snack and are commonly consumed as a standalone light snack or paired with beverages.

Chela machher chorchori is a Bengali dry-style stir-fry dish made with chela fish (a small freshwater fish common in Bengal), cooked with mustard oil, green chilies, turmeric, and assorted vegetables such as potatoes and eggplant. It is prepared by lightly frying the fish and then cooking everything together until the liquid reduces to a semi-dry consistency, and is typically served as a side dish with steamed rice.

Chashushuli is a Georgian stew made by braising beef or veal with tomatoes, onions, and spices including coriander and black pepper until tender. The dish is traditionally served hot as a main course, often accompanied by bread or rice.

Polvo à lagareiro is a Portuguese dish of boiled octopus served with a warm olive oil and garlic dressing, typically accompanied by boiled potatoes and onions. The dish is traditionally finished with fresh coriander and crushed garlic mixed into olive oil, served as a rustic main course.

Bollo sevillano is a traditional Spanish bread roll from Seville, made from wheat flour dough that is shaped into an elongated form with a characteristic folded or twisted top crust and baked until golden. It is commonly served as a table bread or used to make sandwiches, and is a staple in bakeries and households throughout Andalusia.

Somun is a soft, round flatbread originating from Bosnia and Herzegovina, made from wheat flour, water, yeast, and salt, then baked in a traditional oven until it develops a light, pillowy texture with a slightly crisp crust. It is commonly served alongside grilled meats such as ćevapi, used to wrap or accompany the meat, and is a staple bread in Bosnian and regional Balkan cuisine.

Sarpuria is a traditional sweet from Bardhaman in West Bengal, India, made by layering thin sheets of chhena (milk curd) with a paste of khoya, ground cashews, and cardamom, then soaking the assembled cake in sugar syrup. It is typically served chilled or at room temperature as a dessert, often during festivals and special occasions.

Lotte Well Food cereal is a processed breakfast cereal produced by the South Korean food company Lotte Well Food, typically made from grains such as corn or wheat that are shaped, toasted, or puffed through industrial extrusion or baking processes. It is commonly served in a bowl with cold or warm milk and is intended as a ready-to-eat morning meal.

Rold Gold Braided Honey Wheat is a pretzel snack manufactured by Frito-Lay, made from wheat flour and honey, twisted into a braided rope shape before being baked. It is typically served as a ready-to-eat snack straight from the bag, often accompanying dips or consumed on its own as a packaged snack food.

Miguelitos are Spanish pastries made from puff pastry filled with a custard or cream filling, typically topped with icing and often sprinkled with toasted coconut or almonds. They are baked until golden and commonly served as a sweet treat or dessert in Spanish cafes and bakeries.

This Bengali dish features shrimp cooked with colocasia leaves (kochur loti), which are sautéed or braised with the protein in a spiced curry base typically made with onions, garlic, ginger, and spices. The dish is traditionally served as a main course with rice or flatbread, with the tender leaves providing a leafy texture alongside the shrimp.

A pithivier is a French pastry made of two rounds of puff pastry enclosing a filling of almond cream, with the top typically scored in a decorative spiral or radial pattern. It is traditionally served as a dessert or sweet course, often dusted with powdered sugar after baking until the pastry is golden and crisp.

Berliner Luft is a light German dessert made by folding whipped egg whites and whipped cream into a lemon-flavored mixture of egg yolks, sugar, and gelatin, resulting in an airy, mousse-like texture. It is typically served chilled in individual glasses or bowls, often accompanied by a raspberry or red fruit sauce.

Castagnaccio is a traditional Italian flatbread made from chestnut flour, water, and olive oil, often enriched with pine nuts, raisins, and rosemary. The batter is poured onto an oiled pan and baked until golden, then typically served warm as a simple dessert or snack in Tuscany and other regions of central Italy.

Pucca is a Japanese snack produced by Meiji consisting of small, pretzel-shaped wheat shells filled with chocolate cream. The bite-sized pieces are typically eaten as a standalone snack and are recognized by their distinctive star or flower-shaped pretzel exterior encasing a smooth chocolate interior.

Pinza is a traditional Italian sweet bread made with flour, butter, eggs, and dried fruits such as raisins and candied citrus peel, often enriched with anise seeds. It is typically prepared as a round loaf and served during festive occasions, particularly around Christmas and Easter, either sliced plain or with accompaniments like cheese or cured meats.

Angélique is a French confection made by candying the stems of Angelica archangelica through repeated immersions in sugar syrup, resulting in a bright green, crystallized product. It is commonly used as a decorative garnish on pastries, cakes, and desserts, and is a traditional specialty of the town of Niort in western France.

Burma soup is a hearty Southeast Asian curry-based soup made with pork, udon noodles, and a blend of curry powder and chili peppers, combined with vegetables such as eggplant, potato, tomato, kidney beans, and summer vegetables. The dish is traditionally served hot as a main course soup, with the curry-infused broth coating the tender noodles and meat.

Pondkoek is a South African fried dough cake made from a spiced batter that is cooked in oil until golden and puffed. It is commonly served as a street food or snack, often dusted with cinnamon sugar or accompanied by syrup.

Hé (籺) is a category of traditional rice-based food originating from the Hakka and Cantonese communities of southern China, made by grinding glutinous or non-glutinous rice into flour or paste and shaping it into various forms such as dumplings, cakes, or rolls, often filled with savory or sweet ingredients including pork, vegetables, peanuts, or sesame. These preparations are typically steamed, boiled, or pan-fried and are commonly made and consumed during festivals, seasonal celebrations, and ceremonial occasions as culturally significant foods.

Hallaca is a traditional Venezuelan and Colombian dish consisting of a corn dough filling wrapped in banana or plantain leaves and boiled. The filling typically contains seasoned meat, chorizo, raisins, olives, and almonds, with the dough colored with annatto and flavored with wine and vinegar, traditionally served during Christmas festivities.

Kentucky Cream Candy is a traditional confection made by cooking sugar, cream, and butter to a specific temperature, then pulling or beating the mixture until it becomes light, opaque, and develops a soft, chewy texture. It is typically formed into small pieces or logs and served as a holiday or regional sweet, particularly associated with Kentucky's confectionery traditions.

Groundnut soup is prepared by grinding roasted peanuts into a paste and simmering them with broth, vegetables, and meat or fish to create a rich, creamy base. The soup is typically served hot as a main course or starter, often accompanied by rice, fufu, or other starch-based sides in West African cuisines.

Pampapato di Ferrara is a traditional Italian cake from Ferrara made with chocolate, honey, candied fruits, and spices, prepared by mixing these ingredients into a dense batter that is baked until firm. This rich, dark cake is typically served sliced as a dessert or sweet treat, often during festive occasions and holidays.

Coro is a traditional Indonesian ginger-based drink made by boiling fresh ginger with palm sugar and water, sometimes combined with other spices such as lemongrass or pandan leaf. It is typically served warm and consumed as a everyday beverage or as a remedy for warming the body, particularly in cooler weather or during illness.

Semur Jepara is a braised meat dish originating from Jepara, Central Java, in which beef or other proteins are slow-cooked in a dark sauce made from sweet soy sauce (kecap manis), shallots, garlic, candlenut, nutmeg, and cloves. It is typically served with steamed white rice and is distinguished from other regional semur variations by its notably sweet and aromatic flavor profile derived from the spice blend used in Jepara's local tradition.

Strawberry pasta is a sweet dish made by combining cooked pasta with fresh strawberries and sugar, finished with cream to form a light sauce. It is typically served as a dessert or a sweet main course, a preparation found in Polish home cooking and regional Central European cuisine.

Yūan-yaki is a Japanese grilling method in which fish or meat is marinated in a mixture of soy sauce, sake, and mirin, often with a slice of yuzu citrus added to the marinade, before being grilled. It is commonly prepared with fish such as salmon, yellowtail, or sea bream, and is typically served as a main course in Japanese cuisine.

Macher jhuri vaja is a Bengali preparation made by finely shredding or mincing fish and dry-frying it with spices such as turmeric, chili, and mustard oil until the mixture becomes crumbly and lightly crisp. It is commonly served as a side dish or accompaniment with steamed rice in Bengali households across Bangladesh and the Indian state of West Bengal.

Paya is a slow-cooked stew made from animal feet (typically from goats, sheep, or cows) that are cleaned, blanched, and simmered with spices, onions, and aromatics for several hours until the meat becomes tender and the cooking liquid thickens. The dish is traditionally served hot in bowls with the meat, bones, and rich broth, often accompanied by bread for soaking up the sauce, and is common in South Asian and Middle Eastern cuisines.

Ban machher rosa is a Bengali fish curry made with hilsa fish (ban maach) cooked in a spiced tomato-based gravy with onions, ginger, garlic, and aromatic spices. The dish is traditionally served with rice or flatbread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

French onion dip is made by caramelizing onions and blending them with sour cream or a cream cheese base, creating a savory spread with deep onion flavor. It is typically served as an appetizer dip with crackers, bread, or vegetable crudités.

Bullitu is a traditional Sardinian fish stew prepared by simmering a variety of whole local fish together with potatoes, tomatoes, onions, and herbs in a broth seasoned with saffron. It is typically served as a main course, often accompanied by slices of toasted or grilled bread, and is common in coastal communities of Sardinia.

Klatak satay is a Javanese dish originating from Bantul, Yogyakarta, made from skewered goat meat seasoned primarily with salt and grilled over charcoal using iron skewers (commonly bicycle spokes or iron rods) rather than bamboo. It is typically served with a bowl of warm broth, rice, and sometimes gulai (a spiced coconut-based curry), and the name 'klatak' is said to derive from the crackling sound the meat makes during grilling.

Nasi kebuli is an Indonesian rice dish made by cooking rice with meat (typically beef or goat), spices, and ghee or oil, resulting in fragrant, colored grains similar to pilaf. The dish is traditionally served with pickled vegetables and fresh salads, commonly prepared for special occasions and celebrations in Indonesian cuisine.

Gnetum africanum, commonly known as 'eru' or 'okazi,' is a leafy vine native to Central and West Africa whose leaves are sliced into thin strips and cooked in soups or stews alongside ingredients such as palm oil, crayfish, smoked or dried fish, and various meats. It is typically served as a main dish accompaniment with starchy staples such as fufu, water fufu, or pounded yam, and is widely consumed in countries including Cameroon, Nigeria, and the Democratic Republic of Congo.

Ragù is a slow-cooked meat sauce made by browning ground or finely chopped meat with vegetables such as onions, celery, and carrots, then simmering in tomato and broth for extended periods to develop deep flavors. It is traditionally served over pasta, though French versions may vary in their preparation and ingredient ratios compared to Italian interpretations.

Khanom babin is a Thai coconut pancake made by combining grated coconut, rice flour, sugar, and egg into a batter that is cooked in a greased pan or cast-iron mold until golden and slightly crispy on the outside while remaining soft inside. It is a traditional Thai snack commonly sold by street vendors and at markets, often served as a small individual cake or bite-sized piece.

Ezme is a Turkish salad made by finely chopping tomatoes, chili peppers, and other vegetables, then mixing them with olive oil and spices to create a paste-like consistency. It is typically served as a meze (appetizer) or side dish, often alongside grilled meats or bread for scooping.

Chapila machh bhaja is a Bengali fried fish dish made with chapila (a small freshwater fish), coated in spiced flour batter and deep-fried until crispy. The fish is typically seasoned with turmeric, chili powder, and salt, and is commonly served as an appetizer or side dish with rice and curry in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: narkeler dudhe lau shak chingri machh
aliases: narkeler dudhe lau shak chingri machh
description: This Bengali dish combines bottle gourd (lau) and shrimp (chingri machh) cooked in a coconut milk (narkeler dudhe) based curry. The vegetables and seafood are typically simmered together until tender, creating a mild, creamy preparation commonly served with rice as part of a Bengali meal.

Kağıt kebabı is a Turkish meat dish where seasoned meat and vegetables are wrapped in paper or parchment, then cooked in an oven until the paper chars, trapping steam and flavors inside. The package is typically opened tableside, and the dish is served with rice or bread as a main course.

Appenzeller Pantli IGP is a traditional Swiss cheese made from raw or pasteurized cow's milk in the Appenzell region, characterized by its semi-hard texture and distinctive herbal brine wash that gives it a brownish rind and complex flavor. The cheese is typically served as part of a cheese board or melted in traditional Alpine dishes, with its strong aromatic profile making it suitable for both standalone tasting and cooking applications.

Kuftetirş is a Kurdish dish consisting of meatballs made from ground meat and bulgur, cooked in a sour, tangy broth typically acidulated with ingredients such as pomegranate molasses or sour grape juice. It is commonly served as a main course in Kurdish cuisine, particularly in regions of Turkey and northern Iraq, where the tart cooking liquid distinguishes it from other meatball preparations.

Taco pizza combines pizza dough base with taco-seasoned ground meat, cheese, and traditional taco toppings such as lettuce, tomatoes, and sour cream. The dish is typically baked as a single pizza and served sliced, merging Mexican and Italian culinary traditions.

Encharcada is a traditional Portuguese sweet made by cooking egg yolks with sugar syrup, typically flavored with cinnamon, resulting in a rich, syrupy custard with a soft, soaked texture. It is a conventual dessert with origins in Portuguese convent cuisine, commonly served in small portions as a dessert or sweet treat throughout Portugal.

Sojne dantar bora are fritters made from drumstick tree stems (Moringa oleifera), which are chopped, mixed with spiced chickpea flour or lentil batter, and deep-fried until crisp. They are a traditional Bengali preparation typically served as a snack or side dish.

Gondhoraj pomfret fry is a Bengali preparation in which whole or sliced pomfret fish is marinated with Gondhoraj lime juice and zest, turmeric, red chili, and spices before being shallow- or pan-fried until the exterior is crisp and golden. It is typically served as a starter or side dish, with the Gondhoraj lime imparting a distinctive floral citrus aroma characteristic of this variety native to West Bengal.

canonicalName: begun diye parse machher tel jhal
displayName: Begun diye parse machher tel jhal
aliases: begun diye parse machher tel jhal
description: This Bengali fish curry features fish cooked in a spiced oil-based sauce with eggplant (begun) and green chilies, creating a moderately spiced preparation. The dish is typically served with steamed rice and is common in Bengali cuisine across West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Kale noodles are made by incorporating kale—either as a puree, juice, or dried powder—into pasta dough, producing noodles with a green color and mild earthy flavor. They are typically boiled and served with a variety of sauces or stir-fried with vegetables, proteins, and seasonings depending on regional preparation styles.

Fava is a Turkish dish made by cooking dried split fava beans (broad beans) until soft, then puréeing them with olive oil, onion, and seasoning into a smooth, firm paste that sets upon cooling. It is typically served cold or at room temperature, sliced or scooped, and garnished with olive oil, dill, and sometimes caramelized onions, commonly offered as a meze.

Bata machher sorse jhal is a Bengali fish curry made with freshwater catfish (bata) cooked in a mustard-based sauce that combines ground mustard seeds, turmeric, and chili for a spiced, tangy flavor. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as part of Bengali cuisine meals.

Fruit salad is prepared by cutting fresh fruits into bite-sized pieces and combining them in a bowl, often with a light dressing such as honey, citrus juice, or simple syrup. It is typically served chilled as a refreshing side dish, dessert, or breakfast item across many cuisines worldwide.

Zwiebeldinnele is a thin, flatbread-style dish from the Alsace-Baden region, made from a yeast or leavened dough topped with a mixture of onions, cream or crème fraîche, lardons, and sometimes eggs, then baked in a hot oven. It is closely related to tarte flambée (Flammkuchen) and is typically served as a light meal or snack, often cut into portions and shared at the table.

Chingri pokora is a Bengali appetizer made from shrimp coated in a seasoned gram flour batter and deep-fried until crispy. It is typically served hot as a starter or snack, often accompanied by tamarind chutney or green chutney for dipping.

Sheemer bichi diye chingri machh is a Bengali dish prepared by cooking prawns (chingri machh) with hyacinth bean seeds (sheemer bichi), typically simmered with mustard oil, turmeric, green chilies, and aromatic spices. It is commonly served as a side dish with steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Lau patai chingri vapa is a Bengali steamed dish in which prawns (chingri) are wrapped in bottle gourd leaves (lau pata) along with mustard paste, mustard oil, green chilies, and spices, then cooked by steaming until tender. It is typically served as a main course accompaniment to steamed rice and is a traditional preparation associated with Bengali home cooking.

Dambu is a Nigerian dish made by steaming or dry-cooking couscous (or finely shredded meat in some versions) with onion, moringa leaves, cooking oil, and salt until the grains are light and separated. It is typically served as a main dish or side, often accompanied by soups or stews, and is common in northern Nigerian cuisine.

Chitol Machher Kalia is a Bengali curry made with chitol fish (an Indian freshwater fish) cooked in a spiced gravy typically containing onions, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, and other aromatic spices. The dish is traditionally served with rice and is a staple preparation in Bengali cuisine.

Balparmak tatlısı is a Turkish dessert made from a dough of flour, eggs, and butter that is shaped into finger-like pieces, fried until golden, and then soaked or coated in honey, giving the dish its name ('bal' meaning honey, 'parmak' meaning finger). It is typically served as a sweet snack or dessert, often garnished with nuts such as walnuts or pistachios.

Lamprey pie is a traditional dish made by encasing lampreys—eel-like jawless fish—in a pastry crust, typically alongside ingredients such as red wine, spices, and sometimes bacon or leeks, then baked until the filling is set and the pastry is cooked through. It has historical associations with medieval English royal banquets and is occasionally prepared today as a heritage or ceremonial dish.

Water pie is a Depression-era American dish made by filling an unbaked pie crust with water, flour, sugar, butter, and vanilla extract, then baking until the filling sets into a simple custard-like consistency. It was historically prepared as an inexpensive substitute when dairy and eggs were scarce, and is typically served as a dessert at room temperature or chilled.

Ritha machh bhaja is a Bengali fried fish dish in which fish pieces are marinated with turmeric, salt, and spices before being shallow- or deep-fried in mustard oil until crisp. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside steamed rice and dal in traditional Bengali meals.

Bici bici is a Turkish cold dessert made from starch-thickened water that is set into a gel, then cut or shaped into small pieces and served in a bowl with crushed ice, rose water syrup, and shredded coconut or other toppings. It is a traditional summer refreshment particularly associated with the Adana region of southern Turkey, typically sold by street vendors during hot weather.

A tostada is a crispy fried tortilla, typically made from corn, that serves as a edible base or vessel. It is commonly topped with ingredients such as refried beans, shredded meat, lettuce, cheese, sour cream, and salsa, and is eaten as a standalone dish or appetizer in Mexican cuisine.

Tuhuk fish satay is an Indonesian grilled skewer dish made from tuhuk (blue marlin or swordfish), in which the fish meat is typically seasoned, formed onto skewers, and cooked over an open flame or charcoal. It is commonly served as a street food or main dish, often accompanied by rice and dipping sauces, and is particularly associated with coastal regions of Indonesia where large pelagic fish are locally available.

Topfenknödel are Austrian dumplings made from quark (curd cheese), eggs, breadcrumbs, and sometimes semolina, which are shaped into balls and boiled until they float to the surface. They are typically served as a sweet dessert course with plum compote, melted butter, or fruit sauce, and are often dusted with powdered sugar.

Sorse posto parse is a Bengali dish in which hilsa fish (ilish) is cooked in a sauce made from mustard paste (posto refers to poppy seeds, though the dish centers on mustard/sorse) combined with poppy seed paste, green chilies, and mustard oil. It is typically prepared by simmering the fish pieces in the ground mustard and poppy seed mixture and is served with steamed rice as a traditional meal in Bengali households.

Soto ayam is an Indonesian chicken soup made by simmering chicken in a turmeric and spice-infused broth with ingredients such as garlic, galangal, and candlenuts, typically served with rice, hard-boiled eggs, and fresh vegetables. The dish is commonly eaten as a breakfast or light lunch item throughout Indonesia and is often garnished with fried shallots and fresh herbs.

Ganca is a traditional porridge made from ground maize, cooked by boiling the meal in water until it reaches a thick, dense consistency. It is a staple dish commonly served as an accompaniment to stews, vegetables, or other protein-based dishes in parts of Central and Eastern Africa.

Skoblyanka is a traditional Russian dish made by sautéing thin slices or shavings of veal together with edible mushrooms, then combining them with potatoes, typically baked or finished in the oven until cooked through. It is served as a hearty main course, often prepared in a skillet or pot, and represents a straightforward meat-and-mushroom preparation common in Russian home and tavern cooking.

A cheese zombie is a baked sandwich made by filling a split bread roll or bun with processed or melted cheese, then baking it until the bread is toasted and the cheese is fully melted throughout. It is commonly served as a school cafeteria lunch item in parts of the United States, particularly associated with California school lunch programs.

The end of the bread, also known as the heel or crust end of a loaf, is the outermost slice cut from either end of a baked bread loaf, consisting primarily of crust with minimal interior crumb. It is typically served as-is alongside meals, used for dipping into soups or sauces, or repurposed in preparations such as breadcrumbs, croutons, or bread pudding.

Qatayef are small, stuffed pancakes made from a simple batter that is cooked on one side only, then folded and filled with either nuts (pistachios or walnuts) mixed with sugar and spices, or soft cheese. The filled pastries are typically fried until golden and served warm, often drizzled with simple sugar syrup or honey, and are traditionally consumed during Ramadan.

Doi bele machh is a Bengali fish dish in which pieces of fish are cooked in a sauce made with yogurt (doi) and split lentils (bele or mung dal), typically seasoned with turmeric, green chilies, and mustard or cumin. It is commonly served as a main course alongside steamed rice as part of a traditional Bengali meal.

Odawaradon is a Japanese donburi (rice bowl) dish originating from Odawara city in Kanagawa Prefecture, featuring seafood—particularly fish and shellfish sourced from Sagami Bay—served over steamed white rice. It is typically presented as a single-bowl meal in restaurants and food stalls in the Odawara region, showcasing locally caught ingredients such as sea bream, squid, or shrimp.

Rosto is a Goan pot-roasted meat dish, typically made with beef or pork that is marinated in a spiced vinegar-based mixture containing garlic, ginger, and whole spices, then slow-cooked until tender. It is commonly served as a main course in Goan households and restaurants, often accompanied by bread or rice.

Liverwurst is a spreadable sausage made from liver, offal, and meat, typically including pork liver and head meat, mixed with spices and fat, then smoked or cooked. It is commonly served sliced on bread or crackers as a cold cut in appetizers and casual meals, particularly in European and German cuisine.

Topshe fry is a Bengali dish made by coating topshe fish (Polynemus paradiseus, also known as mango fish or paradise threadfin) in a seasoned batter, typically made with gram flour and spices, and deep-frying until crisp. It is commonly served as a starter or side dish in Bengali households and restaurants, particularly in West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Pempek sambal is an Indonesian dish consisting of pempek (a savory cake made from fish and tapioca flour) served with sambal, a spiced chili paste condiment. The pempek is typically fried until golden and accompanied by a hot, tangy sambal for dipping, commonly eaten as a snack or light meal in Indonesian cuisine.

Choco Monaka Jumbo is a Japanese ice cream sandwich produced by Morinaga & Company, consisting of vanilla ice cream and a chocolate wafer filling encased between two crisp monaka wafer shells coated with chocolate. It is sold as a packaged frozen dessert and is commonly consumed as a handheld snack throughout Japan.

canonicalName: five spice powder flavored minced pork spicy curry
displayName: Five-spice powder flavored minced pork spicy curry
aliases: five spice powder flavored minced pork spicy curry
description: This dish is prepared by cooking minced pork seasoned with five-spice powder—a blend of star anise, cloves, cinnamon, Sichuan pepper, and fennel seeds—in a spiced curry sauce that incorporates chili-based heat. It is typically served over steamed rice or noodles as a main course, combining the aromatic depth of five-spice seasoning with the pungency of curry spices.

aliases: sauteed shredded pork in sweet bean sauce
description: Jing jiang rou si is a northern Chinese dish of julienned pork stir-fried with tianmianjiang (sweet bean paste) and onion, producing a savory, sauce-coated filling. It is traditionally served alongside thin sheets of tofu skin or pancakes, which are used to wrap the pork mixture at the table.

Endive with cracklings is a composed salad featuring blanched or raw endive leaves arranged on a plate and topped with rendered pork cracklings (crispy fried pork skin). The dish is typically served as a cold appetizer or light course, with the contrast between the slightly bitter endive and salty, crunchy cracklings forming its characteristic flavor profile.

Kuwah eungkôt yèe is a traditional Acehnese fish curry from the Aceh region of Indonesia, prepared by simmering fish in a spiced broth made with turmeric, lemongrass, galangal, chili, and coconut milk. It is typically served as a main dish alongside steamed rice and is a staple of everyday Acehnese home cooking.

Kajli Machher Jhal is a Bengali fish curry prepared by cooking kajli (kaikka or Gangetic hairfin anchovy) fish in a pungent mustard-based sauce with mustard oil, green chilies, turmeric, and nigella seeds. It is typically served with steamed white rice as a traditional everyday meal in Bengali households of West Bengal and Bangladesh.

Oliebol is a Dutch fried pastry made from a yeast dough mixed with raisins and sometimes apple pieces, then deep-fried until golden brown. It is traditionally served as a warm snack during winter months and New Year celebrations, typically dusted with powdered sugar.

Egg biryani is a rice dish prepared by layering or cooking boiled eggs with long-grain basmati rice, whole spices such as cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon, fried onions, yogurt, and aromatic herbs including mint and cilantro. It is commonly served as a main course alongside raita, a yogurt-based condiment, and is found across South Asian cuisine as well as in international adaptations.

Balungan hayam is an Indonesian dish made from chicken bones (balungan means bones in Javanese/Sundanese, hayam means chicken), typically simmered or cooked in a spiced broth with aromatics such as galangal, lemongrass, shallots, garlic, and chilies. It is commonly served as a soup or broth-based dish, often accompanied by steamed rice, and is a popular street food and home-cooked meal in Java and other parts of Indonesia.

Taiwan Mince is a Taiwanese braised pork dish made by slow-cooking ground or finely chopped pork belly with soy sauce, rice wine, five-spice powder, shallots, and often hard-boiled eggs until the meat becomes tender and richly sauced. It is typically served over steamed white rice, a preparation known in Mandarin as lu rou fan (滷肉飯), and is a common everyday meal found throughout Taiwan.

Yakiimo is a Japanese street food and snack consisting of whole sweet potatoes, typically of the Beniazuma or Satsumaimo variety, slow-roasted in a stone-filled drum or oven until the natural sugars caramelize and the flesh becomes soft and sweet. It is commonly sold from vendor carts during autumn and winter and served whole in its skin, without added seasonings or toppings.

Tarte al d'jote is a savory open-faced tart originating from Nivelles, Belgium, made with a yeast-leavened dough filled with a mixture of fresh beet greens (or chard), young Nivelles cheese (called djote or boulette de Nivelles), eggs, and butter. It is traditionally baked until the filling is set and lightly golden, and is typically served as a main course or starter in Belgian cuisine.

Ahriche is a Moroccan dish made from sheep or goat intestines that are cleaned, stuffed with offal and fat, then skewered and grilled over charcoal. It is commonly sold as street food and served hot, often accompanied by cumin and salt.

Matzah brei is prepared by soaking matzah in water, then mixing it with beaten eggs and frying the mixture in fat or oil until golden. It is traditionally served during Passover as a breakfast or light meal dish, often with toppings such as cinnamon and sugar, jam, or sour cream.

Orange cuttlefish is a Chinese dish prepared by stir-frying cleaned cuttlefish (Sepiida) with aromatics and seasoning to produce a bright orange-colored coating, often achieved through the use of chili, tomato-based sauces, or food coloring. It is typically served as a main or shared dish in Chinese restaurants, with the cuttlefish cut into scored pieces that curl during cooking to expose a tender interior.

# Alur Malai Dom

Alur malai dom is a Bengali potato curry prepared by simmering potatoes in a creamy sauce made from yogurt, coconut milk, and spices such as cumin, coriander, and ginger-garlic paste. This vegetarian dish is typically served as a main course accompaniment to rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

# Lebupatar Ghee Bhat

Lebupatar ghee bhat is a traditional Assamese rice dish prepared by cooking rice with clarified butter (ghee), along with ingredients such as lentils, vegetables, and spices, typically using a one-pot cooking method. The dish is commonly served as a complete meal during festive occasions, family gatherings, and special celebrations in Assam, India.

# Thorer Dhoka

Thorer dhoka is a Bhutanese steamed cake made by fermenting a batter of rice flour, chickpea flour, and various spices including chili, ginger, and garlic, then steaming the mixture in banana leaves or molds until set. It is typically served as a side dish or appetizer during meals, often accompanied by chili and cheese, and is particularly common in Bhutanese cuisine during festivals and special occasions.

Bread and dripping is a traditional Hungarian dish consisting of sliced bread topped with rendered lard (dripping) and caramelized onions seasoned with paprika. The dish is typically served as a simple, hearty meal or appetizer, often accompanied by additional raw red onion on the side.

Macaroni and cheese is prepared by cooking macaroni pasta and combining it with a cheddar cheese sauce made from butter, flour, and Cheddar cheese. The dish is traditionally served as a main course or side dish in English cuisine.

Sticky rice is cooked and then wrapped or served inside a hollowed bamboo segment, which imparts a subtle bamboo aroma to the rice during steaming or cooking. This preparation method is traditional in Southeast Asian cuisine, particularly in Thailand and Laos, where it is commonly served as an accompaniment to grilled meats, curries, or eaten on its own as a staple carbohydrate.

Filipino spaghetti is prepared by tossing cooked spaghetti with a sweet sauce made from banana ketchup, tomato sauce, and ground meat (picadillo), typically combined with sliced sausage for additional flavor and texture. The dish is commonly served as a main course at family gatherings, celebrations, and casual meals, and is characterized by its distinctly sweet taste profile.

Mochar pulao is a rice dish made by cooking basmati rice with banana flower (mocha) and aromatic spices such as cumin, cardamom, and bay leaves, typically prepared using the pilaf method where rice and ingredients are cooked together in a single pot. This vegetarian dish is commonly served as a main course in Bengali and Assamese cuisines, often accompanied by yogurt or a protein-based curry.

Kabli chholar ghugni is a Bengali street food and snack made from boiled white chickpeas (kabli chana) that are seasoned with spices, mustard oil, and often combined with ginger, green chilies, and onions. The dish is typically served warm as a popular casual appetizer or light meal, commonly found at roadside vendors and food stalls throughout Bengal.

Potoler Dolma is a Bengali dish made from pointed gourds (potoler) that are hollowed out and stuffed with a spiced mixture of lentils, peas, and sometimes meat or paneer. The stuffed gourds are then cooked in oil or ghee with onions and spices until tender, and typically served as a vegetable curry alongside rice or bread.

Magur machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared by simmering catfish in a spiced broth made with onions, tomatoes, and a blend of spices including turmeric and cumin. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice and is a staple preparation in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: katla machher jhol alu potol diye
displayName: Katla machher jhol alu potol diye
aliases: katla machher jhol alu potol diye
description: Katla machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry made with katla (a freshwater carp), potatoes, and pointed gourds (potol), simmered in a light, spiced broth typically flavored with turmeric, cumin, and mustard oil. The dish is traditionally served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: alu dhonepata diye boyal machher jhol
displayName: Alu dhonepata diye Boyal machher jhol
aliases: alu dhonepata diye boyal machher jhol
description: Boyal machher jhol is a Bengali fish curry prepared with freshwater boyal fish simmered in a light broth base. The dish is flavored with potatoes (alu) and fresh coriander leaves (dhonepata), creating a mild, aromatic preparation typically served with steamed rice.

Amudi machher begun diye jhol is a Bengali curry featuring amudi fish cooked in a broth with eggplant, traditionally prepared with onions, garlic, ginger, and spices. The dish is typically served as a main course with steamed rice, with the fish and vegetables simmered together until tender in a light gravy.

canonicalName: alu begun diye parse machher jhol
displayName: Alu begun diye parse machher jhol
aliases: alu begun diye parse machher jhol
description: This Bengali fish curry combines pieces of fish with potato and eggplant in a spiced broth, prepared by simmering the ingredients together with onions, tomatoes, and traditional Bengali spices. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

canonicalName: thankuni pata diye latha macher jhol
displayName: Thankuni pata diye latha macher jhol
aliases: thankuni pata diye latha macher jhol
description: This Bengali fish curry features freshwater fish cooked in a light broth with thankuni leaves (water hyssop), a leafy green vegetable common in Bengali cuisine, along with lath (a type of gourd) and traditional spices. The dish is typically served as a main course with steamed rice and is characteristic of Bengali home cooking, emphasizing delicate flavors and simple preparation methods.

Chhoto alur niramish dom is a Bengali vegetarian curry made with small potatoes braised in a spiced onion and tomato-based gravy, typically prepared with mustard oil, cumin, and bay leaves. The dish is traditionally served as an accompaniment to rice or bread in Bengali cuisine.

Moussaka is a layered baked dish consisting of eggplant, spiced meat sauce, and béchamel sauce, typically prepared by alternating these components in a deep pan and baking until golden. The dish is served as a main course, often cut into portions and accompanied by a simple salad or bread.

Bhetki machher kanta chorchori is a Bengali preparation of threadfin fish (bhetki) where the fish is cleaned, cut into pieces, and cooked with spices, onions, and tomatoes in a semi-dry curry style. The dish is typically served with rice as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Bacalhau à Brás is a Portuguese dish made from shredded salt cod (Gadus morhua) that is deep-fried alongside thin-cut shoestring potatoes and typically garnished with sliced hard-boiled egg and black olives. It is traditionally served as a main course in Portuguese cuisine, often accompanied by rice or bread.

Alu Bhaja is a Bengali side dish made from thinly sliced potatoes that are deep-fried until crispy and golden, typically seasoned with salt, turmeric, and nigella seeds. It is commonly served as an accompaniment to rice and curries in Bengali cuisine.

Pastéis de Bacalhau are fried croquettes made from a mixture of dried salted cod, mashed potatoes, onions, parsley, and egg, formed into small cylindrical or oval shapes and deep-fried until golden. They are traditionally served as an appetizer or snack, often accompanied by a lemon wedge or aioli sauce.

Marraqueta is a Chilean bread made from wheat flour, water, salt, and yeast, typically formed into an elongated oval shape with a crispy crust and soft interior crumb. It is commonly served as a staple bread accompaniment to meals or eaten fresh with butter and cheese.

Mollete of Antequera is a traditional Spanish bread from Andalusia made with a simple dough of flour, water, salt, and olive oil, characterized by its soft crumb and crispy crust with a distinctive split down the middle. It is typically served warm as a breakfast or light meal, often split and filled with ingredients such as jamón, cheese, or tomato, or eaten plain with olive oil.

Darnitsa bread is a traditional Ukrainian rye bread made from a mixture of rye and wheat flours combined with dough starter, resulting in a dense crumb with a dark brown color. The loaf is typically baked until it develops a thick crust and is commonly served sliced as an accompaniment to meals or used for open-faced sandwiches in Ukrainian cuisine.

Galician bread is a traditional Spanish bread made from wheat flour, water, salt, and yeast, characterized by its crusty exterior and soft interior crumb. It is typically served as an accompaniment to meals or used for traditional Galician dishes, and is baked using standard bread-making techniques involving fermentation and oven baking.

Marinera is a traditional seafood dish from Cartagena, Spain, prepared by cooking fresh fish or shellfish with tomatoes, garlic, olive oil, and white wine in a flavorful broth. The dish is typically served hot in its cooking liquid as a main course, often accompanied by bread for soaking up the sauce.

Russisch Brot is a German spiced cookie made with rye flour, molasses, and warming spices such as cinnamon, cloves, and aniseed, typically formed into figure-eight or pretzel shapes. The cookies are traditionally glazed with a hard icing coating and served as a sweet treat, particularly during the Christmas season.

Frutta martorana is a Sicilian confection made from marzipan (almond paste and sugar) that is hand-molded into the shape of fruits, vegetables, or other objects and painted with food coloring to resemble realistic produce. Traditionally served during All Saints' Day celebrations and as a dessert or gift item, these decorative treats are eaten as candy and require no cooking after the marzipan base is prepared.

Tarte au sucre de Pérouges is a traditional French pastry from the Pérouges region consisting of a thin, crispy pastry base topped with a sweet caramelized sugar and butter filling. The tart is typically served as a dessert or snack, often enjoyed warm or at room temperature.

Seviyan is a sweet dish made from thin roasted vermicelli noodles cooked with ghee, milk, and sugar, often enriched with nuts and dried fruits such as cashews, almonds, and raisins. It is traditionally served as a dessert or festive dish in South Asian cuisine, particularly during celebrations and religious occasions.

Brigidino di Lamporecchio is a traditional Tuscan waffle cookie made from a thin batter of flour, eggs, sugar, and anise seeds, cooked between heated metal plates until crisp and golden. The small, delicate wafers are typically served as a sweet snack or dessert and are a specialty product of the town of Lamporecchio in Tuscany, Italy.

Brutti e buoni are Italian hazelnut cookies made by roasting hazelnuts and combining them with whipped egg whites and sugar, then baking until crispy. These bite-sized confections are traditionally served as an informal sweet treat or dessert, particularly in the Piedmont region of Italy.

Honeycomb toffee is made by combining toffee or caramel with whipped egg whites and honey, creating a light, airy confection with a honeycomb-like structure through the incorporation of air bubbles. The mixture is typically set, cut into pieces, and often coated with chocolate, served as a candy or dessert component.

Gulab jamun consists of milk solids formed into balls, deep-fried until golden brown, and then soaked in a light sugar syrup infused with rose water and cardamom. This popular South Asian dessert is traditionally served warm or at room temperature, often accompanied by rabri (reduced milk) or ice cream.

Rosogolla is a spongy spherical dessert made from chena (fresh cheese curds) that are kneaded, shaped into balls, and boiled in sugar syrup until they absorb the sweetness and become soft and spongy. Typically served chilled or at room temperature, either in the remaining sugar syrup or as a standalone sweet, and is a traditional Bengali confection popular across South Asia.

Pangas machher kalia is a Bengali curry made with pangas catfish that is marinated and then cooked in a spiced yogurt-based gravy with onions, ginger, garlic, and traditional Bengali spices. The dish is typically served with steamed rice or flatbread as a main course in Bengali cuisine.

Tsuyu-yakisoba is a noodle dish made by stir-frying pre-cooked yakisoba noodles with vegetables and protein, then tossing them in a hot dashi-based broth called tsuyu. It is typically served in a bowl with the noodles submerged in the savory broth and garnished with bonito flakes, green onions, and aonori seaweed powder.

Lobster Thermidor is prepared by removing lobster meat from the shell, mixing it with a creamy sauce made from egg yolk and sherry, then returning the mixture to the shell and browning it under heat. The dish is typically served as an elegant entrée, often presented in the lobster's natural shell as a single serving.

Banana cake is made by mixing mashed bananas with flour, sugar, eggs, and butter, then baking the batter until golden. The cake is typically served sliced at room temperature or slightly warm, often with frosting, and is commonly found in cafes and bakeries worldwide.

Pampushka are small Ukrainian fried dough buns made from a yeast-based dough of wheat flour, baker's yeast, oil, and salt, then deep-fried until golden. They are traditionally served warm as a side dish or appetizer, often accompanied by garlic sauce or borscht.

Salade landaise is a traditional French salad from the Landes region of southwestern France, typically composed of frisée or other bitter greens mixed with duck confit, foie gras, and croutons, dressed with a warm vinaigrette. The dish is served as a starter or light course, often featuring a poached egg on top and garnished with shallots and parsley.

A minced meat patty is formed by mixing ground meat (typically beef, pork, chicken, or lamb) with seasonings and binders, then shaping the mixture into a flat or rounded cake before cooking. The patty is commonly grilled, fried, or broiled and served as the primary component of sandwiches, on plates with side dishes, or as part of composed meals across various culinary traditions.

Pieds paquets is a traditional Provençal dish consisting of sheep's feet and small packets of seasoned offal (typically made from rumen and other organ meats) braised together in a tomato-based sauce with white wine and herbs. The dish is typically served as a main course in French Mediterranean cuisine, often accompanied by potatoes or bread to absorb the sauce.

Mashed potato is prepared by boiling peeled potatoes until tender, then crushing or whipping them with butter, milk or cream, and seasoning with salt and pepper. It is commonly served as a side dish alongside meat, poultry, or vegetables in international cuisine.

Xiaolongbao are steamed dumplings made from thin dough wrappers filled with a mixture of pork, shiitake mushrooms, napa cabbage, and wood ear fungus. These soup dumplings are traditionally served in bamboo steamers as a dim sum dish, with the filling creating a savory broth inside each dumpling when steamed.

Ice cream is churned and frozen to a smooth consistency, then served in a crispy waffle or cake cone for handheld consumption. The cone provides structural support while allowing the ice cream to be eaten directly, making it a popular portable dessert served at ice cream shops and casual venues.

Pichi-pichi is a Filipino steamed cake made from cassava flour mixed with sugar and lye water, creating a gelatinous texture. The cooked cake is typically rolled in grated coconut and served as a sweet dessert or snack at room temperature.

Torcetti are traditional Italian spiral-shaped cookies made from a simple dough of flour, butter, eggs, and sugar, often flavored with anise or vanilla, that are twisted into knot-like forms and baked until golden. These crisp, sweet biscuits are typically served as an accompaniment to coffee or tea, or as a light dessert, and are particularly popular in the Piedmont region of Italy.

The Crocetta of Caltanissetta is a traditional Sicilian pastry made with a crispy fried or baked shell filled with a creamy mixture typically containing ricotta cheese, chocolate, and candied fruits. This small, oval-shaped confection is commonly served as a dessert or sweet snack in Sicily, particularly associated with the Caltanissetta region.

Mawlid sweets are traditional Egyptian confections prepared by combining flour, ghee, and honey, often incorporating nuts such as almonds or pistachios, then shaped and sometimes coated with sesame seeds or powdered sugar. These sweets are typically served during Mawlid celebrations, particularly during religious festivals commemorating the birth of the Prophet Muhammad, and are distributed among family and community members as part of festive traditions.

Chhola bhuna is a dry curry made from chickpeas that are roasted or lightly cooked with spices including turmeric, cumin, and chili powder until the moisture evaporates and the chickpeas become crispy on the outside. The dish is typically served as a snack or side dish and is popular in Indian cuisine, particularly in North India and among street food vendors.

Rujak kuah pindang is an Indonesian fruit and vegetable salad made with raw papaya, pineapple, cucumber, and bean sprouts, dressed with a sweet and sour tamarind-based sauce (kuah pindang) that typically contains palm sugar, tamarind paste, and chilies. The dish is traditionally served as a refreshing appetizer or light snack, often found at street food stalls and casual dining establishments throughout Indonesia.

Ambur biryani is a rice dish prepared by layering basmati rice with marinated meat (typically mutton or chicken) and cooked together using the dum pukht method, where the pot is sealed to allow steam cooking. The dish is traditionally served with raita, pickle, and shorba (broth), and originates from the town of Ambur in Tamil Nadu, India.

Khao kha mu is a Thai dish consisting of braised pig's trotters served over rice, with the meat stewed until tender in a savory sauce typically made with soy sauce and aromatics. The dish is traditionally accompanied by pickled vegetables and is commonly served as a breakfast or lunch item in Thailand.

Wafer cookies are thin, crisp baked goods made from a simple batter of flour, sugar, eggs, and butter that is cooked between two heated plates or in a specialized wafer iron. They are commonly served as standalone snacks, with coffee or tea, or layered with cream filling as a dessert component.

Fried and pickled herring is prepared by first frying Baltic herring fillets and then preserving them in a vinegar-based brine with spices such as onions, bay leaves, and peppercorns. This dish is commonly served cold as an appetizer or part of a smorgasbord in Nordic and Eastern European cuisine.

Koi macher paturi is a Bengali fish preparation where pieces of koi fish are marinated in a paste of coconut, mustard seeds, and spices, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed or baked until cooked through. The dish is traditionally served as a main course in Bengali cuisine, with the banana leaf imparting subtle flavor while keeping the fish moist during cooking.

Macher muro diye khichudi is a Bengali rice and lentil dish cooked with fish head, where the fish head is simmered together with rice, lentils, and spices until the ingredients break down into a soft, porridge-like consistency. The dish is typically served as a comforting one-pot meal, often prepared in Bengali households as a way to utilize fish heads while creating a nutritious, easily digestible preparation.

Cheese fondue is prepared by melting cheese, typically Gruyère, Emmental, or a blend, with white wine and seasonings in a communal pot kept warm over a flame. Diners dip cubes of bread into the melted cheese using long forks, making it a traditional Swiss dish commonly served as an appetizer or main course.

Goulash is a stew made by braising beef or other meat with onions, paprika, and various vegetables such as potatoes and carrots until tender, typically simmered for several hours to develop deep flavors. The dish is traditionally served hot in bowls, often with sour cream and bread, and remains a staple comfort food in Russian and Central European cuisine.

Poularde demi-deuil is a classical French preparation where a whole chicken is poached with vegetables and aromatics, then sliced and traditionally presented with truffles inserted under the skin before cooking to create a distinctive appearance. The dish is typically served hot with the poaching broth or a complementary sauce, representing a refined presentation of poached poultry from French haute cuisine.

Thalassery biryani is a rice dish from Kerala, India, made by layering parboiled rice with meat (typically chicken or mutton) that has been marinated in yogurt and spices, then slow-cooked together with ghee and aromatics like bay leaves and cinnamon. The dish is traditionally served with raita, pickle, and shorba (broth), and is characterized by its use of Thalassery rice and a relatively higher proportion of meat to rice compared to other biryani varieties.

Selat solo is an Indonesian salad originating from Solo (Surakarta) in Central Java, composed of blanched vegetables such as bean sprouts, cabbage, and long beans combined with hard-boiled eggs and served with a peanut sauce dressing. The dish is typically served at room temperature as a side dish or light meal, often accompanying rice or as part of a larger Indonesian meal spread.

Molten banana is an Indonesian dessert consisting of banana wrapped in a thin pastry or batter coating that is deep-fried until golden, creating a crispy exterior with a soft, warm banana interior. It is typically served as a sweet snack or dessert, often accompanied by chocolate sauce, caramel, or condensed milk for dipping.

Arsik is an Indonesian fish dish from South Sumatra, traditionally prepared by simmering fish in a broth made with turmeric, galangal, garlic, shallots, and other spices until the fish is tender and infused with flavor. The dish is typically served hot with rice and is characteristic of Palembang cuisine, known for its rich, aromatic broth.

Asam pedas is a sour and spicy Indonesian soup or curry made by simmering fish or seafood with tamarind paste, chilies, and aromatic spices such as galangal, garlic, and shallots. The dish is typically served hot with rice and is characteristic of the Malay Archipelago, particularly associated with Malaysian and Indonesian coastal regions.

Lupis is an Indonesian sweet snack made from glutinous rice that is cooked with coconut milk and brown sugar, then wrapped in banana leaves in a cylindrical shape and steamed until set. The resulting sticky rice cake is typically sliced and served as a dessert or afternoon snack, often accompanied by grated coconut or palm sugar syrup.

Karedok is an Indonesian salad from West Java made with raw or lightly blanched vegetables such as cabbage, bean sprouts, long beans, and cucumber, dressed with a peanut sauce made from ground peanuts, garlic, chilies, and other seasonings. The dish is typically served at room temperature as a side dish or light meal, often accompanied by fried tofu and shrimp crackers.

Fried plantain is prepared by slicing plantains and deep-frying them in oil until golden and crispy on the outside while remaining soft inside. This dish is commonly served as a side, appetizer, or snack across Latin American, African, and Caribbean cuisines, often accompanied by dipping sauces or eaten plain with salt.

A garden salad is prepared by combining fresh raw vegetables such as lettuce, tomatoes, cucumbers, and carrots, typically dressed with vinaigrette or another light dressing. It is commonly served as a starter or side dish in international cuisine.

Buntil is an Indonesian dish consisting of taro leaves stuffed with a mixture of grated cassava, coconut, papaya, and dried fish, then wrapped and steamed or boiled until cooked through. It is typically served as a main course or side dish in Indonesian meals, with the tender taro leaves providing a wrapper for the savory filling enriched with coconut milk.

cuisine: cuisine of the western united states
tags: cuisine of the western united states
description: Crab Louie is a composed salad featuring fresh crab meat served on a bed of lettuce with hard-boiled eggs, tomatoes, avocado, and olives, typically dressed with a Louie dressing made from mayonnaise, ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, and lemon juice. The dish originated in the western United States and is traditionally served chilled as a lunch or light dinner entrée.

Torta delle rose is a traditional Italian cake from Emilia-Romagna made with a pastry base layered with jam, typically apricot, and topped with a meringue or icing layer. The cake is baked until golden and often dusted with powdered sugar, served as a dessert or sweet treat during celebrations and special occasions.

Manchow soup is a Indo-Chinese preparation made by sautéing mixed vegetables and aromatics, then simmering them in a savory broth seasoned with soy sauce, garlic, and spices. The soup is typically served hot as an appetizer or starter course, often garnished with fried noodles or crispy fried onions for added texture.

Cantonese slow-cooked soup is prepared by simmering a combination of proteins (such as chicken, pork, or seafood), dried ingredients (including mushrooms, goji berries, and Chinese herbs), and vegetables in broth for several hours to extract flavors and nutrients. The soup is typically served hot in bowls as a nourishing dish, often as part of a meal or as a traditional remedy in Cantonese cuisine.

Ilish jeere bhapa is a steamed hilsa fish dish prepared by wrapping the fish with a spice paste containing cumin seeds (jeere), mustard oil, and traditional Bengali spices, then cooking it in a closed container or banana leaf. The dish is typically served as a main course in Bengali cuisine, particularly during the hilsa season, and is accompanied by rice.

Pork skin is prepared by cleaning and cooking pork rind until the outer layer becomes crispy and the underlying collagen transforms into gelatin. It is commonly served as a snack, appetizer, or ingredient in soups and stews across various cuisines, often seasoned with salt, spices, or sauce.

The Classic Combo is a sampler platter featuring fried mozzarella sticks, Buffalo wings, a cheese quesadilla, and tortilla chips served with both artichoke dip and a secondary dipping sauce. The dish is typically served as an appetizer or shareable starter, with items prepared by frying or pan-cooking and arranged together on a single plate.

The McVeggie is a vegetarian burger sandwich consisting of a flame-grilled patty made from vegetables and grains, typically served on a toasted bun with lettuce, tomato, onions, and condiments. It is prepared as a meat-free alternative to standard beef burgers and served as a main item in quick-service restaurant settings.

Waffles are prepared by pouring batter into a heated waffle iron that creates a grid pattern, resulting in a crispy exterior and fluffy interior made from flour, eggs, milk, and butter. They are typically served hot as a breakfast or dessert item, often topped with sweet accompaniments such as syrup, jam, whipped cream, or fresh fruit.

Vegan chicken noodle soup is prepared by simmering plant-based chicken substitute with noodles in a savory broth made from vegetable stock, typically seasoned with herbs and aromatics like onion and garlic. The dish is traditionally served hot as a comfort soup, commonly consumed as a light meal or appetizer.

Romeu and Julieta is a Brazilian dessert consisting of sliced cheese and goiabada (guava paste) arranged together on a plate, typically served as a simple pairing where the contrast between creamy, mild cheese and sweet, dense guava paste creates the flavor profile. The dish is traditionally served at room temperature as a casual dessert or snack, often accompanying coffee or as part of a cheese course.

Bekasang is an Indonesian condiment made from fermented shrimp paste combined with spices, garlic, and chilies, traditionally prepared through a lengthy fermentation process. It is typically served as a pungent accompaniment to rice dishes and meals, functioning similarly to sambal or other flavor-enhancing pastes in Indonesian cuisine.

Es buah is an Indonesian dessert beverage made by combining shaved ice with a variety of fresh fruits, typically including melon, papaya, and pineapple, along with sweet syrup and sometimes condensed milk. The mixture is served chilled in a glass and is commonly consumed as a refreshing treat during hot weather or as a dessert after meals.

Nasi padang is an Indonesian rice dish served with multiple small portions of curried vegetables, proteins, and sambals arranged around a central mound of steamed rice. The dish originated in West Sumatra and is typically served as a complete meal at casual restaurants and food stalls, with diners selecting from the available accompaniments.

Danbing is a Chinese crepe made by spreading a thin batter on a hot griddle, then folding in a savory filling typically consisting of eggs, scallions, and crispy wonton or youtiao strips before rolling or folding. It is commonly eaten as a breakfast or street food item, served hot and often with a side of soy sauce or sweet bean sauce for dipping.

Pizza quattro stagioni is an Italian pizza traditionally divided into four quarters, each topped with different seasonal ingredients such as artichokes, olives, ham, and mushrooms to represent the four seasons. The pizza is baked in an oven until the crust is golden and the toppings are cooked through, typically served whole or sliced as a single portion.

Boal machher dom is a Bengali fish curry where boal fish is cooked slowly in a sealed pot with spices, onions, and a paste of ginger-garlic, resulting in a tender preparation with concentrated flavors. The dish is traditionally served with rice as part of Bengali meals, particularly during special occasions and family dinners.

aliases: ilish machh er dim er jhuri
description: Ilish machher dim bhuna is a Bengali dish made by pan-frying hilsa fish eggs (roe) with spices until they develop a golden, slightly crispy exterior while remaining moist inside. The preparation typically involves tempering mustard oil with onions, green chilies, and spices before adding the roe, and is commonly served as a side dish or appetizer with rice and dal in Bengali cuisine.

Biryani is a rice dish prepared by layering partially cooked rice with marinated meat (typically chicken, lamb, or goat), onions, and aromatics such as saffron, cardamom, and bay leaves, then cooking together in a sealed pot using the dum pukht (slow-cooking) method. It is traditionally served as a complete meal, often accompanied by raita, pickles, and fresh herbs like cilantro.

Tzimmes is a Jewish stewed dish made by slowly cooking root vegetables such as carrots, sweet potatoes, and parsnips with dried fruits like prunes or apricots, honey, and spices until tender. It is traditionally served as a side dish during Jewish holidays, particularly Rosh Hashanah and Passover, and can include brisket or other meat in some regional variations.

Baklava is a layered pastry made by alternating sheets of phyllo dough with chopped nuts (typically pistachios, walnuts, or almonds) and butter, then baked until golden and crisp. After baking, the pastry is cut into diamonds or triangles and soaked with a sweet syrup made from honey, sugar, and spices such as cinnamon, serving as a dessert in Middle Eastern and Mediterranean cuisines.

Dhokla is a steamed cake made from a fermented batter of chickpea flour mixed with ginger, chili pepper, and coriander seeds, leavened with sodium bicarbonate to create a light, spongy texture. It is typically served as a breakfast or snack item, often accompanied by a tempering of mustard oil, curry leaves, and chili peppers drizzled over the top.

Kadyos, baboy, kag langka is a Filipino stew combining pigeon peas (kadyos), pork (baboy), and young jackfruit (langka) simmered together with garlic, onions, and broth until tender. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice and is common in Filipino home cooking and regional cuisines.

Arròs del Senyoret is a Valencian rice dish prepared by cooking short-grain rice with seafood stock, saffron, and typically includes peeled shrimp, mussels, and sometimes other shellfish, with the seafood already removed from shells before serving for ease of eating. The dish is traditionally served in a wide, shallow pan and represents a refined variation of paella designed for diners who prefer not to handle shells at the table.

Gỏi cuốn are spring rolls made by wrapping rice paper around a filling of rice vermicelli, lettuce, herbs including Thai basil, and protein such as pork and shrimp. They are typically served fresh and chilled as an appetizer, often accompanied by a dipping sauce.

Beans with palm oil is a West African dish made by cooking beans, typically black-eyed peas or kidney beans, with red palm oil as the primary fat, often with onions, tomatoes, and spices. The dish is commonly served as a side dish or main course, particularly in Nigerian and other West African cuisines, and can be prepared with or without meat.

Suya is a Nigerian grilled meat skewer made by marinating beef, chicken, or other proteins in a spiced peanut-based paste containing ground peanuts, chili peppers, ginger, garlic, and various spices, then grilling over charcoal until charred on the outside. It is typically served as a street food snack or appetizer, often accompanied by sliced onions, tomatoes, and cucumber, and eaten by pulling the meat directly from the wooden skewers.

canonicalName: kancha tentul diye bok machher tok
displayName: Kancha tentul diye bok machher tok
aliases: kancha tentul diye bok machher tok
description: Bok machher tok is a Bengali fish curry prepared with raw tamarind (kancha tentul) as the primary souring agent, combined with fish pieces simmered in a light broth with minimal spices to highlight the tartness. The dish is typically served with steamed rice as part of everyday Bengali meals, showcasing the region's preference for sour fish preparations.

Mexican pizza is prepared by topping a crispy fried tortilla base with seasoned ground meat, refried beans, cheese, and various toppings such as jalapeños, onions, and sour cream. It is typically served as a casual appetizer or main course, often found in Mexican-American restaurants and casual dining establishments.

Mućkalica is a Balkan stew made by sautéing diced meat, potatoes, and onions together in a single pot, typically seasoned with paprika and other spices until the ingredients are softened and caramelized. The dish is traditionally served as a hearty main course, often with bread, and is common throughout Serbia, Bosnia, and surrounding regions.

Mi krop is a Thai dish made with crispy fried rice vermicelli that is tossed with a sweet and tangy tamarind-based sauce, typically combined with shrimp, chicken, or tofu along with vegetables and garnishes such as lime, chilies, and fresh herbs. The dish is served as a main course at room temperature or slightly warm and is characterized by its contrasting textures between the crispy noodles and the moist, flavorful sauce.

Nasi bakar is an Indonesian rice dish prepared by grilling or baking seasoned cooked rice, typically mixed with ingredients such as eggs, vegetables, meat, and spices until it develops a charred exterior. The dish is traditionally served as a complete meal, often accompanied by sambal, pickled vegetables, and fresh herbs.

Bata machher paturi is a Bengali preparation where fish (typically catfish) is marinated in a spice paste containing mustard, turmeric, and chili, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed or baked until cooked through. The dish is traditionally served as a main course, with the banana leaf wrapping imparting a subtle flavor while keeping the fish moist during cooking.

Pabda machher paturi is a Bengali preparation where pabda fish (a freshwater catfish) is marinated in a spice paste containing mustard, turmeric, and green chilies, then wrapped in banana leaves and steamed or baked until cooked through. The dish is typically served as a main course with rice, with the banana leaf imparting a subtle flavor to the tender, aromatic fish.

Stuffed peppers are prepared by hollowing out bell peppers and filling them with a mixture of ground meat, rice, onions, and spices, then baking until the peppers are tender. This dish is a traditional staple of Macedonian cuisine, typically served as a main course, often accompanied by yogurt or a tomato-based sauce.

Gujeolpan is a Korean nine-section dish consisting of a divided wooden or ceramic plate filled with seasoned vegetables, beef, egg, and other ingredients, each arranged in its own compartment. The components are typically mixed with soy sauce and served at room temperature as an appetizer or side dish, often accompanied by thin crepes or rice.

Fufu is prepared by boiling cassava root until tender, then pounding or mashing it into a smooth, dough-like paste, often with added water to achieve the desired consistency. It is typically served as a starch-based side dish or main component of meals in West African cuisine, often accompanied by soups or stews for dipping.

Pepes is an Indonesian dish where ingredients such as fish, shrimp, or other proteins are wrapped in banana leaves along with spices and aromatics, then steamed or grilled until cooked through. The banana leaf wrapping infuses the dish with subtle flavor while keeping the contents moist, and it is typically served as a main course with rice.

Lokma is a fried pastry made from a simple dough of flour and other basic ingredients, typically prepared by piping small portions into hot oil until golden. These bite-sized pieces are traditionally served warm, often drizzled with honey or syrup and sometimes dusted with cinnamon or nuts, as a popular street food and dessert throughout Turkey and the broader Mediterranean region.

Balkenbrij is a Dutch and Belgian spiced meatloaf made from pork, pork liver, and fat mixed with breadcrumbs, spices, and sometimes offal, then baked in a loaf form. It is typically sliced and served cold or at room temperature as a charcuterie item, often accompanied by bread, pickles, or mustard.

Oxtail is prepared by braising or stewing the tail of cattle for several hours until the meat becomes tender and the collagen-rich bones release gelatin into the cooking liquid. The dish is typically served in a rich, savory gravy or sauce with vegetables such as carrots, onions, and celery, and is common in African, Caribbean, and European cuisines.

Porrusalda is a Basque soup made by simmering leeks, potatoes, and salt cod in broth until tender, then finished with a mixture of garlic and parsley fried in olive oil. The dish is traditionally served as a hearty first course or main course, particularly during winter months in the Basque Country.

Lamajun is a thin, crispy flatbread topped with a spiced meat mixture (typically lamb or beef), onions, tomatoes, and herbs, then baked in an oven until the crust is golden and the topping is cooked through. It is traditionally served hot, often rolled or folded, and commonly accompanied by fresh vegetables and lemon wedges as a casual meal or appetizer in Middle Eastern and Turkish cuisine.

Crab cakes are formed patties made from lump crab meat mixed with breadcrumbs, mayonnaise, eggs, and seasonings, then pan-fried or baked until golden brown. Typically served as an appetizer or main course, often accompanied by tartar sauce or lemon wedges.
